name: Suntory  Yamazaki Cask Umeshu (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
The Suntory plum wine barrel finish is the result 
of a luxurious blend of raw liquor and finished 
with an altitude number, which achieves the 
thickness of the taste that is unaffected by 
soda. 
The plum liqueur is aged in Suntory's whiskey 
barrels and is blended with a hidden flavor. The 
next-generation plum liqueur is enjoyed from a 
meal that matches soda, with a rich taste 
derived from barrels and western sake. 
The label is illustrated with the bottle and barrel 
for a fresh and elegant design that combines 
the fruit with the motif of plum. You can also 
enjoy soda-splitting and rocks and water split.
We recommend a 1:2 ratio of plum wine to soda

name: Guantanamera  Cristales (5 Sticks per Box)
desc_en_short: Guantanamera make a great introduction to Cuban Cigars. As with a good Malt Cuban Cigars are an acquired taste and can often prove overpowering to the less experienced Cigar smoker. The Guantanamera have been rolled using the highly regarded leaves from the Vuelta Arriba region of Cuba but the leaves have not been aged for as long, which means they have a less intense flavour and remain enjoyable for the less experienced, whilst giving a good insight into the flavours that make Cuban cigars so famous. The Guantanamera are machine made, which keeps the price tag more reasonable.

name: Punch  Petit Coronation (Aluminum Tube)
desc_en_short: These little coronas exhibit great amounts of smoke right from the first draw. The start is cedar and oak with a floral hint. In the middle I found more woody notes with a vague citrus flavour and a sweet bean in there that I could not pin down, dammit! Leather taste is starting to build. The last third still exhibits some mild floral notes but with a heavy leather and tobacco end.
desc_en_full: These cigars have always had good construction in my experience. I have smoked at least 20 of these and though I have had to adjust the burn on occasion, I have never had to relight one. Most are self correcting. Canoeing is never found. The draw is uniform across the whole lot I have smoked, that is, never loose, never tight. Be wary of where you cut these though as the cap can catch you out if you cut just that little too far.

name: Montecristo  Puritos (5 Sticks per Box)
desc_en_short: The Montecristo Puritos remains true to the brand, retaining the typical characteristics of the factory's cigars: tobaccos sourced exclusively from the Vuelta Abajo region, with expressive, even aromas.
desc_en_full:
In 1913, Benjamin Menéndez Garcia took control of the Parra cigar factory, where he had been working for some time. After renaming it Menéndez y Cia. In 1923, he surrounded himself with members of his family and a talented cigar maker, "Pepe" Garcia. All the elements were now in place for the Montecristo adventure to begin.

However, it wasn't until 1935 and the purchase of the Particulares factory that Montecristo was born. The brand was built on simple elements, a luxurious yet sober presentation and impeccable craftsmanship. Success followed, and in 1937, Alonzo Menéndez bought the factory H. Upmann and transferred all production there. Montecristo became so well known that in 1955, the Bishop of Havana came to bless the factory.

It was also with Montecristo that Alfred Dunhill created its famous line, Montecristo Dunhill Seleccion Suprema. Montecristo remains one of the most widely distributed Cuban brands in the world.

name: Cohiba  Short (10 Sticks per Box)
desc_en_short: An exciting new size from one of the most prestigious cigar brands has joined the family of Cuban Minis, Puritos and Clubs. The Cohiba Short combines the length of a Cuban Mini with the width of a Puritos and is machine-made in Cuba using tobaccos from the same region reserved for Habanos.
desc_en_full: Cohiba Cigars were introduced in 1968, and quickly became the flagship brand of the Cuban cigar industry. Developed initially as a medium bodied protocol cigar for presentation only by officials of the Cuban government, Cohiba cigars were marketed widely beginning in 1982. Originally 3 cigars were introduced in 1982 and amalgamated into the Classic range introduced in 1989. The initial sizes were the Cohiba Lancero, the Corona Especial and the Panetela, with the Esplendido, Robusto and Exquisito added in 1989.

name: Fever-Tree  Premium Refreshingly Light Indian Tonic Water (200 ml) (Pack 24 Bottles)
desc_en_short: On the nose, evident effervescence with a distinct touch of lemon/citrus. The palate is greeted with the signature Fever Tree burst of fizz, but when it clears, there’s not much else there. There’s only a tinge of bitterness and maybe a slight citric note. It’s clean, and has a touch of bitterness, but it just doesn’t deliver much on its own. It’s not wildly different from the flavor of their club soda, which while delivering plenty of effervescence, it just lacks in flavor and character.
desc_en_full:
Tonics and Calories

For those who are counting the calories in their gin and tonics, it's worth beginning with the gin. Gin comes in at about 100 (give or take) calories per jigger (1.5 oz. pour) of a 40 proof spirit. Like a stronger gin? Then make that about 115 calories for that same 1.5 oz pour. Having two shots in your G&T? Well let's call that a cool 230 from the starting point. Ready to add tonic? Ready to add a light tonic?

Fortunately, there are plenty of tonic waters out there which eschew sweetness without cutting flavor. Quinine, the primary flavor character, adds nearly no calories to the drink. Which is why, in terms of judging tonics, that calories have little effect on how good (or not good) the final product is. Flavor can be added independent of the sweetness. While some will prefer a sweeter tonic, that's fine. And some (like me) prefer a less sweet tonic. So perhaps that's why I find Fever Tree's Naturally Light Indian Tonic Water to be a little underwhelming. Simply because while it does manage to deliver on the low calorie count (and if you're adding 2 oz of gin to this 7oz. of tonic, we're up to 265 calories), it doesn't deliver as much flavor.

Testing Note.

On the nose, evident effervescence with a distinct touch of lemon/citrus. The palate is greeted with the signature Fever Tree burst of fizz, but when it clears, there's not much else there. There's only a tinge of bitterness and maybe a slight citric note. It's clean, and has a touch of bitterness, but it just doesn't deliver much on its own. It's not wildly different from the flavor of their club soda, which while delivering plenty of effervescence, it just lacks in flavor and character.

One to watch.

name: Two Hands  Waterfall Block Shiraz Adelaide
desc_en_short:
Waterfall Block is the newest addition to the Single Vineyard stable, and a very unique and interesting vineyard, close to the Adelaide CBD.

Waterfall Block is complexing steep site that needs to be seen to be believed: It has many short steep rows that are protected from the early morning light from the highest point of the Mount Lofty Ranges behind and again by the searing late day sun from a steep hillside to the north. This is an amazing micro climate that allows the low-yielding vines to ripen at their leisure with a canopy that keeps moving along with minimal stress.

name: La Braccesca By Antinori  Rosso di Montepulciano "Sabazio" DOC
food_matching: Pasta, Pizza, Roasted Chicken, Pork, Medium Cheese
desc_en_short: Sabazio is the fresh, approachable Rosso di Montepulciano from Antinori's La Braccesca estate — a joyful, early-drinking Sangiovese (Prugnolo Gentile) with vibrant cherry, spice, and earthy warmth, ready to enjoy now with classic Tuscan food.
desc_en_full:
La Braccesca is Antinori's estate in Montepulciano — the historic Tuscan town whose Vino Nobile is one of Italy's DOCG greats. Sabazio is the estate's Rosso di Montepulciano, the younger, more accessible appellation made from the same Sangiovese clone — here called Prugnolo Gentile — that goes into the Nobile.

Rosso di Montepulciano wines are by law made with shorter ageing than the Nobile, resulting in a wine that expresses the grape's fresh, joyful side rather than its austere structure. Sabazio delivers exactly this: vivid ruby colour, an expressive nose of fresh cherry, plum, dried herbs, earthy minerals, and warm spice, followed by a palate that is medium-bodied with bright acidity, gentle tannins, and a clean, satisfying finish.

This is a wine made for the Tuscan table — bistecca, pasta al ragù, grilled meats, roast chicken, and aged Pecorino. At its best slightly young, drunk within 3–5 years of vintage.

name: Haras de Pirque By Antinori  Cabernet Sauvignon Albis
flavor_tags: Blackcurrant, Dark Fruit, Eucalyptus, Graphite, Mineral, Cedar, Plum, Game, Oak
food_matching: Red Meat, Grilled Steak, Lamb, Hard Cheese, BBQ
desc_en_short: Albis is the flagship wine of Haras de Pirque, Antinori's prestigious Chilean estate in the Maipo Alto — a powerful, refined Cabernet Sauvignon blend showing the best of the Andes' influence: dark fruit concentration, elegant structure, and mineral depth.
desc_en_full:
Haras de Pirque is Antinori's joint venture with the Matte family on a historic horse-breeding estate in the Maipo Alto, one of Chile's most prestigious wine zones at the foot of the Andes. Albis is the estate's flagship wine — the result of bringing Antinori's Tuscan winemaking philosophy to one of South America's greatest terroirs.

Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant with complementary Carmenère, the wine is aged in French oak barriques. The Maipo Alto's unique combination of altitude, Andean soils, and extreme diurnal temperature variation creates wines of remarkable concentration and freshness. Deep ruby-purple, Albis opens on intense aromas of blackcurrant, plum, eucalyptus, cedar, graphite, and a distinctive Andean mineral quality. The palate is full-bodied and structured, with fine-grained tannins, vibrant acidity, and a long, complex finish.

Pair with prime beef cuts, lamb, game, and mature hard cheeses. A wine that rewards 5–10 years of cellaring.

name: F.X. Pichler  Riesling Burgstall
desc_en_short:
The Burgstall vineyard refers to a crag bedrock plateau towering some 30 metres, bordering on Unterloiben to the east. The name goes back to a small fortification that was built in the Middle Ages to defend livestock against pillages and the site was first mentioned in a document in 1248. It is also assumed that the first settlement “Liupna” was located here, from which the village name Loiben may derive.

This sun-drenched and well-circulated vineyard is situated at the westernmost end of this rock terrace and the soils consist of pure gneiss and granite, with a shallow topsoil of sand.

name: Louis Latour  Puligny-Montrachet
desc_en_short: Home to some of the world's most extraordinary Chardonnays including the famous Montrachet, Chevalier-Montrachet, and Bâtard-Montrachet, Puligny-Montrachet is the 216 hectare geographical middleman of the white wine villages, bordered by Meursault to the north and Chassagne-Montrachet to the south. name: Monin  Passionfruit (700 ml)
desc_en_short: MONIN Passion Fruit syrup is particularly enhanced with other exotic flavours such as mango. One of my favourite uses is with iced tea : mudde lemon slices and MONIN Passion Fruit syrup and top mix with fresh brewed tea.
desc_en_full:
Origin

The tropical fruit, a passion fruit is about the size of an egg with a brittle purple or yellow shell encasing crunchy seeds and soft juicy flesh bursting with a strong, fragrant flavour.

Native to Brazil, Paraguay and Northern Argentina, passion fruit is so-called because it is a vine species of passion flower, also known as the 'purple granadilla' in South Africa. Both the seeds and the pulp are used in salads and puddings such as cheesecake and pavlova. Passion fruit is also delicious in drinks, jellies and mousses.

For The taste of the tropics, add the fruity aroma and sweet and sour taste of MONIN Passion Fruit syrup to cocktails, sodas, lemonades, fruit punches and smoothies.

name: Bodegas Garzon  Reserva Albarino
wine_color: A pale yellow colour with greenish reflections
desc_en_short: Pale yellow with greenish reflections, this Albariño is intense in the nose, with peach and citrus notes. The freshness and minerality mid-palate is superb, with remarkable acidity and a round, crisp finish. Excellent pairings for this wine are cod with cockles, citrus prawn salad, or traditional grilled seafood with a warm mango salad and beans. It is delightfully well suited to Asian and Peruvian cuisine, ceviche, or a fresh tuna salad.
desc_en_full:
Bodega Garzón is close to Punta del Este, La Barra, and Jose Ignacio – Uruguayan Paradise at its best, with mesmerizing landscapes.

Located 18km from the Atlantic Ocean, our estate has more than 1,000 small vineyard blocks covering its hillside slopes, which benefit from varying microclimates, different levels of humidity, and an intense canopy management. These factors allow the vines to develop with maximum exposure to the sun providing rich, expressive fruit. The vineyards are surrounded by lush forests, rocky soils, granite boulders, and naturally occurring palm trees.

Winemaker Notes

The  harvest was a winemakers dream in terms of quality. Quantity however was down from the previous season due to a rough growing season. name: Antinori  Chianti Classico Riserva "Marchese" Tignanello DOCG
flavor_tags: Mineral, Leather, Tobacco, Cherry, Earthy, Cedar, Plum
food_matching: Pasta, Pizza, Roasted Chicken, Pork, Medium Cheese
desc_en_short: The Marchese Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva is one of Tuscany's most prestigious single-estate Riservas — deep, complex Sangiovese from the historic Tignanello estate with concentrated dark cherry, tobacco, cedar, and the firm mineral backbone of Chianti Classico at its finest.
desc_en_full:
The Marchese Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva represents the pinnacle of the family's Chianti Classico production, made only in the finest vintages from select parcels of the Tignanello estate. This is Sangiovese pushed to its fullest, most noble expression — powerful yet refined, structured yet harmonious.

Aged in French barriques followed by extended bottle maturation, the wine develops extraordinary complexity over time. Deep ruby-garnet in colour, it opens on a rich, layered nose of concentrated morello cherry, dried plum, tobacco, cedar, leather, and earthy mineral notes with a hint of iron. The palate is full-bodied and structured, with firm but polished tannins, bright acidity, and a finish of remarkable length that evolves in the glass for hours.

This Riserva rewards patience — it is approachable young but truly shines after 5–10 years of cellaring. Ideal partners: bistecca alla Fiorentina, roast lamb, aged Pecorino or Parmigiano, wild boar ragù.

name: Castello Della Sala By Antinori  Pinot Nero Umbria IGT
flavor_tags: Raspberry, Mushroom, Cherry, Earthy, Rose, Oak
food_matching: Pasta, Pizza, Roasted Chicken, Pork, Medium Cheese
desc_en_short: This Pinot Nero from Antinori's Castello della Sala estate in Umbria is a refined, elegant red — light-to-medium bodied with cherry, raspberry, earthy spice, and silky tannins, showing how well Burgundy's great grape adapts to central Italy's cooler hillside terroir.
desc_en_full:
The Castello della Sala estate in Umbria is better known for white wines — particularly the legendary Cervaro della Sala Chardonnay — but Antinori also produces a Pinot Nero that demonstrates the estate's range and ambition. Planted on the estate's cooler hillside vineyards at altitude, where the diurnal temperature range preserves acidity and aromatic complexity, the Pinot Nero finds an unexpected but convincing home.

Fermented in open-top tanks with gentle extraction to preserve delicacy, and aged in French oak barriques, the wine is translucent ruby in colour — almost Burgundian in appearance. The nose is subtle and refined: morello cherry, raspberry, dried rose, subtle earthiness, and warm spice. On the palate it is light-bodied and silky, with vibrant acidity, fine tannins, and an elegant, long finish.

Serve slightly cool (around 16°C) with roast chicken, duck breast, salmon, mushroom-based dishes, and mild charcuterie.

name: Tenuta Guado al Tasso By Antinori  Il Bruciato Bolgheri
flavor_tags: Blackberry, Rosemary, Cherry, Violet, Cedar, Thyme, Oak
food_matching: Red Meat, Grilled Steak, Lamb, Hard Cheese, BBQ
desc_en_short: Il Bruciato is the approachable entry point into Antinori's prestigious Guado al Tasso estate in Bolgheri — a warm, generously fruited coastal Tuscan red with dark cherry, Mediterranean herbs, spice, and smooth polished tannins.
desc_en_full:
Tenuta Guado al Tasso is one of the jewels of the Antinori empire — a vast coastal estate in Bolgheri where the proximity of the Tyrrhenian Sea, the unique gravelly soils, and outstanding viticulture have established it as one of Italy's great wine properties. Il Bruciato — named after a locality on the estate — is designed as the most accessible expression of the Guado al Tasso terroir.

A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Syrah, aged in French barriques, the wine captures Bolgheri's warm, Mediterranean spirit in an open, generous style. Ruby in colour with violet highlights, it opens on aromas of dark cherry, blackberry, rosemary, thyme, and warm spice, with a gentle hint of cedar from the oak. On the palate it is medium-full bodied, with smooth, polished tannins, good acidity, and a fruit-forward, pleasurable finish.

Perfect with grilled meats, lamb, pasta with meat sauces, pizza, and everyday Tuscan food. Enjoyable from release but equally good with 2–4 years of age.

name: Chateau Ducru Beaucaillou  Madame de Beaucaillou Haut -Medoc
flavor_tags: Graphite, Mineral, Tobacco, Cassis, Cedar, Game, Oak
food_matching: Red Meat, Grilled Steak, Lamb, Hard Cheese, BBQ
desc_en_short: Madame de Beaucaillou is the second wine of the legendary Château Ducru-Beaucaillou — a Saint-Julien Second Growth — offering elegant, refined Haut-Médoc character with cassis, cedar, graphite, and the polished structure that defines this prestigious estate.
desc_en_full:
Château Ducru-Beaucaillou is one of the most revered estates in all of Bordeaux — a Deuxième Grand Cru Classé (Second Growth) from Saint-Julien, consistently producing wines of near-First Growth quality at the heart of the Médoc. Madame de Beaucaillou is the estate's second wine, crafted from younger vines and selected lots to offer the Ducru-Beaucaillou style in a more accessible, earlier-drinking format.

Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant with Merlot, the wine is aged in French oak barriques and benefits from the meticulous viticultural standards of the estate. It carries the elegance and precision that defines Saint-Julien — a touch more refined and aromatic than its Pauillac neighbours, with a hallmark combination of cassis, cedar, tobacco, and mineral graphite on the nose. The palate is medium-full bodied, with fine-grained tannins, good structure, and a long, classical finish.

Approachable within 3–5 years of vintage but will reward patience. Pair with lamb, beef, game, and aged hard cheeses.

name: Santa Cristina by Antinori  Orvieto Classico "Campogrande" DOC
flavor_tags: Green Apple, Mineral, Citrus, Acacia, Apple
desc_en_short: Campogrande is Antinori's flagship Orvieto Classico — a fresh, elegant Umbrian white from the Castello della Sala estate, blending Trebbiano and Grechetto for a clean, aromatic wine with citrus, white flowers, and delicate mineral freshness.
desc_en_full:
Orvieto Classico is one of central Italy's oldest and most storied white wine appellations, produced on the volcanic tufa soils of the hills surrounding the ancient hilltop city of Orvieto in Umbria. Antinori's Castello della Sala estate, acquired in 1940, is among the appellation's finest producers, and Campogrande is their benchmark Orvieto Classico.

A blend of Trebbiano Toscano and Grechetto — the traditional Orvieto varieties — vinified in stainless steel to preserve freshness and aromatics. Pale straw with golden highlights, it opens on fresh citrus, green apple, white flowers (acacia, hawthorn), and a subtle mineral quality that speaks of the volcanic terroir. The palate is light-to-medium bodied, clean and crisp, with good acidity and a refreshing, dry finish.

An ideal everyday white — perfect with light pasta, grilled chicken, fish, antipasti, and the simple, honest cuisine of Umbria.

name: Jermann  Vintage Tunina DOC
grape_variety: Chardonnay, Ribolla Gialla, Malvasia, Sauvignon Blanc
flavor_tags: Apricot, Truffle, Mineral, Floral, Mango, Honey, Oak
food_matching: Lobster, Creamy Pasta, Roasted Chicken, Rich Fish
desc_en_short: Vintage Tunina is Jermann's most iconic wine — a complex, age-worthy white blend from Friuli of extraordinary depth and aromatic intensity, combining Sauvignon, Chardonnay, Ribolla Gialla, and Malvasia Istriana into a wine of rare richness and mineral elegance.
desc_en_full:
Vintage Tunina is one of Italy's most celebrated white wines and a benchmark for the entire Friuli-Venezia Giulia appellation. Created by Silvio Jermann in the 1970s, it was among the first Italian whites to prove that the country could produce world-class dry whites capable of ageing for a decade or more.

The blend — Sauvignon Blanc, Chardonnay, Ribolla Gialla, Malvasia Istriana, and Picolit — is harvested late to maximise complexity, then fermented with indigenous yeasts and aged on fine lees for months in large Slavonian oak casks. The result is a wine of remarkable multi-layered complexity: golden amber in colour, it opens on a rich, evolved nose of ripe apricot, mango, white truffle, honey, toasted almonds, and a distinctive floral-mineral thread. The palate is full-bodied and textured, with seamless oak integration, vibrant acidity, and a finish of extraordinary length.

Best enjoyed with 2–3 years of age or more. Pair with lobster bisque, crab, foie gras, truffle risotto, or aged Montasio cheese.

name: Jermann  Chardonnay DOC
flavor_tags: Minerality, Hazelnut, Mushroom, Vanilla, Truffle, Mineral, Creamy, Peach, Mango, Apple
food_matching: Lobster, Creamy Pasta, Roasted Chicken, Rich Fish
desc_en_short: Jermann's Chardonnay is a beautifully crafted Friulian white — barrel-fermented and aged on its lees for added richness, delivering golden apple, peach, toasted oak, and buttery complexity balanced by the region's signature freshness and mineral precision.
desc_en_full:
Jermann is one of Italy's most admired white wine estates, and their Chardonnay is a showcase for the estate's winemaking philosophy: maximum respect for the fruit, thoughtful use of oak, and an unwavering commitment to freshness. From vineyards in Friuli-Venezia Giulia, where the Adriatic and Alpine influences combine to create ideal conditions for aromatic, high-acid whites.

Partially fermented in French oak barriques and aged on fine lees for several months, the wine develops impressive complexity without losing the freshness that defines Jermann's style. Golden straw in colour, it opens on ripe golden apple, peach, mango, hazelnut, and a gentle vanilla from the oak, backed by a subtle, pleasing minerality. The palate is rich and textured, with a creamy, lees-influenced mid-palate and a clean, persistent finish.

Excellent with grilled fish, lobster, veal with cream sauce, pasta with truffle, mushroom risotto, and semi-aged cheeses.

name: Two Hands  Yacca Block Shiraz Eden Valley
desc_en_short:
Yacca Block sits at the top of Menglers Hill and is a prime example of the role sub-regionality plays in serious winemaking, with this vineyard only a 25 minutes drive from the Western Ranges of the Barossa Valley.

The soil here is a mix of both quartz and ironstone and the vineyard’s high altitude allows for slow ripening that leads to a very soft and elegant Shiraz. This vineyard site also supplies Shiraz for Charlie’s Garden, and Riesling for The Boy.

name: Two Hands  Coach House Block Shiraz Barossa
desc_en_short:
The Coach House Block Shiraz comes from an Estate grown single vineyard near the tiny hamlet of Greenock in the Barossa Valley.

Purchased in 2001, the Coach House vineyard (formally known as the Branson Coach House) produces exceptional Barossa Valley Shiraz, but one block amongst the 18 acres of vineyard has continually proven to be special. Known internally as Highway Block, this special block has consistently come up as Single Vineyard quality each vintage, during our blind tasting barrel trials.

name: Two Hands  Ares Shiraz Barossa Valley
desc_en_short: This year Ares is black on black in colour. The immediate bouquet throws you roasted meat, pain grillé, dried herbs, both blue and black berries, sage, warm earth and tea leaf. The palate is silky and soft. It caresses your tongue and then rolls straight to the late palate. This pull the magic act of being a big wine in a small wine’s body: It creeps up on you slowly and effortlessly, Man this is a seriously fun ride! It rolls and and rolls along and the tannins never really seem to dissolve or stop; pure silk is the best way to describe it. In the end you’re left searching for the acidity, fruit and tannins as they are all so welded together. Nothing sticks out in this wine, its Barossa Shiraz at it best. This wine has a very long life ahead of it and it will age effortlessly for 25 year plus. Sit back, pull the cork and enjoy the ride, you will not be disappointed.

wine_color: Cava an elegant and creamy bubble.
style: Macabeo, Xarel·lo Barrica, Xarel·lo And Parellada.
desc_en_short: Traditional method (second fermentation in the bottle). The particularity of this cava consists in the incorporation into the base wine of selected xarelo from our oldest vineyards, which has been fermented and aged in new oak barrels. This feature gives the cava a permanent and creamy bubble. The nose is fruity at the beginning, evolving towards vanilla and smoke. In the mouth you can perceive the unctuousness that refines the cava, followed by the appearance of compote fruit (membrillo and apple), passing through the smoked notes and the vanilla aromas contributed by its light aging in the barrel, its aftertaste is long and elegant.

name: Campari Negroni Set
desc_en_short:
Campari Negroni Set Includes :

Campari Liqueur (750 ml) x 1

Campari is a bitter Italian aperitif. It is a proprietary blend of herbs and spices, is a brilliant red color, and has an extremely unique flavor. It's prominant flavor is that of a strong bitter orange similar to if you were to drop orange bitters directly on your tongue. Served on the rocks or in simple cocktails such as the Campari and Soda and Negroni. It is used in a number of classic cocktails and though it is frequently paired with gin, works well with other base liquors such as whiskey and vodka.

Cinzano Vermouth Rosso (750 ml) x 1

Cinzano Rosso is dark red in colour and wrapped in intense, delicious citrus flavours. Sweet and Fragrant, it finishes with a unique and pleasantly bitter aftertaste. It is exciting, rich and daring. Always serve chilled. Enjoy pure, over ice, or mixed with apple juice and a dash of cinnamon. Try one of our Rosso cocktails.

Bulldog London Dry Gin (750 ml) x 1

A delicious English gin made with poppy, dragon eye, lotus leaves, citrus, almond, lavender and various other botanicals (12 in total). This is four times distilled in copper pot stills, and Bulldog had the highest rating ever received by a gin from Wine Enthusiast Magazine. It was also voted a Top 50 spirit!.

Campari Rock Glass x 2
desc_en_full: Campari is the result of the talent of Gaspare, master drink maker and the founder of the company. Campari is obtained from an hydroalcoholic infusion introducing alcohol and water in a mixture of herbs, plants and fruits. Alcohol, sugar and water are the only known ingredients of its special and secret recipe. Remaining loyal to the original formula and keeping it strictly confidential, is one of the company’s missions. This recipe is why Campari has always been characterized by a peculiar taste and by a brilliant red

name: Macheatazo Espadin (750 ml)
food_matching: Mexican Cuisine, Ceviche, Grilled Fish, Citrus
desc_en_short: Agave Angustifolia / Espadin native to the state of Oaxaca, is the most commonly used agave in mezcal due to its properties of high production, sugars, weight and volume. Notes of this agave vary depending on the region of Oaxaca it is cultivated from. Our Mezcal Machetazo Espadin 45% has floral and fruit note presence with a light silky texture. These tones are influenced by the sub-tropical climate where the distillery is located. The Espadin Agaves reaches maturity and an ideal sugar concentration between 8-12 years and are cooked in an earthen oven releasing aromas of roasted fruit.

name: Sober Spirits  Whisky Non-Alcoholic Beverage (500 ml)
desc_en_short:
Crafted from an authentic 3-year-old Whisky, this spirit has been 'bourbonized' by redistilling it with torrefied wood bark. The aroma of Sober Spirits W 0.0% - Whisky Bourbon alternative is typical of an American Bourbon, woody and intense in flavor with a gourmand Toffee note (chocolate & caramel) sublimated by a delicious hint of vanilla.

For all your cocktails, our Whisky Bourbon Alternative is to be used in the same way and with the same dosage as an alcoholic whisky (4 to 5cl per cocktail).

Although some love it neat, it is a mixology product, and its full potential is revealed in cocktails.

Awarded Non-Alcoholic Spirit of the Year at the prestigious London Spirits competition, it joins his older brother Sober Spirits R 0.0% - Rum alternative, which has already been acclaimed as the best in its category.
desc_en_full:
AROMATIC

The aroma of Sober Spirits W 0.0% - Whisky Bourbon alternative is typical of an American Bourbon, woody and intense in flavor with a gourmand Toffee note (chocolate & caramel) sublimated by a delicious hint of vanilla.

Awarded Non-Alcoholic Spirits of the Year

Prior to its official launch, Sober Spirits W 0.0% - Whisky Bourbon alternative garnered the esteemed title of 'Non-Alcoholic Spirits of the Year 2023' at the prestigious London Spirits Competition. It impressed the judges, securing its place as one of the world's premier non-alcoholic alternatives. This achievement aligns with our Sober Spirits R 0.0% - Rum alternative, which has already been acclaimed as the best in its category for the past 3 years.

A classic ingredient derived from orchids, vanilla is widely used in baking and desserts for its pure, delicate taste.

MONIN Vanilla is one of the most versatile syrup flavours, enhancing almost any drink and creating mouth-watering coffees, hot chocolates, dessert drinks and cocktails.

Colour Slightly amber.

Tasting notes Vanilla pod flavour sublimate in combination with exotic flavours. Powder taste and slight brandy smell.

Beverage Innovation Directors tips MONIN Vanilla syrup is a great complement of nut flavours in latte applications. It also fits perfectly in a tea preparation in addition to berry flavours. I like it very much in after dinner drinks built straight in a glass filled with dark rum or brandy.

Applications

Coffees

Dessert drinks

Milkshakes

Mocktails

Cocktails

Origin Grenadine is by far the most common and versatile sweetener and flavouring in classic bar mixology. As hard as it is to believe, it has nothing similar with pomegranate syrup. Many cocktails contain Grenadine. It imparts a nice red colour and sweet, slightly tart taste that is very popular by children and adults. MONIN Grenadine syrup presents a flavour of red berries with a touch of vanilla and a bright red colour to enhance non alcoholic beverages, cocktails and even desserts, marinades, and several other recipes.

name: Graham's  20 Year Tawny Port
desc_en_short: Graham’s 20 Year Old is the Master Blender’s quest for perfection, capturing the perfect balance between young and aged Port wines. A Port wine perfected for more than twenty year, to delight those in the quest for excellence.
desc_en_full:
Amber, golden tawny color. An excellent bouquet, with a characteristic "nutty" character, such as almonds, and delicious mature fruit with hints of orange peel. Rich, sweet and smooth onthe palate, it is perfectly balanced, with a long and lingering finish.

Graham's 20 Year Old Tawny Port pairs extremely well with vanilla ice cream or crème brulee. Serve slightly chilled to appreciate thefull complexity and sensuous pleasure of this wine.

Port is best served in classic Port wine glassware or white wine glasses. Avoid cordial or liqueur glasses as they are too small to fully appreciate the wine's aromas.

name: Fantini  Gran Cuvee Rose
desc_en_short: Fresh and aromatic wine, good also as aperitif. Pomegranate pink, with intense and persistent aroma, fruity, with hints of ripe cherry, red currant, wild strawberry and raspberry. In the mouth is pleasantly aromatic, well-balanced and fresh. Elegant and persistent perlage. It matches perfectly with all kinds of seafood. Good also served as aperitif. Charm and elegance of authentic SWAROVSKI diamond in a classy bottle of a limited edition.
desc_en_full:
Vinification : Delicate crushing of the berries, in order to keep only the free-run juice. Decantation and fermentation of the clear must for 20 days at 12° C. After that a second fermentation (prise de mousse) in 4.000 lt pressure tanks for 60 days.

Sensory Features : Fresh and aromatic wine, good also as aperitif. Pomegranate pink, with intense and persistent aroma, fruity, with hints of ripe cherry, red currant, wild strawberry and raspberry. In the mouth is pleasantly aromatic, well-balanced and fresh. Elegant and persistent perlage. Charm and elegance of authentic SWAROVSKI diamond in a classy bottle of a limited edition.

Best served with : It matches perfectly with all kinds of seafood. Good also served as aperitif.

Serving Temperature : 8-10°C

name: Fontanafredda  Alta Langa Limited Edition Brut DOCG
desc_en_short: The cuvée is created with a meticulous blend of crus situated at an average altitude of 500m asl, which guarantee a fresh and fruity wine. A minimum maturing period of 30 months conveys perfect aromatic complexity. The colour is straw yellow with green highlights. The perfume is reminiscent of honey, white peaches, grapefruit and basil. The wine has a savoury feel in the mouth, with a persistent sparkle and a sweet, lingering finish.
desc_en_full:
Vinification:

The grapes are harvested into crates with a capacity of about 20 kg and then poured directly into the press, where they are delicately and softly crushed. The must obtained is transferred to temperature-controlled tanks where static decanting takes place, and then ferments in stainless steel tanks at a low temperature (18-20 °C). When fermentation is complete, the wine is kept on the fine lees for a few months. This is when the cuvée is created, uniting the wines from the different crus selected. In spring the wine undergoes a second fermentation, in the bottle, followed by a period of maturing in contact with yeasts, in the cool, dark and protected environment of the cellars, in observance of the strict rules that govern the production of “Spumante Metodo Classico” (Classic Method Sparkling Wine).

History and terroir

Cabernet Sauvignon is, alongside Merlot, the world’s most widely planted variety.

In 2006, it was estimated that 50,000 hectares were planted in France. Cabernet Sauvignon has certainly spread into the Languedoc over the last few years. With low yields it gives wines of good tannin strength and clean aromas.

Tasting Notes

This wine has a deep crimson colour and offers a range of aromas: dark berries, Morello cherries and liquorice. Powerful, with well-rounded tannins, this is a highly complex wine with good length.

name: Monin  French Vanilla (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Strong vanilla powder nose with almond notes.
Long lasting custard vanilla taste
desc_en_full:
Origin Vanilla has traditionally been used by French cooks in many desserts including a delicious vanilla custard which was later called "French Vanilla". MONIN French Vanilla syrup has a perfect custard vanilla taste which prevails against the bitterness of any strong hot or cold cofee for a delectable experience. Enjoy its truly favourful vanilla taste in your cofees and iced lattes, macchiatos and cappuccinos but also in teas and tea lattes.

Colour Clear gold

desc_en_short: Spearmint smell, perfectly balanced taste of cane sugar and spearmint.
desc_en_full:
Origin The famous Mojito cocktail was born in the sultry bars of pre-revolutionary Havana. Skilled bartenders called upon the local abundance of wild mint "yerbabuena", cane sugar, lime and rum to create a refreshing cocktail sensation that flavoured the sensual Cuban nightlife. Yet the time consuming preparation of mojitos involving making simple syrup and thoroughly muddling lime and mint leaves, as well as its hard to find ingredients did not travel well to the international bar scene. With MONIN Mojito Mint, making mojitos is now easy. Just combine with rum, club soda and a squeeze of lime! The Mojito is now a standout favorite among American bars and restaurants patrons. Additionally, MONIN Mojito Mint is a refreshing mint and lime flavour that can be used beyond the bar in countless non-alcoholic applications.

Colour Transparent.

Tasting notes Spearmint smell, perfectly balanced taste of cane sugar and spearmint.

Beverage Innovation Directors tips In addition to the flavourful Mojito that you can create with Monin Mojito mint syrup, I'm really playing with it in many other recipes. I love to mix it with Monin Vanilla syrup in a frozen coffee with milk.

Applications

name: Monin  Raspberry (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Nose of ripe raspberry, slightly acidic, raspberry candy taste.
desc_en_full:
Origin A fragile, soft fruit that doesn't travel well, the exquisite red berry flavour of raspberry is always accessible with MONIN Raspberry syrup.

Instantly recognisable as a popular childhood sweet flavour, most raspberries have a bright pink red colouring. French legend has it that raspberries were originally white, but that one day while the nymph Ida was looking after the infant Zeus and attempting to soothe his crying by showing him some raspberries, she pricked her finger and her blood dyed the fruit red.

A perennial summer flavour, raspberries are traditionally plentiful in midsummer and are used to make mouth-watering desserts such as cheesecake, ice cream, sorbet, trifle and fool.

Conjure exquisite summer dessert cocktails, smoothies, refreshing teas and ice cold milkshakes with the sweet candy raspberry taste of MONIN Raspberry syrup.

Colour Natural colour of raspberry juice.

Tasting notes Nose of ripe raspberry, slightly acidic, raspberry candy taste.

Beverage Innovations Directors tips With sweet, fragrant and subtle aftertaste, an almost-melt-in-your-mouth texture, MONIN Raspberry has a very delicious taste. Try to combine it with MONIN Blackberry syrup and you will feel a wonderful forest touch.

Applications

name: Cavicchioli & Figli  Cavicchioli Lambrusco Emilia Amabile
food_matching: Oysters, Caviar, Fried Foods, Appetizers, Celebration
desc_en_short: Cavicchioli's Lambrusco Emilia Amabile is a lightly sparkling, gently sweet red that captures the heart of Emilian table culture — fresh red cherry, strawberry, and a hint of violets, with soft bubbles and an easy, food-friendly character.
desc_en_full:
Lambrusco Amabile is perhaps the most Italian of wines — a frizzante (lightly sparkling) red with a touch of natural sweetness that has been the everyday table wine of Emilia-Romagna for centuries. Cavicchioli, based in San Prospero near Modena, is one of the region's most trusted producers of this beloved style.

Made from local Lambrusco grape varieties grown on the Po Valley plains, the wine is ruby-violet in colour with a lively pink foam. The nose is fresh and inviting — red cherry, wild strawberry, raspberry, and violet — with a light earthy, almost rustic quality that speaks of its central Italian origins. On the palate it is gently sweet and softly sparkling, with refreshing acidity that keeps it lively and far from cloying.

This is a wine made for food — and specifically for the rich cuisine of Emilia: cured meats, mortadella, Parma ham, grilled sausages, and aged Parmigiano. Serve lightly chilled at around 12°C.

name: Cavicchioli  Lambrusco Grasparossa di Castelvetro Amabile
flavor_tags: Dark Chocolate, Blackberry, Chocolate, Cherry, Violet, Plum
food_matching: Oysters, Caviar, Fried Foods, Appetizers, Celebration
desc_en_short: Grasparossa di Castelvetro is the most structured of the Lambrusco varieties — this Amabile version from Cavicchioli delivers deep ruby colour, intense dark cherry and plum fruit, bold tannins, and gentle sweetness with satisfying effervescence.
desc_en_full:
Among the many Lambrusco varieties grown in Emilia-Romagna, Grasparossa di Castelvetro stands apart for its deeper colour, more pronounced tannins, and richer fruit concentration. Its name — 'red-stemmed' — refers to the distinctive red hue the vine's stems take on in autumn. Grown on the hillsides around Castelvetro di Modena, it produces the most structured and age-worthy of the Lambrusco wines.

Cavicchioli's Amabile version retains a touch of natural sweetness that balances Grasparossa's naturally firm tannins. Deep ruby-violet in colour with a persistent pink-purple foam, it opens on intense aromas of dark cherry, blackberry, dried violets, and a hint of dark chocolate. On the palate it is medium-bodied with firm, grippy tannins, refreshing acidity, and a sweet-fruity finish that is quintessentially Emilian.

The wine of Modena's trattorias — made for zampone, cotechino, tigelle with salami, and the full range of Emilian cured meats. Serve lightly chilled at 10–12°C.

desc_en_full:
Originating in 1882, the Dogliani family winery has been producing wine for over 100 years. The company was renamed Beni di Batasiolo by Fratelli Dogliani in 1978. Named after the vineyard on which the company has it's headquarters, the change was inspired by the owner's belief in cultivating closeness between farmers and their vineyards.

This delicate sparkling wine from Italy's northern region boasts a rare bouquet of berries, rose petals, with a touch of vanilla and a unique effervescence. One small hand-picked grape bursting with italian promise is all it takes to tickle your taste buds and send you on a journey you'll never forget. Our grapes are handled with great care from the moment they are plucked from the vine. We immediately filter, refrigerate and bottle them must at very low temperature until fermentation to ensure the vibrancy of the wines bouquet. 4-18 degrees Celsius should be chilled before drinking.

Wine should be chilled to cure 48 hours before drinking it. Sparkling wine suitable for drinking kosher . With food or drink or snack from the first time in well with mushrooms and seafood. Avoid heavy lime juice and salt for parties.

THE STORY OF JACOB CREEK

Jacob Creek from Australia In 1847, along with the banks of Jacob’s Creek, Johann Gramp planted his first vineyard. name: Chateau Ste. Michelle  Cabernet Sauvignon

desc_en_full:
VINEYARDS

• Sourced from Columbia Valley vineyards in eastern Washington including our Cold Creek, Canoe Ridge Estate and Indian Wells vineyards. • Columbia Valley vineyards lie in the rain shadow of the Cascade Mountains and receive just 6–8 inches of rainfall annually. • Warm, sunny days and cool evening temperatures create ideal conditions for intense aroma and flavor development in the ripening grapes. • Sandy soils with low fertility and low water-holding capacity enable precise control of vine growth.

WINEMAKING

• Ripe grapes were destemmed and inoculated with various strains of yeast to maximize complexity. • Gentle pumpovers during fermentation were used to extract color and flavor and produce soft, supple tannins. • Aged 16 months in American and French oak, 31% new

name: Montgras  Intriga
wine_color: Deep ruby red with purple notes in colour.
desc_en_short: A powerful, concentrated, daring wine, and with an enigmatic character: this is Intriga. Aged for more than 20 months in French barrels, the final blend is enhanced with a touch of Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot.
desc_en_full:
INTRIGA By Montgras

At the feet of the majestic Andes Mountains, lies the Intriga Estate, over millenary alluvial soils planted with vines that average of 30 years and that give us world-class grapes.

Tasting Notes

Deep ruby red with purple notes in colour, with herbal and fruity aromas that blend together well giving it a delicate,elegant nose. Its flavours are of fine and tense texture with soft, round tannins. A nice mixture off at and grip.

Food Pairings

Because of its structure and power, it's a wine appropiate for marbled red meats, fine cheeses and in general, strong foods with high fatty conent such as duck confit, rosemary lamb ribs or oven roasted goat kid.

name: Chateau Lynch Bages  AOC Pauillac 2018
wine_color: Medium to deep garnet-purple in color.
desc_en_short:
The wine of Château Lynch-Bages is part of the lineage of the great Pauillac wines. Blended mostly from Cabernet Sauvignon, it combines structure and finesse, elegance and longevity. It is further distinguished by its opulence. Generous right from its youth, it develops more flavours and greater complexity as it matures.

The affirmation of the Lynch-Bages style came with the arrival of Jean-Charles Cazes to head the estate in 1934. An innovative winemaker, emblematic of a new generation of owners willing to break with tradition and taking risks to ensure truly ripe harvests, he was known for often being the last to harvest in Pauillac. From 1945 onwards, the fame of the château emerged thanks to a series of great vintages. Some, despite being considered difficult to grow in Bordeaux, are particularly successful at Lynch-Bages. His wines are characterized by their deep colour, their tannic structure, their controlled concentration and elegant sensuality. The vintages vinified by André Cazes, and after him Jean-Michel Cazes, confirm this trend.

The style of the wines has refined over the years, gaining suppleness and softness, whilst their consistency has been established over time. Then as now, Château Lynch-Bages has been characterised by its extraordinary qualitative homogeneity. Powerful, elegant and open, vintage after vintage, the wine has acquired greater accuracy, adding distinction to the hedonistic character that made its reputation.

name: Tenuta dell'Ornellaia  Le Volte dell’Ornellaia
desc_en_short: Le Volte Dell'Ornellaia is a saturated red/ruby color and is a blend of 55% Cabernet Sauvignon, 40% Merlot, and 5% Petit Verdot, raised in used barrels for around 10 months. Always a wine leaning into the fruity and approachable side of things, the nose is fruity and fresh with aromas of candied flowers, crushed raspberries, red apples, and fresh stony earth. Medium to full-bodied, it’s very elegant with a joyful ease.
desc_en_full:
VINIFICATION AND AGEING

The different varietals were fermented separately in small steel tanks to maintain their varietal characteristics intact. The alcoholic fermentation was followed by malolactic fermentation, again in steel tanks. The wine was then aged for 10 months, partly in barrique used for Ornellaia and partly in cement tanks, to obtain the perfect balance between tannic structure and an expression of fresh fruit.

name: Felton Road  Pinot Noir Bannockburn
desc_en_short: A real intensity to the perfume of rose and violets, and a potpourri of spice. The palate dances and while it certainly has power and weight, it is light on its feet and feels effortless. Vibrant raspberry, hints of crushed herbs, silky, but with a continuous seam of polished sinew supplying the muscle that keeps it aloft. The frame reveals itself in the finish and hints that the wine will cellar for the long haul and reward such patience fully. But when it is this seductive, it’s hard to see much chance of a lot of it surviving that long!.

Our "Secreto" range of wines has a closely guarded secret related to land, proportions, and balance. Art and terroir have come together to create these wines in which the varieties have much to express.

name: Chateau Le Puy  Emilien
grape_variety: Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Cabernet Sauvignon, Malbec
desc_en_short: The robe is garnet red. The nose is fruity, with ripe red fruit aromas dominated by blackcurrant and redcurrant, accented with roasted almond notes and occasional hints of mushrooms and undergrowth. The palate is well-rounded, full-bodied and precise, with velvety tannins paving the way for a finish is long and enjoyably complex.
desc_en_full:
Chateau Le Puy  Emilien

The fruit of a plot of vines planted with 85% Merlot, 7% cabernet franc, 6% cabernet sauvignon, 1% malbec and 1% carménère,, Emilien is matured in oak barrels and casks for 24 months.

The robe is garnet red, with hints of ruby in the younger wines and a touch of orange in the older vintages, with a diaphanous onion-skin finesse to the oldest bottles. The nose is fruity, with ripe red fruit aromas dominated by blackcurrant and redcurrant, accented with roasted almond notes and occasional hints of mushrooms and undergrowth. The palate is well-rounded, full-bodied and precise, with velvety tannins paving the way for a finish is long and enjoyably complex. A wine of great finesse, with smooth tannins in its youth but also the potential to age and grow for several decades, revealing the full depth and character of this prestigious terroir.

A wine blessed with superb longevity, which will serve as a perfect partner for traditional rustic dishes and delicate, complex cuisine alike. A small miracle of elegance and refinement.

Aroma

Black Cherry, Blackberry, Cedar Oak

Taste

Dry and almost medium tannin, medium to high body with medium finish

Complimentary

Grilled beef, Barbecue lamb, Hard cheese

THE STORY OF JACOB CREEK

Jacob Creek from Australia In 1847, along with the banks of Jacob’s Creek, Johann Gramp planted his first vineyard. His vision was to create great tasting wines that reflect the true character of the grapes and the land. To this day we live and breathe this vision by producing wines that showcase how each grape variety expresses itself when shaped by the soil and the sun of Australia.

Awarded with 3 gold medals from international wine and spirits competition. Such as, 2014 WSA Wine Challenge, 2013 China Wine & Spirits Awards and 2014 Pacific Rim International Wine Compettition.

name: Eugenio Collavini  Schioppettino Turian DOC
wine_color: The deep ruby is still tinged with purple.
desc_en_short: The deep ruby is still tinged with purple. Intriguingly concentrated, evolved aromatics foreground varietal notes of forest fruits, raspberries, black pepper and spices. Soft tannins on entry with freshness that accompanies progression through to the lingering finale. Food matchings with feathered game, wood pigeon, duck. Pappardelle pasta in hare sauce, wild boar, roe deer. Serve at 16-18 °C.
desc_en_full: The Schioppettino grapes that are handpicked and collected in trays, are spread out over mats that are placed in drying lofts where their flavours and body are further enhanced. This is followed by destemming and fermentation on the skins for about 3 weeks. For the next 18 months that follow, the wine is then fined in Allier French oak barriques. This is a wine that accompanies noble wild fowl dishes. Pappardelle with wild hare, boar and roe deer meat ragùs.

name: Wolf Blass  Black Label 44th Cabernet Shiraz Malbec
desc_en_short: The elite parcels of Shiraz and Cabernet Sauvignon selected for Wolf Blass Black Label are brought together to form a perfect storm of concentrated power. This wine is the darling of collectors as a result, and it should be yours with its magnificent ability to be a rich and concentrated when young and a wonderfully balanced amalgam of flavors with a touch of bottle age. Sublime!
desc_en_full:
Since 1973, the chase to make the perfect wine has been fundamental to creating 44 remarkable vintages of Black Label. Built on complex, multilayered flavour and magnificent structure, Black Label is a powerful expression of the Wolf Blass philosophy of synergistic blending.

The philosophy behind Black Label is simple: to take the year’s very best wines and weave them together into a synergistic whole, the resultant wine being greater than the sum of its parts. It’s about creating a wine with many layers of flavour in a complex composition of intense fruit characters, magnificent structure, a rich lustrous texture, long velvety tannins and a lingering palate.

Harvested at optimum maturity and flavour, fruit from each vineyard was crushed, destemmed and fermented separately for seven to twelve days on skins. The ferments were allowed to warm naturally, with plunging and gentle pumping over used to maximise colour and flavour extraction. Mid-ferment cooling extended fermentation, exploiting time on skins and accentuating line and length. Some batches stayed on skins to dryness, while others completed fermentation in barrel, allowing a balanced integration of oak characters and enhancing texture and complexity.

name: Chateau Lynch Bages  AOC Pauillac 2013
desc_en_short:
The wine of Château Lynch-Bages is part of the lineage of the great Pauillac wines. Blended mostly from Cabernet Sauvignon, it combines structure and finesse, elegance and longevity. It is further distinguished by its opulence. Generous right from its youth, it develops more flavours and greater complexity as it matures.

The affirmation of the Lynch-Bages style came with the arrival of Jean-Charles Cazes to head the estate in 1934. An innovative winemaker, emblematic of a new generation of owners willing to break with tradition and taking risks to ensure truly ripe harvests, he was known for often being the last to harvest in Pauillac. From 1945 onwards, the fame of the château emerged thanks to a series of great vintages. Some, despite being considered difficult to grow in Bordeaux, are particularly successful at Lynch-Bages. His wines are characterized by their deep colour, their tannic structure, their controlled concentration and elegant sensuality. The vintages vinified by André Cazes, and after him Jean-Michel Cazes, confirm this trend.

The style of the wines has refined over the years, gaining suppleness and softness, whilst their consistency has been established over time. Then as now, Château Lynch-Bages has been characterised by its extraordinary qualitative homogeneity. Powerful, elegant and open, vintage after vintage, the wine has acquired greater accuracy, adding distinction to the hedonistic character that made its reputation.

name: Sterling  Vintner S Collection Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: Combining elegance and approachability, our Cabernet Sauvignon opens with rich aromas of blackberry bramble, accented by nutmeg and mocha spices. The palate is smooth and supple, delivering a balanced expression of dark cherries, currants and spice. Fine, chocolaty tannins hold the finish, which is long and luxurious. With its bold flavors and an easy accessibility, our Cabernet Sauvignon pairs well with a variety of foods and occasions, from filet mignon to barbecued ribs.
desc_en_full:
WINEMAKING

To achieve the rich varietal expression and approachable fruit profile in our Cabernet Sauvignon, we harvested our fruit at the peak of ripeness, followed by frequent pump-overs throughout fermentation. This constant contact between the skins and juice enables maximum flavor extraction from the fruit. We then aged the wine for approximately 11 months in a combination of American and French oak barrels to lend sweet, toasty vanilla tones and build structure.

name: Chateau Bellevue St.Emillion Grand Cru Classe 2017
brand: Chateau Bellevue St.Emillion Grand Cru Classe
desc_en_short:
Château Bellevue Saint-Émilion Grand Cru Classé 2017 is a true reflection of Bordeaux’s timeless elegance and winemaking excellence. Nestled in the prestigious appellation of Saint-Émilion, this historic estate dates back centuries, with a deep-rooted tradition of crafting wines that express the essence of its exceptional terroir.

The 2017 vintage showcases the meticulous craftsmanship behind every bottle. The vineyard's limestone-rich soils, combined with expert viticulture and precise aging in French oak, result in a wine that is both powerful and refined. Each sip reveals layers of ripe black fruits, delicate floral notes, and a structured yet silky finish—a tribute to the balance and sophistication that define Grand Cru Classé wines.

Château Bellevue is more than just a wine; it is a legacy of passion and dedication. It tells a story of tradition meeting modern innovation, where the past and present come together to create a Bordeaux that continues to captivate connoisseurs and collectors alike.

name: Wente Vineyards  Wente Morning Fog Chardonnay
desc_en_short: 92 Points in Decanter “Morning Fog Chardonnay is an great expression of Livermore Valley Chardonnay, with diverse alluvial fans of soil consistently creating Chardonnay with beautiful and lingering minerality, which coupled with the natural acidity from the long and cool growing season, creates a beautiful wine” - Karl D. Wente, Fifth Generation Winemaker
desc_en_full:
Vineyard

Since 1883, five generations of our family have handcrafted fine wines that express the very best of our vineyards and winemaking expertise. Our Estate Grown Morning Fog Chardonnay is named for the morning coastal fog pushed by Pacific wind streams into the bowl of the San Francisco Bay and lured inland by Livermore Valley's unique EastWest orientation. This daily phenomenon moderates the climate to produce excellent, beautifully balanced Chardonnay. Planted on various Wente clones, each vineyard block is farmed and harvested separately which maximizes quality and adds an elegant complexity to the wine. Recognized as "California's First Family of Chardonnay", our family was the first in California to produce a varietally labeled Chardonnay in 1936 made from our heritage Wente clones. Today, nearly 80% of the Chardonnay vineyards in California are planted with the Wente clone, which originated from our family's estate.

Winemaking

Fermentation : This wine is fermented in 50% neutral American oak barrels and 50% in stainless steel tanks. The oak provides hints of vanilla and enhances the mouthfeel and the steel preserves the naturally vibrant fruit flavors inherent in this Chardonnay.

Aging : The barrel fermented portion was aged sur lie for 5 months and batonnage, the art of stirring the barrels, was performed monthly. This adds a creamy note to the wine and greatly enhances the texture. From the remaining 50% stainless steel portion, half was aged on lees for 5 months while the other half was racked clean to preserve the fresh fruit character.

name: Pegasus Bay  Riesling
desc_en_short: The bouquet and flavour suggest peaches, apricots, lychees, frangipani, honeysuckle and mandarin. It is concentrated and mouth filling with a backbone of minerality and tangy acidity that complements it’s off dry finish and draws out its length. desc_en_full:
HARVEST AND WINE MAKING

We use traditional Burgundian winemaking methods. After picking in mid-late-April, the grapes, without their stems, were put into small fermenting vats, retaining as many whole berries as possible. They were kept cool over the next few days to help extract the soft, silky tannins from their skins. After this time the grapes had warmed enough to start fermenting. During fermentation the floating cap of grape skins was twice daily gently pushed back under the wine by hand to keep it moist and healthy. When the fermentation finished the vats were sealed and the grape remnants were left to steep in the wine for several weeks to help extract other tannins which add structure and depth. The duration of this period was determined by daily tasting. Subsequently the wine was drained off and put into oak barriques (30% new) from selected artisan Burgundian coopers. In the summer after harvest, when the weather warmed, the wine underwent malo-lactic (secondary) fermentation. Both this and the original fermentation phase were completely natural and without added micro-organisms. After maturing for 18 months in barriques the batches made from the many different plots of pinot were carefully blended in various portions according to taste to produce this wine.

name: Kendall Jackson  Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay
desc_en_short: America's #1 selling Chardonnay for 24 years and counting! It's no wonder Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay is our most popular release. Handcrafted in small vineyard lots throughout the entire winemaking process, our grapes are from California’s cool, coastal growing regions. Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay is fruit-forward, divinely complex, approachable and exceptionally food-friendly.
desc_en_full:
WINERY HISTORY

"When my family and I founded Kendall-Jackson in 1982, we simply wanted to create extraordinary wine from California's best vineyards." -JESS JACKSON, Founder

Ours is as much the story of a winery as it is the story of one man, his family and a dream. In 1974, our founder Jess Jackson, famously bought an 80-acre pear and walnut orchard in rural Lakeport, California, where he planned to try his hand at creating wine from some of his favorite varietals while on weekends away from his successful career as a San Francisco attorney. "I was attracted by the lifestyle," Jess said. "I wanted to get away from law and become a farmer."

By the early 1980s, Jess and his growing family had transformed the orchard into a working winery that produced grapes Jess sold to other wineries. During a fateful harvest in 1980, a large order from another winery was cancelled, and Jess was stuck with a bumper crop of unused grapes: from this the first bottle of Kendall-Jackson was produced. This first bottling, the 1982 Vintner's Reserve, garnered attention for its unique bright fruitiness. It was an immediate, if modest, hit. Though Jess and his family shipped less than 20,000 cases of that legendary '82 Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay, the release went on to become the most popular Chardonnay in America. In 1983, Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay won the first Double Platinum Award ever presented by the American Wine Competition, and has gone on to win accolades year after year.

Now, almost three decades later, Kendall-Jackson Winery produces an array of super-premium wines whose quality, taste and integrity embody the very spirit of our founder's mission. In Jess Jackson's own words, "We grow grapes on our own 15,000 acres of California coastal vineyards. We take the no-compromise, high road approach to quality required to grow our world-class grapes and produce acclaimed award-winning wines."

name: Mount Riley  17 Valley Sauvignon Blanc
desc_en_short: Our Seventeen Valley range is a considered, handcrafted wine for connoisseurs. Our best fruit goes into this range and it's made with our best efforts and only in our best vintages. A special occassion wine, available very selectively.
desc_en_full:
Our Seventeen Valley range is a considered, handcrafted wine for connoisseurs. Our best fruit goes into this range and it's made with our best efforts and only in our best vintages. A special occassion wine, available very selectively.

The grapes for this wine were grown on our Cromarty Vineyard in the Southern Valley sub region, which has older soils of clay and glacial outwash. The sauvignon blanc grown on this site is characterized by fruit of excellent concentration with a riper fruit profile and a profound length of flavour.

The grapes were hand-picked and whole bunch pressed to gently extract juice. The juice was then fermented in seasoned French oak barriques with natural indigenous yeast from Mount Riley's vineyard and winery. Lees stirring was carried out post fermentation to build weight, texture and complexity. The wine matured on lees for 8 months post fermentation with no sulphur added until just prior to bottling.

Wolf Blass Wines, established in 1966, has grown from a humble tin shed to become one of the world's most successful and awarded wineries. name: Sterling  Vintner S Collection Chardonnay
desc_en_short: Crisp Gala apple, Bartlett pear and hints of pineapple are layered by lovely pie spices and toasty vanilla notes. The palate is long and round with rich viscosity that comes from partial malolactic fermentation and barrel aging. Clean, ripe citrus flavors extend the finish and add a refreshing lift to the rich fruit. A go-to Chardonnay, this wine has the perfect poise for any occasion. Pair it with grilled chicken, seafood or light pastas.
desc_en_full:
WINEMAKING

The grapes for our Chardonnay were harvested in the cool morning hours. To preserve the fruit character, we maintained these low temperatures for a majority of the fruit through fermentation. We placed 75 percent of the grapes in stainlesssteel tanks with premium yeasts at temperatures around 58º Fahrenheit. The remaining 25 percent was fermented in barrel to give richness, depth and balance. Additional weightiness was achieved with malolactic fermentation and regular lees stirring. We added subtle accents of baking spice with seven months oak aging.

name: 19 Crimes  Sauvignon Block
desc_en_short: Light but unique in character, this approachable Sauvignon Blanc is filled with aromas of passion fruit, grapefruit, gooseberry and guava. 19 Crimes Sauvignon Block is light bodied with a fresh, crisp finish and a light straw color. A criminally easy wine to enjoy.
desc_en_full:
History

19 Crimes turned criminals into colonists. Upon conviction, British rogues, guilty of at least one of the 19 Crimes, were sentenced to live in Australia, rather than death. This punishment by ‘transportation’ began in 1788, and many of the lawless died at sea. For the rough-hewn prisoners who made it to shore, a new world awaited. As pioneers in a frontier penal colony, they forged a new country and new lives, brick by brick. This wine is borne of the rules they broke and the culture they built. And like our legendary rogues, it’s a taste that’ll live forever in infamy.

Tasting Notes

Light but unique in character, this approachable Sauvignon Blanc is filled with aromas of passion fruit, grapefruit, gooseberry and guava. 19 Crimes Sauvignon Block is light bodied with a fresh, crisp finish and a light straw color. A criminally easy wine to enjoy.

name: Chateau de la Vieille Tour Bordeaux Supérieur AOP & Roses
desc_en_short:
Château de la Vieille Tour Bordeaux Supérieur AOP & Roses

For those seeking a Bordeaux experience with added depth and character, Château de la Vieille Tour Bordeaux Supérieur AOP & Roses offers a refined and confident expression. This Bordeaux Supérieur reveals a more structured profile while remaining smooth and balanced, with aromas of dark fruits such as blackcurrant and ripe cherry, complemented by gentle spice and subtle oak that bring elegance and complexity to the glass.

Paired with fresh roses, this set transforms wine into a meaningful gesture—ideal for a special dinner, a private celebration, or an occasion where thoughtful details truly matter. For optimal enjoyment, serve at 16–18°C and allow brief aeration before drinking.

A graceful choice for creating a memorable moment—polished, expressive, and quietly impressive.

Ordering & Delivery Conditions

• Pre-order required at least 3 days in advance to ensure careful preparation

• Delivery available within Bangkok and surrounding metropolitan areas

• Free delivery, with no additional charges

• After placing your order, you may specify your preferred delivery date and time to match your special occasion
desc_en_full:
Château de la Vieille Tour Bordeaux Supérieur AOP represents a more structured and expressive side of Bordeaux, crafted under the stricter standards of the Bordeaux Supérieur appellation, where lower yields and careful vineyard management enhance depth and concentration.

The wine offers inviting aromas of dark fruits such as blackcurrant, ripe cherry, and plum, layered with gentle spice and subtle oak. On the palate, it delivers firm yet polished tannins, balanced acidity, and a confident, well-rounded structure that carries through to a refined finish.

name: Graham's  fine ruby
desc_en_short: Graham’s Ruby Port is full-bodied with youthful freshness and attractive luscious fruit. Very deep-coloured and intensely aromatic, this Port is bottled young so as to retain the assertiveness of the fruit, a recognizable Graham hallmark. Ruby is an everyday Port, suitable for enjoying anytime.
desc_en_full:
The story of two families across three centuries. For almost two hundred years W & J Graham's has been an independent family business renowned for producing the finest Port wines.

Graham's has always been a pioneer. Graham's was one of the first Port companies to invest in its own vineyards in Portugal's Douro Valley in 1890 and is now at the cutting edge of innovation in winemaking techniques. Today, five Symington cousins share responsibility for every aspect of the company and personally make the Graham's wines. They too have been involved with Port and the Douro for many generations, with ancestry dating back to the mid-17th century.

GRAHAM'S FINE RUBY

Graham's Ruby Port is full-bodied with youthful freshness and attractive luscious fruit. Very deep-coloured and intensely aromatic, this Port is bottled young so as to retain the assertiveness of the fruit, a recognizable Graham hallmark. Ruby is an everyday Port, suitable for enjoying anytime.

name: Chateau Mouton Rothschild  2019 (Premiers Crus)
flavor_tags: Dark Chocolate, Blackcurrant, Dark Fruit, Chocolate, Cigar Box, Graphite, Mineral, Cassis, Oak
food_matching: Red Meat, Grilled Steak, Lamb, Hard Cheese, BBQ
desc_en_short: Château Mouton Rothschild 2019 is a landmark vintage from Pauillac's most celebrated First Growth — an opulent, majestic Cabernet Sauvignon-dominated blend with extraordinary concentration, precision, and the architectural grandeur that defines great Bordeaux.
desc_en_full:
Château Mouton Rothschild is one of the most storied estates in the world of wine. Elevated to Premier Grand Cru Classé status in 1973 — the only château to have been reclassified since the famous 1855 classification — it occupies a unique place in Bordeaux's pantheon. Each vintage is graced by an original artwork on the label, from Picasso to Warhol to Jeff Koons, making these bottles as sought-after as art objects as they are as wines.

The 2019 vintage is widely regarded as one of the finest produced in Pauillac in a generation. Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant with smaller contributions of Merlot and Cabernet Franc, it spent 18 months in 80% new French oak barrels. Deep, brilliant ruby-purple in colour, it opens with extraordinary complexity: fresh blackcurrant and cassis, crushed violets, cigar box, graphite, dark chocolate, and a seamless overlay of perfectly integrated new oak. The palate is profound — massive but poised, with layers of dark fruit, mineral depth, and velvety tannins of extraordinary refinement. The finish is seemingly endless.

This is a wine to cellar for 15–20 years minimum. It will reward patience with a lifetime of drinking pleasure. A once-in-a-generation Mouton.

name: Cavicchioli  Prosecco Extra Dry
flavor_tags: Green Apple, Blossom, Acacia, Floral, Peach, Apple, Melon, Pear
food_matching: Oysters, Caviar, Fried Foods, Appetizers, Celebration
desc_en_short: Cavicchioli's Prosecco Extra Dry is a bright, fruit-forward Italian sparkling wine — lively and refreshing with green apple, pear, white peach, and delicate floral notes, finishing with a gentle sweetness and clean effervescence.
desc_en_full:
Cavicchioli is one of the Emilia-Romagna region's most established sparkling wine producers, known throughout Italy for their accessible, well-made bubbles. Their Prosecco Extra Dry is made from Glera grapes grown in the Prosecco DOC zone of the Veneto, using the Charmat (tank) method to preserve its fresh, aromatic character.

Note that 'Extra Dry' in Prosecco terminology is slightly sweeter than 'Brut' — a traditional style that balances the grape's natural fruitiness with a gentle touch of residual sugar. Pale straw with fine persistent bubbles, it opens on fresh green apple, ripe pear, white peach, and delicate acacia blossom. The palate is clean and refreshing, with a soft, approachable sweetness and a lively, satisfying finish.

A versatile aperitif that pairs beautifully with light antipasti, prosciutto and melon, mild soft cheeses, and fresh fruit desserts. Also excellent in a classic Aperol Spritz.

name: Antinori  Marchese Cuvee Royale Franciacorta DOCG
flavor_tags: Brioche, Mineral, Citrus, Almond, Creamy, Peach, Lemon, Cream
food_matching: Oysters, Caviar, Fried Foods, Appetizers, Celebration
desc_en_short: Antinori's Marchese Cuvée Royale is a prestigious Franciacorta DOCG — Italy's answer to Champagne — with fine persistent bubbles, toasted brioche, ripe citrus, and stone fruit complexity, showing the elegance and refinement this appellation delivers at its best.
desc_en_full:
Franciacorta DOCG, produced in the hills south of Lake Iseo in Lombardy, is Italy's most serious traditional-method sparkling wine — made by the same labour-intensive méthode champenoise as Champagne, with extended lees ageing required by law. Antinori's Marchese Cuvée Royale is a reflection of the house's commitment to quality across all wine categories.

Made predominantly from Chardonnay with Pinot Nero and Pinot Bianco, the wine undergoes secondary fermentation in bottle and extended ageing on its lees to develop its characteristic complexity and creamy texture. Pale straw-gold with fine, persistent perlage, it opens on a sophisticated nose of toasted brioche, ripe lemon, white peach, almond cream, and a subtle mineral thread. The palate is refined and full, with lively acidity, a creamy mousse, and a long, elegant finish.

Serve as a celebratory aperitif, with oysters and shellfish, light risotto, grilled white fish, or lobster.

name: Livon  Braide Alte Grand Cru IGT
wine_color: Intense straw yellow color, with golden reflections.
desc_en_short: Intense straw yellow color, with golden reflections. The bouquet is intense, very complex, with spicy hints. On the palate it is warm, full-bodied, very elegant and complex. It evolves in the glass with the temperature changes.
desc_en_full:
History

A fifty year-long story, between 1964-2014, that has its roots in the family's passion and traditions: a true love for the homeland, Friuli or better, the Collio and the Colli Orientali del Friuli, the far north-eastern corner of Italy and gateway to Eastern Europe. My dad Dorino Livon acquired his first vineyards on the Collio hills in 1964, and slowly, plot by plot, expanded his property. In the early nineteen eighties, my brother and I start working in the family company. We both brought new ideas and strategies to the table setting our goal on high quality products. Indeed, since 1989 our commitment to quality has been well recognised and is represented by the Winged Woman created by Ertè. The decade following such brave decision of diversifying the production was spent consolidating our new strategy. The years between 1992 and 1997 saw yet another strategical change for the company. We realised that we needed to remain competitive in the rapidly-changing and ever evolving market, so we decided to buy two estates in areas particularly suitable to winegrowing though beyond the borders of Friuli: Borgo Salcetino which is located in the very heart of the Chianti Classico area, in Tuscany and Fattoria Colsanto, in Bevagna (PG) in the Montefalco DOCG area, in Umbria. In 2008, after a period dedicated to the renovation of the ancient Locanda Villa Chiopris, acquired in 2003, the rural countryside villa returned to its original splendour and has become the venue of wine tasting events as well as a very pleasant boutique Bed and Breakfast, which can boast the peace and best views of the surrounding vineyards.

Now, after fifty years, our children represent the legacy not only of our family, but also of the values and life philosophy that have helped us build and develop our trademark and have our wines internationally recognised. Such a rewarding outcome seamed possible only in our dreams when we were younger.

name: The 4 Barmen  Speedy Bottle Pourer (Rose Gold)
desc_en_short: The 4 Barmen Speedy Bottle Pourer in Rose Gold is a stylish, professional free-flow pourer that combines elegant aesthetics with functional precision — delivering controlled, drip-free pours from spirit bottles with a warm metallic finish that looks great on any bar.
desc_en_full:
The 4 Barmen is a specialist barware brand bringing professional-grade tools to both home bars and commercial settings. The Speedy Bottle Pourer in Rose Gold adds a touch of warmth and contemporary elegance to any bar setup without sacrificing the functional performance that professionals demand.

The rose gold metallic finish gives this pourer a distinctive, premium look that sits beautifully alongside quality spirit bottles. The rubber collar creates a secure, airtight seal on most standard bottle necks, while the precision-calibrated free-flow spout allows for accurate free-pour measurement by count — a key skill for any competent bartender.

Perfect for gin, whisky, rum, vodka, and most standard spirit bottle sizes. Easy to clean and durable for regular use. A stylish gift for cocktail enthusiasts and a practical tool for the serious home bar.

name: Champagne Vollereaux  Celebration Premier Cru
desc_en_short:
Champagne Vollereaux established the winery in Pierry in 1933, a village classified as Premier Cru and located in the heart of the Champagne region.

We, therefore, decided to elaborate a champagne exclusively with the vines planted in this very special hill of Pierry. Our Cuvée Célébration Premier Cru perfectly expresses the soul of our “terroir”.

The six years of aging time on lees in our cellars conferred to this champagne a great complexity of flavors, brioche, peach in syrup, pineapple. The great finesse, which characterizes our House Style, is fully present.

Festive champagne, it can be served on every occasion, by itself or accompanied with grilled sesame bread sticks or wasabi crispy prawns.
