name: Rock Rose  Original Edition Gin (700 ml)
food_matching: Mediterranean, Grilled Vegetables, Light Salads, Sushi
desc_en_short: Initially the earthy warm Italian juniper will give way to the lemon sherbert of the Bulgarian juniper, before a nice jammy flavour develops thanks to the blaeberries, hawthorne and rowan berries. Finishing off with liquorice, cinnamon and our lovely Rhodiola rosea!
desc_en_full:
Rock Rose Gin is a family owned Scottish, handcrafted gin that is zesty and flavourful but surprisingly smooth. It is made with an infusion of traditional and local botanicals, 5 of which are hand foraged from local cliffs and forests. The precious botanicals are blessed with the freshest air and purest water from the Scottish Highlands.

The name Rock Rose Gin comes from one of the local botanicals, Rhodiola rosea, a rose, found off Caithness's wild coastline where it was once harvested by Vikings over 1000 years ago for its strength and vitality. We select our juniper from two different countries, Bulgarian juniper to create a real depth of flavour alongside Italian juniper to give slightly more delicate lemon sherbet notes. Our locally foraged botanicals include rowan and hawthorn berries and sea buckthorn. Each bottle is hand waxed, numbered and signed before it leaves the distillery. Rock Rose Gin is made using a slower vapour infused process to extract the botanical flavours and it is made in some of the most rugged but beautiful scenery in Scotland making it a true 'Wonder of the Wilderness!'

Tasting Notes:

Initially the earthy warm Italian juniper will give way to the lemon sherbert of the Bulgarian juniper, before a nice jammy flavour develops thanks to the blaeberries, hawthorne and rowan berries. Finishing off with liquorice, cinnamon and our lovely Rhodiola rosea!

Signature Serve:

25 ml of Rock Rose Gin, 50 ml of a premium tonic. Garnish with a curl of orange or a wee sprig of rosemary, toasted for full effect!

The Story Behind Every Drop

Deep within the virgin forest of Khao Soi Dao, where mountain mist meets ancient granite, lies one of Thailand’s last untouched water sources.

Filtered naturally through 360 meters of subterranean rock layers, every drop of Mont Étoile is protected from modern contamination, emerging crisp, soft, and perfectly balanced.

This is water born in silence — unhurried, unseen, and refined by nature itself.

Bottled at source under ultra-filtration technology that preserves its natural mineral profile, Mont Étoile carries the serene energy of Thailand’s eastern highlands in every sip.

 

Why It Feels Different

Naturally Silica-Rich (SiO₂) – supports radiant skin and hair health.

Fluoride-Free & Low Sodium – gentle on the body, ideal for daily wellness.

Award-Winning Purity – honored with Monde Selection Grand Gold Award 2025, placing Mont Étoile among the world’s finest waters.

Close your eyes as you sip — notice how the texture feels softer than ordinary water, how your mind associates that softness with calm. That’s not coincidence; it’s neurosensory comfort, proven to increase perceived luxury and relaxation.

 

Taste Profile

Aroma: Clean mountain air, faint hint of mineral stone.

Palate: Silky texture, medium-light body, with subtle sweetness from natural minerals.

Finish: Lingering freshness — like mist dissolving at sunrise.

 

Perfect For

Daily hydration rituals and wellness routines.

Fine-dining service and wine pairing tables.

Hotels, spas, and offices seeking a statement of refinement.

Travelers and health-conscious families who prefer purity in every moment.

 

Sustainability Promise

Mont Étoile is bottled using eco-certified PET and energy-efficient production at the source in Chantaburi.
Each case you choose supports local employment and our mission to preserve the Khao Soi Dao watershed — ensuring Thailand’s natural purity flows for generations.

name: Michel Lynch  Bordeaux Rose
wine_color: Bold Raspberry Pink, Limpid And Bright
desc_en_short: A bold raspberry pink, limpid and bright colour; a delicate nose of red fruit and pomegranate; a hint of menthol when swirled around the glass. It is both soft and fresh in the mouth, offering fruit drop and tangy aromas, with a long fruity finish.
desc_en_full:
Michel Lynch also comes in Rosé wine with a shiny raspberry colour. It is a selection of wines made in the finest Bordeaux tradition. Michel Lynch Rosé is bled - this relates to the running off, or bleeding of a certain amount of first-run juice from red grapes. This technique allows us to make a delicate rosé wine from intensely flavoured grapes. The resulting Michel Lynch rosé is fruity and flavourful, with a light and elegant structure. Perfect wine for the summer it is also enjoyable throughout the year thanks to its vibrant aromatic qualities and deepness, typical of the best Bordeaux Rosés!

Best served between 8°C and 10°C. Perfect as an original aperitif, you can also enjoy Michel Lynch Rosé with your salads, grilled meats or vegetarian dishes.

name: Clarendelle  Bordeaux Rose Inspired by Haut-Brion AOC
desc_en_short: Thanks to very short maceration in a controlled atmosphere and direct pressing, the wine has a beautiful pale pink colour as well as a rich, smooth flavour. The powerful bouquet features floral aromas such as rose, as well as fruity overtones of blood orange, apricot, and grapefruit. The aromatic complexity on the nose carries over onto the palate, which includes lovely flavours of ripe fruit like redcurrant. The wine is suave and concentrated, while remaining tremendously fresh. A truly elegant vintage for Clarendelle Rosé!
desc_en_full: Our oenologists are pleased and satisfied: 2016 gives every indication of being a remarkable vintage in Bordeaux, both in terms of quality and quantity. The wines are deeply-coloured, fruity, and concentrated. This is wonderful news because year did not start out very propitiously, with poor spring weather. However, this was followed by a very dry summer. The limited rainfall in early September and Indian summer during the harvest enabled the grapes to ripen magnificently, paving the way for a beautiful vintage. Our selected rosé wines come from dedicated plots, picked in September or October depending on the maturity of the different grape varieties. The grapes were in tip-top condition.

name: Bottega  Valpolicella Superiore Ripasso DOC
wine_color: Intense ruby red with purplish highlights.
desc_en_short: The production process starts with the traditional vinification of Valpolicella Classico. This wine is then fermented once again, along with the residual marcs of Amarone or Recioto and goes through daily pump-overs, so to obtain Ripasso, whose distinctive feature lies in this process of double fermentation. After 15-20 days of pump-overs the wine, completely fermented, is decanted and extracted through the racking. The wine is then naturally stabilized, aged in oaks, filtered and bottled. It is put on the market after 2 years.

name: Nicolis  Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG
wine_color: Deep dark ruby red which gradually turns into garnet.
desc_en_short: Ruby red color of medium intensity, which tends to garnet upon aging. It has a characteristic perfume that is pleasant and delicate, with hints of fruit that grow in the area: cherries, marascas, and plums. The flavor is dry, bodied, slightly bitter, savory, and harmonious.
desc_en_full:
Wine making :

After the grapes are selected and harvested, they are placed in small cases in dry, well aired rooms. In this way, the grapes dry naturally and gain an extraordinary high concentration of sugars, glycerine, and aromas. After 3 months, the grapes are softly pressed. Due to the low temperature, the process of fermentation is long and slow; maceration takes also more than one month. The wine makes then an ageing in medium-sized Slavonia oak barrels, where it remains for about 30 months. The bottle ageing is at least 8 months before release.

desc_en_full:
Wallace By Ben Glaetzer

Wallace is an ancestral name of Colin's wife, Judith, whose family hail from the old market town of Cupar in Fife, Scotland. Judith's Celtic ancestry is reflected in the three elements of the Wallace symbol - the Celtic cross, thistle and the Celtic knot pattern.

The Glaetzer History

The first Glaetzers settled in the Barossa Valley in 1888 after emigrating from Brandenburg, Germany. The family left Germany on board the Nord-Deutscher Lloyd steamer "Habsburg" and took seven weeks to reach their final destination of Port Adelaide. From here they settled in a country town called Nuriootpa in the Barossa Valley where they started their new life in Australia. The family were some of the earliest recorded viticulturalists in the Barossa Valley and Clare Valley.

Over a century later, winemaking patriarch Colin Glaetzer established Glaetzer Wines, a boutique family owned winery producing premium Barossa Valley wines. Today Colin's son Ben carries on the wine making traditions at Glaetzer Wines. Indeed it was the birth of Glaetzer Wines that signalled a new era for Colin's family which boasts more than its fair share of winemakers.

name: Chateau Quinault L' Enclos  2018
wine_color: This wine is a beautiful crimson red color.
desc_en_short:
Winemaker Notes

Thanks to the restructuring of the vineyards and the major renovation of the vat room and cellar at Quinault, as well as the meticulous efforts of the Cheval Blanc team, Quinault L'Enclos has affirmed its position as an emerging estate. The 2018 vintage nose displays black fruit aromas of blackberry and cherry, going on to reveal spicy notes such as black pepper and gingerbread, combined with rose and peony overtones. The wine starts out rich and straightforward on the palate, becoming full-bodied and broad-based. It is incredibly smooth and well-balanced thanks to beautiful acidity. The aftertaste is wonderfully long, culminating in a crunchy finish.

name: Wolf Blass  Grey Label Shiraz
desc_en_short:
Shiraz was sourced from low yielding Barossa vineyards selected for elegance and intensity of fruit, and rich, bright character.

Individual vineyard batches were fermented in small open-top fermenters for 5 to 10 days. Plunging and gentle irrigation were used to work the cap and optimise flavour, colour and tannin extraction, providing for concentration and depth of fruit flavour, while keeping tannins plush, hallmarks of the Grey Label style. Select batches completed fermentation in barrel, contributing mid-palate texture, while others finished fermentation on skins, providing structural length and focus.
desc_en_full: Wolf Blass Grey Label red wines are unique and distinctive, rich in regional character and varietal expression. Highly acclaimed and proudly iconic, the Grey Label range offers benchmark South Australian reds from exceptional vineyards, crafted with the character to age impeccably yet with plush, rich, mid-palate fruit that allows for immediate enjoyment.

name: Ceretto  Barbaresco BIO DOCG
desc_en_short: This classic Barbaresco is a blend of our finest vineyards, a small part of Asili, the part with younger vineyards, and the lowest part of Bernadot in Treiso. With the union of these two different expressions, the wine achieves a better balance than vinifying the two wines as separate parcels. While on the one side there is the explosive finesse of Asili and on the other, the fullness of the Treiso cru, in the glass we find the right balance; the result is a delicious and traditional wine, a perfect example of an area that moves between structure and elegance.

name: Valdubon  Roble
desc_en_short:
Valdubón Roble is made entirely with the Tinta del País grape (Tempranillo). The original vineyards of Valdubón are distributed by different terms of Ribera del Duero, their main origins being the terms of Roa and Milagros.

An exhaustive monitoring of the care applied to the vineyard is carried out, focusing efforts on a severe thinning of bunches that leaves the strain with an average production of 7-8 bunches per strain, which represents a maximum yield of 5,000 kg / Ha.

During the harvest, the evolution of the Baumé degree, acidity and color potential are controlled. Based on the results obtained, a first classification is made, with Valdubón Roble being destined for those vineyards that stand out year after year for providing very fruity wines, with medium-high structure, good acidity and ripe tannins.

The vineyards destined for Valdubón Roble are harvested at night to guarantee an entry into the cellar at a low temperature and a soft start of fermentation that keeps all the fruit on the nose and in the mouth. The cut grape is transferred to the winery as soon as possible, being ground within 6 hours after its harvest.

name: Chateau Branaire-Ducru  Saint-Julien
wine_color: A medium to deep garnet-purple color
desc_en_short: Château Branaire-Ducru is located in the heart of the Medoc, between Margaux and Pauillac in the Saint-Julien appellation area. It is surrounded by the other great Classified Growths that populate this outstanding terroir. The vines grow in a mixture of gravel and pebbles, deposited there by the River Garonne in the Quaternary Period. From the top of their hillocks, called “mounds”, they overlook the vast Gironde Estuary, locally referred to as “the river”.

name: Juris  Golser St.Laurent
wine_color: Very dark garnet with purple reflections.
desc_en_short:
"JURIS" is also the name for the family estate Stiegelmar in Gols, for a more precise name and for elimination of
future confusion. The family as germ-cell, in the last centuries always has been our refuge of energy. This
structure remained alive until today's time. Axel und Herta Stiegelmar has led the winery on the highest level of
Austrian estates. Two aspects are responsible for this: One of them is the consistency and strength in all wine
categories, offering regularly high quality with dry white wines, highly profiled red wines and sweet wines.
JURIS comprises about 18 ha (2/3 for red wines and 1/3 for white wines) of vineyards, which are situated all over
the wine-region of Gols. Gols is located in the wine region Burgenland between Vienna and Budapest in the warm
Pannonian climate with permeable soils of gravel and sand. Caused by the geologic conditions in our area, a great
number of the grape varieties growing in Austria, are also planted in our estate. It has however crystallised, that
the family of the Pinot-varieties is cultivated very often. JURIS strive to produce wines with character and style
that are well balanced and possess complexity, aging potential, and a certain elegance.
The highlight of father - Georg Stiegelmar - was the international rewards of the "Robert Mondavi World Wine
Trophy" for the Winemaker of the year 1995 (International Wine & Spirit Competition) in London.

desc_en_full:
The Vineyards : La Mancha is the largest vineyard in the world, occupying 50% of Spain's Vineyard. There is more "land under vine" here than in the entire countries of Australia and Chile combined. Included among our prolific vineyards are 50 hectares of experimental vineyards for research and development works with different grape varieties, both for young wines as Crianzas and Reservas. La Mancha is located on large plain and it is a dry area which often suffers from long drought periods. Soil is rich in limestone rock, original from miocene sediments. The combination of these conditions put stress on the vines which results in high character and quality.

name: Freixenet  Reserva Real Brut
wine_color: Straw Yellow With Hints Of Green
desc_en_short: In a glass this cava presents fine fruity aromatics (especially of white fruits) that are in harmony with freshly baked pasty notes. There are also nut aromatics and traditional Catalan style crème brûlée notes. This cava has very well-integrated effervescence that are followed by a creamy finish.
desc_en_full: A Champagne-style cuvée, an assemblage where wines from different years are blended to produce this legendary cava. It was brought to the public in 1987, to mark the occasion of the visit of the King and Queen of Spain to Freixenet. This is one of the very few cavas produced with a real cork, which is reminiscent of the early years of the house. This causes the cava to undergo a special evolution throughout the course of its ageing, over more than 30 months, and requires an entirely manual production process. It is always produced as a brut, dosage with 4 g/l of sugar and wine aged for eight months in chestnut barrels. A different, yet classic product. Just like a fine wine, it is a harmonious companion to any hearty meal.

name: G.H. Mumm  Cordon Rouge Brut (750 ml)
wine_color: This champagne is a brightly sparkling, light golden yellow liquid with an abundance of fine and elegant bubbles.
desc_en_short: This champagne is a brightly sparkling, light golden yellow liquid with an abundance of fine and elegant bubbles. The nose reveals initial aromas of ripe fresh fruit (white and yellow peaches, apricots), tropical notes (lychee and pineapple). It then opens up with the fragrance of vanilla before developing notes of milky caramel, breadcrumbs and yeast, culminating in aromas of dried fruit and honey. An explosion of freshness in the mouth, followed by strong persistence. The complex aromas of fresh fruit and caramel perpetuate the intensity. Ideal as an aperitif and beyond, Mumm Cordon Rouge works as well as an accompaniment to white meat, as it does to fish or fine cured meats (Italian ham, smoked duck breast).
desc_en_full:
The History Behind The Red Silk Ribbon

In 1876, as a tribute to some of their most prestigious clients, Mumm decorated a few of their bottles with a crossed red silk ribbon (a reference to the Legion of Honour) and sealed them with an oval label. The success of Cordon Rouge was established.

As the Champagne des Souverains, so great was the fame of the Cordon Rouge wine that it became the symbol of the House and of champagne as a whole. The wine lovers of today continue to worship Mumm Cordon Rouge and have made the cuvée France's premier and one of the world's favourite champagnes.

Georges Hermann Mumm is the man to whom we owe the House's iconic cuvée. In 1876, to assert the quality of its champagne, the House decided to adorn their bottles with a red silk ribbon, a reference to the Ordre de Saint Louis and to the Grand Cordon de la Légion d'Honneur. Its success was immediate: a symbol of Maison Mumm's excellence and expertise, the Cordon Rouge cuvée henceforth became a true benchmark all over the world.

name: Dom Perignon  Rose (Luminated Label)
desc_en_short:
The beginning of winter was particularly rigorous in 2009. The Champagne region saw intermittent snowfall, and temperatures remained quite low. Spring was warm but rainy. The weather did not encourage flowering and there was a threat of mildew. 
The summer, despite stormy periods that raised concerns that the health of the vines might suffer, was dominated by sunshine, especially in August. The weather remained hot, dry and very sunny for five consecutive weeks. On September 4 a hailstorm hit the vineyards in Hautvillers, Verzenay and Chouilly. The summery weather returned the very next day, however, and the grapes continued to ripen in complete serenity.
The harvest thus began on September 12 under absolutely idyllic conditions. The grapes showed exceptional health and their maturity, underlined by a wonderful aromatic expression, was superlative.

name: 19 Crimes  Blanc De Blancs
desc_en_short: Blanc de Blancs translates as ‘white of whites’ and to make this wine we stole grapes from our Hard Chard snatching them off the vine early to lock in their fresh crispness and light straw colour. A light bodied sparkling, clean and zesty style, with a crisp finish. A criminally easy wine to enjoy in many ways. Break out a bottle and celebrate with your gang!
desc_en_full:
History

19 Crimes turned criminals into colonists. Upon conviction, British rogues, guilty of at least one of the 19 Crimes, were sentenced to live in Australia, rather than death. This punishment by ‘transportation’ began in 1788, and many of the lawless died at sea. For the rough-hewn prisoners who made it to shore, a new world awaited. As pioneers in a frontier penal colony, they forged a new country and new lives, brick by brick. This wine is borne of the rules they broke and the culture they built. And like our legendary rogues, it’s a taste that’ll live forever in infamy.

desc_en_full:
Vinification

After light maceration of the skins with the must in the press, it is fermented in new, lightly toasted casks of French-oak from the Nevers district. Once alcoholic fermentation is complete it spends a further three months in the casks with lees stirring twice per week to keep its fine lees in suspension in the wine, protecting it from oxygen and giving the wine a smoother mouth-feel and harmony. Before bottling it is stabilised by cooling.

WINEMAKING

After de-stemming and a light crushing, some contact between the must and the skins is encouraged in order to extract the desired aromatic compounds. desc_en_full:
Black Bottle Classic Brandy

AN AUSTRALIAN ICON; ITS DELICATE COMPLEXITY HAS WON ADMIRERS AND AWARDS WORLDWIDE.

The distinctive, black Four Star Brandy bottle appeared in circulation around 1950. The bottle – like the double pot distilled brandy it holds within – has altered little since.

This curiously squat, ridged bottle has a certain charm. Since its introduction it has graced many a bar, sideboard and dinner table. The name and ownership may have changed over the years, but Black Bottle’s quality and consistency have remained the same.

TASTING NOTE

A mellow bouquet gives way to a palate that’s smooth, round, and framed in vanilla and lifted spice. Careful blending from selected batches gives a delicate complexity that has won this Australian icon many awards. To be relished neat or on the rocks.

name: Laird's 100 Proof Straight Applejack (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
Laird’s 100 Proof Straight Applejack is more than just a spirit — it’s a piece of American history in a bottle. The Laird family distillery, founded in 1780 in New Jersey, is recognized as the oldest licensed distillery in the United States, and its Applejack has been enjoyed by generations since the birth of the nation.

Made entirely from fresh, hand-selected apples grown in the cool orchards of Virginia and New Jersey, this expression follows the same traditional methods used by Robert Laird — a soldier in George Washington’s army and the pioneer of American apple brandy.

Unlike blended versions, Laird’s 100 Proof Straight Applejack is 100% apple brandy, aged in charred American oak barrels to develop warmth, depth, and a golden-amber hue. Bottled at 100 proof (50% ABV), it reflects both authenticity and bold character — a true embodiment of America’s rustic spirit and pioneering craftsmanship.
desc_en_full:
A bold, warming apple brandy with layers of orchard sweetness, oak spice, and old-world charm.

Aroma: Rich and inviting — notes of baked apple, cinnamon, toasted oak, and caramel, with a hint of vanilla and clove.

Palate: Full-bodied and robust, revealing toffee, ripe apple, and nutmeg, balanced by a dry oak backbone and a gentle smokiness.

Texture: Velvety yet powerful; firm structure with lingering warmth.

Finish: Long and satisfying — echoes of caramelized apple, spice, and charred oak remain with elegant persistence.

name: Capel Pisco Reservado Transparente (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Capel Pisco Reservado Transparente represents the essence of Chile’s Elqui Valley — a land where sunlight, altitude, and craftsmanship converge to create one of the world’s most expressive piscos. Produced by Cooperativa Capel, Chile’s most respected pisco house founded in 1938, this expression honors the tradition of distilling grapes into a spirit of unmatched clarity and soul.

Crafted from Moscatel grapes grown under the high Andean sun, this Reservado pisco undergoes slow, precise distillation in copper pot stills, yielding a crystal-clear, aromatic elixir that reflects both the purity of the Andes and the passion of Chilean artisans.

“Transparente” is more than its appearance — it’s a statement of honesty, precision, and balance. Every drop reveals the delicate heart of Chilean terroir, distilled to perfection
desc_en_full:
A fresh, floral, and elegant pisco — crystalline in body, luminous in character.

Aroma: Fragrant and lifted, with white flowers, muscat grapes, and light citrus peel, accented by a hint of herbal freshness.

Palate: Smooth and balanced; flavors of pear, jasmine, and honeydew melon, mingled with soft minerality.

Texture: Silky and pure, with a clean, refined body.

Finish: Long, bright, and elegant — leaving a memory of grape blossom and citrus zest.

name: Mathilde  Poire Liqueur (350 ml)
desc_en_short:
We use the Bon-Chrétien Williams pear, also called Bartlett, to make Mathilde Poire. When ripe, it boasts a golden skin and a very tasty and juicy flesh. In order to extract as much as possible from the pears, we press the fruits into a juice that is fermented before the final distillation.

The pear distillate is then enhanced with a touch of quince, a pear-like fruit with an intense aroma. This particular step intensifies the pear flavor. A one-week maceration process ensues that, in turn, produces a fresh and luscious pear liqueur.

name: Campari  Cask Tales : A Celebration of Legacy in a Bottle (1 L)
desc_en_short:
Campari Cask Tales is a unique expression of the classic Campari, finished in bourbon barrels, launched to

celebrate 150 years since the birth of Davide Campari. Bourbon barrel finishing smooths bitter notes with
fruity, sweet and creamy tones, providing delicate oaky smokiness to the legendary Campari liquid. Inspired by
bartenders to enthuse fellow bartenders, their opinion throughout the process was essential to define the best
variation of the spirit, which led to the unique creation of Campari Cask Tales. ABV 25%
desc_en_full:
Campari Unveils a Unique Expression of Classic Campari Finished in Bourbon Barrels

NEW YORK CITY (September 10, 2019) – Steeped in a rich heritage dedicated to the world of art and craftsmanship, Campari®, Italy’s iconic bittersweet red aperitif, is introducing a new expression to the United States in limited quantity to celebrate over 150 years of the legacy of Davide Campari. Created by revered Herbalist & Master Blender, Bruno Malavasi, Campari Cask Tales represents a new chapter in Campari’s illustrious history as a contemporary take on the classic Campari that is finished in bourbon barrels, resulting in a unique and unprecedented palate experience.

Adding a twist to the legacy that was passed down by Gaspare Campari to his son, Davide, Campari Cask Tales is an exploration into the world of cask-finishing. Bruno Malavasi experimented with casks from multiple sources to find the ideal environment to age and flavor the liqueur, which finally led to Bruno’s selection of second-fill bourbon casks, resulting in a liquid masterpiece.

On the nose, Cask Tales spotlights a traditional Campari herbal profile, balanced with a subtle spiciness from finishing in wood. On the palate, the bourbon barrel finishing smooths the bitter notes with fruity, sweet, and creamy tones, providing a delicate oaky smokiness to the legendary Campari liquid.

One of the world’s most iconic aperitifs, Campari takes great pride in celebrating and emboldening bartenders to create intriguing drinking experiences. Campari Cask Tales was largely inspired and informed by passionate bartenders from around the globe, including Leo Robitschek, Gaz Regan, and Jeffrey Morgenthaler, who shared their expertise and guidance alongside Bruno. Their invaluable insight coupled with the patience of time within the cask were essential to define the best variation of the spirit.

While Campari Cask Tales was crafted to inspire bartenders to experiment and create their own masterpieces, the unique spirit can also be enjoyed in classic, simple serves such as neat or on the rocks. Cask Tales also shines in the form of a Shakerato, where the spirit is shaken vigorously with ice and served up in a chilled cocktail glass. A simple-to-execute and delicious cocktail, the Shakerato exquisitely highlights Cask Tales’ nuanced and remarkable flavor profile.

ABOUT US :

Chalong Bay was born from the passion of two individuals for rum and spirits, entrepreneurship, and Asia.

Our dream was to unite two traditions: the centuries old Thai sugarcane culture and the French know-how and traditions in spirit and rum production.

Our vision is to create super premium spirits that are authentic, modern, with a unique character, marrying traditions and craft production methods that are sustainable and respectful of the environment and the community.

Chalong Bay's innovative craftsmanship continues to produce award-winning rum of incredible character, originality and smoothness in its uncompromising pursuit of natural purity.

Our new Tropical Notes series, using a rare distillation method of vapor infusion, continues to apply our philosophy of combining traditional extraction methods with sustainable production to release the aromas of handpicked, 100% natural, locally grown Thai sugarcane, Sweet Thai Basil for a truly unique taste experience.

name: Diplomatico  Planas (700 ml)
desc_en_short: This successor of Blanco rum is more intense: this elegant white measure 46 ° alcohol by volume (7° more than its predecessor). It is filtered with coals and takes its name from a region close to the Diplomatico distillery.
desc_en_full:
Diplomatico Planas (700 ml)

Planas is the replacement for Diplomatico Blanco. It is made the same as the Blanco expression, but has one key difference: the ABV is now 46%.

Due to Venezuelan law, rum cannot be bottled until it is as least two years old. However, in this case some of the rums are aged up to six years. The blend is then charcoal filtered up to six times, removing all colour and creating a very refined, smooth white rum, perfect for mixing a mojito or daiquiri. A very unique white rum.

Tasting Notes :

Nose : Sweet initially, with marshmallows and barley sugar picking up pleasantly sour citrus notes as the rum sits in the glass. Buttery notes and a hint of mint develop over time.

Mouth : The barley sugar from the nose leads on the palate, closely followed by candied citrus zest and sherbert lemons. Rich toffee notes come in behind that, contrasting with peppermint creams and grassy spice.

Finish : Short and sweet, with a burst of fluffy marshmallow quickly fading to soft spice and a hint of clove.

Comment : A rich and flavoursome white rum, with a pleasantly oil texture, well-balanced sweetness and an intriguing herbal note. A good sipper with enough character to make its presence known in cocktails.

name: Diplomatico  Mantuano (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Mantuano is the replacement for Diplomatico's Reserva rum. 'Mantuano', meaning 'nobleman', reflects Don Juancho's status and his passion for travel and the discovery of new spirits, mainly rums. Don Juancho is present on each of Diplomatico's rums.
desc_en_full:
Diplomatico Planas (700 ml)

Mantuano is the replacement for Diplomatico's Reserva rum. 'Mantuano', meaning 'nobleman', reflects Don Juancho's status and his passion for travel and the discovery of new spirits, mainly rums. Don Juancho is present on each of Diplomatico's rums.

Aged for up to eight years, Mantuano is a blend of Column, Batch Kettle and Pot Still rums and made from molasses and sugar cane honeys. It is fantastic when mixed in cocktails, or sipped neat.

Tasting Notes :

Nose : Thick, heavy and sweet – like wading through molasses. With some time, black treacle, cherries and Millionaire's Shortbread come through, wrapped in sweet liquorice and milk chocolate.

Mouth : Spicier than expected from the nose, but rich and oily in texture. Cinnamon and nutmeg lead into sharper notes of polished oak before caramel sauce, soft toffees and hints of balancing bitter barrel char.

Finish : Soft and sweet, with herbal hints slowly fading to leave spicy oak.

Comment : Dark and richly flavoured with a texture to match – oily and sticky. The sweetness is tempered by gentle notes of oak and char, making it an excellent easy drinking sipping rum with enough punch to add a rich backbone to a cocktail without getting lost.

name: Kosapan  Sugarcane Rum (700 ml)
food_matching: Tropical Fruit, BBQ, Spiced Desserts, Chocolate
desc_en_short: Everything is carefully handcrafted from the start to finish. From harvesting to peeling the fruits for mash and fermentation, the alcohol is then double or triple-distilled by hand in traditional French copper alembic stills and aged in limited batches. This French artisanal method is rarely practiced in modern manufacturing process due to its time-consuming nature however, we find, that it gives distinctly complex aroma and exceptionally delicate notes. Additionally, we are proud to say all Kosapan Distillery products are 100% natural without any additives.
desc_en_full:
It all began one evening …

A group of passionate friends were discussing Thailand's riches — an exotic country with alluring cultures and culinary delights. Thai cuisine is world famous for its savoury spices and explosion of tastes, of which came from the finest ingredients.

And it was drawn to them they could amalgamate the French art of traditional distillery to Thai natural treasures, and it would be the best of both worlds. Reminiscing 300 years of the French-Thai diplomatic history, the spirits conceived was named after Kosa Pan, one of the very first Siamese diplomats who led the embassy to France. After many researches and creative innovations later, the Kosapan Distillery is ready to make its mark.

name: Game of Thrones Single Malt Scotch Whisky Collection @Blue Moon Siam Paragon
desc_en_short:
Game of Thrones fans, this one’s for you. Each glass represents a different house and its story. From bold Highland smoke to smooth Speyside sweetness, each sip brings the saga back to life, one kingdom at a time.

 

Game of Thrones Single Malt Scotch Whisky Collection

1. The Night’s Watch – Oban Bay Reserve (15 ml)

2. House Stark – Dalwhinnie Winter’s Frost (15 ml)

3. House Targaryen – Cardhu Gold Reserve (15 ml)

4. House Greyjoy – Talisker Select Reserve (15 ml)

5. House Tully – Singleton of Glendullan Select (15 ml)

6. House Lannister – Lagavulin 9 YO (15 ml)

7. House Baratheon – Royal Lochnagar 12 YO (15 ml)

 

Experience Details & Requirements

• 15 ml per tasting label

• E-Ticket provided upon purchase via LIQ9 (redeemable at the venue)

• 10% service charge waived on all tasting flights

• Advance reservation required (minimum 1 day)

• Available daily from 11:00 – 23:00

• Venue: Bluemoon Bar & Restaurant, Siam Paragon
desc_en_full:
Game of Thrones Single Malt Scotch Whisky Collection

1. The Night’s Watch – Oban Bay Reserve

Region: Highlands, Scotland ABV: 43%

NOSE: Aromas of sea breeze and iodine weave together with the sweetness of malt and honey. Notes of mandarin orange emerge alongside gentle smoke and soft spice.

PALATE: A well-balanced interplay between ripe fruit sweetness and subtle maritime salinity. Flavors of toasted malt, milk chocolate, and mild spice unfold over a smooth, rounded mouthfeel.

FINISH: Medium in length, lingering with light smokiness, a touch of sea salt, and a gradual fade of malty sweetness.

2. House Stark – Dalwhinnie Winter’s Frost

Region: Highlands, Scotland ABV: 43%

NOSE: Fresh aromas of honey, sweet malt, and white fruits, layered with notes of wildflowers, vanilla, and a cool mineral nuance that reflects the crisp Highland climate.

PALATE: Soft and refined, showing honeyed sweetness and gentle malt at the core, followed by dried fruits, delicate spice, and well-balanced oak. The texture is light, elegant, and poised.

FINISH: Long and warming, lingering with honeyed sweetness, malt, and subtle spice, fading out in a clean and refreshing manner.

3. House Targaryen – Cardhu Gold Reserve

Region: Speyside, Scotland ABV: 40%

NOSE: Sweet aromas of baked apples, toasted malt, and honey, layered with notes of raisins, vanilla, and gentle oak spice, with a subtle floral lift in the background.

PALATE: Soft and sweet in character, offering rounded flavors of ripe fruit, toffee, and sweet malt, followed by touches of spice, oak, and milk chocolate. The mouthfeel is smooth, approachable, and easy-drinking.

FINISH: Medium in length, lingering with balanced notes of malt sweetness, toffee, and mild spice without becoming heavy.

4. House Greyjoy – Talisker Select Reserve

Region: Isle of Skye, Scotland ABV: 45.8%

NOSE: Peaty smoke and cracked black pepper lead the aroma, layered with malty sweetness and ripe fruit. Maritime notes of sea spray and iodine emerge alongside hints of mandarin orange.

PALATE: Bold and smoky in character, with a warming spiciness. Toasted malt sweetness and tropical fruit notes unfold, followed by black pepper, coastal salinity, and dark chocolate. The texture is full-bodied and powerful.

FINISH: Long and warming, lingering with smoky peat, peppery heat, and an enduring touch of sea salt.

5. House Tully – Singleton of Glendullan Select

Region: Speyside, Scotland ABV: 40%

NOSE: Bright aromas of fresh fruit, green apple, and honey, supported by sweet malt, vanilla, and wildflower notes. The profile feels fresh, open, and approachable.

PALATE: Soft and gently sweet, showing ripe fruit, toffee, and toasted malt at the core, followed by vanilla, light spice, and oak. The mouthfeel is smooth, rounded, and easy-going.

FINISH: Medium in length, leaving lingering notes of malty sweetness, toffee, and subtle spice, clean and effortless on the close.

6. House Lannister – Lagavulin 9 YO

Region: Islay, Scotland ABV: 46%

NOSE: Intense aromas of peat smoke and iodine, layered with dark fruits, dark chocolate, and seaweed. Despite its youth, the profile shows notable depth and complexity.

PALATE: Dense, smoky flavors with a strong maritime character. Sweet toasted malt leads into bitter dark chocolate, cracked black pepper, and dried fruits. The mouthfeel is rich, powerful, and assertive.

FINISH: Very long, lingering with peat smoke, coastal salinity, and persistent bitter chocolate—unmistakably classic Lagavulin in style.

7. House Baratheon – Royal Lochnagar 12 YO

Region: Highlands, Scotland ABV: 40%

NOSE: Elegant aromas of honey, sweet malt, and dried fruits, layered with gentle spice, floral notes, and a whisper of smoke. The overall impression is refined and delicate.

PALATE: Smooth and well-balanced, showing honeyed sweetness and malt at the core, followed by spice, raisins, and oak. A touch of soft smoke adds nuance and quiet complexity.

FINISH: Medium to long, lingering with honey, malt, gentle spice, and light smoke, finishing in a clean and graceful manner.

name: Domaine Leflaive  Bienvenues Batard Montrachet Grand Cru
desc_en_short:
WINE MAKING

Biodynamic.
Manual harvesting with grape sorting and optimisation of choice of date through parcel-by-parcel ripeness monitoring.
desc_en_full:
ORIGIN

Sol argilo-calcaire 1 parcel. Years when planted: 1958 and 1959. Surface: 1ha 15a (2.84 acres).

METHOD OF CULTURE

Long, gentle pneumatic pressing, decanting over 24 hours, then racking and running into cask of the must. Alcoholic fermentation in oak casks, 25% new (maxi 1/2 Vosges, mini 1/2 Allier). Maturing: after 12 months in cask, the wine is aged 6 months in tank where it is prepared for bottling. Homeopathic fining and very light filtering if necessary.

name: Tenuta San Guido  Le Difese
desc_en_short: The wine is distinguished by a good structure and is extremely supple making it very pleasant and drinkable.
desc_en_full:
Wine making notes :

A very gentle crushing and de-stemming of the grapes was followed by fermentation in temperature controlled steel vats (between 30° - 31° C, without the addition of yeast). The maceration on the skins lasted 9 to 12 days for Cabernet Sauvignon and about 15 to 18 days for Sangiovese. It was followed by pumpovers of the musts and délestages in order to soften the wine tannins. The secondary fermentation (Malolactic fermentation) also took place in steel vats.

desc_en_short:
** Free delivery for Bangkok Area. For other provinces, delivery fees are calculated based on distance.

 

Compressor wine cooler, dual-zone, freestanding or built-in, 46 bottles Enhance your kitchen with this dual-zone wine cooler that can be seamlessly built-in to create an elegant style in any home. It has room for 46 Bordeaux bottles attractively presented on FSC certified wooden shelves.

 

Stunning Minimalist Design

Store your treasured wine collection in a beautifully designed Dometic wine cooler. With its minimalist all-black exterior and attractive interior lighting, the Design range of Dometic wine coolers will enhance any space. Allow the Dometic wine cooler to take center stage in your kitchen. Enjoy a stylish interior feature and drink your wine at its very best.

 

Optimal Storage Environment

For wine to unfold its full bouquet and flavor, both the storage conditions and the serving temperature must be right. Dometic wine coolers ensure the optimal environment for unlocking the wine's full flavors and charecter. The temperature is accurately controlled thanks to electronic regulation and a constant humidity is maintained between 50-70% ensuring the corks don't dry or mold. A fan circulates the air inside the wine cooler while the integrated carbon filters eliminates any potential odors. The low emission treatment of the triple-glazed doors on Dometic wine coolers stops ultraviolet rays that can damage and change the character of the wine.
desc_en_full:
About Dometic

Wine connoisseurs, gourmets and restaurateurs all agree: For a wine to unfold its full bouquet and flavour, both the storage conditions and the serving temperature must be right. It has been our job for decades to make sure they are. As specialists in mobile refrigeration and climate control, we have created a comprehensive product range for keeping wines at their optimum temperature.

Dometic MaCave wine cellars and multi-temperature wine cabinets have proven their worth in bars, restaurants and hotels as well as in offices and private households. They meet the highest demands on quality and design and are available in numerous sizes and versions. All wine refrigerators are supplied fully equipped, including the shelves and all essential accessories.

The right glassware can mean the difference between a good glass of wine and a great one. Like listening to great music requires the right acoustics, great wine needs the right glass to show you everything it has to offer.

name: Riedel  Performance Cabernet / Merlot (Pack 2 piece)
desc_en_short: The machine-made Cabernet glass of the technologically advanced glass collection Performance is the new ultimate loudspeaker for fine wine. This glass showcases the majestically structured red wines in all their complexity and finesse. Performance is the first RIEDEL wine glass series ever to feature bowls with a light optic impact, which not only adds a pleasing visual aspect to the bowl, but also increases the inner surface area. All RIEDEL glasses are dishwasher safe.
desc_en_full:
With three generations of research, development and experience in creating functional, grape varietal specific glassware, Georg and Maximilian RIEDEL challenged themselves to improve on what had gone before. The result is Performance – a technologically advanced and user-friendly collection of grape varietal specific glasses. Performance is the first RIEDEL wine glass series ever to feature bowls with a light optic impact. Detailed research by the RIEDEL family, aimed at improving the organoleptic wine experience, revealed that increasing the inner surface area of the glass had a positive impact on the perception of the bouquet and flavour of the wine.

This was achieved through a unique optical effect, which not only adds a pleasing visual aspect to the bowl, but also increases the inner surface area, allowing the wine to open up and to fully show every aroma and subtle nuance. Consisting of seven machine-made, lightweight, durable and dishwasher-safe shapes, Performance glasses are executed in sparkling fine crystal and feature fine stems and large stable bases. The new bowl shapes in Performance, when combined with the optic effect, deliver ultimate wine enjoyment, making Performance the new essential 'must-have' wine glass collection for all wine-lovers.

desc_en_short: The original Blue Curacao is best-selling in the world. A sparkling blue colour, a powerful orange flavour and a superb and unique aftertaste to any cocktail. As well as being a great substitute for Triple Sec in cocktails like the Blue Margarita and electric lemonade, it is also delicious when served long with soda or orange juice.
desc_en_full:
BOLS BLUE

BOLS Blue the original Blue Curacao. Bols Blue was, and still is, the world's best-selling blue curaçao. Known in the previous century as Creme de Ciel. it is one of the oldest flavours in the modern Bols liqueur range. Bols Blue adds a sparkling blue colour, a powerful orange flavour and a superb and unique aftertaste to any cocktail. As well as being a great substitute for Triple Sec in cocktails like the Blue Margarita and electric lemonade, it is also delicious when served long with soda or orange juice. Most Blue Curaçaos only offer colour to a drink, but Bols Blue adds a rich, varied orange flavour from a special type of oranges and its peel. This gives Bols Blue a unique finish.

COLOUR : Deep blue.

AROMA :   Fresh mandarine-like scent.

FLAVOUR : Orange flavour from fruit and peel with a unique finish.

desc_en_full:
Vinification:

the freshly harvested grapes are pressed softly, the resulting juice sent to storage tanks where it remains for a few hours at a temperature close to zero before being filtered. The must is kept cold until its preparation begins. At this point, the temperature is raised to 18°C, fermentation starts and, when the alcoholic level reaches 5°C, fermentation activity is stopped by lowering the temperature to 0°C.

Ageing: :

this is a wine that should be drunk very young, even a few weeks after harvesting it is already ready for drinking.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Cote des Roses Rose
wine_color: Tender, pale and brilliant rosé with bluish reflections evolving with time to more orange nuances
desc_en_short: Cote des Roses celebrates the Mediterranean lifestyle. The Languedoc appellation stretches alongside the Mediterranean coast The soils vary, mainly with hard limestone and schist, but also gravel transported from the rivers of the Languedoc region The bottle is original with its base in the shape of a rose, created by a young designer from the Ecole Boulle A wine to be given in the same way you would offer a bunch of roses!
desc_en_full:
Winegrower’s note

Cote des Roses celebrates the Mediterranean lifestyle. The Languedoc appellation stretches alongside the Mediterranean coast The soils vary, mainly with hard limestone and schist, but also gravel transported from the rivers of the Languedoc region The bottle is original with its base in the shape of a rose, created by a young designer from the Ecole Boulle A wine to be given in the same way you would offer a bunch of roses!

Winemaker’s note

The various grape varieties are harvested separately when each of them is just at the right ripeness level. The winemaking process is managed to respect the specific characteristics of the grape varieties and the qualities of the soil. The grapes are harvested mechanically with a built in sorting system The grapes are de stemmed, cooled down to 8 C and transferred to the press to extract the rosé wine must.  Particular attention is paid to the pressing to ensure that only the first, highest quality juice is kept The juice is then left to settle according to the precise aromatic profile that is being targeted. The fermentation lasts between 15 and over 30 days, depending on the degree of clarification and the temperature. Finally, after a light fining, the wine is bottled quite early to preserve the fresh, fruity character of the wines.

Tasting notes

The robe shows a soft, pale, brilliant pink with bluish tints developing over time towards more orangey nuances.

The bouquet releases aromas of summer fruits, cassis and redcurrant Floral notes of rose along with hints of grapefruit complete the picture.

On the palate the impression is fresh and full, with great aromatic persistence and balance The finish is fresh, offering notes of candy.

Food & Wine pairing

This Languedoc is ideal at 10 C as an aperitif or with shellfish and subtly spiced dishes

desc_en_full:
Nestled between the Orcia and Ombrone rivers on the southern slopes of Montalcino, Tuscany, the Castello Banfi vineyard estate is unique for its wine culture and tradition. The wines of Banfi Toscana range, produced with grapes selected from specific Banfi vineyards as well as other noble areas of the region, express the genuine and authentic character of Tuscany. They reflect the love for Tuscany, revealing joy and genuine nature in their easy-drinking style.Thanks to an inherent liveliness and freshness, these wines are appropriate for everyday consumption, yet always reflect the Banfi standard of character and quality.

GRAPE VARIETIES Traditional varieties of Chianti Classico with an absolute predominance of Sangiovese.

PRODUCTION TECHNIQUE The fermentation takes place with a traditional skin contact of 8-10 days. A short wood aging follows. Bottling is carried out in the Summer subsequent to the harvest.

DESCRIPTION Ruby red color. Intense bouquet with varietal notes of cherry, plum and violet. Fresh, balanced, typical.

WINEMAKER'S NOTES Celebrating the best Tuscan tradition Banfi offers a family of Chianti wines varied and complementary, unique in its style. Banfi Chianti Classico perfectly interprets the unique personality of this historic appellation with its alluring bouquet, rich flavours, supple tannins and good acidity.

FOOD PAIRINGS A wine for every dish, matches meat dishes superbly.

name: Villa Girardi  Valpolicella Classico Superiore
desc_en_short: Very fresh and fruity, with lots of cherry notes. Fairly intense, sweet cherry flavours. Well balanced and full of flavours. Recommended with  pasta, pizza, vegetables, white and red meats, fresh cheeses.
desc_en_full:
VILLA GIRARDI

Villa Girardi is a family-owned winery established in 1986. Located in the valley that joins San Pietro in Cariano and Fumane, it is a part of the historical classic Valpolicella wine region northwest of Verona. The estate is a typical Venetian villa dating back to the end of the 1700's, designed by the architect Luigi Trezza and set in a large park with century-old trees and shrubs. It is surrounded by 30 hectares of vineyards , all belonging to the property itself. The land is made of limestone and the climate is mild, both necessary conditions to grow the following local grape varieties: Corvina Veronese, Rondinella, and Molinara. The "Bure Alto" vineyard is located on a hill where the grapes for the production of the most historical and typical wines of the region, namely Valpolicella Classico, the Amarone, and the Recioto, are cultivated.

Appellation : Valpolicella Classico Superiore.

Grape Varieties : Corvina Blend.

Food Matching : All red meats, poultry or veal dishes, pasta with tomato and meat sauces, cheeses, pork, stews, roasts. Perfect with grilled meats.

desc_en_full:
PALACIOS REMONDO LA VENDIMIA

New vintage of this young red wine produced by the winery Bodegas Palacios Remondo, made of Tempranillo and Red Grenache grapes. The fermentation of the must takes place in stainless steel vats with different pumping over, and the partial carbonic maceration lasts 20 days. The malolactic fermentation and the ageing of Palacios Remondo La Vendimia take place in wooden vats and French oak barrels for 5 months.

COLOR

Clean and bright ruby red colour with maroon reflections.

NOSE

Some nuances provided by the carbonic maceration appear first and give way to subtle notes of fresh orchard fruit, wild berries and ripe blood oranges and light spicy hints provided by the oak during the ageing.

FLAVOUR

Young wine with a good intensity. The hints of red and black fruit provided by the carbonic maceration are dominant. It shows a fresh and fun attack, a light mid palate and a medium, very nice finish. Meant to drink now through the next four years, this consumer-friendly wine can be served slightly chilled and is the perfect accompaniment to any dish.

Producer detail:

Bodegas Palacios Remondo is based in Alfaro in the Rioja Baja. This old family firm has been revitalised by the arrival of Alvaro Palacios who has already made his name, starting Made from scratch, with his extraordinary Priorato wines.

He has since returned to the family property in Rioja Baja, and in a very short space of time, has decreased production by 60% and increased quality by a far higher factor! The most Southerly of the Rioja vineyards, Baja centres on the rich silty soils of the River Ebro, its best vineyards, such as this, at quite considerable altitude (550 metres).

name: Langmeil  Three Gardens, Shiraz-Mourvedre - Grenache
desc_en_short: Medium depth crimson in color. Lifted, fresh and bright fruit leaps out of the glass with dark cherries, Turkish delight and white pepper dominating the aroma. The bright and juicy fruit coats the palate and is well balanced with peppery and sweet spice. Medium bodied in structure with great complexity and lovely silky tannins which flow through the lengthy and savoury finish.
desc_en_full:
'Three Gardens' Shiraz, Mataro, Grenache

The old Barossa vigneron referred to his vineyard as his garden. Our Three Gardens in Tanunda, Lyndoch and Vine Vale are the source of this red blend. These Shiraz, Mataro and Grenache vines demonstrate perfectly the benefit of combining pedigree varieties, a traditional quality blend of the region.

Shiraz, Mataro and Grenache have provided inspiration for the Barossa since the 1840s. When blended together they are responsible for some of the great wines of the world and have stood the test of time with a proven pedigree over the past 200 years. Maturation in seasoned oak for twelve months ensures we don't overpower the fruit and spice, retaining the finesse.

name: Concha Y Toro  Marques de Casa Concha Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: Marqués de Casa Concha Cabernet Sauvignon is a benchmark Chilean red from Concha y Toro, delivering a deep, richly structured wine with a deceptively silky texture that gives way to firm, lingering tannins — a classic expression of Puente Alto fruit at its finest.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in Chilean wine as Concha y Toro, and the Marqués de Casa Concha tier represents the estate's most focused single-varietal expressions. The Cabernet Sauvignon is sourced predominantly from the Puente Alto subzone of the Maipo Valley — a gravelly, alluvial terroir at the foot of the Andes that consistently produces some of Chile's most structured and age-worthy reds. Moderate yields and careful canopy management coax genuine concentration from the vines, while extended maceration and ageing in French oak barrels add layers of complexity and a supple, integrated texture.

In the glass, the wine pours a deep, vibrant ruby with violet highlights. On the nose, classic Maipo Cabernet character emerges: ripe blackcurrant and plum lead, with supporting notes of dark chocolate, cedar, dried herbs, and a whisper of tobacco. The palate is where Marqués de Casa Concha truly impresses — a smooth, almost silky entry belies the firm tannic architecture beneath, which builds gracefully toward a long, persistent finish that reveals the wine's true depth and structure.

This is a wine built for the dinner table. Pair it with a prime rib or slow-braised beef short ribs, a platter of aged cured meats, or anything coming off a charcoal grill. Those who enjoy cellaring will find it rewards two to five additional years of bottle age, developing even greater complexity and softness over time.

name: Catena Zapata  Catena Alta Melbec
wine_color: A deep violet color, with blue reflections.
desc_en_short: Catena Alta Malbec shows a deep violet color, with blue reflections. The nose is elegant and complex with ripe red and black berry fruits, notes of violets and lavender and a touch of leather, spice and vanilla falvors. The palate is full and rich with soft and sweet tannins and a silky, smooth structure. Mulitple layers of rich cassis, black currant and blackberries are interwoven with hints ofl icorice and black pepper. Its lengthy finish is marked by wonderful minerality, finely grained tannins and lively acidity.
desc_en_full:
Historical :

Catena Alta is an assemblage of historic rows within the Catena family Estate vineyards. The blend of these historic rows, like the marriage of sounds that create a symphony, yields a highly aromatic and elegant wine that speaks for the earth and the vines that have been tended by the Catenas for four generations. The Catena Alta wines can usually be drunk starting at three years after harvest and into the following one to two decades.

Description :

Nicola Catena planted his first Malbec vineyard in Mendoza in 1902. His grandson, Nicolás Catena, is known as the man who revolutionized Argentine wine and introduced high altitude Malbec to the world. The Historic Catena Zapata vineyards are planted with the Catena family's proprietary selection of malbec plants: the Catena Cuttings. Catena Alta Malbec is sourced from Block 18 of the Angélica vineyard, Block 4 of the La Pirámide vineyard, Block 1 of the Nicasia vineyard and Blocks 3 & 9 of the Adrianna vineyard.

name: Chateau Bonnet  "Reserve" Bordeaux
desc_en_short: Deep garnet in colour, nose of red fruits blending with spicy, licorice notes. Soft upfront, the palate shows a delightful opulence and fruit core with toasty nuances, and velvety tannins still a touch austere on the finish.  Will gain balance over the next two years in bottle.
desc_en_full:
Chateau Bonnet Reserve

Chateau Bonnet is one of Bordeaux's consistently reliable wines. Vignobles André Lurton are committed to making wines that are both fullbodied and balanced, like this Reserve cuvee, which typifies classic Bordeaux.  Its faultless pedigree is revealed in its elegant balance, depth and charm.

Appareance

Very intense purple with crimson tints.

Nose

The initial impression on the nose is vanilla, closely followed by an intense panoply of oaky aromas. The ripe fruit appears gradually, delicate at first then more powerful after agitation.

Mouth

Opulent, smooth and silky upfront, but the tannic concentration is right there, unusually for this level in the range, a testament to the quality of the vintage and the skill of the teams at Bonnet. The finish is as long as it is full-flavoured. A wine to grace the most exalted cellar, before accompanying veal roulades or undercut of beef with shallot confit.

name: Cordero Di Montezemolo  Barbera d'Alba DOC
desc_en_short: Delicious, fine and elegant wine of ruby color with cherry scent and fruity flavor. New planting of Nebbiolo clones, fermentation in stainless steel desserts, apple dairy fermentation in baricades and the subsequent combination of large and small barrels for wine maturation make the Cordero di Montezemolo a modern winery, perfectly combining tradition with modernity. We recommend grilled, cooked and steamed meat.
desc_en_full:
Farming tradition that found its rightful dimension in the mid-1990s.
The Barbera grape has always been grown in Piedmont, but because of its, undeserved, reputation, it was neglected for many years and associated with the image of “farmer’s wine” in the derogatory sense that this term once held. Its potential emerged with new energy in the early 1990s thanks to various producers and now, having found its legitimate dignity, it is an indispensable and distinctive representative of this area. This wine is obtained from the fusion of all the plots of Barbera managed by the winery which, due to their different age, location and soils, are harvested separately, then aged for a short period of 4/6 months in wood and finally joined together in a single cuvée to maintain a freshness, balance, pleasantness and genuineness that make it ideal for every palate.

name: Chateau Ducru-Beaucaillou  Le Petit Caillou, St Julien
desc_en_short: A Saint-Julien countercurrent, produced by the team of Château Ducru-Beaucaillou, prestigious Cru Classé of Saint-Julien . With Le Petit Caillou, Bruno Borie declines his legendary wine in a vintage accessible from his earliest youth, coming from vineyards located on the western part of the Saint-Julien appellation . Blend of cabernet-sauvignon (50%), merlot (40%) and cabernet franc , matured in barrels with a third of new wood, Le Petit Caillou offers elegant aromas of black fruits, tobacco and cedar with tannins sweet.
desc_en_full: Le Petit Caillou is not exactly a second wine - La Croix de Beaucaillou fulfills this role. It is more of a special cuvée made from parcels decommissioned in the vineyard of the castle Ducru-Beaucaillou. We are no longer in a Classified Growth but simply in the Saint-Julien appellation, always with a magnificent terroir of gravel that looks at the estuary of the Gironde, this gigantic air conditioner that tempers the local heat. 3 grape varieties are present: a large half of cabernet sauvignon, a small half of merlot and the rest in cabernet franc. The grape harvests are manual for a winemaking in vats and a rearing in barrels with little new wood. The result is a very pleasant wine to discover Saint-Julien, with a dense and elegant material, And a nose over the flowers and red fruits. It is to drink within 6 or 7 years.

name: Orma by Tenuta Sette Ponti  Bolgheri IGT
desc_en_short: Orma epitomizes the style of wine that has made Bolgheri a household name among enthusiasts of the 'Super-Tuscan' phenomenon.  Bordelaise grape varieties achieve superlative expression in Bolgheri, but they do so in a style that is unique to this low-lying, sun-drenched, and breezy zone near the Tyrrhenian Sea. Orma's composition of 50% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Sauvignon, and 20% Cabernet Franc blends the ripeness and density that one might associate with Napa's sunny skies with earthiness and power similar to that for which Bordeaux is the benchmark.  A backbone of vibrant acidity and aromas of dusty earth keep Orma's identity firmly Italian.

Tenuta Sette Ponti Oreno

Oreno, named after the river that runs through the property, is the flagship of Tenuta Sette Ponti. It is a wine of international renown, as evidenced by the American magazine Wine Spectator, which more than once has placed Oreno among the best wines of the annual Top 100 Wines, along with Wine Advocate by Robert Parker ratings.

name: Altos Las Hormigas  Reserve Malbec
wine_color: Impenetrable black colour with a bright purple rim.
desc_en_short: A benchmark Uco Valley Malbec from Altos Las Hormigas, grown on limestone-rich soils across Altamira, Vista Flores, and Gualtallary, then aged 18 months in large untoasted foudres — delivering layered mineral complexity, fine-grained tannins, and a signature chalky finish that sets this apart from fruit-forward Malbec conventions.
desc_en_full:
Altos Las Hormigas has long been one of Argentina's most thoughtful voices in Malbec, and this Reserve bottling makes a compelling case for the Uco Valley as the grape's finest address. Fruit is sourced from three of Mendoza's most celebrated high-altitude subzones — Altamira, Vista Flores, and Gualtallary — each contributing calcareous, limestone-laced soils over alluvial deposits that impart the wine's unmistakable mineral backbone. Active lime content of 7–10% in the subsoil drives the chalky texture and structural precision that defines this cuvée.

In the winery, Altos Las Hormigas keeps intervention to a minimum: native yeast fermentation, gentle pigeage to avoid over-extraction, and no tartaric acid corrections. Extended ageing in 3,500-litre untoasted foudres for 18 months allows the wine to breathe and integrate without imposing heavy oak character, followed by a further six months of bottle rest before release. The result is a Malbec of impressive depth and restraint — violet florals, chalky earth, and subtle mineral lift woven together with fine-grained tannins and a freshness that keeps the long finish precise rather than heavy.

This is the bottle to reach for when quality cuts of Argentine asado, a slow-braised lamb shoulder, or a dry-aged ribeye are on the table. Decant for at least 30 minutes and allow the wine's layered character to slowly reveal itself — it rewards patience generously.

name: Glaetzer  Anaperenna
desc_en_short: The wine is a seamless fusion of two varieties: Shiraz and Cabernet Sauvignon. Bright and vibrant dark purple color with an expressive nose of black currant, liquorice and anise. A finely structured and elegant tannin structure supports luscious flavors of chocolate and dark berries with fresh acidity. A wine that will evolve very well with long term cellaring .
desc_en_full:
The Glaetzer History

The first Glaetzers settled in the Barossa Valley in 1888 after emigrating from Brandenburg, Germany. The family left Germany on board the Nord-Deutscher Lloyd steamer “Habsburg” and took seven weeks to reach their final destination of Port Adelaide. From here they settled in a country town called Nuriootpa in the Barossa Valley where they started their new life in Australia. The family were some of the earliest recorded viticulturalists in the Barossa Valley and Clare Valley.

Over a century later, winemaking patriarch Colin Glaetzer established Glaetzer Wines, a boutique family owned winery producing premium Barossa Valley wines. Today Colin’s son Ben carries on the wine making traditions at Glaetzer Wines. Indeed it was the birth of Glaetzer Wines that signalled a new era for Colin’s family which boasts more than its fair share of winemakers.

Bright ruby-red color, this wine offers layered notes of cherry, mulberry and raspberry. On the palate, it is complex and supple, with ripe berry flavors framed by silky tannins. A delicious Sicilian red at a great value!

Food Pairing

The right choice for eggplant parmigiana, pasta with meat sauce, meatballs, sausages, pizza and roasted meats.

desc_en_full: This is the new solo venture in Puglia of the Farnese group (responsible for Gran Sasso, Zabu, Vesevo, Tufarello and others). The wines were made at two wineries in the province of Taranto on the western flank of Salento. The style of the wines - fresh whites, soft and generous reds - will be familiar to those who buy their other wines, as winemaker Filippo Baccalaro is the driving force behind this new label. Filippo, a native of Piemonte, has been working in Puglia for almost two decades. He was attracted by the rich, ripe flavours of the fruit, and the ability to capture these flavours with minimal use of technology and a lot of know-how. Filippo has the latter, as is illustrated by these wines. The star wines are, of course, those made from native grapes like Verdeca, Negroamaro and, of course, Primitivo.

name: Angove  Family Crest Mclaren Vale Shiraz
desc_en_short:
Deep red with purple hues. Spice, pepper, red cherry and black chocolate aromas precede a palate of dark chocolate and liquorice, red cherry/raspberry with mouth-filling presence and soft lingering tannins.

James Halliday Australian Wine Companion - Dec 2013 - 94 Points
desc_en_full:
Winemaking

Blended from a number of parcels from our Warboys Vineyard and special growers, this is an example of just how well Shiraz performs in the McLaren Vale district. One parcel was from the north western part of the vineyard, high on the slope which faces the Angove cellar door, while the other parcel was from the mid part of the opposite slope. The vines in this area of our vineyard could be up to 80 years old, with local knowledge dating them as being planted sometime prior to the Second World War. The vineyard is in the process of being renovated, bringing yield back into balance with the vine age and structure; nurturing these venerable stalwarts of Australia's viticultural history. Biodynamic and organic practices help ensure the vineyard soils stay healthy and are able to support and sustain the vines as they produce their intense fl avoursome fruit

Testing Nose

Deep red with purple hues. Spice, pepper, red cherry and black chocolate aromas precede a palate of dark chocolate and liquorice, red cherry/raspberry with mouth-filling presence and soft lingering tannins.

name: Luce Della Vite  Luce Brunello di Montalcino DOCG
wine_color: The appearance is a shimmering ruby red
desc_en_short:
The 2020 season was characterised by a mild, dry winter, which encouraged a rapid budbreak, starting in the
last week in March. Fine weather continued throughout the spring as well, with some light rains, particularly in
May, which favoured normal canopy development, up to flowering in early June. Rains arrived between the
second and third weeks of June, crucial for development and growth of the newly-formed berries as well
coping with the summer which turned out to be quite hot and dry until August. Towards the end of August,
three providential rainstorms arrived, bring relief to the vines and preventing heat stress. After some weeks of
beneficial day-night temperature differentials, harvest started on 23 September. The grapes were in perfect
condition, perfectly ripe and healthy.
desc_en_full: The 2020 season was characterised by a mild, dry winter, which encouraged a rapid budbreak, starting in the last week in March. Fine weather continued throughout the spring as well, with some light rains, particularly in May, which favoured normal canopy development, up to flowering in early June. Rains arrived between the second and third weeks of June, crucial for development and growth of the newly-formed berries as well coping with the summer which turned out to be quite hot and dry until August. Towards the end of August, three providential rainstorms arrived, bring relief to the vines and preventing heat stress. After some weeks of beneficial day-night temperature differentials, harvest started on 23 September. The grapes were in perfect condition, perfectly ripe and healthy.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Morgon
desc_en_short:
This Morgon is juicy, layered, and graceful, offering generous red berry fruit, a touch of earthiness, and a mineral backbone. It's more serious and composed than your average Beaujolais, showcasing the complexity Gamay can achieve in top terroirs.

Perfect for pairing with bistro cuisine, charcuterie, or sipping on a cool evening when you crave freshness and finesse in one glass.
desc_en_full:
Joseph Drouhin, founded in 1880, is one of Burgundy’s most respected family-run domaines. Known for crafting precise, terroir-expressive wines, Drouhin brings their Burgundy expertise into Beaujolais, applying finesse to the Gamay grape.

Morgon is one of the most celebrated crus of Beaujolais, known for producing structured, age-worthy wines—and Drouhin’s version captures this beautifully, bridging the vibrancy of Gamay with the elegance of Pinot Noir–like textures.

name: Kendall Jackson  Vintner's Reserve Merlot
desc_en_short: 89 Point by Wine Enthusiast "There's good cut to this minerally red, which features luscious raspberry, dark plum and blackberry flavors. Well-structured, with a spicy and chocolaty finish. Drink now through 2020."
desc_en_full:
Winemaking

Aged about one year in French (62%) and American (37%) oak for a wine that is strong and round, roughly 14% of which is new. Artisan winemaking techniques such as pre-fermentation, cold soaking on the skins, saignée method and gentle pumpovers were used to enhance and simultaneously soften the tannins, deepen the color, and intensify the flavors.

Tasting Notes

Our Vintner's Reserve Merlot has intricate layers of black cherry, plum, currant, and wild berry that mingle with a hint of spice. The soft, delicate tannins of this Bordeaux varietal carry these aromas and flavors to a long lingering finish.

name: Merryvale  Napa Valley Merlot
desc_en_short: The Wine is dark and inky, made in big, bold style. Cedar, pine and tart black-currant notes provide one side of its personality, as more richly concentrated chocolate and blackberry flavors give it oomph.
desc_en_full:
2014 offered excellent ripening conditions—perfect temperatures and just the right amount of light to allow the Merlot to ripen slowly. We were able to bring incredible fruit into the cellar with beautiful acidity that will maintain the freshness of this wine for many more years. The tannins are very soft and fine grained, demonstrating the texture and complexity of this wine and showcasing the delicate nature of Merlot. Enjoy this wine at release and for the next 5 to 10 years. Critical Acclaim

name: Layer Cake  Malbec
desc_en_short: Big, brooding, black fruit, then rich earth, truffles and dark cocoa are at the front. The Sea of Stones vineyard is at its core, always evident with the thread of minerality that the deep alluvial cobblestones lend. This wine is elegant in the mouth with fresh-picked blackberries, simmering chocolate sauce on the stove, and somewhere someone’s cooking bacon.
desc_en_full:
WINEMAKER NOTES

We allow Layer Cake Malbec to ripen thoroughly on the vine. We tend to harvest four to six weeks after all other neighboring vineyards; a crucial element to the wine's quality. Each cluster, once harvested by hand, is then destemmed and sorted – also by hand. Born at the base of the Andes and fed by the purest spring waters…I love this place.

VINEYARD NOTES

The fruit for Layer Cake Malbec comes from a vineyard that is flanking the Mendoza River in Argentina -a rocky terrain, strikingly similar to that of Chateauneuf du Pape in the Rhône Valley. The once 5-mile- wide Mendoza River is now mostly vineyard, abundant with some of Argentina's most pristine Malbec vines. A plethora of smooth cobbles on the top bewilders the eye and imagination as to how the vines actually thrive in such a rugged terrain. Silt and gravel make up the subsoil under the larger cobbles. Our vineyard sits at an elevation of about 3,000 feet.

Climate: The climate is arid, with very low rainfall totals. Warm days are followed by cool evenings as the breezes from the Andes Mountain Range flow down to the Mendoza Valley floor.

TASTING NOTES

Big, brooding, black fruit, then rich earth, truffles and dark cocoa are at the front. The Sea of Stones vineyard is at its core, always evident with the thread of minerality that the deep alluvial cobblestones lend. This wine is elegant in the mouth with fresh-picked blackberries, simmering chocolate sauce on the stove, and somewhere someone's cooking bacon…so many layers, so little time. Drinking a bottle of this wine will certainly lead to, well, opening another.

name: Yellow Tail  Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: Yellow Tail Cabernet Sauvignon is an approachable, fruit-forward Australian red brimming with cassis, mulberry, and crushed herbs, backed by soft oak and feathery tannins — easy to enjoy now yet capable of rewarding a few years in the cellar.
desc_en_full:
Few brands have done more to introduce the world to Australian wine than Yellow Tail, and its Cabernet Sauvignon remains the flagship expression of that mission — honest, generous, and instantly likeable. Sourced from warm-climate vineyards across South Eastern Australia, the wine is crafted to showcase everything the Cabernet Sauvignon grape does best: concentrated fruit, a natural herbal edge, and a structure that never feels intimidating.

In the glass, expect a deep ruby colour that signals the wine's ripe, concentrated character. The nose leads with blackcurrant (cassis) and mulberry, brightened by a lift of violets and underscored by crushed green herbs — that classic Cabernet eucalyptus note that speaks unmistakably of Australian terroir. A whisper of cracked black pepper and subtle vanilla oak round out a bouquet that is both inviting and layered.

On the palate, the wine is full-bodied yet soft, with medium acidity keeping everything fresh and feathery tannins that allow the fruit to glide through to a long, smooth finish. The winemaking team at Yellow Tail has deliberately preserved concentration and texture, giving this everyday bottle a seriousness that belies its accessible price point.

Pour this alongside a pepper-crusted scotch fillet, a slow-cooked lamb shank, or a platter of cured meats straight off the barbecue. It handles bold flavours with ease, making it the kind of bottle that disappears quickly at a casual dinner or weekend cookout.

name: Catena Zapata  Malbec Argentino
wine_color: A saturated dark violet color with ruby reflections
subregion: Luján de Cuyo / Valle de Uco
desc_en_short: Catena Zapata's Malbec Argentino is a landmark expression of high-altitude Argentine Malbec, weaving together cold maceration, whole-cluster complexity, and 15–18 months in French oak into a wine of remarkable depth, dark fruit richness, and earthy elegance.
desc_en_full:
Few producers have done more to define Argentine Malbec on the world stage than Catena Zapata, the Mendoza estate whose relentless pursuit of altitude has reshaped the category entirely. Malbec Argentino is a celebration of the grape's extraordinary journey — from its origins along the banks of the Lot River in Cahors, France, through the devastation of phylloxera in the Old World, to its triumphant renaissance in the high-altitude vineyards of Mendoza's Luján de Cuyo and Valle de Uco. The wine's striking label — depicting four female figures including Eleanor of Aquitaine and Adrianna Catena herself — tells this ten-century story in vivid symbolic form.

In the cellar, winemaker Alejandro Vigil employs an extended cold maceration of up to ten days at below 10°C, incorporating 10–30% whole clusters to preserve aromatic lift and structural complexity. Fermentation runs for up to 28 days, with total maceration reaching nearly 40 days for maximum depth and texture. The wine is then aged for 15–18 months in French oak — a mix of first, second, and third-fill barrels — integrating oak seamlessly without overwhelming Malbec's inherent fruit character.

In the glass, expect a deep violet core with aromas of ripe plum, black cherry, dried violets, and a whisper of graphite-laced earth. The palate is full-bodied yet structured, with rounded tannins and a finish that lingers with dark chocolate and subtle cedar notes. Pour this alongside a prime Argentine asado, grilled lamb shoulder, or an aged Manchego for a pairing that honours the wine's cross-continental heritage.
