name: De Stefani  Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: From old vines rooted in the clay soils of the Piave DOC, De Stefani's Cabernet Sauvignon delivers an intense ruby pour with ripe red and wild fruit aromas, a smooth full body, and a pleasantly spicy finish — a classically structured Italian Cabernet built for the table.
desc_en_full:
De Stefani is one of the defining estates of the Piave DOC in northeastern Italy's Veneto region, a territory long celebrated for producing Cabernet Sauvignon of genuine character and regional identity. The family's vineyards are planted on clay-rich soils that stress the vines just enough to concentrate flavour, and the estate draws on the advantage of old vine material to achieve a natural balance of ripeness without the need for intervention at harvest.

In the glass, this Cabernet Sauvignon opens with a deep, intense ruby colour and a welcoming bouquet of ripe red fruit — think black cherry and cassis — layered with notes of wild berry and a subtle earthen undercurrent. The palate is full-bodied yet polished, with tannins that are firm but well-integrated, medium acidity providing freshness, and a finish that lingers with fruity and gently spicy complexity. It is the kind of everyday Italian red that punches well above its price point.

This wine is made for the dinner table. Pour it alongside a slow-braised beef ragù over tagliatelle, a Sunday roast, or grilled bistecca. It is equally at home with cured charcuterie boards or hearty barbecued meats — anywhere bold, savoury flavours need a confident red to match them step for step.

name: Marchesi Di Barolo  Barolo di Barolo DOCG
desc_en_short: The colour is garnet red with ruby nuances. The aroma is intense and persistent, with clear scents of spices, cinnamon, absinthe, tobacco and dog rose. Full and elegant taste, with soft and enveloping tannins. The spicy and woody notes merge perfectly together.
desc_en_full:
Barolo di Barolo

The grapes that contribute to the production of this particular Barolo cuvée come exclusively from the estate’s "Historical Crus" vineyards. They are located in the municipality of Barolo on sloping hills made up of calcareous clay interspersed with compact layers of grey sandstone and covered with quartz sand and fine silt. Nebbiolo is the quintessential eclectic grape that extracts many mineral nutrients from the compact clay and limestone soil, enriching its structure. At the same time, it takes on the fragrant and spicy aromas from the loose sand and silt soil. The goal is to obtain the best cuvée possible by harmoniously synthesizing the vintage’s many characteristics, taking into account the climatic trends and Nebbiolo’s overriding personality, which are based on the microclimate and the quality of the soil of each single vineyard.

Initially, the wine ages in small oak barrels then it is blended in the traditional big Slavonian oak barrels, making it harmonious and well-balanced, and enhancing the elegance of the bouquet. Before going onto the market, the wine completes its fining in the bottle for a few months. Barolo del Comune di Barolo reaches maturity 3 years after being harvested and the peak condition is when it is 30 years old.

name: Santa Ema  Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: Santa Ema Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Maipo Valley delivers a classically structured red with aromas of sour cherry, prune, mint, and tobacco, backed by ripe tannins and a lingering finish that suits grilled meats and aged cheeses beautifully.
desc_en_full:
Santa Ema has been one of Maipo Valley's most reliable custodians of Cabernet Sauvignon for decades, farming vineyards in a region widely regarded as Chile's spiritual home for the variety. Sitting at the foot of the Andes, Maipo's warm days and cool nights allow grapes to build both phenolic ripeness and the fresh herbal character that has become the valley's signature — a quality that shines clearly in this Gran Reserva bottling.

In the cellar, the fruit is crushed and fermented in stainless steel at a warm 30 °C for seven days, extracting generous colour and structure, then extended through six additional days of post-fermentation maceration to build texture and depth. Malolactic fermentation softens the palate before the wine rests in barrel for eight to ten months, adding layers of toast and subtle spice without overwhelming the fruit.

The result is a wine that opens with inviting aromas of sour cherry and dried plum, laced with fresh mint, tobacco leaf, and a hint of toasty oak. On the palate it's full-bodied yet approachable, with ripe, well-integrated tannins and a persistent, savoury finish. Pour it alongside a prime-cut grilled ribeye, slow-smoked brisket at a weekend barbecue, or a platter of hard aged cheeses. Spice-forward dishes — think a robust lamb stew or a peppery chimichurri — are equally at home here. A dependable, food-friendly Chilean Cabernet Sauvignon that punches above its price point.

Vinification

The grapes were crushed and fermented in stainless steel tanks for 7 days at 86ºF (30ºC) followed by another 6 days of post-fermentation macerations. Upon completion of the malolactic fermentation, the wine was barrel aged for 8–10 months to obtain and deliver greater complexity and structure.

name: Chateau Musar  Jeune Red
desc_en_short: This vintage is a deep burgundy red with an attractive red cherry and violet aroma.  Juicy, sweet summer fruits; raspberries, black cherries with hints of elderberry and hibiscus dominate the palate which is beautifully balanced with soft tannins and fresh underlying acidity and incense like spice.
desc_en_full:
Musar Jeune Red

Chateau Musar first started producing the Musar Jeune range of wines in 2006 from the fruit of low-yielding Bekaa Valley vines at around 1,000 metres above sea level. Altitude keeps the average temperature at 25 degrees Celsius with hot sunny days and cool nights and with 300 days of sunshine a year, grapes are ripened to perfection.

Unoaked and made according to Musar’s natural non-interventionist winemaking philosophy, Musar Jeune wines are vibrantly fruity, richly concentrated and this vintage is no exception.

The Musar Jeune Red 2014 is a blend of old vines Cinsault (45%), Syrah (45%) and Cabernet Sauvignon (10%) from organically certified vineyards, harvested during the last week of August 2014. The wines fermented in concrete vats between 27 and 29 degrees Celsius, they were not fined or filtered and the sedimentation occurred naturally. Blending of the 3 varieties took place in March 2015, it rested in concrete vats again until being bottled in the summer of 2015.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mapu Merlot (Screw Cap)
desc_en_short: A vibrant, fruit-forward Merlot from Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Chilean Mapu range, bursting with bilberry, redcurrant, and blackberry character, silky tannins, and a fresh, balanced finish that makes it an effortlessly enjoyable everyday red.
desc_en_full:
Baron Philippe de Rothschild needs little introduction — the legendary Bordeaux dynasty behind Mouton Rothschild has long extended its winemaking philosophy beyond France, and the Mapu range represents its approachable, New World expression crafted in Chile's Central Valley. The name "Mapu" comes from the Mapuche word for "land," a fitting tribute to the sun-drenched terroir that gives this Merlot its generous, fruit-first personality.

In the glass, expect a deep, attractive ruby red with real clarity and vibrancy. The nose is clean and inviting, offering lifted aromas of bilberry, redcurrant, and fresh cherry — classic Merlot hallmarks given a Chilean warmth and ripeness. On the palate, the wine opens with an elegant, fruit-driven attack before revealing a succulent, stylish mid-palate packed with blackberry and black cherry flavours. Silky, low tannins keep the texture smooth and accessible, while the finish is fresh, well-balanced, and gently lingering.

Sealed with a practical screw cap to preserve freshness, this is a wine built for relaxed enjoyment without ceremony. Pour it alongside a grilled lamb chop, a slow-braised beef ragu, or a casual backyard barbecue. It's equally happy on a weeknight dinner table as it is at a relaxed weekend gathering — proof that the Rothschild name can deliver both pedigree and pure everyday pleasure.

name: Wynns  Coonawarra  Estate  Black Label Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short:
Wynns Black Label Cabernet is a true icon of Australian wine. First produced in 1954, it has a reputation for ageing gracefully and displaying excellent varietal and regional characteristics. The wine is produced from only the top quality 20 to 25 per cent of Cabernet Sauvignon fruit grown in our terra rossa vineyards.

A wine of style and stature - and a perennial favourite in auction circles - ‘Black Label’ is one of Australia’s most collectable wines and Australia’s benchmark Cabernet Sauvignon. It consistently delivers potential for medium to long-term cellaring.
desc_en_full:
Wynns Coonawarra Estate was founded by Scottish pioneer John Riddoch, who was a true visionary. He planted vineyards and in 1891 and completed the estates triple-gabled winery in 1896.

In 1951, the Wynn family purchased the iconic winery and vineyard, convinced of the region’s potential to produce top-quality wines. Melbourne designer Richard Beck’s now iconic woodcut illustration of the winery façade adorns the first wine released under the Wynns Coonawarra Estate label in 1952. Over the past 18 years, the Wynns Coonawarra Estate vineyards have been meticulously rejuvenated resulting in wines of greater refinement to capture the true essence of Coonawarra – varietal character with finesse.

Today Wynns Coonawarra Estate is Coonawarra’s pre-eminent wine producer and largest single vineyard holder, with the longest-established vineyards in the region. Under the guidance of Sue Hodder (Senior Winemaker since 1998) and Allen Jenkins (Wynns Viticulturist) Wynns wines are regarded as benchmarks for the district, lauded for their consistent quality and depth of flavour.

On the palate, Alanera is full-bodied with elegant and velvety tannins. Vibrant acidity brings balance and freshness, and supports a long and harmonious finish.
desc_en_full:
Alanera is a tribute to Sergio’s legacy of bringing understanding, wisdom and rigorous work to viticulture in Valpolicella.

Alanera’s fruit hails from the Costalunga vineyard. The site’s landscape is remarkable as it overlooks the city of Verona, while partly hugging Lake Garda and being seemingly embraced by the impressive Monte Baldo and Lessinian mountain ranges.

Alanera’s Three Swallows serve as symbol to these landscape elements as well to its trio of native grapes: Corvina, Rondinella and Corvinone.

With the limited edition Barossa Valley Explorer pack, we celebrate the best of this famed region with two different expressions. Bin 150 is a stylish and contemporary shiraz born from the ancient soils of Marananga, while Bin 138 is a plush and approachable blend of shiraz, grenache and mataro.

Penfolds Bin 150 Marananga Shiraz

A boysenberry/mulberry slurry, almost a coulis, with a smidgeon of cola/creamy soda flavours. Prominent tannins augment and propel an impression of a svelte film of raspberry seeds/pips, to heighten textural appeal. Penfolds Bin 138 Barossa Valley GMS

Deep crimson. Intense blackberry, musky plum, inky aromas with roasted walnut notes. Generous velvety wine with deep set juicy blackberry, dark chocolate, musky plum fruits, fine plentiful chocolaty firm textures, savoury oak complexity and integrated fine acidity. Finishes chocolaty leafy firm with musky plum aniseed notes. Delicious early drinking style with superb richness of flavour and power.

Wine making

Baron de Lestac red wine is made from these three iconic Bordeaux varieties. The grapes are vinified with great care and attention before being aged in our barrel cellar for six months in American oak. This ageing helps the wines develop their subtle notes and tannins. desc_en_full:
Antigal Winery was established on the site of a historic winery in Russell, Maipú, Mendoza – Argentina, whose origin dates back to 1897.

With its completed refurbishment, Antigal achieved the very best in high-tech functionality while respecting the original appearance of a historically important landmark in Mendoza, Argentina. This historic building house a state-of-the-art facility whose gravity-flow platform system allows for a largely “hands-off” approach to winemaking eliminating the need for forceful pumps and crushers

desc_en_full:
Winemaking:

The winemakers at Château Magnol, aim to extract high quality tnanins and flavours in order to produce a wine typical of the appellation: robust and powerful. After careful selection and minimal interference in the natural ripening of the grapes, alcoholic fermentation generally takes place at 30°C with one rack and return per day. The wine stays on the skins for about 3 weeks. After alcoholic fermentation, Château Magnol is aged in vat on its lees with controlled oxysgenation. The wine is then blended and put into oak barrels, where it stays for 12 months. In order to provide an elegant, well-integrated oak presence, Bernard Fulloy decided to use 350 litre French oak barrels, of which 30% are new every year. Once barrel ageing is over, the wine is fined and bottled.

name: Miguel Torres  Las Mulas Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: Las Mulas Cabernet Sauvignon from Miguel Torres Chile delivers vibrant red fruit aromas layered with spicy notes, supported by round, elegant tannins and a long, structured finish — an organically farmed expression of Cabernet at an approachable everyday price.
desc_en_full:
Miguel Torres is one of the wine world's great internationalists, and while the family's Spanish roots run deep in Penedès, it is their Chilean venture — Miguel Torres Chile — that produces the Las Mulas range, one of Latin America's pioneering certified-organic wine lines. Las Mulas takes its name from the mules once used to work the vineyards, a nod to the low-intervention, environmentally conscious farming philosophy that defines every bottle in the range. The Cabernet Sauvignon is sourced from organically managed vineyards in the Central Valley of Chile, where warm days and cool Pacific-influenced nights allow the grape to ripen fully while retaining freshness.

In the glass, this Cabernet pours a deep, vivid ruby. The nose opens with generous aromas of blackcurrant, ripe cherry, and red plum, backed by appealing hints of black pepper and dried herbs — classic signatures of Chilean Cabernet grown at moderate altitudes. On the palate, the structure is polished and approachable: medium body, well-integrated acidity, and those characteristically round, supple tannins that make this wine easy to enjoy relatively young, without sacrificing a satisfyingly consistent, fruit-forward finish.

This is a weeknight-friendly bottle that over-delivers for its price point. Pour it alongside a grilled ribeye, aged manchego, or slow-smoked barbecue ribs. It also shines at casual outdoor gatherings where you want something crowd-pleasing yet genuinely crafted. Serve slightly below room temperature — around 16–18 °C — to keep the fruit lively and the tannins smooth.

name: Castello Monaci  Piluna Primitivo Salento IGT
desc_en_short: Piluna is a wine characterized by a dark purple red color. It has a wide scent of ripe grapes, with hints of pepper and vanilla and flavors of Mediterranean scrub. On the palate it expresses a robust, concentrated, soft but firm taste with a graceful bottom of jam of small black fruits and long-lasting liquorice.
desc_en_full:
Piluna is a primitive wine produced at the Castello Monaci winery. It is produced with Primitivo grapes harvested from vineyards cultivated with Apulian sapling and Guyot on deep and dry clay soils with the presence of tuffaceous limestone, in the Salice Salentino area in the province of Lecce. Harvesting is done by hand only from dawn to nine in the morning, when the temperature is cooler.

The vinification is carried out at a controlled temperature with about two weeks of maceration and frequent pumping over. Once the malolactic fermentation has taken place, one part ages for 6 months in French barriques and with different toasting, while the rest remains in steel.

Piluna is a wine characterized by a dark purple red color. It has a wide scent of ripe grapes, with hints of pepper and vanilla and flavors of Mediterranean scrub. On the palate it expresses a robust, concentrated, soft but firm taste with a graceful bottom of jam of small black fruits and long-lasting liquorice. To be served at a temperature of 16 ° C.

Piluna is one of the best wines that can be found in Italy for value for money. A wine without too many frills, but perfect for those who still want to feel the true character of the Primitivo del Salento

name: Chateau La Fleur-Petrus  Pomerol
desc_en_short:
Château La Fleur-Pétrus is one of the most renowned vineyards of Pomerol. This historic estate, situated exclusively on the plateau, was named in the 18th century for its position across the localities ‘Petrus’ and ‘La Fleur’. Established in Libourne since 1937, Jean-Pierre Moueix perceived early on the excellent quality of the Pomerol appellation. Château La Fleur-Pétrus was his very first acquisition in 1950. Quickly after, he purchased Château Trotanoy in 1953.

The personality of Château La Fleur-Pétrus lies in the careful association of the famous gravel and clay terroirs of the plateau of Pomerol. The vineyard, located at 33 to 38 meters above sea level, draws its identity from the nuances of soil and elevation of each parcel. Those parcels which are dominated by gravel tend to yield wines of great elegance, while those dominated by clay are distinctly velvety, dense, and structured.

Château La Fleur-Pétrus is composed predominantly of Merlot, which lends silkiness and generosity to the wine. Cabernet Franc, planted on gravel and fine clay, contributes rigor and complexity, while a small percentage of Petit Verdot, planted on fine gravel, offers a hint of spice to the finish.

The wine combines a unique elegance with the generosity and structure typical of the great vineyards of Pomerol. An attentive tasting reveals remarkable refinement, complexity, and a touch of violet.

name: Chateau Barrail Du Blanc  Saint-Emilion
desc_en_short:
Château Barrail du Blanc produces wines that are exceptional for their depth and consistency. Owned by the same family for over 150 years, this property is located in the commune of Saint-Sulpice-de-Faleyrens, in the south of the Saint-Émilion Grand Cru appellation.

The vineyard, averaging 25 years of age, is planted on gravel and clay soils that allow for good drainage and are particularly well-suited to Cabernet Franc. The grapes are treated with attentive care by Monsieur Elliès and his daughter, Nathalie, in the vineyard and in the cellar. Ploughing, green harvesting, and leaf cropping are favored in order to ensure a consistent maturity of the fruit. The wine is vinified in thermoregulated stainless-steel tanks before aging partly in French oak barrels (50% new).

The resulting wine is charming, supple, elegant, and can be kept for five to ten years.

name: Phelps Creek  Le Petit Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: Phelps Creek Le Petit Pinot Noir delivers inviting aromas of strawberry and warm spice with bright, refreshing acidity and silky-smooth tannins — an approachable, lighter-styled Pinot Noir that shines as a warm-weather red, equally happy at the table or on its own.
desc_en_full:
Nestled in Oregon's Columbia Gorge AVA, Phelps Creek Vineyards has quietly earned a devoted following for its estate-grown Pinot Noir, a variety that thrives in the Gorge's dramatic interplay of cool Pacific winds and warm continental sunshine. The Le Petit Pinot Noir — whose name signals an intentionally lighter, more playful touch — represents the winery's invitation to enjoy Pinot Noir without ceremony, offering everything that makes the grape so beloved at an accessible, everyday level.

In the glass, this is a wine that leads with charm. The nose opens with fragrant notes of fresh strawberry, dried cherry, and a dusting of cinnamon-tinged spice that gives the aromatics a gentle warmth. On the palate, the wine's hallmark bright acidity carries those red-fruit flavours with lively energy, while the deliberately low tannin structure keeps the texture soft and easy-drinking — more silk than structure. Think of it as the Willamette cousin who traded seriousness for sheer drinkability.

This quality makes Le Petit Pinot Noir a remarkably versatile companion at the table. Its freshness and lift cut through the richness of lamb chops or a herb-crusted beef tenderloin, yet it's equally at home alongside charcuterie boards, grilled salmon, or a simple roast chicken. Serve it with a slight chill — around 14–16 °C — to amplify those bright fruit characters and make it the undisputed star of any alfresco summer gathering.

name: Ballade De La Pointe Pomerol Second wine of Chateau La Pointe
flavor_tags: Black Cherry, Dried Herbs, Dark Fruit, Mushroom, Mineral, Cherry, Earthy, Gravel, Plum, Oak
desc_en_short: Ballade de La Pointe is the second wine of Château La Pointe, one of Pomerol's largest estates — an elegant, approachable Right Bank Bordeaux showing the appellation's trademark Merlot richness with dark fruit, subtle earthiness, and silky tannins.
desc_en_full:
Château La Pointe is one of Pomerol's largest and most historic properties, with its château and vineyards occupying a prominent position on the plateau. Ballade de La Pointe is the second wine, offering an earlier-accessible expression of the estate's terroir at a more everyday price point.

Pomerol's terroir — a mix of gravel, sand, and the famous blue clay plateau — is uniquely suited to Merlot, which here develops an extraordinary combination of richness, texture, and mineral depth. Ballade de La Pointe is predominantly Merlot with Cabernet Franc, aged in French barriques.

Ruby with garnet highlights, it opens on ripe dark plum, black cherry, earthy minerals, dried herbs, and a gentle spice from the oak. The palate is medium-full bodied, with Pomerol's characteristic silky texture, soft tannins, and a warm, satisfying finish. Drink now or cellar for 3–5 years.

Pair with roast beef, duck, lamb, and mushroom-based dishes.

name: Pomerol By Clinet
flavor_tags: Blackberry, Truffle, Earthy, Cocoa, Plum, Oak
desc_en_short: Pomerol by Clinet is the second wine of the prestigious Château Clinet estate — a plush, approachable Right Bank Bordeaux with concentrated dark plum, blackberry, truffle, and velvety Merlot richness that showcases Pomerol at its most accessible.
desc_en_full:
Château Clinet is one of Pomerol's most celebrated properties, occupying a privileged position on the plateau's finest clay-over-iron soils. 'Pomerol by Clinet' is the estate's second wine, made from younger vines and parcels not destined for the grand vin, but vinified with the same meticulous care.

Like all great Pomerol, the wine is dominated by Merlot — the variety that thrives on the plateau's cool, moisture-retaining clay soils — with a small complement of Cabernet Franc for structure and aromatics. Aged in French oak, it develops the plushness, depth, and truffle-like complexity that defines Right Bank Bordeaux at its finest.

Deep ruby in colour, it opens on concentrated dark plum, blackberry, violets, cocoa, and a distinctive earthy, fungal complexity that whispers of the Pomerol terroir. The palate is rich and velvety, with soft, opulent tannins, warmth, and a long, flavourful finish. Approachable now but will improve with 3–7 years' ageing.

Serve with roast beef, duck confit, truffle-based dishes, and aged hard cheeses.

name: Chateau Laujac  Cru Bourgeois (Médoc)
desc_en_short: The Chateau Laujac wine is a red Bordeaux wine produced in the Médoc region. It is made from a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Petit Verdot grape varieties. The vineyards are planted on gravelly soils, which are ideal for producing high-quality wines. Chateau Laujac is known for its aromas of red and black fruits, as well as its spicy and woody notes. On the palate, it is structured with firm tannins and a balanced acidity, making it a perfect wine to accompany red meat or cheese dishes.
desc_en_full:
The Estate

THE CRUSE FAMILY IN LAUJAC SINCE 1852

Alongside his wine trading business, Hermann Cruse began a policy of acquiring wine estates by buying Chateau Laujac in 1852, rapidly followed by the purchase of Chateau Pontet-Canet and Chateau Giscours.

Laujac wines already had a good reputation, but in 1816 the wine writer, Jullien, rated Laujac in the fourth level in his “Topography of all known vineyards” and likened it to the famous wines of Pauillac.

The Cruse family undertook major modernization and embellishment works with the help of their famed estate managers: the Skawinski family. A beautiful Médoc style vat-house still remains from this time along with oxen paddocks, workers housing and a beautiful park. The Laujac site is included in the Inventory of Historical Monuments.

name: Corryton Burge  South Australian Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: A fruit-driven South Australian Cabernet Sauvignon from Corryton Burge, offering deep aromas of black cherry, liquorice, and leather with a palate of ripe cherry, dark chocolate, and gentle spice, all wrapped in characteristically soft, velvety tannins.
desc_en_full:
Corryton Burge draws on the sun-drenched vineyards of South Australia — one of the New World's most celebrated homes for Cabernet Sauvignon — to produce a classic expression of the variety that is approachable, generous, and thoroughly satisfying. South Australia's warm continental climate gives Cabernet Sauvignon the long ripening season it thrives on, building layers of dark fruit intensity while the region's well-drained soils contribute structure and definition to the finished wine.

In the glass, this Cabernet Sauvignon pours a deep crimson red with alluring ruby hues, already hinting at the ripe fruit character within. The nose is inviting and expressive — black cherry and liquorice dominate, underscored by earthy leather notes that add complexity and depth. A whisper of oak spice rounds out the aromatic picture.

On the palate, those vibrant cherry flavours carry through with impressive persistence, joined by dark chocolate and a gentle lift of spice that keeps each sip engaging. The tannins are the real talking point here: soft and velvety in texture, they provide enough framework to complement food without ever feeling grippy or austere. Medium acidity keeps the wine fresh and balanced throughout.

This is a bottle that excels at the dinner table. Pair it with a slow-roasted lamb shoulder, a pepper-crusted beef fillet, or a hearty braised short rib for a combination that brings out the best in both the food and the wine. Equally enjoyable on its own on a relaxed evening.

name: VIK  Millahue
grape_variety: Cabernet Sauvignon, Carmenère, Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Syrah
desc_en_short:
VIK Millahue – The Ultimate Wine from the “Golden Valley” Awarded a Perfect 100 Points by James Suckling

VIK Millahue is the flagship wine of the VIK brand, born from the vision of Alexander Vik, a Norwegian investor who dreamed of "creating the best wine in the world." He and his team of experts selected over 4,300 hectares of land in the Millahue Valley, which translates to "Place of Gold," to establish a vineyard that integrates unique terroir, science, and winemaking artistry.

VIK Millahue is the pinnacle of that philosophy — a wine that not only brilliantly showcases the potential of the Millahue region but also embodies "a wine that reflects time, patience, and perfect refinement."
desc_en_full:
Highlights:

✔ Perfect 100 Points from James Suckling:

The latest vintage of VIK Millahue received a flawless 100/100 score from world-renowned critic James Suckling, reinforcing its iconic status in Chile and globally.

✔ An "Extraordinary" Bordeaux-Style from Chile:

A blend of Bordeaux varietals including Cabernet Sauvignon, Carmenère, Cabernet Franc, Merlot, and Syrah results in a wine with a firm structure, complex aroma, and elegance.

✔ Deep Aroma of a Fine Wine:

Opens with rich dark fruit aromas like plum and blackberry, complemented by notes of oak, herbs, minerality, and hints of cocoa, vanilla, and bark.

✔ Complex and Powerful Palate:

The taste is rich and smooth, with fine tannins that gradually unfold in layers. It has a long, deep finish and can develop beautifully in the bottle, making it ideal for collectors or high-end fine dining.

✔ Meticulously Aged in 100% New French Oak:

VIK Millahue wine is aged in new French oak for over 24 months to develop structural complexity and enhance its profound smoothness.

name: Concha Y Toro Frontera Merlot
wine_color: Bright ruby red with youthful purple reflections.
desc_en_short: Concha y Toro's Frontera Merlot is a soft, fruit-forward Chilean red from the Central Valley, offering ripe plum and berry flavours with smooth, low tannins — an approachable, easy-drinking bottle perfect for everyday occasions.
desc_en_full:
Frontera is one of the best-known ranges from Concha y Toro, the iconic Chilean producer whose wines have earned a loyal following across the globe for delivering reliable quality at accessible prices. This Merlot draws its character from the sun-drenched vineyards of Chile's Central Valley, where warm days ripen grapes to generous softness while cool overnight temperatures preserve a gentle natural freshness that keeps the wine lively in the glass.

The winemaking philosophy behind Frontera is deliberately unfussy — grapes are handled to highlight clean, varietal Merlot fruit without leaning on heavy oak intervention. The result is a wine that leads with inviting aromas of ripe plum, red cherry, and a hint of dark berry, with subtle violet and soft spice notes in the background. On the palate it is round and smooth, medium in body with velvety tannins that never grip, making it one of the most beginner-friendly reds on the shelf without ever feeling thin or dull.

This is exactly the kind of bottle to reach for on a weeknight when dinner calls for something satisfying but uncomplicated. Pour it alongside a slow-braised beef stew, grilled lamb chops with rosemary, or a simple burger — its soft structure bridges casual comfort food beautifully. It also works well on its own as a relaxed evening sipper, shared among friends without ceremony. For anyone building a go-to house red, Frontera Merlot offers consistent, crowd-pleasing character at a price that invites a second bottle.

desc_en_short: Moët Ice Impérial, the first and only champagne especially created to be enjoyed over ice. The profiles of the wines used in the assemblage have been meticulously selected for the specific contribution made by each one :

name: Veuve Clicquot  Cuvee Rose
wine_color: A luminous color with attractive pink glints.
desc_en_short:
Winemaker Notes

This non-vintage Rose is the end result of a desire to create a Rose champagne with a delightfully luscious, fruit-based charm. Jacques Peters, the cellarmaster, and his team wanted a champagne that would be accessible and naturally engaging while conserving Veuve Clicquot's essential values in terms of style.

The wine has a luminous color with attractive pink glints. The nose is generous and elegant, with initial aromas of fresh red fruit (raspberry, wild strawberry, cherry, blackberry), leading to biscuity notes of dried fruits and Viennese pastries (almonds, apricots and brioche).

The fresh attack is followed by a fruity harmonious sensation on the palate. The wine is perfectly balanced in the best Veuve Clicquot style of pink champagnes, combining elegance and flair. The wine works its magic—this delectably full champagne can be enjoyed as a true delicacy. A deliciously fruity wine in earlybloom, this is a wonderful aperitif to be shared as a twosome or simply with friends. Made using 50 to 60 different crus, the cuvee is based on Brut Yellow Label's traditional blend: 50 to 55% Pinot Noir, 15 to 20% Pinot Meunier, and 28 to 33% Chardonnay.

name: Laurent Perrier  Ultra Brut  (750 ml)
wine_color: Limpid, bright yellow with a fine bead
desc_en_short:
Laurent-Perrier Ultra Brut is Champagne in its most natural form. In 1980, Laurent-Perrier reintroduced the traditional Champagne concept of brut nature, a non-dosage Champagne that requires considerable know-how to the craft. Its style appeals to a growing number of wine lovers who discover with it many new ways of pairing food with Champagne. 

With nearly equal parts of Chardonnay and Pinot Noir, Ultra Brut is made exclusively with grapes from very ripe harvests. Selection is rigorous to ensure that only grapes with both high sugar levels and low acidity are used.  This is essential as there is no dosage added

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mapu Sauvignon Blanc (Screw Cap)
desc_en_short: Sauvignon Blanc gives vivacious dry wines of great refinement. The Mapu range of wines is made by Baron Philippe de Rothschild in Chile. Fresh gooseberry fruit and cut grass notes. Well balanced and clean on the palate - good quality. Enjoy drinks with seafood, tarragon chicken and stuffed crab.
desc_en_full:
Mapu Sauvignon Blanc 2012

Vintage Weather conditions for the 2012 vintage were very favourable, reminiscent of 2009. March was one of the hottest in the last 30 years. The positive effects of the hot, dry weather in 2012 were to ensure excellent maturity of the grapes and naturally high concentration of flavour. The wines of the 2012 vintage are generous and powerful while retaining a texture of velvet and silk. They have a definite Mediterranean cast, especially in the expression of the fruit. That ripeness, combined with a concentrated mid-palate, gives complexity and richness and expresses the full identity of the terroir. All the charm of Chile is revealed in the 2012 wines, which have excellent ageing potential.

Maturing Mapu Sauvignon Blanc 2012 is matured in vats for a relatively short period of around six months in order to preserve all its freshness and fruit.

Grape Variety Sauvignon Blanc gives vivacious dry wines of great refinement. Their rich flavours and crisp texture mean that they can be drunk young or after a few years' ageing.

Food Pairing Mapu Sauvignon Blanc 2012 is great as an aperitif. also works well with seafood, tarragon chicken and stuffed crab.

The experience ends with a very fine aromatic aftertaste that has subtle saline notes.
desc_en_full:
After the ravages of the phylloxera epidemic at the end of the 19th century the Latour family took the then, unusual decision to tear up the dead Aligoté and Pinot Noir vines, and replace them with Chardonnay. This decision lead them to create one of Burgundy's most celebrated white whites.
Today the Latour family owns 10.5 hectares which makes them the biggest owner in the appellation. The Latour's Corton-Charlemagne vineyards are located on a prime section of the Corton hillside where the south-easterly aspect ensures maximum exposure to the sun. The fruit is harvested as late as possible to guarantee maximum ripeness.

name: Echeverria  Reserva Chardonnay
desc_en_short:
Bright yellow with golden hues. Fresh and juicy, this delicate Chardonnay expresses aromas of pineapples, melon and peach combined with exquisite citrus notes. The palate is silky and vibrant with a long tasty finish.

Sustainable Winery

ISO 9001-HACCP
desc_en_full:
ORIGIN : Chile Maipo Valley

GRAPE VARIETY : 100% Chardonnay proprietor's single vineyard grapes, from ungrafted selected clones of pre-phylloxera French rootstock.

HARVEST : February 29th to march 6th and 15th, 2012, during the morning. Hand-picked in 18 Kg. boxes and carried to the winery immediately after cutting. Carefully selected before processing.

VINIFICATION :

Destemmed and chilled to 8ºC. Direct pneumatic pressing.

24 to 48 hours of static sedimentation at 8-10º C prior to fermentation.

Fermented in stainless steel tanks at 14-18º C constant temperature for 25 days with selected yeasts.

Partial malolactic fermentation.

Stored in steel tanks during 6 weeks with continuous stirring of fine lees.

Stored in temperature controlled stainless steel tanks through bottling.

Serve between 10° and 12°C.

name: Monteverro  Chardonnay IGT
wine_color: Beautiful grey gold with touches of mercury and green.
desc_en_short: It owes its golden yellow colour, complexity and notes of caramel and toasted bread to ageing in barriques and concrete eggs, and its aromas of lemon, pear, quince and pineapple, of almonds and ripe apple to the ideal climatic conditions.
desc_en_full:
Even though the defining stylistic feature of this wine comes from Burgundy, it is 100% Tuscan. The influence of the sea breeze and cool nights make it possible to produce this white wine in selected parcels, which ties in with the great role models with its elegant, silky texture, and its freshness and finesse. It owes its golden yellow colour, complexity and notes of caramel and toasted bread to ageing in barriques and concrete eggs, and its aromas of lemon, pear, quince and pineapple, of almonds and ripe apple to the ideal climatic conditions. As great lovers of Chardonnay, the production of this white wine in small quantities (approx. 5,000 bottles per vintage) is something close to our hearts.

Testing Nose

Color: Beautiful grey gold with touches of mercury and green.

On the nose: Explosion of cedar, tropical fruit, baked apple, slate, fresh mango, white chocolate, quince lingering on honey and peaches with touches of brioche.

On the plate: Nice round entrance evolving on fresh acidity with touches of yoghurt, peaches, pear lingering on grapefruit and white flowers with touches of marzipan and lemon pie.

name: La Soufrandiere  Pouilly-Vinzelles  Climat “les Quarts”
desc_en_short:
Why not walk, listen and smell ? You are in the middle of the ploughed vines of la Soufrandière in Pouilly-Vinzelles Climat « Les Quarts », this terroir we think is one of the best in Burgundy, because we are deeply in love with it, but it could be that we have deep conviction. The earth here is soft, aerated, smelling of liquorice. It is alive. The Terroir is present, simply, surely. The climat « Les Quarts » is undoubtedly the best of appellation Pouilly-Vinzelles.

The almost magic position of the South-East facing 40 to 68 year-old vines of Chardonnay here, plus the living clay and limestone soil (Bajocian with a lot of siliceous crystals) give birth to wines for the long term. A great Burgundy wine : a citrussy mineral nose and balance, complexity and richness in the mouth. 2007 proves it once again.

History and terroir

The “Vins de Pays” are the outcome of special legislation that regulates them strictly in terms of area, grape varieties and vinification. Chardonnay, which tends to be at its best in Burgundy, has become well established in the Pays d'Oc, flourishing in the stony limestone soils under the intense summer sun that concentrates the life force of the wine in the grape.

Tasting Notes

A pale, golden straw colour. The nose is powerful and delicate, with intense pear, fig, pineapple and roasted hazelnut aromas and discreet hints of honey. Unctuous and lively on the palate, with hints of fig and vanilla.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Orange Gold
style: Chardonnay, Grenache Blanc, Viognier, Marsanne , Mauzac, Muscat
grape_variety: Chardonnay, Grenache, Viognier, Marsanne, Mauzac, Muscat
desc_en_short:
Orange Gold pays homage to the first orange wines of Georgia 4500 years ago.
Gérard Bertrand wants to create a new profile of orange wines, very fresh. With this unique name, Orange Gold pays tribute to the power of the sun and the beautiful sunsets of the South of France.
desc_en_full:
Winemaker's note

A very precise monitoring and selection of the berries is made in order
to obtain perfectly ripe berries and stalks. Orange Gold is produced with
an ancestral winemaking method. The white grapes are vinified and
fermented in whole clusters, as with red wines. This maceration with
stalks and skins brings a remarkable orange color with golden reflections
and singular tannins. It is an exceptional blend of seven grape varieties,
mainly from the Mediterranean: Chardonnay, Grenache Blanc and
Viognier bring volume and Marsanne, Mauzac and Muscat enrich the
aromatic complexity of the wine. The perfect mastery of the vinification
and the choice of grape varieties make it an orange wine of an
unprecedented profile, with a remarkable finesse and subtlety.

name: Serra Ferdinandea  Bianco IGT
wine_color: Yellow color slightly tinged with sea green.
desc_en_short: Distant lands come together and complete one another. After his decade of experience in Sicily as Planeta’s oenologist, Florent Dumeau decided to blend two quite different but so very complementary varieties; This wine is all about its bouquet, a combination of fragrances and unusual scents. Wood is used masterfully to enhance the perfumes and prepare this white for happy aging.
desc_en_full:
VINIFICATION:

the grapes are harvested by hand in bins that are refrigerated overnight in a cold room until they drop to 12°C; the next morning they are selected by hand on a sorting table and sent whole to a soft press. The free run must obtained is sent to steel vats for two weeks’ cold settling and stirred daily. At the end it is poured into tonneaux, steel and oak barrels for fermentation (25% steels; 60% barrels; 15% barriques and tonneaux). The wine is aged in oak wood for 9 months, of which 50% in large 35.25 hl barrels, and 50% in 5 hl tonneaux.

name: Max Ferd. Richter  Brauneberger Juffer Riesling Kabinett
flavor_tags: Green Apple, Honeysuckle, Minerality, Mineral, Petrol, Peach, Apple, Slate, Lime
desc_en_short: From one of the Mosel's most historic estates, this Kabinett Riesling from the famous Brauneberger Juffer vineyard is a delicate, off-dry masterpiece — gossamer light with green apple, peach, honeysuckle, and a rapier-sharp slate minerality.
desc_en_full:
Max Ferd. Richter is one of the Mosel's oldest and most respected family estates, farming steep slate vineyards in and around Brauneberg, Mülheim, and Veldenz since the 17th century. The Brauneberger Juffer — literally 'the maiden of Brauneberg' — is one of the Mosel's great classified vineyard sites, facing due south on slopes of pure blue Devonian slate that capture maximum sunlight while the river below reflects warmth onto the vines.

Kabinett is the lightest and most delicate category in Germany's Prädikat system, made from barely-ripe grapes that produce wines of extraordinary finesse and low alcohol. This one is a model of the style: just 8% alcohol, with a barely-there sweetness balanced by the vineyard's signature razor-sharp acidity and stony minerality.

Pale silver-green, it opens on fresh green apple, white peach, lime zest, honeysuckle, and a delicate hint of petrol that signals serious Mosel Riesling. The palate is impossibly light yet intensely flavoured, with a long, mineral, appetising finish that leaves you reaching for another sip.

Perfect with grilled trout, freshwater fish, light Asian salads, or simply on its own as a meditative aperitif.

name: Schieferkopf  Pfalz Gewurztraminer Trocken Sec
flavor_tags: Tropical Fruit, White Pepper, Lychee, Floral, Ginger, Pepper, Slate, Rose
desc_en_short: Schieferkopf's Gewurztraminer Trocken is a dry, aromatic Alsatian white of striking intensity — lychee, rose petal, ginger, and exotic spice leap from the glass, followed by a richly textured palate that finishes dry and long.
desc_en_full:
Schieferkopf — meaning 'slate head' — is Michel Chapoutier's acclaimed Alsace estate, producing wines that express the region's aromatic varieties with both power and precision. Their Gewurztraminer Trocken (dry) is one of the most expressive examples of the variety in a fully dry style.

Gewurztraminer is perhaps the most intensely aromatic white grape in the world, and in Alsace it reaches its fullest expression: the combination of the region's continental climate and unique geology produces wines of incredible perfume and texture. Chapoutier's biodynamic viticulture adds further depth and definition.

Deep golden in colour, it bursts on the nose with lychee, rose water, crystallised ginger, white pepper, and exotic tropical fruit. The palate is richly textured and full-bodied, surprisingly dry and structured, with spice and floral notes that persist through to a long, aromatic finish.

Pair with Alsatian classics — choucroute garnie, Munster cheese, foie gras — or with aromatic Asian cuisine: Thai green curry, Vietnamese pho, or Sichuan spiced duck.

name: Beau Reve De Tradition  Moelleux Bordeaux Aoc
flavor_tags: Orange Blossom, Apricot, Blossom, Orange, Peach, Lemon, Honey
desc_en_short: Beau Rêve de Tradition is a sweet Moelleux from Bordeaux — gently honeyed and luscious with ripe apricot, peach, orange blossom, and a refreshing acidity that prevents any heaviness, making it an accessible and charming dessert wine.
desc_en_full:
Moelleux Bordeaux — 'soft' or 'mellow' in French — is a style of sweet white wine produced throughout the Bordeaux appellation, typically from Sémillon, Sauvignon Blanc, and Muscadelle grapes affected by late harvesting or noble rot (Botrytis cinerea). Less concentrated than the great Sauternes but genuinely sweet and fragrant, Moelleux Bordeaux represents an approachable entry into the world of French dessert wine.

Beau Rêve de Tradition is a straightforward, pleasurable example: golden in colour with aromas of ripe apricot, white peach, honey, orange blossom, and a hint of candied lemon. On the palate it is sweetly generous with a medium body, refreshing acidity to balance the sugar, and a clean, fruit-led finish.

Serve lightly chilled as an aperitif or with foie gras, fresh fruit tarts, crème brûlée, blue cheeses such as Roquefort, or simply on its own as a gentle after-dinner indulgence.

name: Karthäuserhof  Bruno Riesling Dry
desc_en_short:
Riesling, slate, Mosel – exactly this way and in this order, the nose 
gives an early idea of what will come next on the palate. Very 
classic with cool, light citrus fruit and lots of spicy slate aromas, 
delicate but with a sensational drinking flow. A fun wine that is 
both, enjoyable on its own or with food.
desc_en_full:
Terroir:

The grapes are grown in steep vineyards in the Mosel and Ruwer valleys with perfect exposition and late soil.

Vinifizierung:

Harvested early October, all grapes were picked by hand and gently pressed. In the cellar it is done as little intervention as possible. But with one major investment: time. Patience always pays off in wine and winemaking. No fining, all natural sedimentation, only sulfur added just before bottling.

name: Argiolas Iselis Nasco Bianco Di Cagliari
desc_en_short: A refined Sardinian white floral, textured, and gracefully mineral.
desc_en_full:
The Whisper of the Mediterranean

Argiolas Iselis Nasco Bianco di Cagliari DOC comes from the sun-kissed hills of Serdiana, near Cagliari in southern Sardinia — a land where sea breezes meet ancient limestone soils. Produced by the Argiolas family, whose winemaking heritage spans over 80 years, this wine revives the nearly forgotten Nasco grape, one of Sardinia’s oldest native varieties.

“Iselis” represents harmony and authenticity. The Argiolas family carefully cultivates low-yielding vines to preserve aromatic purity, then ages the wine partly in acacia barrels and on fine lees for added texture and complexity. The result is a deeply expressive white — Mediterranean at heart, elegant in spirit, and utterly unique in character.

Description :
Strong ruby red colour. Intense, classic bouquet which suggests ripe fruit, spices and tobacco. Full-bodied, generous, warm and long lasting. It leaves a pleasant dry and sapid mouth.

Wine making :
This wine, obtained from the grapes harvested in the vineyard called with the same name, is left to re-ferment with the skins of the Amarone and Recioto grapes, which are still intact and slightly sweet. In this way the body, colour, extract, aromas and alcoholic content of the wine are increased and the wine becomes more suited for a long aging in Slavonia oak barrels, where it remains for 16 months. The ageing in the bottle is at least six months before release.

name: Dal Forno Romano  Amarone della Valpolicella 2017
wine_color: Inky ruby-black, dense and opaque with serious depth
brand: Dal Forno Romano  Amarone della Valpolicella
desc_en_short:
Dal Forno Amarone is the perfect fusion of power and precision. With a richness comparable to vintage Port, yet a striking balance of acidity and elegance, this is a wine that commands attention without losing grace.

Its structure is firm, its tannins are refined, and its complexity unfolds slowly and steadily. The finish is extraordinarily long—more like an epic than an afterthought. It’s a wine for connoisseurs who seek intensity, but also poetic nuance in every glass.
desc_en_full:
Dal Forno Romano is the name that redefined Amarone—transforming it from a traditional regional wine of Veneto into one of the most powerful and age-worthy reds on the world stage.

Founded by Romano Dal Forno, the estate is driven by an obsession with quality and inspiration drawn from the legendary Giuseppe Quintarelli. Dal Forno masterfully combines cutting-edge innovation—like humidity-controlled drying rooms and precise vinification systems—with the traditional appassimento method (air-drying grapes) to produce wines of exceptional concentration and finesse.

The 2017 vintage represents a triumph of meticulous vineyard selection, patient craftsmanship, and unwavering commitment to excellence.

name: Fromm Winery  Pinot Noir La Strada
desc_en_short: Wild strawberries and ripe dark cherries with aromatic spice and brambly earthiness The palate is concentrated, layered and textural with fine tannins and a long and generous finish
desc_en_full: In almost a contradiction of terms this Pinot is both understated and revealing. The nose offers attractive red florals with hints of wild strawberries (due in no small part to the larger portion of whole berries in the ferment), black cherries and bramble. There is a lacing of sweet spices (liquorice, cloves, star anise) and an underlining subtle earthiness. The palate is very structured with layered tannins that are fine and persistent. Tipping its hat to the wines of Europe in the traditional sense, this wine is defined by a seductive yet elusive flavour profile that almost evades the aroma wheel of descriptors. Certainly a terroir driven style.

name: Chateau de la Graveirette Bon ju Red
desc_en_short: Lively French red blend with juicy red and black fruits, soft spices, and gentle tannins — a perfect everyday wine for meats, stews, and cheeses.
desc_en_full:
Château de la Graveirette Bon Ju Red is a playful yet well-crafted red blend from the Rhône Valley (โรน วัลเล่ย์) that brings together the best of ripe fruit and soft structure. With a fresh, easy-drinking style, this wine is designed to be enjoyed on any occasion — from casual gatherings to hearty meals.

Tasting Notes: Deep ruby in color, this vibrant red bursts with aromas of ripe red berries, blackberries, and plums , gently laced with subtle spices, herbs, and hints of black pepper. On the palate, it's juicy and rounded, featuring soft tannins and bright acidity that give it a refreshing, smooth finish. Easy to enjoy and versatile at the table.

Winemaking & Terroir: Grapes are sourced from organic vineyards in Rhône Valley, grown on limestone and clay soils that add freshness and balance to the wine. Fermented with native yeasts to highlight natural fruit purity, aged in stainless steel and neutral vessels to preserve brightness and lift. The result is a wine full of life, with no heaviness — perfect for everyday sipping.

Food Pairings & Serving Tips: Ideal for grilled meats, roasted vegetables, hearty stews , and a variety of cheeses. Serve slightly chilled at 10-12°C to enhance its fruity and refreshing character. Ready to drink now — no need for aging or decanting.

Aging Potential & Cellaring: Best enjoyed within 2-3 years to savor its fresh fruit notes. Not intended for long-term cellaring.

desc_en_short: The bouquet is sumptuous, multilayered, with soft ripe yellow fruit, oak notes and a touch of herbs. On the palate it is full, firm, rich, and somewhat tannic due to extended maceration. Mineral.
desc_en_full:
Triton Lex

A harmonic balance of international and local varieties – Chardonnay, Sauvignon Blanc, Ribolla Gialla – a third of each. Precise and careful selection is the key to more complex, deep, clean wine. Firmly structured and incredibly easy to drink for its size. Chardonnay and Sauvignon Blanc make it powerful and concentrated, whereas Ribolla Gialla soothes them, making the wine more approachable, mineral, with a local character. At the same time, it expands the wine and enhances its complexity. The potential of ageing is up to ten years.

Tasting notes :

The bouquet is sumptuous, multilayered, with soft ripe yellow fruit, oak notes and a touch of herbs. On the palate it is full, firm, rich, and somewhat tannic due to extended maceration. Mineral.

Food matching :

For all occasions, also with meat dishes, for example veal or lamb, if we wish to carry out the whole meal with only white wines.

name: Kendall Jackson  Grand Reserve Chardonnay
desc_en_short: Lush tropical fruit intertwined with lemon, lime and floral notes. Elegantly layered, this wine exhibits a rich texture and firm backbone with a hint of vanilla and spice to round out the long finish.
desc_en_full:
WINERY HISTORY

"When my family and I founded Kendall-Jackson in 1982, we simply wanted to create extraordinary wine from California's best vineyards." -JESS JACKSON, Founder

Ours is as much the story of a winery as it is the story of one man, his family and a dream. In 1974, our founder Jess Jackson, famously bought an 80-acre pear and walnut orchard in rural Lakeport, California, where he planned to try his hand at creating wine from some of his favorite varietals while on weekends away from his successful career as a San Francisco attorney. "I was attracted by the lifestyle," Jess said. "I wanted to get away from law and become a farmer."

By the early 1980s, Jess and his growing family had transformed the orchard into a working winery that produced grapes Jess sold to other wineries. During a fateful harvest in 1980, a large order from another winery was cancelled, and Jess was stuck with a bumper crop of unused grapes: from this the first bottle of Kendall-Jackson was produced. This first bottling, the 1982 Vintner's Reserve, garnered attention for its unique bright fruitiness. It was an immediate, if modest, hit. Though Jess and his family shipped less than 20,000 cases of that legendary '82 Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay, the release went on to become the most popular Chardonnay in America. In 1983, Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay won the first Double Platinum Award ever presented by the American Wine Competition, and has gone on to win accolades year after year.

Now, almost three decades later, Kendall-Jackson Winery produces an array of super-premium wines whose quality, taste and integrity embody the very spirit of our founder's mission. In Jess Jackson's own words, "We grow grapes on our own 15,000 acres of California coastal vineyards. We take the no-compromise, high road approach to quality required to grow our world-class grapes and produce acclaimed award-winning wines."

name: Hennessy  V.S.O.P (With Box) (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Hennessy V.S.O.P Privilège is a balanced cognac, expressing 200 years of Hennessy's know-how. The fruit of nature’s uncertainties, this unique blend has tamed the elements to craft and embody the original concept of cognac. The emotion awakened by the pleasure of tasting it continually reveals new facets of its personality.

The savoir-faire of the Maison Hennessy is fully expressed in this balanced cognac: the selection of eaux-de-vie, aging and assemblage. A cognac of remarkable consistency and vitality, Hennessy V.S.O.P Privilège conveys all of the savoir-faire of the Hennessy master blenders who have ensured the continued success of this harmonious assemblage for 200 years.
desc_en_full:
History

Inspired by a cognac created in 1817 in response to a request from the future king of England, George IV, Hennessy V.S.O.P Privilège has built its reputation across nearly 2 centuries.

Today Hennessy V.S.O.P Privilège has become established as the world’s favorite VSOP Cognac, and is recognised for its harmonious and well-structured blend.

Tasting Note

Appearance : Golden amber colour

Nose : vanilla, spices, clove, cinnamon, oak barrels

Palate : grape, orange , chocolate

Finish : long-lasting , smooth

Alcohol : 40.0%

name: Hine  Cigar Reserve (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Cigar Reserve is an invitation to embark upon a Gauguin-like journey through precious wood and sweet cinnamon. Rich and corpulent in character, its exotic aromas reveal a taste for adventure. Its deep amber tones signal a cascade of aromatic complexity: zingy spices – fresh pepper and tobacco – underscore notes of walnut, honey and lightly toasted flavours. Discreetly enveloping the palate, it is the perfect companion for the first puffs of a powerful and fragrant tobacco. Cue an almost irresistible urge to jump up and don a panama…
desc_en_full:
Appellation Cognac Contrôlée XO

A blend of twenty eaux-de-vie produced using Grande Champagne, Petite Champagne and Fins Bois grapes.
Created by Bernard Hine and Nicholas Freeman (Hunters & Frankau) in their quest to find the perfect match between the most expressive of cigars and House of Hine cognacs.

Cigar Reserve is an invitation to embark upon a Gauguin-like journey through precious wood and sweet cinnamon. Rich and corpulent in character, its exotic aromas reveal a taste for adventure. Its deep amber tones signal a cascade of aromatic complexity: zingy spices – fresh pepper and tobacco – underscore notes of walnut, honey and lightly toasted flavours. Discreetly enveloping the palate, it is the perfect companion for the first puffs of a powerful and fragrant tobacco. Cue an almost irresistible urge to jump up and don a panama…

Cigar Reserve is a distinctive expression of the house's expertise, where a blend of eaux-de-vie aged for over fifteen years sustains an exchange with another great taste sensation: the cigar.

name: Larsen VSOP Reserve (700 ml)
food_matching: Chocolate, Dried Fruits, Cheese, After Dinner
desc_en_short:
Capel Pisco Reservado Transparente represents the essence of Chile’s Elqui Valley — a land where sunlight, altitude, and craftsmanship converge to create one of the world’s most expressive piscos. Produced by Cooperativa Capel, Chile’s most respected pisco house founded in 1938, this expression honors the tradition of distilling grapes into a spirit of unmatched clarity and soul.

Crafted from Moscatel grapes grown under the high Andean sun, this Reservado pisco undergoes slow, precise distillation in copper pot stills, yielding a crystal-clear, aromatic elixir that reflects both the purity of the Andes and the passion of Chilean artisans.

“Transparente” is more than its appearance — it’s a statement of honesty, precision, and balance. Every drop reveals the delicate heart of Chilean terroir, distilled to perfection
desc_en_full:
A fresh, floral, and elegant pisco — crystalline in body, luminous in character.

Aroma: Fragrant and lifted, with white flowers, muscat grapes, and light citrus peel, accented by a hint of herbal freshness.

Palate: Smooth and balanced; flavors of pear, jasmine, and honeydew melon, mingled with soft minerality.

Texture: Silky and pure, with a clean, refined body.

Finish: Long, bright, and elegant — leaving a memory of grape blossom and citrus zest.

desc_en_short:
818 Tequila Blanco (750 ml) X 1

Produced in Jalisco, Mexico using a carefully selected blend of highland and lowland agave to create a blanco with brilliant clarity, an aroma of citrus and hint of mineriality on the pallet and a very smooth finish.

818 Tequila Reposado (750 ml) X 1

818 Reposado Tequila by Kendall Jenner is made using 100% agave azul handcrafted in small batches in Jalisco Mexico. For almost 4 years Kendall Jenner has been on a journey to create the best tasting tequila. After dozens of blind taste tests and trips to distilleries 818 Tequila was born.

818 Tequila Añejo (750 ml) X 1

818 Añejo Tequila starts its journey in the the Highland and Lowland agave fields of Jalisco. The 100% pure Weber Blue agave is harvested by hand, roasted, fermented for days, and double-distilled. Although not mentioned on the label, our sources tell us that all that magic takes place at the famous La Cofradia Distillery located in the town of Tequila. Then the Spirit is aged between one and three years in oak. So, this is a serious Tequila Añejo from serious people.

818 Tequila Eight Reserve (750 ml) X 1 

818 Eight Reserve Anjeo began with an ambitious goal: to become one of the greatest tequilas ever crafted. In the heart of Jalisco, Mexico, 818’s Master Tequileros used their decades of expertise and passion to develop Eight Reserve by 818.
818 Eight Reserve is a masterful blend of Añejo aged up to 8 years, aged in French and American barrels. What emerged is a painstakingly crafted blend, bringing a level of complexity, softness and depth never before experienced.

818 Tequila Premium Cap X 1
desc_en_full:
OUR STORY

We work closely with local, family-owned farms in Jalisco, Mexico to bring you the smoothest, most natural, and best tasting tequila possible. Only the highest quality ingredients are used in its creation.

The area code 818 is home to me, but the earth is home to all of us. So, from the agaves we grow, to the production of the bottle, sustainability and transparency of our supply chain was our core focus. We feel a great sense of responsibility and pride to have a positive impact on individuals, communities, and the environment. We've had so many magical nights drinking 818 and we hope you enjoy it as much as we do.

name: Gilbey's  Gin (1 L)
food_matching: Tonic, Seafood Cocktails, Light Appetizers, Citrus Dishes
desc_en_short:
Tasting Notes

The nose is classic with plenty of juniper, with orange zest and angelica present as well. The lower notes have the bright, sappy, angelica still accompanied by the orange. The nose is simple, but familiar and inviting. I don’t think I would guess the price by the nose. It’s fresh and generally quite positive.

The palate is assertive and sharp, with lots of classic gin botanicals making an appearance: pine-forward juniper, bitter orange rind, coriander mid-palate— surprisingly obvious for a second and then quickly vanishing from consciousness— the finish is sharply citrus with a mineral-tinged, calciferous/slightly chalky astringency. Quite dry, with a roar of heat that is more expected at this price point.

The finish is moderately long, with that orange/citrus note enduring.

name: Fords Gin (750 ml)
desc_en_short: "At 90 proof Fords Gin holds in a lot of flavor. When tasting, we recommend a splash of water to release the flavours. You'll notice the high oil content just by looking in the glass. The coriander, citrus and florals really come to life. It's also a good way to see how Fords gin will show in a cocktail." - Simon Ford, The 86 Co.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Notes

Nose :

Juniper, coriander and angelica root up front with developing layers of citrus, jasmine and hints of vanilla in the back. Very pleasant on the nose, bright and aromatic.

Mid Palate :

Prominent mid palate presence, with juniper oils on the center tongue and full aroma on top palate. Citrus oils excite the side palate beautifully. Under tongue stays dry yet animated. Excellent mid palate structure to serve as base for mixing.

Dryness :

Dryness coming from jasmine.

Front of palate :

Light sweetness on tip of the tongue. Gentle introduction of the botanicals to follow. Notes of orris and angelica roots.

Mouth Feel :

Full body with rather fresh mouth feel and high oil content.

Finish :

Long, lingering finish of grapefruit rind and juniper with a balance of the floral notes.

desc_en_short:
Four Pillars Rare Dry Gin is crafted to deliver the best of all worlds: a perfect, classic gin and also something that would fascinate and delight even the most hardened gin fanatic. It’s spicy but with great citrus, a truly modern Australian gin.

desc_en_full:
Four Pillars Rare Dry Gin is crafted to deliver the best of all worlds: a perfect, classic gin and also something that would fascinate and delight even the most hardened gin fanatic. It's spicy but with great citrus, a truly modern Australian gin.

Four Pillars Rare Dry Gin captures the essence of contemporary Australia's heritage in Europe and Asia. They use whole oranges which is unusual but Australian citrus is highly aromatic and supports the spicier botanicals like cardamom.

The cinnamon and star anise add rich fruitcake tones, the Tasmanian pepperberry leaf provides warmth rather than heat, and the lemon myrtle is a beautiful alternative to lemon peel.

Four Pillars Rare Dry Gin has been awarded medals at a number of prestigious international competitions: Double Gold at the San Francisco World Spirits Competition in 2016 and 2014, Gold at the Global Gin Masters 2016, and Gold at the Hong Kong International Wine & Spirits Competition 2014.

name: Beefeater  24 London Dry Gin (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
Beefeater 24 is a sophisticated blend of fine botanicals and exotic teas. The inclusion of Japanese Sencha and Chinese Green Tea in the botanical blend distinguishes Beefeater 24 from any other gin, helping to shape its unique aroma and smoothness, while the addition of grapefruit peel ensures that Beefeater's signature citrus flavour remains present. The result is a gin with an incredible smoothness making it effortlessly mixable and perfect for modern cocktail culture.

Awards :

Gold - London Dry - 2020 : The Gin Masters (The Spirits Business)
desc_en_full:
The Superior London Dry Gin

Made in the heart of London from 12 hand-selected botanicals including Japanese Sencha and Chinese Green teas, Beefeater 24 is our Master Distiller, Desmond Payne's, contribution to the Beefeater legacy: a sophisticated and modern interpretation to inspire top mixologists around the world to create contemporary cocktails.

A light bulb moment when travelling in Japan inspired Desmond Payne, our… Master Distiller, to realise tea's potential as a botanical. In Japan, quinine was strictly banned from the production of tonics resulting in a far sweeter mixer for the traditional G&T. To quench his thirst, Desmond chose to mix Beefeater gin with chilled green tea, as an alternative to the sweet local tonics. The resulting gin and tea combination was remarkable and inspired Desmond to spend the next 18 months trialing and experimenting with different teas and botanical blends.

name: Four Pillars  Spiced Negroni Gin (700 ml)
desc_en_short: This gin was originally the result of a conversation between Cameron (our distiller) and Jason (then Manager of Sydney bars including The Rook and one of the World’s Top Ten Bartenders) back in 2014 - they wanted to create a gin specifically for the Negroni. Something to cut through the Campari and vermouth. And as the Negroni has always been a favourite at Four Pillars, Cameron jumped at the chance to make the first collaboration in our Bartender Series a tribute to the very definition of a perfectly balanced cocktail.
desc_en_full:
Four Pillars Spiced Negroni Gin is a highly aromatic, rich and (yes) spicy gin with great power and intensity. First, we took our base botanicals and upped the amount of Tasmanian pepperberry leaf and cinnamon. Then we added an exotic West African spice called Grains of Paradise. This is one of the most unusual spices in the world, with clove and sichuan characters. But although very powerful, the spice tends to glow rather than become hot.

We also decided to use beautiful organic blood oranges in the botanical basket, as well as some ginger. These wonderfully fragrant fresh botanicals help lift the spice to another level. Finally, we opened up the plates to add weight and intensity to the gin.

desc_en_short:
Fine juniper meets fruity notes of pineapple and currant. An incomparably fruity and complex gin, which knows the variety and the possibilities of a gins to use. Our 1st product on the market that has created its own unique niche and convinces. In addition to the main ingredients - juniper, pineapple, currant - our gin is rounded off with hibiscus blossom and carrot.

A 3-times distilled gin, which rounds for a 48-hour! Maceration.
desc_en_full: Every drop of our Gin contains care, love and a lot of manual work. We take our time and give it to our gin as well. 48 hours maceration, then be carefully distilled. 6 weeks of ripening and resting, so that the ingredients can develop their full aroma. By comparison, industrial Gin gets attention for 1-2 minutes per liter! Much work and care is also in the selection of ingredients. Not randomly thrown together, but rather balanced in terms of taste and compatible on a chemical basis - this is how you make a gin.

name: Ki No Bi  Kyoto Gin (700 ml)
desc_en_short: KI NO BI Kyoto Dry Gin is created with Japanese botanicals such as yellow yuzu from the north of Kyoto Prefecture, hinoki wood chips (Japanese cypress), bamboo, gyokuro tea from the Uji region and green sanshō (Japanese peppercorn) berries.
desc_en_full:
KI NO BI ('The Beauty of the Seasons') is inspired by tradition and is distilled, blended and bottled in Kyoto. Our gin is made in a recognisable dry style but with a distinct Japanese accent.

KI NO BI Kyoto Dry Gin is created with Japanese botanicals such as yellow yuzu from the north of Kyoto Prefecture, hinoki wood chips (Japanese cypress), bamboo, gyokuro tea from the Uji region and green sanshō (Japanese peppercorn) berries.

Bottled at 45.7% ABV, KI NO BI uses a rice spirit base and peerless water sourced in the famous sake–brewingdistrict, Fushimi.

Mastering not only the science of distillation as others have done before, but also the age-old art of blending, we separate the botanicals in our gin into six different categories: Base, Citrus, Tea, Herbal, Spice and Floral and distil each category individually. Then we blend them in perfect balance and create the distinct KI NO BI flavour.

—Tasting comment—

KI NO BI- a gin that speaks of its place of birth.

As clear as the air in the sacred mountains of Kyoto.

A fresh aroma of yuzu, and a thread of sansho that lingers like a morning mist in a bamboo forest.

The juniper complements and showcases the Japanese botanicals. Ginger root gives a slightly warming, spicy finish.

TAKE A JOURNEY WITH US DEEP INTO THE HEART AND SOUL OF THAILAND

When we set out on this adventure, gin was our ambition, but saneha was our driving force. The word saneha translates to ‘passion’ with a twist of sophistication, playfulness and seduction. This one word thoroughly exemplifies who we are and what we’re all about. The spirit of saneha is distilled in everything we do. It’s in our devotion to natural ingredients and quality, in our commitment to sustainability and fine craftsmanship, and in the passion of our people, all working closely together to create a compelling Thai spirit that’s as pure as the hearts poured into it. Welcome to the world of Saneha. We hope you’ll enjoy savouring it as much as we enjoy bringing it to life.

desc_en_short:
"A MUST FOR YOUR COCKTAILS"

Generous Gin "Purple" is a real innovation in the gin world!

Much more than a color, Generous Gin "Purple" is the first gin to contain polyphenols and grape anthocyanins.

Polyphenols are the molecules that make up the tannins in wine, anthocyanins bring the texture and color of red wine; they are also known for being antioxidants.

The delicate blend of Generous Gin imparts a smooth and dry texture combined with the freshness of the pink grapefruit flavour for this seductive gin.

A perfect balance between the aromatic typicity of the different juniper berries, the contribution of other botanicals and the original sensation of structure provided by the anthocyanins.

CONCLUSION

A model of the kind, complexity, power and seduction.

desc_en_full:
Sod Chaeng Wild Flower Gin (750 ml)

Gin from 15 different herbs (distilled twice and filtered through 7 steps)

Sod Chaeng Story

from a locally-owned small-batch distillery in Ubon Ratchathani Province, Thailand dedicated to pay homage to the hard work and wisdom of the local farmers

Sod Chaeng Distillery was started with the purpose of supporting local communities by adding value to their agricultural products and creating sustainable local employment for everyone from farmers, visual artists to musicians. This results not only in career development opportunities but also Thai-Isan culture promotion.

Sod Chaeng turned the local success of high-quality organic agricultural products, enabled by natural effective microorganisms (EM), into premium local spirits.

Combining over 10 years of experience in liquor creation with traditional Isan wisdom, Sod Chaeng has successfully developed premium spirits

name: Rivo  Mediterranean Gin (500 ml)
food_matching: Tonic, Seafood Cocktails, Light Appetizers, Citrus Dishes
desc_en_short:
A MAGICAL GIN FROM A MAGICAL PLACE

For centuries, local women have scoured the mountainside meadows surrounding the lake for herbs and flowers to prepare traditional medicines and remedies. While some might have considered them witches we see them as pioneers of herbal medicine. And it is the theme of magic that inspired our packaging and labeling. Crisscrossing geometrical lines create abstract representations of the two key elements of Lake Como’s geography: The mountains and the waves.
desc_en_full:
RIVO GIN : LAKE COMO’S MAGIC IN A BOTTLE

It was an early spring day and we were once again admiring “our” lake – Lago di Como, that is. As it so often does, the lake was showing off its complete splendor: Imposing peaks surrounding deep blue water with small waves glistening in the mellow light of the sun. The breeze tousled our hair as it carried the fresh scent of nature in full bloom.

Taking it all in, we thought: There has to be a way to distill this beauty and bottle it forever! So that's what we did. We decided right there to create RIVO Gin.

name: An Dulaman	Irish Maritime Gin (500 ml)
desc_en_short:
An Dúlamán Irish Maritime Gin is born where land meets sea — along the rugged coastline of Donegal, Ireland, where Atlantic winds, ocean mist, and ancient Gaelic traditions intertwine. The name “An Dúlamán” comes from the Irish word for “seaweed”, an homage to the ocean’s deep influence on this unique gin.

Crafted by Sliabh Liag Distillers, one of Ireland’s pioneering independent distilleries, this gin reflects a maritime soul shaped by the wild northwest coast. What sets it apart is the use of five native seaweeds harvested by hand from the Atlantic shores — including Dulse, Sweet Kelp, Carrageen Moss, Channel Wrack, and Pepper Dulse, known as the “Truffle of the Sea.” These are blended with classic gin botanicals such as juniper, angelica, and citrus peel, creating a spirit that is both profoundly coastal and unmistakably Irish.

Each small batch is distilled slowly in a copper pot still named Méabh, allowing the oceanic botanicals to reveal their delicate minerality and briny complexity. The result is a gin that tastes like the Atlantic breeze itself — crisp, fresh, and full of character.
desc_en_full:
An Dúlamán Irish Maritime Gin captures the essence of Ireland’s Atlantic coast — wild, invigorating, and soulful.

Aroma: Fresh sea air mingled with juniper, citrus zest, and maritime herbs, underpinned by a subtle saline minerality.

Palate: Complex and balanced — layers of coastal salt, umami from seaweed, lemon peel, and coriander seed unfold gently, revealing a soft sweetness and earthy undertone.

Texture: Silky and mouth-coating, with a refreshing briny lift that awakens the senses.

Finish: Long, clean, and slightly savory — echoing the taste of sea spray and driftwood.

name: Chiyomusubi Craft Gin Impact (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Chiyomusubi Craft Gin “Impact” hails from Tottori Prefecture, Japan, a coastal region where mountains, forests, and the Sea of Japan converge. It is crafted by the historic Chiyomusubi Shuzo, a brewery founded in 1865, long admired for its exceptional sake and shochu. With over 150 years of distilling expertise, the house brings its deep fermentation knowledge into the world of gin — creating a spirit that bridges Japanese purity and modern intensity.

The name “Impact” reflects its bold yet balanced character — a gin designed to awaken the senses with a wave of Japanese botanicals. Distilled in small batches, it features a core of juniper berries, harmonized with local ingredients such as Nashi pear, Yuzu citrus, Japanese pepper (Sansho), and green tea. Each botanical is distilled separately to capture its purest aroma before being artfully blended into one cohesive expression.

This careful process allows Chiyomusubi to achieve the essence of Japanese craftsmanship (Monozukuri) — precision, respect for nature, and harmony in every drop.
desc_en_full:
hiyomusubi Craft Gin “Impact” delivers an exhilarating balance of freshness, depth, and distinctly Japanese complexity.

Aroma: Vibrant and aromatic — layers of juniper, citrus peel, and Yuzu zest mingled with floral notes and a hint of green tea umami.

Palate: Smooth and dry at first, unfolding into sweet Nashi pear, earthy herbs, and peppery Sansho spice, giving both warmth and clarity.

Texture: Silky and full-bodied, yet crisp, with a cooling minerality that speaks of mountain water purity.

Finish: Long, elegant, and aromatic — lingering with notes of Yuzu peel, spice, and tea leaves.

name: Carlo Pellegrino Grappa Di Marsala (500 ml)
desc_en_short:
Carlo Pellegrino Grappa di Marsala is a distillation of Sicilian soul — a spirit born from the sun-drenched vineyards of Marsala, on the western coast of Sicily, where tradition and terroir meet the art of transformation.

Produced by Casa Pellegrino, one of Sicily’s most historic wine houses founded in 1880, this grappa captures the essence of Marsala wine production — transforming the pomace left from those noble grapes into a refined, aromatic distillate. Crafted using traditional copper pot stills, it reflects the warmth, energy, and generosity of Sicilian craftsmanship.

The result is a grappa that carries the perfume of the vineyard: fragrant, soft, and luminous — with the sun of Marsala shining through every drop.
desc_en_full:
An elegant and expressive grappa — smooth, floral, and subtly fruity.

Aroma: Bright and delicate, with white flowers, dried apricot, and toasted almond, lifted by gentle spice.

Palate: Soft and balanced; flavors of grape must, golden raisin, honey, and a touch of citrus, blending warmth with finesse.

Texture: Silky and rounded, with a clean Mediterranean brightness.

Finish: Long and fragrant, leaving traces of vanilla, fruit, and sunlit sweetness.
