name: Carlo Pellegrino  Grappa Nero Di Avola (500 ml)
desc_en_short:
Carlo Pellegrino Grappa Nero d’Avola is a powerful and elegant expression of Sicilian heritage — distilled from the noble Nero d’Avola grape, known as “the black pearl of Sicily.” It represents the island’s warmth, volcanic soil, and passion for craftsmanship, all captured through centuries of winemaking tradition.

Produced by Casa Pellegrino, established in 1880 in Marsala, this grappa is born from the pomace of Nero d’Avola, Sicily’s most celebrated red grape. After the wine is made, the grape skins are distilled slowly in traditional copper pot stills, preserving their bold character and aromatic depth.

The result is a grappa that combines strength and grace — a true reflection of Sicilian contrast: sun and sea, fire and softness. Each sip carries the warmth of the Mediterranean and the craftsmanship of Pellegrino’s distilling mastery.
desc_en_full:
A rich, characterful grappa — warm, spicy, and beautifully balanced.

Aroma: Intense and expressive, with dark cherry, plum, and cocoa, enriched by notes of spice and toasted almond.

Palate: Smooth and full-bodied; flavors of ripe red fruit, vanilla, and a hint of black pepper, blending fruitiness with subtle sweetness.

Texture: Silky and enveloping, with a warm Mediterranean finish.

Finish: Long and elegant, leaving traces of oak, spice, and dried fruit.

name: Jagermeister  (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Jagermeister is an herbal, bitter liqueur from Germany made of a secret blend of over 50 herbs, fruits and spices. Over recent years it has gained a notorious reputation as a liquor that will get you very intoxicated, very fast. This is due mostly to its use in shooters, especially the infamous Jager Bomb. However, Jagermeister does have a place in many "fancy" cocktails to add a complex, herbal back to drinks.
desc_en_full:
Jägermeister Made

     A secret recipe of 56 ingredients which include "cinnamon-bark from Ceylon, bitter orange skins from Australia, santal from East India, ginger roots from southern Asia and of course, some secret herbs." That is about as much as the distillers will tell the public. However, we do know that whatever the mixture is is macerated for 5 months in alcohol and water. This concentrate is blended and filtered then stored in oak for a year, after which it is blended with sugar, caramel and more water and alcohol before bottling. 

 Jägermeister Cocktails
     
     The distiller recommends drinking Jägermeister well-chilled, however I have found that it is far better mixed into the "fancy" cocktails below (those without "shooter" after them). Yet, I still recommend Jägermeister in moderation, as with all drinks, - there is something about the herbal mixture that seems more intoxicating than other spirits.
 
 Jäger Bomb

      Jägermeister is a very potent liqueur with a slight anise taste and the "depressant" effects of the alcohol are offset by the energy drink Red Bull that it is dropped into. This dropped shot is not for the faint of heart and it is very easy to have one too many without knowing it because of the caffeine-like effects of Red Bull. Also, be careful so the shot glass doesn't hit your teeth hard, you could crack a tooth.

name: Bols  Curacao Dry Orange (700ml)
desc_en_short: BOLS Dry Orange Curaçao is a dark orange liqueur flavored with distillates of Curaçao orange peels and a hint of rum. It leaves fine citrus on the palate, balanced by fruity sweetness for a dry and firm finish. The colour suggests a bright full-bodied liqueur, rich and full of flavour.
desc_en_full:
BOLS DRY ORANGE CURAÇAO

     BOLS Dry Orange Curaçao is a dark orange liqueur flavored with distillates of Curaçao orange peels and a hint of rum. It leaves fine citrus on the palate, balanced by fruity sweetness for a dry and firm finish. The colour suggests a bright full-bodied liqueur, rich and full of flavour. Dry Orange Curaçao is one of the old Bols liqueurs of which various recipes were in use in the past. It was introduced at the World Exhibition in Antwerp in 1885 when a journalist described it as: "Nectar of the gods, distilled from sweet sugar from the West (Indies)."Sometimes overshadowed by the success of Triple Sec Curaçao, Dry Orange is nonetheless THE only ingredient to use for some of the world's most important rum cocktails, like the Mai Tai. Bols Dry Orange Curaçao is itself based on a molasses distillate, and mixes far better in cocktails than grain or brandy-based liqueurs. This liqueur is famous for its unparalleled aroma and taste and its beautiful deep orange glow.

name: Bols  Amaretto (700 ml)
desc_en_short: BOLS Amaretto is a gold-brown liqueur. Amaretto, which originated in Italy, is famous for its sweet almond aroma and nutty caramel flavour. A distillation of carefully selected ingredients creates this exquisite liqueur.
desc_en_full:
BOLS AMARETTO

BOLS Amaretto is a gold-brown liqueur. Amaretto, which originated in Italy, is famous for its sweet almond aroma and nutty caramel flavour. A distillation of carefully selected ingredients creates this exquisite liqueur.

Amaretto liqueur is said to have been invented by the woman who modeled for Renaissance-era Italian painter, Bernardino Luini, in 1525. Luini, a follower of Leonardo da Vinci's art style, chose the mother of a local child to be his model for the Madonna. In return she gave him ringraziarlo – a gift, a drink distilled from almonds.

Bols Amaretto is essential for classic drinks such as the Amaretto Sour, and also for more modern drinks like the Alabama Slammer and Screaming Orgasm.

Colour : Warm golden brown.

Aroma : Sweet Almond.

Flavour : Nutty caramel flavour.

Drinking : Straight, on the rocks, excellent in cocktails and mixed drinks. Also delicious when poured over coffee and other warm drinks.

name: Bittermens  Hopped Grapefruit (125 ml)
desc_en_short: Extraordinary bitters, Bittermens Hopped Grapefruit Bitters are made with grapefruit essences and peels, hops for bitterness, and bottled at 53% abv. We recommend these be used in cocktails such as Daiquiris, Gimlets, Gins and Tonic or even Tequila.
desc_en_full:
The essence of grapefruit combined with the most noble of hops. We recommend adding a dash to stirred cocktails made with tequila and long drinks made with gin.When is a grapefruit not just a grapefruit?

Avid beer drinkers know that certain varieties of hops, when used in high concentration, deliver a strong grapefruit flavor. We've decided to use the grapefruity quality of Pacific Northwest hops along with grapefruit peel to help create a concoction with layer upon layer of vegetal bitterness. Designed to enhance young (silver) rums, tequilas and mezcal, the Hopped Grapefruit bitters play well with most cocktails where you need a little citrus punch. Try it in a daiquiri, gimlet, or even a gin and tonic (with a splash of maraschino).

INGREDIENTS

name: Amaro Lucano  (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Born in 1894, Amaro Lucano is based on a secret recipe passed from generation to generation of the Vena family.

Amaro Lucano consists of the careful blend of more than 30 specially selected herbs, which characterizes its strong and delicately balanced flavour. During the production process, special attention is given to the ripening phase, because at this stage the infusion must be left to rest for a long period of time. During this procedure, the flavour develops its finish, and a balanced, consistent flavour, with citrus and floral notes, the distinctive mark of Amaro Lucano.
desc_en_full:
THE PRODUCTION

Since 1894 to date, the history of Amaro Lucano is tightly intertwined with that of the Vena family. It all began in the back Cavalier Pasquale Vena's biscuit factory and it continues today, with the current factory, handled by the fourth generation of the family, which continues to procure the precious liqueur with passion, determination and authenticity.

Incredibly smooth, to be enjoyed over ice, a true tasting experience that enhances its unique and fresh taste.

History of Amaretto Disaronno

Amaretto Disaronno is a sweet Italian liqueur made from almonds, apricots and herbs. It is produced in Saronno, a town in the Lombardy region of Italy.

Disaronno is made according to a secret recipe that dates back to the Italian Renaissance. The liqueur is obtained from bitter apricot kernels, almonds, vanilla and sugar, which are mixed and macerated in neutral alcohol. The mixture is then distilled to extract the aromas and flavors.

name: Luxardo  Bitter Bianco (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Bitter Bianco shares the same infusions of bitter herbs, aromatic plants and citrus fruits with the “red” Bitter. Before the blending process, however, most of these infusions are distilled, which causes the product to become colorless and slightly modifies the flavor profile. At the end of the process, an infusion of Roman wormwood is added to enhance the bitter taste, giving the product an ivory color. Bitter Bianco is perfect for making a “twist” of classic cocktails such as Negroni which becomes a Negroni Bianco, a Paloma in a Paloma Bianco etc. But it can also be enjoyed straight or with ice as a classic aperitif.

Mariposa Cream Liqueur Yuzu is a graceful fusion of Japanese craftsmanship and modern indulgence, capturing the essence of Yuzu, Japan’s most aromatic citrus, in a silky, creamy form.

Crafted with meticulous precision, this liqueur reflects the beauty of balance — the harmony between the fresh brightness of Yuzu and the smooth richness of cream. The result is a refined expression of Japan’s pursuit of perfection, where simplicity and sophistication coexist in every sip.

name: Plantation  XO 20th Anniversary Rum (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
This extraordinary vintage was introduced to mark the 20th anniversary of Maison Ferrand. An assemblage of fine rums 12 - 20 years old, all originating in Barbados, this is a wonderful reflexion of the island itself. 

As with the other rums in the Plantation range, this exotic spirit is aged first in its original country, Barbados, and then benefits with further aging and nurturing in Cognac. Resting in casks originally used for Pierre Ferrand cognac, and meticulously nurtured by Cellar Master of Maison Ferrand, a unique expression is achieved using traditional local methods.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Notes

The stunning flora/fruity opening aroma is transcendent, ethereal and intensely marshmallow-like; further time in the sampling glass of eight minutes releases additional fragrance of vanilla, cocoa and milk chocolate. Entry is cinnamon-like and gently sweet, as the sugary molasses taste turns very soft and chewy; mid-palate taste profile reminds me of holiday spice cake, rum-flavored chocolates and sugar cookies. Concludes medium-long, delicate and only mildly sweet. Three years on, the quality has remained as constant as the North Star.

name: Phraya  Gold Rum (700 ml)
desc_en_short: The only luxury rum from Asia, PHRAYA sits among the finest rum in the world, by using only carefully-selected top quality rums. It is distilled, aged, blended and bottled in the Kingdom of Thailand. Undergoing a deep maturation technique, developed in accordance with Southeast Asian climate, PHRAYA draws out an enticing exotic taste for the palate. Such that the style of this rum is as rich as the history and culture of its country of origin.
desc_en_full:
Phraya Gold Rum

Heritage & Origin

Sangsom Distillery, established in 1977, is one of the oldest distiller in Thailand. It has been producing top-selling brands of Thai rums, for several decades. In 2011, PHRAYA was introduced using the distiller's innovativeness and expertise.

Liquid Gold

"PHRAYA" is a Thai word used in the old days. Its meaning refers to one of the highest ranks bestowed upon a person by the King. This rank is granted only for outstanding service to the monarch and the country, thus reflecting the exceptional ability of a person. Hence this word is chosen for its befitting meaning of the fine spirit label with unparalleled characteristics.

PHRAYA is crafted in the province of Nakhon Pathom, the nation's first and oldest city. Not only important archaeological remains are found here, sugarcane farms are a common sight making sourcing of this primary ingredient effortless. Abundance of freshly-cut sugarcane and temperate climate of this centrally located city provide a perfect setting for the art of rum-making.

Deep Maturation

The technique used in the making of PHRAYA has been specifically developed to suit the humid climes of Thailand. This deep maturation technique involves spirits being aged in oak barrels. The warehouse used to store these barrels has been built to sit over river-mouth lagoons. The coolness of the lagoon's environ effectively slow down the aging process allowing the rum to nurture and develop its distinctive depth. Upon its coming of age between seven and twelve years, only barrels containing the very finest rum are selected for blending by the skillful master distiller. And so, PHRAYA, a rum of unsurpassed quality is created.

Tasting Notes

40% ABV

Overall

Smooth, rich in flavour and perfectly balanced.

Appearance

Golden amber with a slight copper tint.

Aroma

Rich vanilla, honey and coconut, layered with gentle spiciness of cloves and raisins.

Flavour

Smooth, creamy, crème brûlée and butterscotch, with an underlying fruitiness of fresh pineapple and citrus.

Finish

Smooth delicate sweetness, slight spiciness with a long-lasting vanilla ice cream finished.

name: Chalong Bay  Spiced Series (White Spiced) (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Our Asian twist on the traditional spiced rum is distilled with organic, first-press Thai sugarcane and 9 natural, exotic Thai and Southeast Asian botanicals, such as Thai sweet basil, chilli, coffee, vanilla, cinnamon and pandan. All of our ingredients are ethically grown and harvested in partnership with small-scale local farmers without field burning, chemical pesticides or fertilizers. Along with an imported copper still from France, we craft Chalong Bay White Spiced without any additives, added sugar or flavour enhancers, utilizing a rare and specialized distillation process which extracts the natural botanical essences for that pure, extra smooth tropical taste.

Tasting Notes

Nose : Intense notes of cinnamon and vanilla.

Mouth : Nice scents of citrus evolving toward richer hints of pastry, marzipan and gingerbread followed by herbal notes such as coriander and dry chilli.

Finale : Long, spicy aftertaste with intense fragrance of cinnamon, coffee and cocoa.
desc_en_full:
ABOUT US :

Chalong Bay was born from the passion of two individuals for rum and spirits, entrepreneurship, and Asia.

Our dream was to unite two traditions: the centuries old Thai sugarcane culture and the French know-how and traditions in spirit and rum production.

Our vision is to create super premium spirits that are authentic, modern, with a unique character, marrying traditions and craft production methods that are sustainable and respectful of the environment and the community.

Our distillery is located in Phuket, where all our products are developed with our team.

name: Kah  Tequila Blanco (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
A surprisingly well-balanced and pungent tequila with a ‘grassy’ palate of white pepper and cooked agave. Light and pleasant in the mouth with a medium finish which hits the back of the throat.

Awards :

  • Silver Ultra-Premium Blanco - 2019 :The Tequila & Mezcal Masters (The Spirits Business)
  • Silver Super Premium Blanco - 2018 : The Tequila & Mezcal Masters (The Spirits Business)
desc_en_full:
Information Product

Kah Day of the Dead Tequila Blanco Kah "Day of the Dead" Tequila Blanco KAH Tequila Blanco contains intense sweet notes of the cooked agave, followed by a spicy white peppery punch. It is silky and delicate, leaving a sachet of spices on the palate. The KAH Tequila Blanco bottle was inspired by traditional Calaveras (skulls made from sugar) which are used in Day of the Dead rituals to symbolize death and rebirth. Sugar skulls are given to family still living and they are invited to "eat their own death", to acknowledge the belief that death is nothing but the passing from this life into the next. Bolivians celebrate their version of the Day of the Dead by keeping the skulls of their loved ones, calling it the "Day of the Skulls". These decaying heads are brought out each year on November 8th and taken to the cemetery where Bolivians feed them, give them cigarettes and Tequila. Keeping and entertaining the skulls of their deceased is seen as a way for the dead to be immortalized. They believe the skulls of their ancestors protect the living by warding off evil spirits.

desc_en_full:

Produced in one of the world's longest operating Polish distilleries that has been making vodka since 1910, Belvedere's Master Rye Distillers draw from a 600-year Polish vodka-making history to artfully craft Polska rye into an extraordinary vodka of distinct taste and character. Belvedere's Polska rye is a special heritage grain that imparts distinct taste and character to the vodka. Long-term relationship with Polish its selected agricultural partners, enables 100% traceability of the grain. Belvedere believes in being natural and simple, by transforming simple ingredients from nature into extraordinary vodka – with zero additives. A natural philosophy that is at the core of the brand.

Aroma : Soft and inviting, with notes of vanilla and cream.

Testing Palate : Full and round with medium body and a naturally smooth, rich and velvety texture. Light vanilla notes sway between sweet and savory, with a hint of black pepper and spice.

name: Douglas Laing's  Scallywag (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Scallywag has a rich, dark colour extracted from the many fine Spanish Sherry Butts selected. It carries a richly spiced character on the nose woven into delicious vanilla from the Bourbon Hogsheads selected. On the palate detect layer on layer of sweet stewed fruit, Christmas pudding & dark chocolate fused with spice (nutmeg and cinnamon) much of which can be attributed to years slumbering in those Sherry Butts. The finish displays a burst of zesty orange with sweet tobacco, cocoa & rich fruit cake.
desc_en_full:
With over 65 years of 'pedigree' in the Scotch Whisky industry, leading independent Scotch Whisky specialist, Douglas Laing & Co today announce the release of their brand new Speyside Vatted Malt – Scallywag.

Douglas Laing's Scallywag is a Small Batch bottling created using only the finest Speyside Malts including – amongst others – Mortlach, Macallan and Glenrothes.

Bottled at 46% alcohol strength and non chill-filtered, this Small Batch bottling has a distinct rich, dark colour – extracted from the many fine Spanish Sherry Butts used during maturation. The result is a truly charming Malt with a richly spiced character fused with vanilla (from the bourbon casks used), sweet stewed fruit, Christmas pudding, dark chocolate, zesty orange and sweet tobacco.

No attention to detail has been missed in creating the Scallywag packaging. Inspired by the long line of Douglas Laing family Fox Terriers, which are renowned for being sweet yet rather mischievous dogs, it was therefore felt the Scallywag name and Fox Terrier design was most fitting for this richly spiced and deliciously sweet Speyside Vatted Malt.

Says Douglas Laing's Managing Director (and proud Fox Terrier owner) Fred Laing: "From a chance sketch by a designer of our sweet, wee, rascal of a Fox Terrier, our Scallywag Malt Whisky was born! "Sweet" was the key which focused us on Speyside. Its cask selection is self-evidently heavy on the sherry background, balanced by first-fill bourbon casks – so the resulting 'Vatted' Malt comes with an excellent pedigree".

Douglas Laing's Scallywag will be available from specialist Whisky retailers from November 2013.

The Glenlivet French Oak 15 Years (700 ml)

name: Michter's  US*1 Sour Mash (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Michter’s Original Sour Mash Whiskey made a triumphant return in 2012 with its introduction into the US*1 line. Staying true to the profile and palate of its predecessor, Michter’s US*1 Sour Mash has quickly become a favorite of whiskey enthusiasts.
desc_en_full: During the 1970s and 1980s, Michter's Original Sour Mash Whiskey was the distillery's single most popular product. While the "Sour Mash" moniker originated from the whiskey production process of the same name – whereby some previously fermented mash is used as the starter for the new mash to be fermented, much like making sourdough bread – Michter's Original Sour Mash earned distinction for its unique taste. With its unique grain selection, it cannot be categorized as a rye or a bourbon. After disappearing from the market in 1989, Michter's Original Sour Mash Whiskey made a triumphant return in 2012 with its introduction into the US*1 line. Staying true to the profile and palate of its predecessor, Michter's US*1 Sour Mash has quickly become a favorite of whiskey enthusiasts.

name: Old Pulteney  Huddart (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Inspired by our award winning Old Pulteney 1989, Huddart is matured in American oak ex-bourbon barrels, before being finished in ex-peated malt casks. Bottled at 46% It is not only named after the street where it lies, but also Captain Joseph Huddart who provided maps and charts for the British Fisheries Society who were the builders of Pulteneytown and its harbour. He was also for a period a Director of the British Fisheries Society.
desc_en_full:
History

Old Pulteney Distillery is located in the far north of the Scottish mainland. Wick is an area rich in maritime history, once known for its herring fisheries, and the distillery's opening coincided with this boom in the fishing trade. The fishing industry all but died out there, but the distillery has long endured. Fun fact: the shape of the bottle with the bulbous neck is a reflection of the unique stills, which are missing the traditional "swan neck" because they were too tall for the original still house. This single malt is matured entirely in American oak ex-bourbon barrels, mostly second-fill. 

Tasting Note

Nose: Caramel apple, a whiff of bonfire smoke followed by creamy vanilla.

Palate: Burnt sugar and fried banana, alongside a touch of brine.

Finish: Lingering thyme honey and earthy malt.

Located in Tottori, a coastal prefecture on southern Honshu, the whiskies produced by the Matsui distillery are characterised by ageing with the pure underground waters of Daisen Mountains. The sweet fruit and chocolate aroma displayed by this Kurayoshi Pure Malt Whisky is generated by allowing it to age in sherry casks for more than 3 years. A balance of sweetness and acidity spreads all over the mouth when enjoying this elegant Japanese whisky.

desc_en_full:
A blended Japanese whisky produced at Kyoto Shuzo which is made up of malt and grain whisky, some of which were imported to Japan.

Interestingly, this whisky was bottled before the introduction of Japanese whisky labelling standards in February 2021. Going forward, all Japanese whiskies must must ensure their products meet strict requirements when it comes to raw materials, production location, water, distillation, ageing, packaging, colouring and strength if they want to label their product as a Japanese whisky.

A traditional craft known as Nishijin-ori was used to produce the fabric label. This craft originated in the city of Kyoto.

name: The Glenlivet  Licensed Dram (700 ml)
food_matching: Dark Chocolate, Smoked Meats, Aged Cheese, Nuts
desc_en_short: A spiritual successor to the fantastic Illicit Still, the Licensed Dram celebrates a period slightly later in the long history of the Glenlivet, when George Smith was granted royal assent to the production of whisky. So synonymous with the style that malt whisky was sometimes generically known as "Glenlivet" for some time! First-fill ex-Bourbon and ex-sherry casks and a higher-than-usual proof come together for a dram that emphasizes the rounded, slightly fruity baking-spice signature taste of the Glenlivet. Like the Illicit Still before it, this one's also a limited edition.

name: Glendalough  Canteiro Aged Madeira Cask Finish Single Cask Irish Whiskey (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Glendalough Single Cask - Canteiro Aged Madiera Finish is a premium single malt Irish whiskey produced by Glendalough Distillery, located in Wicklow, Ireland. The whiskey is made using 100% malted barley and aged in ex-bourbon casks before being finished in Canteiro Aged Madiera casks, giving it a unique and complex flavor profile. This release is a single cask release, meaning each bottle comes from a single cask, making each bottle unique in terms of flavor and character.
desc_en_full:
Glendalough Single Cask - Canteiro Aged Madiera Finish is a premium single malt Irish whiskey produced by Glendalough Distillery, located in Wicklow, Ireland. The whiskey is made using 100% malted barley and aged in ex-bourbon casks before being finished in Canteiro Aged Madiera casks, giving it a unique and complex flavor profile. This release is a single cask release, meaning each bottle comes from a single cask, making each bottle unique in terms of flavor and character.

The whiskey has a deep golden color and a complex aroma, with notes of fruitcake, sherry, and spice on the nose. On the palate, it is full-bodied and smooth, with flavors of toasted oak, dried fruit, and a hint of caramel. The finish is long and warming, with a subtle hint of spice and a hint of Madiera wine.

Glendalough Single Cask - Canteiro Aged Madiera Finish is a versatile whiskey that can be enjoyed neat or with a drop of water. The Madiera finish gives it a unique and complex flavor profile that pairs well with desserts, chocolate, and cheese.

The Glendalough Distillery, established in 2011, is a relatively new distillery, but it's already known for its commitment to quality and the use of traditional techniques. The distillery has a focus on producing unique and high-quality whiskey, and the Glendalough Single Cask - Canteiro Aged Madiera Finish is a great example of that.

name: Prakaan  Single Malt Whisky Tribura Series Peated Malt (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
PEATED MALT

Aged in Ex-Bourbon & Ex-Sherry Oak Cask

Kamphaeng Phet is one of the most ancient cities of Thailand, a prestigious connecting point in the prosperous era, famous for its impressive fortified walls. For centuries these walls have stood strong in defense of the Kingdom of Thailand. Honoring the social and cultural significance of this proud history, PRAKAAN single malt whisky pays respectful tribute to this iconic city with its venerable walls and keeps the legacy alive.

PRAKAAN single malt whisky was born in Kamphaeng Phet amidst unspoiled natural abundance where pure spring water is slowly filtered into underground reservoirs; an ideal environment for making fine quality whisky.

Our whisky is crafted by our proud craftsmen and women who combine their passion about nature and centuries old whisky making methods. PRAKAAN single malt whisky is expertly distilled and matured with great pride in the Kingdom of Thailand.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Notes

NOSE :

Fresh tropical fruit complemented by a layer of soft wood smoke, evocative
of a fire on the beach. Spices and rich vanilla sauce in the background
bring elegance to this delicious bouquet.PALATE :

Sweet and textured. Smoke comes to the fore but keeps its gentle,
round quality. Caramels and runny honey follow with citrus and
pineapple shining through.

FINISH :

Warming and long, the finish descends slowly from bright fruitiness
to sweet smoke.

name: Compass Box  Nectarosity (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Process

Special American oak barrels are deployed twice. First to impart sweet spice to grain spirit, which finishes maturing in other casks. Once 'prepared', the barrels age malt whisky. We expertly blend these two characterful whiskies with selected others to dial up the fruit and toffee notes.

Flavour profile

Zesty with honey, poached pears, nectarine and lychee, then juicy apricot and pineapple excite the palate. Toffee, vanilla and butterscotch sauce follow, with a lasting flavour of cinnamon bun.

Serving recommendations

Nectarosity will bring an irresistible dynamism everywhere it goes, from classic cocktails to boilermakers. Partner with a dry pilsner to create a soft centre of fruity caramel notes.

Technical details

Bottled at 46% ABV.

Natural colour.

Not chill-filtered.

Blended Scotch Whisky.
desc_en_full:
WHISKY MEETS PATISSERIE

Behind the most exquisite patisseries are test kitchens of invention. Places buzzing with energy where chefs obsess over every flake of fractured crust, every curl of custard cream.

Part artistry, part alchemy, this is mastery at work. And when their experimentation creates a true harmony of flavour, it has to be shared.

Because generosity drives this endeavour. If nectar is the food of the gods, and joy comes from celebrating with others, then we need a new word for that irresistible urge.

And that word is ‘Nectarosity’.

name: Compass Box  Orchard House (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Process

Harvest a fruitful selection of malt whiskies at just the right time, then blend to showcase their bright fruit characteristics. With a light oak influence, a gentle honey sweetness comes through from first-fill bourbon barrels.

Flavour profile

Aromas of apple and pear dominate, with hints of pineapple, lemon and lime zest, and Earl Grey tea. Take a sip and note the malty and gingery flavours. These are soon joined by honey, wild strawberries and vanilla shortbread.

Serving recommendations

Vibrant when neat, syrupy when enjoyed on the rocks. Enjoy as a boilermaker alongside a crisp pale ale or pilsner for a delightful pairing

Technical details

Bottled at 46% ABV.

Natural colour.

Not chill-filtered.

Blended Scotch Whisky.
desc_en_full:
FRUIT-FORWARD & SPIRIT-DRIVEN

Come with us on a stroll to Scotland's Orchard House. The terrain is gentle and there's no need for a map – it's easy to find even for those who have never ventured this way before. ​

There's a sweet tang of strawberries in the air. The soft scent of pears and apples drifts by. Perhaps a hint of pineapple too? It's as if an entire summer's harvest is ripening at once, and you get the feeling the place would be just as alluring whatever the season. ​​

Can you see it just over that ridge up ahead? Be patient though, slow down, there's no rush. Better to pause a moment and take it all in. Yes, there's a lush fruitiness and citrus, but can you detect a creamy vanilla, and maybe a sprinkling of cinnamon too? ​

You see, journeys like these are much more rewarding when you really pay attention to your senses. Be curious, explore, and you'll discover so many more layers to the world around you.

How can somewhere like this exist? Such an abundant landscape can only have flourished by searching far and wide, hand-picking varieties from the very best stock, at the perfect time, and bringing them together to share with generosity.​​

But hold that thought because we've arrived at our home among the fruit trees, where everyone is welcome. The door open as always, the embers of a fire smouldering in the hearth and the farmhouse table filled with the gathered harvest. Inviting isn't it? So pull up a chair, grab a glass and let's raise a toast to celebrate this monument to fresh fruit, this dedication to deliciousness.

desc_en_full:
Balblair 15 YO Special Pack- This exquisite Balblair 15- Year-Old Single malt scotch whisky commemorates the 170th anniversary of diplomatic relations between Thailand and the United Kingdom.It stands as a testament to the enduring friendship and collaboration between both countries. Celebrate and savour the richness of our intertwined heritage, trade and cultures.

House Style

Every Scotch whisky distillery is different. From its location and water source, to its still shape and wood policy. These are all determining factors in how a whisky tastes.

The Balblair collection is intriguingly complex and satisfying. We capture the intense esters at the start of the spirit cut to give our Whisky the core characteristics of apricots, oranges, spices, floral notes and green apples. The heavier oils and lipids at the end of the cut are also captured, giving leathery, nutty and full-bodied characteristics

Tasting Note

AROMA : Leatherwood honey and spicy gingerbread followed by juicy prunes and fresh lemon peel

PALATE : Velvety in texture, bursting with dark chocolate, tropical fruit and spices

FINISH : Long and mellow, with notes of creamy vanilla and citrus

name: Prakaan  Single Malt Whisky Tribura Series Double Cask (700 ml) (Special Pack)
desc_en_short:
DOUBLE CASK

Aged in Ex-Bourbon & Ex-Sherry Oak Cask

Kamphaeng Phet is one of the most ancient cities of Thailand, a prestigious connecting point in the prosperous era, famous for its impressive fortified walls. For centuries these walls have stood strong in defense of the Kingdom of Thailand. Honoring the social and cultural significance of this proud history, PRAKAAN single malt whisky pays respectful tribute to this iconic city with its venerable walls and keeps the legacy alive.

PRAKAAN single malt whisky was born in Kamphaeng Phet amidst unspoiled natural abundance where pure spring water is slowly filtered into underground reservoirs; an ideal environment for making fine quality whisky.

Our whisky is crafted by our proud craftsmen and women who combine their passion about nature and centuries old whisky making methods. PRAKAAN single malt whisky is expertly distilled and matured with great pride in the Kingdom of Thailand.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Notes

NOSE :

Rich tropical fruit smothered in layers of melting dessert chocolate and vanilla. Raisins and candied citrus peels follow, balancing this vibrant and decadent composition.

PALATE :

Rich and delicious. Pineapples in syrup and ripe mangoes underlined by elegant oaky vanilla and dark sweetness of warm toffee.

FINISH :

Full-bodied and smooth with oaky and leathery tones contrasted by a light touch grated orange zest.

name: Johnnie Walker Gold Label XR 21 Chinese New Year Of horse (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Johnnie Walker Gold Label XR 21 – Chinese New Year: Year of the Horse is a refined limited edition crafted to honor progress, vitality, and the spirited energy of the horse. Blended from rare whiskies aged a full 21 years, it delivers a smooth, honeyed richness with gentle spice and soft smoke—an elegant choice for New Year celebrations and collectors.
desc_en_full:
Johnnie Walker Gold Label XR 21 – Chinese New Year: Year of the Horse is a distinguished limited-edition release created to celebrate the vitality, determination, and forward-flowing spirit associated with the Year of the Horse. This elegant expression blends time-honored Scotch whisky craftsmanship with the cultural richness of Lunar New Year, resulting in a bottle that is both meaningful and visually captivating.

The Whisky

Gold Label XR 21 is created using rare whiskies aged for a full 21 years, sourced from select distilleries across Scotland, including several renowned “ghost distilleries.” These long-aged components add layers of depth and refined character, resulting in one of the most polished and harmonious blends in the Johnnie Walker portfolio.

Aroma & Taste

The nose reveals rich notes of honeycomb, vanilla cream, and citrus zest, accompanied by gentle oak and a soft hint of smoke. On the palate, the texture is exceptionally smooth—flavors of honeyed malt, dried fruits, roasted almonds, and warm baking spices unfold gradually. The finish is long, elegant, and slightly smoky, with a golden sweetness that lingers beautifully.

Design Highlights

This limited-edition bottle is adorned with a flowing illustration of the horse in motion, rendered with modern lines and gold detailing. Its luxurious presentation captures the essence of Lunar New Year—good fortune, momentum, and new beginnings—making it an exceptional choice for display, gifting, or collecting.

How to Enjoy

Best savored neat or over a single large ice cube to reveal its multi-layered flavors. Serving at 17–20°C allows the fruity, floral, and gently smoky notes to express themselves with clarity and elegance.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Gris Blanc Rose
wine_color: A very pale pink colour plus grey and whitish tints.
desc_en_short: An original rosé with a very pale pink colour plus grey and whitish tints. The bouquet reveals delightful red berry scents. A slight sparkle on the palate gives the wine all its freshness and brings out its fruit to the full. The elegant finish is pleasantly fresh and mineral.
desc_en_full:
VINIFICATION AND MATURING

The grapes are picked by hand and pressed directly.

All the handling operations are carried out in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to protect the delicate aromas of the Grenache grapes until the start of fermentation which is carried out at low temperatures (from 15 to 18°C).

The wine is matured on the fine lees for several weeks before bottling.

TASTING NOTES

An original rosé with a very pale pink colour plus grey and whitish tints. The bouquet reveals delightful red berry scents.

A slight sparkle on the palate gives the wine all its freshness and brings out its fruit to the full.

The elegant finish is pleasantly fresh and mineral.

Grenache is the king of grape varieties when it comes to making light, racy, fruity rosés in contemporary style.

Grenache reveals all its suppleness and cherry aromas. Its fig and sometimes cocoa flavours, plus its rounded sensations on the palate, make it one of the great varieties of the South of France.

FOOD AND WINE PAIRING

To be savoured at 10-12°C as an aperitif, with seafood, Mediterranean-style salads, Asian dishes, baked fish and spicy and stronger-tasting dishes such as Thai curries or tajines.

name: Frescobaldi  Alie Rose Ammiraglia Tuscany IGT (Syrah, Vermentino)
desc_en_short: A beautiful pale pink color. To the nose stand out intense fruity and floral scents such as peach, rose and peony. Citrus notes perfectly integrated in the context give a wonderful crispness even more accentuated by delicate iodine hints. The bouquet is all in progress and in a second moment appear sensations of aromatic herbs such as thyme and sage. The taste-olfactory correspondence is impressive, moreover to the palate is perceived a wonderful crispness together with sapidity. Alìe is a full bodied, silky and smooth wine.
desc_en_full:
Vinification and ageing:

The secret of Alìe lies in the combination of territorial characteristics, vineyard management and, of course, the human factor. Grapes, once harvested, were transported to the winery in a short time in order not to waste or damage the bunches.  One of the most important moments of Alìe's production was represented by the pressing phase. Bunches, whole and in absence of oxygen, were loaded in the press. Pressures applied during this phase were extremely soft and delicate; this process, together with an accurate selection of musts and a subsequent decanting of them, for no less than 12 hours, allowed the exaltation of the distinctive characteristics of this seductive rose.  Fermentations took place in stainless steel tanks at a controlled temperature lower than 19° C (66° F). After a few months of aging on fine lees, subsequently including a period in bottle, Alìe 2021 was ready to seduce and fascinate making every sip passionate.

name: Montes  Alpha Merlot
desc_en_short: Montes Alpha Merlot from Chile's Colchagua Valley delivers a lush, ruby-hued wine brimming with black cherry, red berries, and black pepper, rounded out by a velvety mid-palate with vanilla, toast, and a long, elegant finish.
desc_en_full:
Montes is one of Chile's most celebrated and export-driven estates, founded in 1988 with a mission to produce premium wines that showcase the country's extraordinary terroir. The Alpha range sits at the heart of their portfolio — wines crafted to express the full potential of individual varietals grown across their Colchagua Valley vineyards, one of Chile's most acclaimed red-wine regions thanks to its warm, dry climate and diverse Andean-influenced soils.

This Merlot is blended with a signature 10% Carmenère — Chile's adopted heritage grape — which lends an extra layer of herbal depth and structure to complement Merlot's naturally plush, fruit-forward character. The result is a wine that punches well above its price point in terms of complexity and refinement.

In the glass it shows a handsome deep ruby. The nose is immediately inviting: ripe red berries and black cherry lead, followed by cracked black pepper and subtle hints of tobacco leaf. On the palate, those flavours translate beautifully — the mid-palate is round and velvety, enriched with touches of vanilla and toasted oak from careful barrel ageing, before rolling into a long, smooth, and genuinely elegant finish.

Serve at 16–18 °C alongside a slow-braised lamb shoulder, duck confit, or a rich Bolognese. It also pairs effortlessly with cured meats and aged hard cheeses for a more relaxed occasion. A confident, crowd-pleasing bottle that rewards both casual weeknight dinners and more considered gatherings alike.

name: San Pedro  Gato Negro Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: Gato Negro Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Central Valley delivers ripe berries, cassis, and a touch of vanilla and chocolate, backed by smooth tannins and a satisfying finish — an everyday Chilean red that punches well above its price point.
desc_en_full:
San Pedro is one of Chile's most storied and widely exported wine producers, with roots stretching back to 1865 in the Curicó Valley. The Gato Negro range has long been the label that put approachable, fruit-forward Chilean wine on tables around the world, and this Cabernet Sauvignon remains its flagship expression.

Sourced from sun-drenched vineyards across the Central Valley, this blend leans on 95% Cabernet Sauvignon with a quiet 5% Syrah woven in to deepen colour and add a gentle peppery lift. The result is a dark ruby-red wine with an inviting nose of ripe berries, black cherry, and cassis layered over subtle notes of vanilla, milk chocolate, and soft oak spice — hallmarks of cool-room fermentation and careful oak ageing that San Pedro has refined over generations.

On the palate it delivers exactly what a weeknight Cabernet should: medium body, silky-smooth tannins, and a fresh, clean finish that lingers without demanding your full attention. The Syrah component adds just enough backbone to keep things interesting without pushing the wine into austere territory.

Pour it alongside a grilled ribeye, slow-cooked pork ribs, or a charcuterie board loaded with hard cheeses and cured meats. A quick 30-minute chill to around 16–18 °C takes the edge off and lets those red-fruit aromatics really shine. Ideal for barbecues, casual dinner parties, or simply unwinding after a long day.

name: San Pedro  Gato Negro Merlot
wine_color: Ruby red colour with purples tones.
desc_en_short: Gato Negro Merlot from San Pedro delivers ripe blackberry and plum aromas lifted by sweet vanilla and gentle toast, flowing into a smooth, medium-bodied palate with supple tannins — an approachable everyday Chilean red that over-delivers for its price.
desc_en_full:
San Pedro is one of Chile's most established wine producers, and its Gato Negro range has long been a benchmark for approachable, fruit-forward wines that punch well above their price point. Sourcing primarily from the sun-drenched vineyards of the Maipo Valley — Chile's heartland for red varieties — the Gato Negro Merlot blends 95% Merlot with a 5% splash of Syrah, a touch that adds a subtle peppery depth and structural lift to the otherwise plush, soft-fruited profile.

In the glass, the wine pours a vivid ruby-red with inviting violet edges. The nose opens generously with fresh blackberry and ripe plum, layered with smooth toasted oak and a hint of sweet vanilla — hallmarks of careful, light oak treatment that never overwhelms the fruit. On the palate, it's everything the bouquet promises: medium-bodied and approachable, with silky tannins and just enough acidity to keep things lively through a clean, pleasing finish.

Gato Negro Merlot is the kind of bottle that earns its place at any casual table. Pour it alongside a slow-braised lamb shank, a herb-crusted pork tenderloin, or a duck confit — its smooth tannins handle richness effortlessly. It's equally at home with a hearty Bolognese, cured meats on a charcuterie board, or even a rustic pizza fresh from the oven. Serve at 16–18°C to let the fruit shine. Whether it's a weeknight dinner or a relaxed garden gathering, this is an easy, confident choice.

name: La Braccesca By Antinori  Rosso di Montepulciano "Sabazio" DOC
style: Prugnolo Gentile 15% Merlot And Other Red Varieties
desc_en_short: Sabazio is the fresh, approachable Rosso di Montepulciano from Antinori's La Braccesca estate — a joyful, early-drinking Sangiovese (Prugnolo Gentile) with vibrant cherry, spice, and earthy warmth, ready to enjoy now with classic Tuscan food.
desc_en_full:
La Braccesca is Antinori's estate in Montepulciano — the historic Tuscan town whose Vino Nobile is one of Italy's DOCG greats. Sabazio is the estate's Rosso di Montepulciano, the younger, more accessible appellation made from the same Sangiovese clone — here called Prugnolo Gentile — that goes into the Nobile.

Rosso di Montepulciano wines are by law made with shorter ageing than the Nobile, resulting in a wine that expresses the grape's fresh, joyful side rather than its austere structure. Sabazio delivers exactly this: vivid ruby colour, an expressive nose of fresh cherry, plum, dried herbs, earthy minerals, and warm spice, followed by a palate that is medium-bodied with bright acidity, gentle tannins, and a clean, satisfying finish.

This is a wine made for the Tuscan table — bistecca, pasta al ragù, grilled meats, roast chicken, and aged Pecorino. At its best slightly young, drunk within 3–5 years of vintage.

name: Tenuta Guado al Tasso By Antinori  Il Bruciato Bolgheri
desc_en_short: Il Bruciato is the approachable entry point into Antinori's prestigious Guado al Tasso estate in Bolgheri — a warm, generously fruited coastal Tuscan red with dark cherry, Mediterranean herbs, spice, and smooth polished tannins.
desc_en_full:
Tenuta Guado al Tasso is one of the jewels of the Antinori empire — a vast coastal estate in Bolgheri where the proximity of the Tyrrhenian Sea, the unique gravelly soils, and outstanding viticulture have established it as one of Italy's great wine properties. Il Bruciato — named after a locality on the estate — is designed as the most accessible expression of the Guado al Tasso terroir.

A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Syrah, aged in French barriques, the wine captures Bolgheri's warm, Mediterranean spirit in an open, generous style. Ruby in colour with violet highlights, it opens on aromas of dark cherry, blackberry, rosemary, thyme, and warm spice, with a gentle hint of cedar from the oak. On the palate it is medium-full bodied, with smooth, polished tannins, good acidity, and a fruit-forward, pleasurable finish.

Perfect with grilled meats, lamb, pasta with meat sauces, pizza, and everyday Tuscan food. Enjoyable from release but equally good with 2–4 years of age.

name: Bodegas Salentein  Portillo Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: Portillo Cabernet Sauvignon from Bodegas Salentein delivers classic Mendoza depth — dark fruit, black pepper spice, and a smooth, fruit-forward palate with firm tannins that make it an effortless match for grilled red meats and hearty weekend dinners.
desc_en_full:
Bodegas Salentein is one of Mendoza's most celebrated names, a Dutch-owned estate that carved its reputation from the high-altitude vineyards of the Uco Valley. The Portillo range represents the entry point into the Salentein family — unpretentious, approachable, and consistently well-made, earning recognition from critics including an 85-point nod from Wine Spectator.

This 100% Cabernet Sauvignon draws on Mendoza's combination of intense sun, cool nights, and well-drained soils to produce grapes with genuine ripeness and natural structure. The result is a wine that is intensely ruby red in the glass, pulling the eye in before the nose has a chance to speak.

On the palate, expect ripe blackcurrant and dark plum layered with cracked black pepper and a whisper of warm spice — hallmarks of well-grown Argentine Cabernet Sauvignon. The tannins are firm but polished, framing a smooth, fruit-forward finish that lingers without heaviness.

Portillo Cabernet Sauvignon is the kind of bottle that works just as hard on a Tuesday night as it does at a weekend asado. Pour it alongside a grilled ribeye, slow-braised lamb shoulder, or a charcuterie board piled with cured meats and aged hard cheese. Pasta with a spicy tomato-based sauce is another natural partner, making this a genuinely versatile red for the table.

name: Marques De Riscal  Riscal 1860
wine_color: Intense, black cherry colour, with violet glints.
desc_en_short: A vibrant everyday Rioja from the iconic Marqués de Riscal house, blending Tempranillo with Syrah, Merlot, and Cabernet Sauvignon for a fresh, fruit-forward red with aromatic spice, smoky oak, and a satisfying finish that punches well above its price point.
desc_en_full:
Named in honour of the year Marqués de Riscal first planted vines in Rioja, Riscal 1860 is the estate's accessible, everyday expression of what this legendary Spanish appellation does best. Founded in 1858, Marqués de Riscal holds the distinction of being one of Rioja's oldest and most celebrated bodegas — a producer that essentially introduced Bordeaux-inspired winemaking to Spain and never stopped innovating. Riscal 1860 reflects that heritage while embracing a modern, approachable style.

The blend anchors around 85% Tempranillo, Rioja's signature red grape, lending the wine its characteristic dark cherry fruit and structural backbone. A 10% addition of Syrah deepens the colour and adds a seductive note of black pepper and violet, while small percentages of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon round out the mid-palate and extend the finish. The result pours a deep ruby with violet-purple highlights at the rim.

On the nose, expect stewed red fruits, a hint of burnt chocolate, cracked black pepper, and a whisper of smoky oak. The palate delivers that same freshness up front — bright and fruity — with well-integrated tannins that frame rather than dominate. Medium acidity keeps everything lively, and a long, oak-laced finish lingers pleasantly.

Pour this alongside a slow-roasted lamb shoulder, a board of Spanish cured meats, or a juicy grilled beef ribeye. It's the kind of bottle that makes a Tuesday dinner feel like a celebration.

name: Chateau Cissac  Cru Bourgeois
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 20% Merlot And 5% Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: A classic Médoc from the reliable Cru Bourgeois estate of Château Cissac, this Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant blend delivers lifted cassis, cedar, and pencil-shaving aromas with a rich, smooth palate of blackberry and blackcurrant framed by elegant structure and a refined finish.
desc_en_full:
Nestled in the Haut-Médoc on the western bank of the Gironde, Château Cissac has long been regarded as one of the most dependable estates in the Cru Bourgeois tier — a producer whose wines consistently punch above their classification and reward those who appreciate traditional, unfussy Bordeaux craftsmanship. The estate's terroir of deep gravelly soils is ideally suited to Cabernet Sauvignon, which forms the backbone of this characterful blend.

The assemblage of 75% Cabernet Sauvignon, 20% Merlot, and 5% Petit Verdot reads like a textbook Médoc formula, yet the result is anything but generic. On the nose, lifted cassis, black cherry, and a signature waft of pencil shavings and cedar signal the Cabernet's confident presence. There is a quiet complexity here — fruit that is pure and focused without being heavy. The Merlot brings plumpness and mid-palate generosity, while the Petit Verdot adds a subtle violet-edged spice and structural depth that stretches the finish gracefully.

On the palate, flavours of blackberry and blackcurrant are bolstered by a refreshing acidity that keeps the wine lively, while the tannins are smooth and well-integrated — firm enough to suggest cellaring potential, supple enough to enjoy now. Decanting for 30 minutes is recommended to allow the wine's full character to unfold.

An ideal partner for a Sunday roast, braised short ribs, charcuterie boards, or anything coming off the grill. This is classic Bordeaux for the table — unpretentious, consistent, and satisfying.

name: Frescobaldi  Remole Toscana IGT
desc_en_short: A Tuscan everyday classic from the Frescobaldi estate, Remole blends Sangiovese and Cabernet Sauvignon into a luminous, fruit-driven red brimming with blackberry, cherry, and warming spice — versatile enough to grace any table, any night of the week.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in Tuscany as Frescobaldi, a family whose winemaking roots stretch back over 700 years across some of the region's most celebrated estates. Remole Toscana IGT is the house's approachable, everyday expression — crafted at the Remole estate on the outskirts of Florence and bottled under the broad, liberating IGT Toscana classification that allows the winemakers to blend beyond the strict rules of Chianti Classico or Brunello.

The blend — 85% Sangiovese anchored by 15% Cabernet Sauvignon — showcases what makes Tuscan blending so compelling. Sangiovese delivers the wine's vivid purple-ruby colour, tart cherry brightness, and lifted acidity, while Cabernet Sauvignon adds structure, a darker fruit dimension, and a little extra mid-palate weight. The result pours a luminous, intense purple-red and opens with an inviting bouquet of blackberry, morello cherry, and blackcurrant, threaded through with black pepper, vanilla, and a whisper of star anise.

On the palate it is warm, fruit-forward, and persistent — lively acidity keeping everything fresh and the tannins firm but well-integrated. This is a genuinely versatile food wine: pour it alongside slow-braised lamb shoulder, a bistecca Fiorentina, or aged Pecorino. It equally shines with a hearty pasta all'amatriciana or a board of charcuterie. Reliable, honest, and unmistakably Tuscan.

name: Tommasi  Ripasso Valpolicella Classico Superiore IGT
desc_en_short: Tommasi's Ripasso Valpolicella Classico Superiore is a richly layered Italian red, re-fermented over Amarone grape skins to deliver concentrated dark fruit, warming spice, and a smoky depth — a compelling step between everyday Valpolicella and the grandeur of Amarone.
desc_en_full:
Few techniques in Italian winemaking are as seductive as the ripasso method, and few families have mastered the art of Valpolicella as thoroughly as the Tommasi family. Rooted in the Valpolicella Classico hills since 1902, Tommasi is widely regarded as one of the Veneto's most dependable and quality-driven estates — a producer whose wines deliver genuine character vintage after vintage.

This Ripasso Valpolicella Classico Superiore IGT is crafted from a traditional blend of Corvina Veronese (70%), Rondinella (25%), and Corvinone (5%). Following primary fermentation, the wine is re-passed — ripassato — over the dried grape skins left from Amarone production. This second maceration imparts remarkable richness, texture, and structure, elevating the wine far beyond a standard Valpolicella while keeping it approachable.

In the glass, expect deep ruby colour and an expressive nose of dark cherry, dried plum, and blackberry, threaded with dried herbs, cinnamon, clove, and a whisper of woodsmoke. The palate is full-bodied and generously textured, with high tannins and a bright, food-friendly acidity that keeps everything lifted and clean through to a satisfyingly dry finish.

This is a wine built for the table. Pair it confidently with a slow-braised beef short rib, a rack of lamb with rosemary, or aged hard cheeses. It also shines alongside hearty pasta dishes — a ribollita or a rich Bolognese would be outstanding matches. Decant for 30 minutes to let its layers fully open.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Naturalys Syrah
wine_color: A lovely garnet-red colour with violet tints.
desc_en_short: Gérard Bertrand's Naturalys Syrah is a sun-drenched Languedoc red delivering supple blackberry fruit, garrigue-kissed aromatics, and firm yet polished tannins — a Mediterranean expression of Syrah at its most generous and food-friendly.
desc_en_full:
Gérard Bertrand is one of the Languedoc's most celebrated names, a passionate champion of the Mediterranean art de vivre who has spent decades coaxing the best from southern France's exceptional terroirs. The Naturalys Syrah sits within his commitment to expressive, terroir-driven winemaking — drawing on the sun-baked limestone and schist soils of the Languedoc, where low rainfall concentrates flavours and long, warm days ripen Syrah to its fullest, most characterful potential.

In the glass, this 100% Syrah opens with an inviting nose of dark plum, blackberry, and crushed violet, layered with the wild herbal signature of garrigue — that unmistakable blend of thyme, rosemary, and lavender that is the Languedoc's aromatic calling card. A whisper of black pepper and warm spice adds depth, nodding to the variety's Northern Rhône heritage while remaining firmly rooted in the south.

On the palate, the attack is immediately supple and aromatic, the fruit seamlessly intertwined with earthy, garrigue-inflected complexity. Full-bodied yet balanced, the wine is framed by high-quality tannins that are firm without being aggressive, carrying the finish to a long, satisfying close.

This is an ideal partner for a Sunday côte de boeuf or a slow-roasted leg of lamb with herbs de Provence. It also holds its own beautifully alongside spiced dishes — think Moroccan lamb tagine or a hearty Thai massaman beef curry, making it a versatile and exciting choice for tables in Thailand seeking a structured, flavour-forward red.

name: Yalumba  Y Series Shiraz Viognier
desc_en_short: Yalumba's Y Series Shiraz Viognier is a signature South Australian blend delivering exotic aromas of Turkish delight, violets, and black cherry, with a generous, spiced palate of rhubarb and blueberry fruit that finishes beautifully round and soft.
desc_en_full:
Yalumba is one of South Australia's most storied family-owned wineries, with roots stretching back to 1849 in the Barossa Valley. The Y Series range represents the estate's commitment to making approachable, characterful wines that draw on a broad network of premium South Australian vineyards — a patchwork of fruit sources that gives the winemaking team tremendous flexibility to balance quality and consistency vintage to vintage.

This Shiraz Viognier is a classic co-fermentation play, a technique long championed in the Northern Rhône — think Côte-Rôtie — where a small percentage of white Viognier grapes fermented alongside Shiraz lifts the aromatics, deepens the colour, and adds a seductive floral dimension that straight Shiraz rarely achieves alone. The result here is immediately evident in the glass: deep crimson with violet hues, and an exotic nose layered with Turkish delight, cinnamon bark, dried violets, and ripe black cherries.

On the palate, the wine is medium-bodied and generously textured, offering blueberry compote, rhubarb, and spiced fruit cake alongside soft, fleshy tannins built through careful warm fermentation and post-ferment maceration. The finish is round, long, and inviting — never aggressive.

This is the kind of red that performs brilliantly at the table. Reach for it alongside sticky American-style BBQ pork ribs, a slow-roasted lamb shoulder fragrant with cumin and coriander, or a richly spiced Thai beef curry. It also holds its own beside a good charcuterie board on a relaxed evening.

name: Yalumba  Organic Shiraz
wine_color: Magenta in colour with purple hues.
desc_en_short: Magenta in colour with purple hues, the Yalumba Organic Shiraz 2011 is bursting with varietal aromas of plums and violets, sweet fruit with a forest floor finish. On the palate the aromas continue through to the palate with plums and violets joined by peppercorns and spice. This is a fruit driven, medium bodied wine with fine, soft tannins and a savoury, persistent finish.
desc_en_full:
Winemaking / Viticulture

Made with organic winemaking techniques with very little winemaking intervention. The grapes for this wine were sourced from four family owned vineyards that are organically certified. The Barich family vineyard is located on the Murray River at Loxton, The Palena family vineyard lies near Barmera, the Strachan family vineyard sits in the Sellicks foothills subregion of McLaren Vale, and the Carypidis family of Virginia are in the Adelaide Plains.

Vintage Conditions

After years of drought, the weather changed this vintage and South Australia experienced a cool and wet season that was a challenge for all. Through meticulous care in the vineyards and without compromising their organic principles, our organic Shiraz growers again produced grapes that had great flavours.

name: Millaman  Estate Reserve Merlot
desc_en_short: A plush, fruit-forward Merlot from Chile's Curico Valley, Millaman Estate Reserve delivers ripe plum and cherry flavours with soft vanilla notes, round tannins, and a warm, lingering finish that makes it effortlessly approachable.
desc_en_full:
Nestled in Chile's Curico Valley, one of the country's most historically significant wine regions, Millaman has built its reputation on coaxing expressive, fruit-driven wines from vines that benefit from warm days, cool nights, and well-drained soils fed by Andean meltwater. The Estate Reserve Merlot sits at the heart of this philosophy — a step up from everyday drinking, crafted with enough care to reflect the estate's terroir without sacrificing the easy-drinking charm that makes Chilean Merlot so beloved.

In the glass, expect a deep ruby-red with inviting purple edges — a visual cue to the ripe, generous character within. The nose opens with classic Merlot hallmarks: juicy plum and black cherry, layered with subtle hints of vanilla and gentle spice, likely shaped by a period of oak maturation. On the palate, the wine is plush and medium-bodied, with soft, well-integrated tannins and bright acidity that keeps each sip lively. The finish is long, warm, and satisfying.

This is a wine that punches above its price point and rewards those who take a moment to appreciate it. Pour it alongside a slow-braised lamb shoulder, a hearty beef ragu over pappardelle, or simply a plate of aged cheese after dinner. Equally at home on a weeknight table or at a relaxed weekend gathering, Millaman Estate Reserve Merlot is the kind of bottle worth keeping a few of on the shelf.

name: Millaman  Limited Reserve Zinfandel
wine_color: Intense red color with violet tinges.
desc_en_short: A bold Maipo Valley Zinfandel from Millaman, delivering intense red fruit, spice, and seamlessly integrated oak with a full-bodied palate, soft mature tannins, and a long, fruity finish — a rare and expressive example of this Italian-origin variety thriving in Chilean terroir.
desc_en_full:
Zinfandel is an uncommon sight in Chilean wine country, which makes Millaman's Limited Reserve offering from the Maipo Valley all the more compelling. Millaman is a producer known for drawing out site-specific character from this storied valley, and here they've cultivated 100% Zinfandel on alluvial soils with excellent drainage — conditions that encourage deep root development and stress the vine just enough to concentrate flavour without sacrificing elegance.

The vineyard uses traditional trellis training, and the combination of dry Maipo summers, gentle autumn conditions, and the cooling influence of the Maipo River has allowed this ancient Italian variety to find a genuine sense of place. The result is a wine with real typicity: ripe and generous, yet structured enough to earn the "Limited Reserve" designation.

In the glass, expect a deep ruby-red with striking violet tinges. The nose is intense and complex — dark cherry, ripe plum, and dried raspberry are woven together with notes of baking spice, subtle florals, and well-judged oak. On the palate, the wine delivers impressive concentration and volume while remaining surprisingly soft and polished, with mature tannins and a finish that lingers with bright red fruit and a whisper of earth.

At the table, this Zinfandel is remarkably versatile. Reach for it alongside grilled beef ribs, slow-braised short rib, or a wood-fired burger. It's equally at home with aged hard cheeses, duck confit, spiced lamb, or hearty pasta with a rich meat ragù. Serve slightly cool at around 15°C to let its aromatics open fully.

name: Louis Jadot  Beaujolais Villages
desc_en_short: Strong red purple colour. Fresh red fruits on the nose with a hint of dark cherry. Slightly spicy with a touch of grey pepper, liquorice and a touch of rose flower. The whole wine is very well balanced with a nice acidity and the tannins presence on the finish invites food pairing such as with Terrines, Charcuteries, Grilled red meat, white fish, cheese or simply as the sole wine of a meal.
desc_en_full:
Characteristics

The terroir is mainly composed of granite. Among the 55.000 acres (22.000 hectares) of vineyards planted in the Beaujolais region , about 15.000 acres (6.000 hectares) are under the name Beaujolais Villages « Appellation d'Origine Contrôlée » which stands between the Beaujolais Appellation and the Crus appellation (there are 10 crus).

It's a very important wine for us: for a long time, Louis Jadot has been producing a consistent Beaujolais-Villages and we are one of the most important producers in the region.We are really involved in the region where we have a winery dedicated to vinify a large part of the total volume (Cellier de la Vauxonne close to Saint Etienne des Oullières) We have 250ha in contracts and it's representing actually 60% of the total volume of our Beaujolais-Villages. We are producing around 250 000cases of Beaujolais-Villages every year and of course it means that we are blending maybe 4 or 5 different cuvees that we are bottling all year long.

name: Dourthe  Grands Terroirs Medoc
desc_en_short: This Médoc wine boasts a beautiful, deep garnet-red colour and a nose dominated by red berry fruit aromas. Initially powerful on the palate, the wine reveals good structure, with perfectly ripe, delicious tannins leading into a long, aromatic finish.
desc_en_full: To the North-West of Bordeaux stretches the Médoc appellation, known as the « middle ground » because it is situated between the Atlantic Ocean and the Gironde Estuary. The vineyards enjoy a relatively hot, wet climate combined with a lot of sun, which is beneficial to the grapes as they ripen. The Médoc Grands Terroirs is the result of a particularly strict selection procedure carried out by the Dourthe oenologists. From among hundreds of batches, only the most interesting wines are selected, those with a well-balanced structure and a personality typical of their appellation. The blend then creates a wine of character with its complex fruity notes and its supple tannins. This Médoc reveals good structure, with perfectly ripe, delicious tannins.

name: Dourthe  Grands Terroirs Saint-Emilion
desc_en_short: A classic Saint-Émilion from Dourthe's Grands Terroirs range, this Merlot-dominant blend delivers ripe fruit, warming spice, and the appellation's signature rounded elegance — approachable yet structured, with supple tannins and a satisfying length.
desc_en_full:
Dourthe is one of Bordeaux's most respected négociants, and the Grands Terroirs range represents the house's commitment to authentic, terroir-expressive wines sourced from the finest appellations on both banks. For this Saint-Émilion, Dourthe's oenologists conduct an unusually rigorous selection process — evaluating hundreds of individual batches from the appellation's characteristically diverse soils, a mosaic of clay, limestone, sand, and gravel that defines the Right Bank east of Bordeaux along the Dordogne.

The blend centres on 85% Merlot, the dominant grape of Saint-Émilion, fleshed out with 10% Cabernet Franc for aromatic lift and structure, and a touch of Cabernet Sauvignon for definition. The result is a wine of genuine character: a deep ruby colour leads to an expressive nose of ripe dark plum, black cherry, warming spice, and a subtle earthy, mineral undercurrent that speaks directly to the appellation's limestone-rich terroir. On the palate, the hallmark roundness of great Saint-Émilion takes centre stage — generous yet refined, with supple tannins, well-balanced acidity, and a persistent, elegant finish.

Pour this alongside a slow-roasted leg of lamb with herbed jus, a prime beef tenderloin, or a hearty pasta Bolognese for a textbook Right Bank pairing. It also shines with cured charcuterie as part of a relaxed cheese and charcuterie board. Accessible on release yet capable of short-term cellaring, it is an ideal everyday-elegant Bordeaux red.

name: Oak Vineyards  Merlot
desc_en_short: Oak Vineyards Merlot from California is a deep, dark-maroon red brimming with plum, fresh cherry, and ripe raspberry fruit, layered with enticing notes of coffee and dark chocolate — an approachable yet full-bodied wine that delivers bold California character in every glass.
desc_en_full:
Oak Vineyards crafts this California Merlot as a textbook expression of the Golden State's ability to produce richly fruited, generously structured red wines that feel both crowd-pleasing and genuinely characterful. Merlot thrives in California's sun-drenched vineyard sites, ripening to deep, concentrated fruit while retaining enough structure to reward a proper pour.

The wine opens with inviting aromas of ripe plum and dark fruit, underscored by seductive whispers of espresso and dark chocolate — hallmarks of thoughtful oak integration that adds complexity without overwhelming the fruit. On the palate, bold flavors of fresh cherry, sun-warmed raspberry, and blackcurrant unfold with a lush, velvety generosity typical of well-crafted California Merlot. The full body is balanced by medium acidity and firm tannins that provide just enough grip to carry the wine through a substantial meal.

This is a versatile table wine built for the dinner table. Pour it alongside a slow-braised lamb shoulder, a classic pasta Bolognese, or a board of cured meats and aged cheeses. For a more playful pairing, try it with Swedish meatballs or finish the meal daringly with a rich dark chocolate tartufo — the wine's cocoa and fruit notes mirror the dessert beautifully. Serve slightly below room temperature, around 16–18 °C, to let the aromas fully bloom.

desc_en_full:
About McManis

The McManis family has been farming in the northern interior region of California for four generations. In 1990, fourth generation farmer Ron McManis, and his wife Jamie founded the McManis Family Vineyards. Currently, they farm over 2,600 acres of premium wine grapes located within the premier growing areas of the northern interior. In 1998, the company designed and built a state?of-the?art winery and today the McManis Family Vineyards label comprises a range of red and white varietals sourced from ten estate vineyards. Son Justin, daughter Tanya and son in law Dirk Heuvel, are the fifth generation of the McManis family and are also involved in the family business.

name: Main Divide by Pegasus Bay  Cabernet Sauvignon Merlot
desc_en_short: The bouquet and flavour suggest blackberries, purple plums, cassis, cigar box and vanilla pod. There is an initial impression of softness but the wine then expands in the mouth to become muscular with a generous spine of ripe tannins that support and draw out the lingering aftertaste.
desc_en_full:
The Harvest and Winemaking

We use traditional Burgundian winemaking methods. The fruit was picked in late April and the grapes, without the stems, were put into small fermenting vats, retaining as many whole berries as possible. After several days of being kept cool, primary fermentation started through the action of the fruit's indigenous yeasts. During this fermentation the floating cap of grape skins was gently submerged twice daily to keep it moist and healthy and to aid extraction. After fermentation stopped the wine was kept contact with the grape remnants for a number of days. During this time it was tasted regularly, the aim being to optimise structure and mouth-feel. The wine was then separated and put into oak barriques from selected artisan Burgundian coopers, where it matured for 18 months. In the spring it underwent natural malolactic (secondary) fermentation by the action of the wine's own microorganisms. The various vats were then carefully blended according to taste before bottling.

desc_en_short:

Along with these fruits appear spicy notes of clove and cinnamon complemented by a background of well-integrated wood and graphite notes. The palate is intense, with a pleasant volume and acidity. It has characterful but elegant tannins that give it presence and vitality.
desc_en_full: Los Vascos Grande Reserve is a benchmark for elegance and finesse in Chile. A blend consisting mainly of Cabernet Sauvignon, it is produced using traditional vinification and maturing techniques. The wine is characterized by the complexity and persistence of its bouquet of cherries and blackcurrants (Cabernet Sauvignon), strawberries (Malbec), raspberries (Syrah) and plums, with notes of bay leaves and black pepper (Carmenère).

name: Oxford Landing  Merlot
wine_color: Crimson in colour with purple hues.
desc_en_short: Crimson in colour with purple hues. Enticing aromas of milk chocolate, plum and raspberries with subtle cedar and spices. The medium bodied palate starts with vibrant flavours of plums and although tightly structured, the finish is rich and generous with persistent fruit flavours. Soft, velvety tannins are a feature of the supple palate. Roast lamb with rosemary and garlic, or asparagus fettuccine with tomato cream sauce would be a lovely accompaniment.
desc_en_full:
Wyndham Hill Smith found Oxford Landing in 1958 on the banks of the River Murray, South Australia. The winery has matured into one of Australia's most innovative viticultural estates, delivering flavours that reflects the unique vineyard terroir. Oxford Landing vineyard near the South Australian town of Waikerie is an idyllic place, distinguished by clear blue skies, rich red soil and sunshine the perfect environment for growing grapes. The grapes are from select fruit parcels on the Oxford Landing block. Warm fermentation allows the best extraction of fruit flavours and tannins for this style of wine. This is a bright and friendly wine that is also very affordable...light to medium bodied, with vibrant raspberry fruit flavours, nice, fresh acidity and soft tannins.." Toni Paterson MW, December 2007.

name: Montes Winemaker's Choice  Merlot
desc_en_short: A approachable Chilean Merlot from Montes, offering fresh strawberry and cherry aromas layered with creamy caramel and toffee notes, silky tannins, and a long, satisfying finish — ideal for everyday dining.
desc_en_full:
Montes is one of Chile's most respected wine producers, having helped put the country on the global fine wine map since the late 1980s. The Winemaker's Choice label represents the winery's commitment to crafting honest, expressive wines that over-deliver at their price point, drawing on fruit sourced from carefully selected vineyards across Chile's cooler growing zones.

This Merlot pours a deep, inviting ruby-red, hinting at the ripe, generously fruited style within. The nose is immediately appealing — fresh red fruits like strawberry and cherry take centre stage, while a distinctive lactic character introduces notes of caramel, yoghurt, and soft toffee, likely the result of full malolactic fermentation lending a creamy, rounded complexity. Oak integration is handled with restraint, adding gentle warmth and structure without overwhelming the fruit.

On the palate, soft and round tannins make this an accessible, food-friendly Merlot with genuine everyday appeal. The lactic mid-palate carries through to a long, pleasing finish that lingers well beyond the last sip.

Pour this alongside chicken in a rich mushroom cream sauce, a hearty meat and vegetable soup, or a plate of herb-crusted lamb. It also pairs naturally with braised beef, slow-cooked pasta ragu, or a charcuterie board loaded with cured meats and aged cheese. A reliable, versatile bottle to keep on hand for weeknight meals or casual entertaining.

desc_en_full:
Montes Alpha Carmenere

The grapes come from our El Arcángel Marchigüe estate, located at the western end of Colchagua valley, where, 18 kilometers from the Pacific Ocean, the relatively cool climate allows this late-ripening variety to mature slowly and smoothly and reach full ripeness. Clay soils give strength and power to the wine. After fermentation, the wine is moved to French oak barrels where it is aged for 12 months.

name: Kaiken  Estate Malbec
desc_en_short: Kaiken Estate Malbec from Mendoza pours a deep violet with inviting aromas of ripe dark fruit, tobacco, and butterscotch, followed by a full-bodied, juicy palate with fleshy tannins and a long, harmonious oak-kissed finish — a textbook expression of Argentine terroir.
desc_en_full:
Kaiken was founded by the Clos des Fous and Montes families — pioneering Chilean winemakers who crossed the Andes to explore Mendoza's extraordinary potential for Malbec. Named after the wild Andean goose that migrates between Chile and Argentina, Kaiken embodies that spirit of bold ambition and cross-cultural craft. The Estate Malbec sits at the heart of the range, drawing fruit from Mendoza's high-altitude vineyards where dramatic day-to-night temperature swings preserve aromatic freshness while ripening grapes to generous depth.

In the glass, this 100% Malbec pours a striking deep violet with vivid purple hues. The nose is warm and expressive — plush dark plum and blackberry mingle with alluring notes of tobacco leaf, floral violets, and a buttery hint of butterscotch that signals thoughtful oak integration. On the palate, it delivers the full-bodied richness Mendoza Malbec is celebrated for: intense and enveloping, with fleshy, medium-grip tannins that frame a juicy, fruit-driven core. The finish is long and persistent, where fruit and wood resolve in genuine harmony.

This is the kind of wine made for the grill. Pour it alongside thick-cut Argentine-style beef ribs, a butterflied leg of lamb with chimichurri, or slow-cooked barbecue. It equally rewards a quiet evening with a well-aged hard cheese board. Accessible on release yet capable of short-term cellaring, Kaiken Estate Malbec is a reliable, crowd-pleasing bottle that never punches below its weight.

desc_en_full:
A New Lucky Charm

For as long as we can remember, an intriguing black cat has shadowed us in our vineyards. For luck, our first winemaker fastened a number-nine amulet to its collar, representing the nine mythical lives of a cat.

Since the amulet first appeared, each vintage, each wine has resulted more and more elegant, regardless of hardship or affliction. The enigmatic black cat has become our constant companion amidst the vines, somehow outliving us all.

This 9 Lives reserve is crafted using the best grapes as a tribute to our mysterious friend.
