name: Bodegas Salentein  Portillo Malbec Organic
desc_en_short: Portillo Organic Malbec from Bodegas Salentein delivers a fruit-forward Mendoza red with aromas of ripe plum and blackberry, lifted by a whisper of vanilla and supported by round, approachable tannins — an everyday organic Malbec built for easy enjoyment.
desc_en_full:
Bodegas Salentein is one of Mendoza's most respected and ambitious estates, with vineyards spread across the high-altitude Valle de Uco. The Portillo range sits at the approachable, crowd-pleasing end of their portfolio, and the Portillo Organic Malbec adds an important dimension: certified organic farming that reflects a genuine commitment to sustainable viticulture in Argentina's sun-drenched wine country.

In the glass, this 100% Malbec opens with an inviting nose of ripe plum, fresh blackberry, and a gentle touch of vanilla — the kind of uncomplicated but genuinely satisfying aromatic profile that makes Mendoza Malbec so universally loved. The palate follows through with juicy, generous fruit character, while the tannins remain sweet and round rather than grippy, making this an easy-drinking red that never feels heavy-handed. The finish is long and fruity, carrying those dark berry notes cleanly to the end.

The organic credential matters here beyond marketing: grapes grown without synthetic pesticides or fertilisers tend to express cleaner fruit character, and that freshness is evident in every sip of Portillo Organic.

This is a natural match for the grill — pour it alongside beef asado, lamb chops, or smoky barbecued ribs. It also handles a simple weeknight pasta with a rich tomato-meat sauce with ease. Serve slightly below room temperature, around 16–17 °C, to keep the fruit vivid and the tannins at their most welcoming.

desc_en_short: This organic blend of Tempranillo and Cabernet Sauvignon is a bright cherry red in colour with aromas of red berries and cherries on the nose. The palate is smooth with crunchy red fruits and a refreshing acidity. Soft tannins add structure to the wine which finishes long and fruity.
desc_en_full:
Region

The great plain of La Mancha, to the south of Madrid and east of Toledo, represents the largest viticultural area of Spain. The climate is arid, extreme in both summer and winter, tempered by strong prevailing winds. The widely planted white Arien grape variety is slowly losing favour to Tempranillo, Shiraz and Garnacha and the old wineries are giving way to gleaming modern facilities heralding a revolution for the region`s winemaking.

Producer

Dominio de Punctum is situated in the town of Las Pedroneras at the north end of La Mancha. It was founded in 2005 and run by the Fernandez family, the estate currently includes over 150 hectares of vineyards. The winery is located adjacent to the vineyards allowing to keep the maximum quality in the production of the whites, reds, young and aged wines. Since the beginning they have focused on organic and biodynamic production.
 

Tasting Notes
 
This organic blend of Tempranillo and Cabernet Sauvignon is a bright cherry red in colour with aromas of red berries and cherries on the nose. The palate is smooth with crunchy red fruits and a refreshing acidity. Soft tannins add structure to the wine which finishes long and fruity.

name: Chateau Puy Favereau Bordeaux Superieur
wine_color: Deep ruby (รูบี้เข้ม)
desc_en_short: Château Puy Favereau's Bordeaux Supérieur is a classically structured Merlot-led blend offering ripe red and dark fruit with gentle spice, silky tannins, and a balanced finish that rewards both immediate drinking and short-term cellaring.
desc_en_full:
Château Puy Favereau is a traditionally minded estate producing Bordeaux Supérieur — a designation that demands stricter ripeness and yield standards than a simple Bordeaux AOC, signalling genuine quality commitment from the producer. Situated in France's most celebrated wine region, the château draws on Bordeaux's temperate, maritime-influenced terroir to craft a blend that speaks clearly of its origins.

This wine is built around a backbone of Merlot at 50%, lending plush, round fruit character and an approachable mid-palate, while Cabernet Sauvignon at 30% contributes structure, dark fruit depth, and the tannic architecture needed for ageing potential. Cabernet Franc at 20% weaves in a lifted herbal and floral note — a subtle aromatic signature that adds complexity and length to the finish. Traditional fermentation and maturation techniques allow the fruit to express itself without overworking the wine, preserving its natural balance.

On the nose, expect ripe red cherry, blackcurrant, and plum layered with warm baking spice and a hint of cedar. The palate is smooth and medium-bodied with well-integrated tannins, lively enough acidity to keep things fresh, and a satisfying savoury depth that builds toward the finish.

This is a versatile table wine that pairs beautifully with grilled beef — a ribeye or a slow-braised short rib would be ideal — as well as herb-crusted lamb. It's equally at home at a relaxed dinner with friends or alongside a simple cheese board. Serve at around 16–18°C for best expression.

name: Maison Castel  Syrah Pays d'Oc
wine_color: Deep garnet robe with dark purple hints
desc_en_short: A generous, fruit-forward Syrah from Maison Castel in the sun-drenched Pays d'Oc, crafted to balance vibrant dark-fruit character with supple structure — an approachable yet expressive southern French red that punches well above its everyday price point.
desc_en_full:
Maison Castel is one of the Languedoc's most established négociant houses, drawing on decades of expertise across the vast Pays d'Oc IGP to source and craft varietally expressive wines that showcase the region's sun-drenched terroir. Syrah thrives in this part of southern France, where warm days and cooling Mediterranean breezes allow the grape to ripen fully while retaining enough freshness to keep the wine lively in the glass.

This Syrah delivers a classic warm-climate expression of the variety — deep ruby in colour, with an inviting nose of ripe blackberry, plum, and black cherry layered over gentle notes of cracked black pepper and subtle violet. The palate is generous and rounded, with soft tannins and a hint of smoky, savoury spice on the mid-palate that adds genuine complexity. A touch of dried herb — the garrigue of the Languedoc landscape — lingers through to a satisfying, medium-length finish.

The approachable structure makes this an ideal everyday red that requires no decanting and suits a wide range of occasions. Pour it alongside a slow-roasted lamb shoulder with herbs de Provence, a grilled ribeye steak, or a hearty beef and olive daube for a classically satisfying pairing. It also holds its own next to aged hard cheeses or a charcuterie spread on a relaxed evening. Serve slightly cool, around 16–17 °C, to keep the fruit bright and vivid.

name: Annalina  Marlborough Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: From the Babich family's Marlborough range, Annalina Pinot Noir delivers a graceful, multi-layered red brimming with raspberries, dark plum, truffle, and dried herbs, finishing long and spicy — elegant proof that Marlborough does far more than Sauvignon Blanc.
desc_en_full:
Annalina is the Marlborough-focused label crafted by the Babich Family, one of New Zealand's most storied winemaking dynasties, whose roots in the country stretch back over a century. The name itself — meaning graceful light — was chosen deliberately, and it captures everything this wine aspires to be: poised, luminous, and quietly confident.

Marlborough's Pinot Noir has long lived in the shadow of the region's world-famous Sauvignon Blanc, yet the combination of long, cool growing days, free-draining alluvial soils, and sharp diurnal temperature swings produces a red of genuine complexity. Annalina is a compelling argument for giving it serious attention.

In the glass, the wine opens with an inviting perfume of fresh raspberries, cinnamon, and a whisper of wood smoke, before deeper layers of dark plum, dried herbs, and earthy truffle begin to emerge. On the palate it is characteristically light-framed and silky — the hallmark of cool-climate Pinot Noir — with vibrant acidity that carries the fruit cleanly to a lingering, gently spiced finish.

This is a bottle made for the table and for company. Pour it alongside roast chicken thighs with herbs, a pan-seared duck breast, or a good-quality beef burger. It also shines alongside mushroom risotto for those keeping it plant-forward. Serve slightly cool — around 14–16 °C — to keep that freshness singing.

name: Of Stone + Gold  Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: Of Stone + Gold Pinot Noir delivers a fresh, vibrant expression of the variety, layering dark cherry and plum fruit with earthy mushroom, forest floor, and spice complexity — all wrapped in silky soft tannins and a lingering finish.
desc_en_full:
Of Stone + Gold is a South Australian label crafted for those who appreciate Pinot Noir that leans into both its bright fruit personality and its more contemplative, earthy side. While South Australia is more commonly associated with bold Shiraz and Cabernet, this Pinot Noir demonstrates that the region's winemakers are adept at coaxing elegance and nuance from this notoriously demanding grape variety.

In the glass, this wine opens with an inviting garnet hue and a nose that wastes no time announcing itself — dark cherries and ripe plum burst forward, framed by a more introspective layer of dried mushroom, forest floor, and a whisper of warming spice. It's a classic Pinot Noir duality: fruit-forward yet grounded in the earth.

On the palate, soft, well-integrated tannins carry the wine effortlessly from entry to a finish that lingers well beyond expectations. Medium acidity keeps everything lively and food-friendly, while the savoury undertones add the kind of complexity that rewards a slow sip rather than a hasty pour.

This is a versatile table wine that earns its place alongside duck confit, roasted pork loin, or a generous board of ripe brie and camembert. It also makes a confident choice for casual gatherings where the conversation matters as much as the glass. Serve slightly chilled at around 15–16°C to let its layers fully unfold.

name: Maison Castel  Cabernet Sauvignon Pays d'Oc
desc_en_short: Maison Castel's Cabernet Sauvignon Pays d'Oc is a classically structured French red that balances ripe blackcurrant fruit with gentle herb notes and a polished finish — an approachable yet characterful expression of one of the south of France's most versatile IGP appellations.
desc_en_full:
Maison Castel is one of France's most established négociant houses, with deep roots in the Languedoc and a consistent track record of translating the south's sun-drenched terroirs into wines that feel genuinely French in their balance and restraint. This Cabernet Sauvignon is sourced from the Pays d'Oc PGI — a broad, geographically diverse designation stretching across the Aude, Hérault, and Gard departments — giving the winemaking team access to a mosaic of microclimates and soil types that add genuine complexity to the finished wine.

The hillsides and inland plateaux of the Hérault contribute freshness and structural backbone, while the foothills lend a firm, focused character and the influence of the Mediterranean coastline softens the whole into something immediately approachable. The result is a Cabernet Sauvignon that stays true to the variety — think ripe blackcurrant, a whisper of cassis, and that signature green-herb edge — without sacrificing the easy-drinking quality that makes this style so versatile at the table.

On the palate, expect medium-weight red fruit, subtle cedar and dried herbs, and a clean, moderately tannic finish that never overpowers. It's the kind of bottle that works equally well poured mid-week alongside a grilled lamb cutlet or a slow-braised beef ragu, yet holds its own at a weekend dinner table. Serve at around 16–18 °C for best results.

desc_en_full:
WINEMAKING NOTES

Following harvest, the grapes from each vineyard were sorted and destemmed, then fermented in individual lots. As the lots are aged in oak for 16 months, the barrels are tasted frequently to assess tannin and flavor development. Finally, the lots were blended together to balance the characteristics of each growing region.

name: Church Road  Grand Reserve Merlot Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short:
A core of dark berry fruit, cherry and plum is lifted by top notes of subtle spice, violet, rosewood and cedar. A savoury, dark, earthy complexity brings a more serious, brooding undertone to the wine.

On the palate the wine is full-bodied with good mid-palate flesh.
desc_en_full:
PROVENANCE

The Merlot Cabernet Sauvignon blend is one of the most challenging and exciting wine styles to produce. There is a synergy between Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot that, when blended sensitively, produces arguably the most complete, complex, fragrant and satisfying style of red wine in the world. The warm climates and light, stony soils of the Bridge Pa Triangle and Gimblett Gravels Winegrowing District produce low volume crops of concentrated, fully ripe, fragrant fruit that makes for classically structured wines with power, elegance, fragrance and finesse.

name: Luigi Bosca  De Sangre Malbec
desc_en_short: Luigi Bosca's De Sangre Malbec is a richly expressive Mendoza red bursting with ripe cherry, warming spice, and coffee-laced oak — full-bodied and vigorously structured with firm tannins and a long, compact finish that showcases everything Malbec does best.
desc_en_full:
Luigi Bosca is one of Mendoza's most storied family estates, with over a century of winemaking heritage rooted in the Luján de Cuyo heartland. The De Sangre range — whose name evokes something deeply in the blood — represents the house's dedication to expressing the raw power and generosity that 100% Malbec draws from Argentina's high-altitude terroir.

In the glass, De Sangre Malbec opens with an inviting bouquet centred on ripe cherry and red plum, layered with warming spice and subtle roasted coffee notes that speak to careful oak aging. The integration of wood is seamless — enhancing complexity without masking the vivid fruit character that defines great Mendoza Malbec.

On the palate, the wine is robust and mouth-filling, delivering waves of ripe red fruit alongside a gentle spiced edge. Firm yet polished tannins provide backbone and structure, while balanced acidity keeps the whole composition fresh and lively. The finish is long, compact, and satisfying — the hallmark of a wine crafted with real intention.

This is a bottle made for the table. Pour it alongside a slow-grilled Argentine-style ribeye or a rack of lamb with herb crust, and it will shine brilliantly. Earthy wild mushroom dishes and aged hard cheeses — think Manchego or Parmigiano — are equally rewarding pairings. Decant for 30 minutes to let the tannins fully open up and the aromatic complexity unfold.

name: Luigi Bosca  Los Nobles Malbec
wine_color: A deep violet red wine with ruby hues.
desc_en_short: Luigi Bosca Los Nobles Malbec is a richly expressive Mendoza red blending 94% Malbec with a touch of Petit Verdot, delivering layered red and black fruit, sweet spice, and delicate florals with velvety tannins and a long, complex finish.
desc_en_full:
Luigi Bosca is one of Mendoza's most storied estates, with over a century of winemaking heritage rooted in the Luján de Cuyo area — a region long regarded as the spiritual home of Argentine Malbec. The Los Nobles range draws on the winery's deep understanding of high-altitude viticulture, crafting wines that reflect both varietal character and the nuanced terroir of the Andes foothills.

This bottling blends 94% Malbec with 6% Petit Verdot, a thoughtful addition that lifts the aromatic complexity and adds a subtle structural edge without overwhelming Malbec's signature approachability. The result is a wine that feels both polished and characterful — expressive aromas of ripe red cherry, blackcurrant, and wild blueberry weave together with sweet spice, violet florals, and a gentle whisper of oak-derived smokiness from careful barrel ageing.

On the palate, Los Nobles is voluptuous yet balanced, offering medium-bodied richness anchored by fine, silky tannins and lively freshness. The fruit and spice layers carry through to a clean, lingering finish that hints at genuine depth and complexity.

This is a natural companion to grilled Argentine-style beef, slow-braised lamb shoulder, or a charcuterie board of cured meats and aged cheeses. It also shines at a relaxed dinner party where a crowd-pleasing, well-made Malbec is exactly what the evening calls for.

name: Domaine Jaume  Cotes-Du-Rhone Rouge AOP
desc_en_short:
This red blend offers vivid red fruit, soft tannins, and a touch of spice, making it a perfect everyday wine. Notes of cherry, blackcurrant, herbs, and black pepper invite easy pairing and enjoyable sipping.

Perfect for grilled meats, pasta with ragu, or a cheese board, it’s also an ideal gateway for those new to French reds looking for something easygoing yet authentic.
desc_en_full:
Domaine Jaume, founded in 1826, is a respected name in the Southern Rhône, especially in Vinsobres, where the family continues to craft wines that reflect the region’s balance of sunshine, structure, and spice.

Their Côtes-du-Rhône Rouge is a classic GSM blend (Grenache, Syrah, Mourvèdre) from vineyards rooted in sandy, stony soils. The Mediterranean climate brings ripeness and aromatic charm, resulting in a wine that is approachable, versatile, and full of Rhône character.

name: Chateau Tour Bellevue Haut-Medoc
desc_en_short: Château Tour Bellevue is a structured Haut-Médoc red blending Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon from the gravelly Left Bank, delivering dark fruit concentration, polished tannins, and layered complexity of spice, oak, and earthy minerality.
desc_en_full:
Château Tour Bellevue is situated within the Haut-Médoc appellation, one of the most consistently celebrated subregions of Bordeaux's Left Bank. Neighbouring prestigious Grand Cru Classé estates, the château benefits from the Médoc's signature well-drained gravelly soils — terrain that naturally stresses the vine, concentrating flavour and building the structural backbone for which this corner of Bordeaux is world-renowned.

The blend of Merlot (55%) and Cabernet Sauvignon (45%) strikes a confident Left Bank balance: Cabernet Sauvignon supplies the firm framework and longevity, while Merlot lends a plush, generous mid-palate that rounds out the wine's considerable depth. Careful vineyard management and harvesting at optimal ripeness allow both varieties to express themselves fully without sacrificing freshness.

In the glass, expect a deep garnet hue and an inviting nose of blackcurrant, dark plum, and bramble, underscored by notes of cedar, warming spice, and subtle earthiness. On the palate, concentrated dark fruit unfolds with layers of graphite minerality and toasty oak, supported by polished, medium-weight tannins and a clean, persistent finish.

Château Tour Bellevue is an ideal companion for a classic Sunday roast of Bordeaux-braised lamb shoulder or a well-aged côte de bœuf. Its structure also makes it a rewarding cellar candidate — decant young bottles an hour before serving to unlock the full aromatic complexity this Haut-Médoc has to offer.

name: Francis Coppola  'Diamond Collection' Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: Francis Coppola's Diamond Collection Cabernet Sauvignon blends fruit from multiple California appellations — including Sonoma, Paso Robles, and Monterey County — to deliver a fruit-forward, multi-dimensional red with supple tannins, dark berry richness, and a touch of spice that's ready to enjoy the moment it's opened.
desc_en_full:
Francis Ford Coppola may be best known for his cinematic masterpieces, but his Sonoma-based winery has built an equally compelling legacy in California wine. The Diamond Collection range sits at the heart of that story — a lineup designed to showcase the best of what multi-appellation blending can achieve, and the Cabernet Sauvignon is its standout ambassador.

Rather than tethering the wine to a single terroir, the winemaking team sources Cabernet Sauvignon fruit across contrasting California growing regions, allowing each appellation to contribute something distinct. Paso Robles lends generous dark fruit, warming spice, and earthy mineral depth; Sonoma brings natural acidity and structural finesse; while Monterey County adds richness and mid-palate concentration. The result is a Cabernet that feels greater than the sum of its parts — layered and complex without ever becoming austere.

In the glass, expect a deep ruby-garnet colour followed by aromas of blackcurrant, dark cherry, and ripe plum, threaded with hints of cedar, baking spice, and a whisper of cocoa. On the palate, the fruit is plush and confident — blackberries and cassis lead the way — framed by medium-weight, polished tannins and a finish that lingers with subtle oak and warm earth. The tannin structure here is deliberately approachable, making this a Cabernet that doesn't demand years of cellaring.

Pour it alongside a grilled ribeye, slow-braised lamb shoulder, or a well-aged hard cheese for a genuinely satisfying California red wine experience — any night of the week.

name: Idi Di Marzo  Vulgaris Montepulciano D'Abruzzo DOC
wine_color: Ruby ​​red color, enriched by purple hues.
desc_en_short: Idi di Marzo's Vulgaris Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is a characterful, full-bodied red from Italy's sun-drenched Abruzzo hills, delivering deep ruby colour, generous berry and currant aromas, and a well-structured palate with balanced tannins and a satisfying, lingering finish.
desc_en_full:
Idi di Marzo is a producer rooted in the rugged, sun-baked hillside vineyards of Abruzzo, a region on Italy's central Adriatic coast long celebrated for coaxing extraordinary depth from the native Montepulciano grape. The Vulgaris — the name a nod to the everyday, unpretentious character of the wine — is anything but ordinary. It is crafted from 100% Montepulciano d'Abruzzo, a thick-skinned variety renowned for its inky colour, generous fruit, and naturally sturdy structure.

In the glass, the wine pours a striking deep ruby red shot through with vivid purple hues, a visual testament to the careful canopy management and low-yield viticulture practised in the vineyard. The nose opens with inviting aromas of ripe blackberries, dark cherries, and cassis, with a subtle earthy undercurrent that speaks to Abruzzo's clay-rich soils. On the palate, the wine is dry and full-bodied, with medium tannins that are ripe and well-integrated rather than grippy, framed by medium acidity that keeps everything fresh and lively. The finish is persistent, leaving a warm, fruit-forward impression.

Vulgaris is an ideal companion for a generous Sunday lunch — think slow-roasted lamb shoulder seasoned with rosemary and garlic, a thick-cut ribeye, or a rustic board of aged Pecorino and Parmigiano-Reggiano. Its structure also makes it a natural match for hearty pasta dishes such as a rich Bolognese or lamb ragù. A reliable, honest expression of one of southern Italy's most food-friendly appellations.

name: Pajaru Nero Di Troia Auru IGT - Organic
desc_en_short: From the sun-scorched heel of Italy's boot, Pajaru's Nero di Troia Auru is a compelling organic expression of Puglia's most characterful indigenous red grape — rich with ripe plum, dark chocolate, and cocoa in a full-bodied, organically farmed package built for the table.
desc_en_full:
Nero di Troia is one of southern Italy's great forgotten grapes, and Puglia-based producer Pajaru is doing quietly serious work to remind the world of its potential. The Auru IGT is crafted from organically grown Nero di Troia vines rooted in the limestone-rich soils of inland Puglia, where the intense Mediterranean sun concentrates flavour and the region's breezy nights preserve freshness and structure.

The wine pours a deep, intense ruby red — almost opaque at its core — and announces itself with an expressive nose of ripe plum, dark cherry, and subtle dried fruit. With air, tertiary notes of dark chocolate, cocoa powder, and a faint hint of Mediterranean scrubland begin to emerge, adding complexity and a distinctly Puglian sense of place. On the palate, the fruit weight is generous and mouth-filling, underpinned by medium tannins and a pleasing savoury backbone that keeps everything in balance through to a lingering finish.

Organic farming brings an extra layer of authenticity to this bottle — the grapes arrive at the winery expressing the honest character of the land rather than interventional viticulture. This is a wine made for the table. Pour it alongside a slow-braised beef ragù, a board of aged Pecorino or Caciocavallo, or a generous spread of Puglian cured meats such as capocollo or soppressata. It also holds its own beside grilled lamb and hearty pasta al forno.

name: T' Spot  Bordeaux Red
desc_en_short: T' Spot Bordeaux Rouge is an approachable, fruit-forward Bordeaux blend led by Merlot, delivering ripe cherry, raspberry, and plum with a touch of vanilla — classic Left Bank charm with smooth, well-integrated tannins and an easy-drinking finish.
desc_en_full:
T' Spot Bordeaux Rouge offers a welcoming entry point into the world of classic French red wine, blending the pillars of the Bordeaux tradition into a bottle that over-delivers for its style. Anchored by 80% Merlot — the grape responsible for the region's most approachable, plush expressions — the wine is rounded out by 15% Cabernet Franc, which lends subtle herbaceous lift and red-fruit vibrancy, and a final 5% Cabernet Sauvignon for structure and depth.

In the glass, T' Spot pours a bright ruby red. The nose opens with generous ripe red berries — raspberry and dark cherry most prominent — lifted by a gentle whisper of vanilla that hints at careful winemaking. On the palate, the Merlot-dominant blend delivers what Bordeaux does best: layered fruit expression spanning fresh raspberry and cherry through to darker blackberry and plum, all framed by medium-bodied elegance and the hallmark balance that has made this appellation the benchmark for red wine the world over. Tannins are firm but beautifully melted, never grippy, making this wine remarkably easy to enjoy young without sacrificing complexity.

T' Spot Bordeaux is the kind of bottle to reach for on a weeknight without hesitation — equally at home poured alongside a slow-braised lamb shank or a simple grilled ribeye steak. It also makes an excellent introduction to Bordeaux-style blends for guests discovering French red wine for the first time. Serve at 16–18°C to let its fruit-forward character fully shine.

name: Laura Hartwig Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: A single-vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Cachapoal Valley, Laura Hartwig delivers deep cassis and red fruit aromas layered with tobacco, dark chocolate, and elegant spice — all framed by a silky, full-bodied structure that speaks to the precision of this boutique estate.
desc_en_full:
Nestled in Chile's Cachapoal Valley, Laura Hartwig is one of the Colchagua and broader O'Higgins region's most respected boutique producers, known for crafting wines that express the character of specific parcels rather than blended typicity. This Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon is a focused expression of that philosophy — 100% Cabernet Sauvignon drawn from a single site, where warm days and cool Andean nights allow the variety to develop concentration without sacrificing finesse.

In the glass, the wine opens with a deep ruby-red color and striking violet hues, a visual cue to its youthful intensity. The nose is immediately complex: ripe cassis and red berry fruit lead the way, followed by layers of tobacco leaf, dark chocolate, and a floral lift that adds elegance to what could otherwise be a heavier profile. Subtle oak spice weaves through the aromatics with admirable restraint.

On the palate, this Cab delivers the full-bodied richness the Cachapoal Valley is celebrated for, yet it moves with surprising grace. Tannins are medium in weight but beautifully silky in texture, and medium-bright acidity keeps the finish fresh and persistent. It's a wine built for the table as much as the cellar.

Ideal alongside a grass-fed Argentine-style asado, a slow-braised lamb shoulder with rosemary, or a well-aged hard cheese. For those who love structured reds with real complexity, this is a bottle that consistently over-delivers for its tier.

name: Terre Di Rai 931 Vino Rosso
desc_en_short: A characterful Veneto red blending Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and the native Raboso grape, Terre Di Rai 931 delivers vivid aromas of cherry and sour cherry with balsamic undertones, and a soft, well-balanced palate of blackberry and plum framed by gentle tannins.
desc_en_full:
Terre Di Rai is a producer rooted in the northeastern Italian region of Veneto, a land celebrated for its viticultural diversity and its embrace of both international varieties and indigenous grapes. The 931 Vino Rosso is a compelling expression of that philosophy, blending the internationally familiar Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot with Raboso — a fiercely local Veneto variety prized for its natural acidity, deep colour, and rustic character. The inclusion of Raboso is what sets this blend apart, lending it a distinctly northeastern Italian personality that no purely international blend can replicate.

On the nose, the wine opens with an inviting intensity: bright cherry and sour cherry dominate, lifted by subtle balsamic notes that hint at herbal complexity — a hallmark of Raboso's contribution. The palate is generous and approachable, offering blackberry and plum fruit that feels soft and round, supported by medium tannins and balanced acidity that keep the finish clean and well-structured without any harshness.

This is a versatile everyday red that performs beautifully at the dinner table. Pour it alongside a slow-braised beef short rib, a hearty lamb ragù over pappardelle, or a simple grilled bistecca to let the fruit-forward character shine. It is equally at home at a relaxed weekend gathering, served slightly cool at around 16–17°C to preserve its freshness. Honest, expressive, and distinctly Venetian in spirit.

name: Montes Alpha Cabernet Sauvignon Year Of The Snake
wine_color: Deep ruby (รูบี้เข้ม)
desc_en_short: A collector's edition of Chile's most iconic Cabernet Sauvignon, this Year of the Snake release from Montes Alpha brings the brooding power of Maipo Valley to a beautifully commemorative bottle — dark fruit, spice, and structure in perfect balance.
desc_en_full:
Few Chilean wineries have shaped the country's international reputation as decisively as Montes. Founded in 1988 by winemaker Aurelio Montes Sr., the estate helped pioneer premium viticulture in Chile, and the Montes Alpha Cabernet Sauvignon remains its calling card — a wine that has consistently set the benchmark for what Maipo Valley Cabernet can achieve.

This special Year of the Snake edition wraps that benchmark wine in a limited commemorative label, making it as compelling on a dinner table as it is in a gift box. The grapes are sourced from Maipo Valley, the heartland of Chilean Cabernet Sauvignon, where Andean-influenced soils and warm days tempered by cool nights concentrate flavour while preserving natural acidity. The wine spends time in French and American oak barrels, adding layers of vanilla, cedar, and mocha to the fruit-driven core.

In the glass, expect a deep ruby-garnet colour followed by aromas of blackcurrant, black cherry, and dark plum, interwoven with hints of tobacco leaf, dried herbs, and baking spice. The palate is full-bodied and confident, with firm but polished tannins that give the wine excellent structure and genuine ageing potential — drink now or cellar for five to eight years to watch the profile deepen further.

Pour this alongside a slow-roasted rack of lamb, a wood-fired ribeye, or an aged hard cheese for a pairing that does justice to the wine's Chilean terroir and Montes's decades of winemaking expertise. A truly memorable gift for the Year of the Snake.

name: San Marzano Crus Collezione Cinquanta 50 Vino Rosso D’Italia (Red Blend)
wine_color: Ruby red colour with purple reflections
desc_en_short:
Ruby red colour with purple reflections; an intense and complex bouquet with fruity notes of prune and red jam, and a spicy scent of vanilla and licorice.
Intense on the palate, full-bodied and soft with a long aftertaste
desc_en_full:
A Celebration in Every Bottle “Collezione Cinquanta” was created to honor San Marzano’s 50th anniversary. Not bound to a specific vintage, it gives winemakers the freedom to craft a blend that best expresses a style of generosity and intensity.

The wine is a marriage of old-vine southern Italian grapes, primarily Primitivo and Negroamaro, sourced from sun-drenched vineyards. Maturation in oak enhances the structure and adds aromatic complexity.

With its balance of fruit richness and approachable elegance, Collezione Cinquanta is versatile. It embodies Puglia’s warmth, generosity, and vibrant winemaking heritage.

name: Montecillo Crianza Rioja
wine_color: The wine displays a bright cherry red colour with indigo highlights.
desc_en_short: Bodegas Montecillo's Crianza from Rioja blends 88% Tempranillo with 12% Garnacha for a classic Spanish red — ripe cherry and plum fruit lifted by gentle spice and well-integrated oak, delivering elegance and approachability in every glass.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in Rioja as Bodegas Montecillo. Founded in 1874, this historic estate has spent nearly a century and a half mastering the art of traditional Riojan winemaking — all while adopting modern precision in the cellar to keep its wines fresh and relevant. The Montecillo Crianza is a textbook expression of what made Rioja famous in the first place.

The blend of 88% Tempranillo and 12% Garnacha is a time-honoured Riojan pairing. Tempranillo provides the backbone — structured, aromatic, and deeply fruited — while Garnacha rounds the palate with warmth and suppleness. Rioja's unique continental climate, caught between Atlantic cool and Mediterranean warmth, allows both varieties to ripen fully without losing the acidity that keeps the wine lively at the table.

As a Crianza, this wine earns its classification through mandatory oak barrel ageing followed by bottle rest before release. The result is evident in every sip: aromas of ripe red cherry, dried plum, and bramble are threaded through with vanilla, sweet spice, and a whisper of toasty oak — complexity without weight. On the palate it is medium-bodied and smooth, with polished tannins and a satisfying, spice-dusted finish.

Uncork this alongside slow-roasted lamb shoulder, a hearty beef stew, or pork ribs glazed with paprika. It handles roasted duck and herb-crusted chicken with equal confidence — an all-purpose Spanish red that earns its place at any dinner table.

name: Il Bastardo Sangiovese di Toscana
desc_en_short: A spirited, everyday Tuscan red from the Masi family, Il Bastardo delivers classic Sangiovese character — ripe cherry, bright acidity, and a smooth finish — with an unpretentious, approachable style that's made for sharing around the table.
desc_en_full:
The name may provoke a smile, but Il Bastardo Sangiovese di Toscana is entirely serious about delivering honest, characterful wine from one of Italy's most celebrated regions. Produced by the Masi family — three generations deep in Tuscan winemaking — this is a bottle that wears its origins proudly without demanding ceremony in return.

Crafted from 100% Sangiovese, Tuscany's defining red grape, the wine is fermented in stainless steel to keep things clean, vibrant, and fruit-forward. The result is a medium-bodied red that leads with juicy red cherry and ripe plum, layered with subtle hints of dried herbs and a whisper of dried violet. Medium acidity gives it that characteristic Sangiovese brightness and lift, while soft, well-integrated tannins carry the wine to a smooth, refreshing finish.

This isn't trying to be a Brunello or a Chianti Classico Riserva — and that's precisely the point. Il Bastardo sits squarely in the tradition of Sangiovese di Toscana IGT, a category built for approachability and everyday pleasure. Pour it slightly cool alongside a bowl of pasta al ragù, a plate of aged Pecorino, or a simple Florentine-style grilled lamb chop. It also holds its own against a robust beef stew or a charcuterie spread on a relaxed weeknight.

Unpretentious, food-friendly, and rooted in genuine Tuscan tradition — Il Bastardo is the kind of wine that earns its place on the regular rotation.

name: Finca Fella Altado Monastrell Tinto
desc_en_short: From bush-trained vines at over 1,000 metres in Alpera, southeastern Spain, Finca Fella's Altado Monastrell delivers the brooding intensity and rustic elegance that defines high-altitude Monastrell — bold, sun-ripened fruit balanced by cool-night freshness and a genuine sense of place.
desc_en_full:
Finca Fella's Altado Monastrell Tinto is a compelling portrait of southeastern Spain at its most honest and elemental. The estate's vineyards sit at over 1,000 metres elevation in Alpera, a rugged corner of Albacete where ancient bush-trained vines grip rocky, mineral-rich soils and the climate swings between fierce daytime heat and restorative cool nights — the ideal rhythm for slow, balanced ripening of Monastrell.

Known to the wider wine world as Mourvèdre, Monastrell is a grape of serious character: thick-skinned, sun-loving, and capable of tremendous depth when treated with respect. Finca Fella applies gentle extraction and controlled fermentation to honour varietal purity, then finishes with a short maturation that smooths the edges without muting the variety's earthy, brooding soul.

In the glass, Altado Monastrell shows a deep, inky ruby. The nose opens with dark plum, black cherry, and dried herbs layered over notes of scorched earth, cracked black pepper, and a whisper of iron-rich minerality. The palate is full-bodied yet balanced, with medium-grip tannins and warm, concentrated fruit that finishes long and satisfying.

This is a red built for the table — pour it alongside slow-braised lamb shoulder, a charcoal-grilled ribeye, or a hearty stew of chickpeas and chorizo. Serve at around 17–18 °C to let its character fully unfold.

name: Comte Desclos Bordeaux
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot And Cabernet Franc.
desc_en_short: Comte Desclos is a classic Bordeaux red blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Cabernet Franc, offering aromas of red currant and cherry alongside a well-balanced palate of red berry, leather, and spice — an approachable, everyday expression of traditional Bordeaux character.
desc_en_full:
Comte Desclos is an entry into the world of classic Bordeaux blending, produced to showcase the hallmark character of France's most celebrated red wine region. Drawing on the region's three cornerstone varieties — Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Cabernet Franc — this wine is a reliable, food-friendly expression of everyday Bordeaux at its most straightforward and satisfying.

In the glass, it pours a deep, vibrant crimson. The nose opens with inviting forward fruit: red currant, ripe cherry, and a whisper of dried herbs characteristic of the Bordeaux appellation. As it opens up, subtle earthy undertones and a gentle spice lift add complexity beyond its modest price point.

On the palate, Comte Desclos delivers a well-structured, medium-bodied experience with medium acidity keeping things fresh and lively. Flavours of red berry, leather, and warming spice play out across a smooth mid-palate, while medium tannins lend just enough grip to make this a natural match for the dining table rather than solo sipping.

This is a versatile companion for weeknight meals — pour it alongside a grilled ribeye, slow-roasted lamb shoulder, or a simple charcuterie board. Its balanced profile also makes it a dependable choice for casual entertaining, delivering classic Bordeaux personality without demanding special occasion treatment.

name: Viticcio Prunaio Gran Selezione Chianti Classico DOCG
wine_color: The wine is a dense ruby red in color
desc_en_short: Viticcio's Prunaio Gran Selezione is a single-estate Chianti Classico DOCG built from hand-selected Sangiovese clones, weaving ripe plum and floral iris into a wine of striking elegance, concentration, and a beautifully long finish.
desc_en_full:
Nestled in the heart of the Chianti Classico zone between Florence and Siena, Viticcio has long been one of the region's quietly serious estates — meticulous in the vineyard, thoughtful in the cellar, and unafraid to push boundaries. The Prunaio Gran Selezione sits at the very top of the Chianti Classico DOCG pyramid, the category reserved for a producer's finest single-vineyard or best-selection wines with extended aging requirements, and Viticcio delivers something genuinely distinctive here.

What makes Prunaio stand apart is the foundation: 100% Sangiovese vines propagated from clones personally selected by the estate, ensuring a depth of character that mass-produced clonal material simply cannot match. Yet the winemaking philosophy carries a Super Tuscan sensibility — precise, modern, and keenly attentive to texture and harmony — rather than the rustic austerity of older-school Chianti.

In the glass, Prunaio opens with captivating aromas of ripe plum, dried cherry, and violet iris, supported by earthy nuances of dark soil and a whisper of spice. On the palate, the wine is structured yet silky, with bright acidity keeping the concentrated dark fruit lively and fresh. The tannins are polished and well-integrated, building to an exceptionally long, elegant finish that rewards patience in the glass.

Approachable now alongside a Florentine bistecca, slow-braised lamb shank, or aged Pecorino Toscano, Prunaio also has the architecture to cellar for a further five to ten years, gaining complexity with every vintage. This is Chianti Classico at its most ambitious and most refined.

name: Kings of Prohibition  Shiraz
desc_en_short: Kings of Prohibition Shiraz is a bold, fruit-forward Australian red with ripe dark berries, warming spice, and a smooth, generous texture — crafted for modern drinkers who want intensity without pretension.
desc_en_full:
Named after an era defined by defiance and character, Kings of Prohibition channels that rebellious spirit into a Shiraz built for maximum enjoyment with minimum fuss. The label draws on the mystique of the 1920s underground scene — confident, expressive, and unafraid to break convention — and that ethos carries straight through to the glass.

Sourced from vineyards spread across South Eastern Australia, the winemaking team prioritises vibrant, ripe fruit over heavy extraction or excessive oak influence. This multi-regional approach gives the team flexibility to select parcels that deliver the richest expression of Shiraz each vintage — a smart strategy that keeps the wine consistent and crowd-pleasing year after year.

On the palate, expect a generous wave of blackberry, dark plum, and blueberry, underpinned by a seductive thread of cracked black pepper and sweet warming spice. A lick of toasty oak adds complexity without dominating, while the medium tannins and smooth, rounded finish make this remarkably easy to return to glass after glass.

This is a Shiraz that earns its place at the table — literally. Pull the cork alongside a slow-cooked lamb shoulder, a char-grilled ribeye, or a weekend barbecue spread. It handles smoky, charred flavours with ease and has enough body to stand up to bold seasonings and rich sauces. Equally at home on a Friday-night sofa as it is at a dinner party centrepiece.

name: Xanadu Circa77 Cabernet Sauvignon
wine_color: Deep ruby with dark garnet hues.
desc_en_short: Xanadu Circa 77 is a classically expressive Margaret River Cabernet Sauvignon, delivering concentrated cassis and dark fruit with fine, polished tannins and carefully integrated oak — structured yet approachable, with the poise to age beautifully.
desc_en_full:
Xanadu has been one of Margaret River's benchmark estates since the late 1970s, and the Circa 77 label is a direct nod to that founding era — a name that carries both historical pride and a commitment to contemporary precision. Where some producer ranges chase volume, Circa 77 is positioned as an accessible expression of everything that makes Margaret River Cabernet Sauvignon so internationally compelling.

Margaret River's reputation for Cabernet Sauvignon is no accident. The region's maritime-influenced climate — moderated by the Indian Ocean — delivers a long, even growing season that allows Cabernet's thick-skinned berries to ripen slowly and completely. The result is grapes with genuine depth of black fruit character, natural acidity, and structural tannins that never feel forced. Gravelly, free-draining soils add a pleasing mineral backbone that lifts the wine and extends its finish.

In the glass, Circa 77 opens with classic Margaret River Cabernet aromatics: blackcurrant cassis, a whisper of dark plum, and dried herb notes typical of the variety at this latitude. Careful oak maturation adds subtle cedar and spice complexity without smothering the fruit. On the palate, medium-bodied and well-balanced, it feels polished and confident — fine-grained tannins provide structure without austerity, and the finish lingers with earthy, mineral length.

An ideal companion to a slow-roasted rack of lamb or a herb-crusted beef fillet, this is also a wine that rewards patience — open a bottle tonight or cellar it for a few years to watch those tannins integrate further. Perfect for a midweek dinner upgrade or a relaxed weekend table.

desc_en_full:
A Romantic champagne

The production secrets and the vinification method of this cuvée go back to the origins of the House of Billecart-Salmon and have been handed down for seven generations. The Brut Rose is a blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Meunier and Pinot Noir vinified as red wine.

 

Appearance : A pale yet radiant pink colour adorned with warm glints of gold.

Aroma : This cuvée unveils a subtle aroma leading to an elegant, delicate bouquet of fine notes of red berries and zest of citrus fruits.

Palate : Its special method of vinification gives this cuvée a light, elegant flavour, followed by a fresh finish with a taste of raspberry.

Serving : This rosé champagne is an ideal partner as an aperitif and can also accompany a dish of wild salmon or sushi. For dessert, it will add a sublime touch to red fruit flavours.

desc_en_full:
Tasting notes :

The house style at Deutz inclines towards the more elegant end of the spectrum as opposed to the rich and robust. The nose of the Brut Classic is definitely in keeping with the aforementioned profile; the fruit characteristics including peach, apricot and lemon zest, whilst the palate is full of biscuit and honey which, when combined with the exceptionally fine mousse, gives the wine great balance and poise.

Property :

Deutz are one of the grandest Champagne houses in the village of Ay, where they are near neighbours to Bollinger. Founded in 1838 and now under the discrete ownership of Maison Louis Roederer, their finely-crafted range is currently inspiring great critical acclaim, from their outstanding Classic range all the way up to the rare deluxe cuvées, William Deutz and the sublime Blanc de Blancs, Amour de Deutz, which is only released in the very best years. Along with Charles Heidsieck, Deutz is making the most exciting range of any of the Grandes Marques at the moment.

This fine champagne is a blend of 40 carefully selected crus from the Champagne region. It is made up of three grape varieties: Pinot Noir, Pinot Meunier, and about one-third Chardonnay. This subtle blend is then aged in the Pommery cellars for about three years in order to develop its full aroma. Fresh and vivacious, with a supple, fruity finish. Pommery is one of the top Champagne houses, producing over 500,000 cases of Champagne a year. Located right in the town of Reims, it is certainly one of the most beautiful champagne wine estates to visit with ancient Roman caves, art nouveau tasting rooms and a never-ending magnificent staircase down into the chalk caves.

name: Villa Conchi  Brut Seleccion
wine_color: Brilliant green and gold color. Elegant with fine bubbles.
desc_en_short: Brilliant green and gold color. Elegant with fine bubbles. Good intensiy with fruits aromas and toasted nuances. Fresh, warm, pleasant and round, with a long finish. Perfect as an aperitif as well as an accompaniment for salads, rice and light fish dishes.
desc_en_full:
With the aim of paying homage to Conchi, a woman who loved French sparkling wines, our Cava is a tribute to women around the world.

A careful selection of vineyards in the heart of Catalunya, protected by the mountain of Montserrat, has been the main focus for the production of Villa Conchi. Our Ca-vas aim to present a fresher style from a Mediterranean climate: the altitude of the plots has the crucial advantage of cooler temperatures during the night which help us obtain grapes with a pleasant acidity. And the advantage of using the Chardonnay variety in the blend brings us more fruity and elegant notes.

Villa Conchi is full of pleasures and emotions to share in good company. It is certainly the best way to start enjoying the fine "Tapas culture"

name: Pol Roger  Brut Reserve
wine_color: Greeny-gold in color with a fine mousse
desc_en_short:
Pol Roger Champagne Reserve Brut

Pol Roger Non-Vintage has long been instantly recognisable not only for the classic script of the label, but for the distinctive white foil closure which forms its "Trademark"; hennce the popular sobriquet "White Foil"

The style is versatile, suited to most "champagne occasions". "White Foil" can be enjoyed as an aperitif, as a complement to a meal, or at any time as the elixir of celebration.

Grape Valiety :

The Brut Réserve is a blend in equal parts of the three champenois grape varieties: pinot noir, pinot meunier and chardonnay from 30 different crus. 25% reserve wines are used to produce this cuvée: - The Pinot Noir (bringing structure, body and power) is mainly produced from some of the best crus of the Montagne de Reims. - The Pinot Meunier (ensuring freshness, roundness and fruitiness) is issued from several crus from the Vallée de la Marne and from the Epernay area. - The Chardonnay (adding its aromatic complexity, finesse, elegance and lightness) comes from Epernay and some of the best crus of the Côte des Blancs. After four years ageing in our cellars, its style combines complexity, balance and distinction.
desc_en_full:
91 points Decanter

"Pol is always a great Champagne to serve to your guests: a classy, balanced expression of the three grape varieties allowed by law. It has plenty of rich apple fruit along with a streak of lemon zest and just a hint of juicy tangerine. The palate is round but fresh, and has a long, steely finish which leaves you wanting another sip."

wine_color: Brilliant, straw yellow, fine and persistent perlage.
desc_en_short: U.S. wine & spirits importer Palm Bay International and Bottega SpA, Italian producer of fine wines and spirits, introduced the Accademia Prosecco Rainbow Collection. Inspired by the innovation and craftmanship of Venice, Accademia’s vibrant rainbow color packaging celebrates the art of Venetian glass-making and the city’s festive carnival atmosphere, the companies says. Tasting Note: 

Color and Appearance

Brilliant, straw yellow, fine and persistent perlage.

Nose 

Elegantly fruity with notes that recall, in particular, green apple, white peach and citrus; closes with a delicate floral of acacia and wisteria flowers.

Taste 

Lively, light and pleasantly fresh, it presents a balance between acid and sugar component which gives elegance and refinement.

 Pairings

Excellent as an aperitif and for the preparation of cocktails, it goes perfectly with fish appetizers and seafood risottos but, being a wine for the whole meal, even with cooking based on spontaneous herbs, meat dishes, pizza, vegetables (especially asparagus and Treviso radicchio), cold cuts and cheeses.

name: Montelvini Prosecco Doc Rose Treviso Brut (Serenitatis Collection)

desc_en_full:
WINEMAKING :

The fruit was night-harvested, then immediately pressed to tank to capture and preserve its fresh, pure juice. To accentuate the crisp, zesty character and enhance the texture and complexity of our coastal Sauvignon Blanc grapes, we fermented the must (juice and skins) in stainless tanks at 50°F and aged it sur lie (on the yeast lees) for several months. desc_en_full:
VARIETY: 100% Sauvignon Blanc

ORIGIN: Molina Vineyard. VII region, Chili (Central Valley) La Huerta Vineyard. Cold area with Andes influence. Nice tropical fruit. San Miguel Vineyard. Low afternoon temperatures by Pulmodon hills influence., perfect for developing fresh fruit.

HARVEST: The grapes harvested during night with 20-21° brix, taking advantage of low temperatures, protecting their full virtues and avoiding oxidation of aromas. Harvest usually take place during March with a vineyard yield of 12 to 15 tons per hectare.

VINIFICATION: The grapes are processed immediately when they arrive to the cellar. The grapes macerate at low temperatures (10-12°C) for 4 to 6 hours and then goes to the press where first and second must is separated, leaving the option of mixing them later in the final blend. The must is left for decantation at low temperatures (11°C) for 24 to 48 hours in stainless steel tanks. Clean must is separated and is inoculated with selected yeast. The fermentation is carried out during 20 days in stainless steel tanks with controlled temperatures below 14°C in order to develop and to preserve the citric aromas and fresh character of the variety plus it fruit intensity.

TASTING NOTES: This SB has a pale yellow colour with hints of green tones. SB Intense where you can find a blend of the herbals notes like tomato leaf and the fresh tropical fruit such a grapefruit, pineapple and mango. It's a wine the fresh style with a balanced acidity that improve the fruity mouth sensation and give a long pleasant finish. Serving Suggestion: First courses, seafood, ethnic dishes-pastas, curries, salsas, spicy sausages, vegetables dishes, luncheon salads, olive oil based dishes, tomato sauces, goat cheese. Ideal temperature for drinking: 10-12°C

name: Santa Cristina by Antinori  Orvieto Classico "Campogrande" DOC
wine_color: Straw yellow color with tinges of lime green.
desc_en_short: Campogrande is Antinori's flagship Orvieto Classico — a fresh, elegant Umbrian white from the Castello della Sala estate, blending Trebbiano and Grechetto for a clean, aromatic wine with citrus, white flowers, and delicate mineral freshness.
desc_en_full:
Orvieto Classico is one of central Italy's oldest and most storied white wine appellations, produced on the volcanic tufa soils of the hills surrounding the ancient hilltop city of Orvieto in Umbria. Antinori's Castello della Sala estate, acquired in 1940, is among the appellation's finest producers, and Campogrande is their benchmark Orvieto Classico.

A blend of Trebbiano Toscano and Grechetto — the traditional Orvieto varieties — vinified in stainless steel to preserve freshness and aromatics. Pale straw with golden highlights, it opens on fresh citrus, green apple, white flowers (acacia, hawthorn), and a subtle mineral quality that speaks of the volcanic terroir. The palate is light-to-medium bodied, clean and crisp, with good acidity and a refreshing, dry finish.

An ideal everyday white — perfect with light pasta, grilled chicken, fish, antipasti, and the simple, honest cuisine of Umbria.

desc_en_full: Harvest : The grapes are mechanically harvested during night with 20-22° brix, taking advantage of low temperatures, protecting their full virtues and avoiding oxidation of aromas. Harvest usually takes place during March, reaching a vineyard's yield of 12 to 15 tons per hectare. Vinification : The grapes are processed immediately when they arrive to the cellar. The grapes macerate at low temperatures (10-12°C) for 4 to 6 hours and then, go to the pneumatic press where first and second must are separated, leaving the option of mixing them later in the final blend. The must is left for decantation at low temperatures (11°C) for 24 to 48 hours in stainless steel tanks. Clean must is separated and inoculated with selected yeast. The fermentation is carried out during 15 days in stainless steel tanks with controlled temperatures below 14°C in order to develop and preserve the citric aromas and fresh character of the variety, added to its fruit intensity. Prior to bottling, the wine is clarified with bentonite, cold stabilized and filtered with sterile plaques.

desc_en_full:
SOPRASOLE VERMENTINO DI SARDEGNA DOC
Denominazione Di Origine Controllata

Vermentino is the most modern of the Sardinian vines, it has been part of the heritage of this island in the middle of the Mediterranean for four hundred years. Thinking about Vermentino means thinking about Sardinia, which has become his land of choice, his homeland. As happens in the vineyards that the Pala family has cultivated since 1950, always using the same clone, reproduced and adapted in these limestone hills overlooking the sea of the Gulf of Cagliari.
Soprasole is the Sardinia of brilliant light, of white cliffs, hills, emerald-colored vineyards as well as the waters of its sea in the light of the sun that caresses them… Soprasole is the blue night full of sparks and lights of memorable evenings; it is memories of walks on the beach scented with a thousand Mediterranean essences. It is a kite flying on the beach lost in the immense blue of the sky… it is the elegance and glamour of the most beautiful evenings. Soprasole is closing your eyes and finding yourself in Sardinia.

name: Jacob's Creek  Reserve Chardonnay (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
Jacob's Creek Reserve Chardonnay has attractive melon and fresh fruit flavors complemented by subtle toasty oak are hallmarks of this fresh crisp wine. Premium wines that capture the character of Australia’s best wine regions.

 

Wine Score : 90 POINTS, Jane Faulkner - Halliday Wine Companion 2018 (Aug-17)

Award : GOLD, China Wine & Spirits Awards 2018
desc_en_full:
Jacob's Creek Reserve Chardonnay has attractive melon and fresh fruit flavours complemented by subtle toasty oak are hallmarks of this fresh crisp wine. Premium wines that capture the character of Australia’s best wine regions.

Aroma

Peach, Citrus, Roasted nuts

Taste

Dry with medium acidity, medium body with long aftertaste

Complimentary

Creamy pasta, Rich Fish, Chicken

THE STORY OF JACOB CREEK

Jacob Creek from Australia In 1847, along with the banks of Jacob’s Creek, Johann Gramp planted his first vineyard. His vision was to create great tasting wines that reflect the true character of the grapes and the land. To this day we live and breathe this vision by producing wines that showcase how each grape variety expresses itself when shaped by the soil and the sun of Australia.

Awarded with 3 gold medals from international wine and spirits competition. Such as, 2014 WSA Wine Challenge, 2013 China Wine & Spirits Awards and 2014 Pacific Rim International Wine Compettition.

WINE MAKING PROCESS OF JACOB CREEK RESERVE

1 ) THE FIRST TASTE, ON THE VINES

Our viticulturists and winemakers visit every the vineyard to taste the fruit and determine the optimal time to start harvesting.

2 ) THE HARVEST

Grapes are picked. Most are picked with a harvester to minimise the time it takes to get the grapes to the winery to best retain freshness of the fruit. Some are picked by hand during the day, such as the old delicate vines or where the site is too steep.

3 ) THE TRANSFER

Time is critical. We fill the grape bins in the vineyard and take the grapes by truck to our winery as soon as possible.

4 ) THE DE-STEMMER

The de-stemmer gently removes the stalks from the grapes

5 ) THE FERMENT

Reds are fermented on skins, as it’s the skins that contribute most of the colour and tannin to the wine. Yeast is added and fermentation takes place under the careful watch of our winemakers. Individual parcels are fermented separately, so the timing varies. Typically, a parcel destined for our Reserve Shiraz spends around 10-12 days fermenting before it is ready to be pressed.

As the juice ferments, bubbles rise to the surface and carry grape skins with them. They can form a ‘cap’ which needs to be broken up using processes such as ‘pumping over’ (pumping the wine from the bottom to the top) and occasionally ‘pushing down the caps’ (with a plunger). This also maximises the extraction of colour and tannins.

6 ) THE PRESS

After primary fermentation, red grapes are then pressed to separate out the wine from the skins and pulp.

7 ) MALOLACTIC FERMENTATION

All of our red wines undergo malolactic fermentation, a natural process where malic acid is converted to softer lactic acid. Some parcels of our premium red wines are selected for barrel maturation to add oak nuances and texture, and to soften tannins.

8 ) OAK MATURATION

Depending on the desired wine style, the wine is left to mature on oak for a period of 6-24 months. Aromatic-styles, such as Pinot Noir with its inherently softer tannins spends less time on oak, while more assertive styles such as, young Cabernet Sauvignon spends more time on oak.

9 ) FILTRATION AND BOTTLING

Through filtration, the wine takes its final, clear and bright form. This is done with the lightest touch possible to deliver you a wine that showcases the character of our regions.

We bottle our reds mostly in screw cap for preservation of quality, and a small quantity also in traditional cork for the few countries which prefer it.

10 ) BOTTLE MATURATION

After bottling, some premium reds are left for a further period, to mature perfectly before being released for sale. The timing is determined by the winemaker who considers when the flavours have become well-integrated and the wine has entered the start of its ideal drinking window.

With the premium nature of our Reserve range, it is well suited to medium-term bottle maturation. With a few years of careful cellaring, the wine can evolve, during which the overt fruit flavours will become subdued and more savoury complexity will develop.

name: Jermann  Chardonnay DOC
wine_color: Brilliant straw-yellow colour with greenish highlights.
desc_en_short: Jermann's Chardonnay is a beautifully crafted Friulian white — barrel-fermented and aged on its lees for added richness, delivering golden apple, peach, toasted oak, and buttery complexity balanced by the region's signature freshness and mineral precision.
desc_en_full:
Jermann is one of Italy's most admired white wine estates, and their Chardonnay is a showcase for the estate's winemaking philosophy: maximum respect for the fruit, thoughtful use of oak, and an unwavering commitment to freshness. From vineyards in Friuli-Venezia Giulia, where the Adriatic and Alpine influences combine to create ideal conditions for aromatic, high-acid whites.

Partially fermented in French oak barriques and aged on fine lees for several months, the wine develops impressive complexity without losing the freshness that defines Jermann's style. Golden straw in colour, it opens on ripe golden apple, peach, mango, hazelnut, and a gentle vanilla from the oak, backed by a subtle, pleasing minerality. The palate is rich and textured, with a creamy, lees-influenced mid-palate and a clean, persistent finish.

Excellent with grilled fish, lobster, veal with cream sauce, pasta with truffle, mushroom risotto, and semi-aged cheeses.

desc_en_short: This all-in-one mojito set has the tools you need to make the perfect mojito. Includes two classic tom collins glasses and a wooden muddler for crushing ingredients.
desc_en_full:
This all-in-one mojito set has the tools you need to make the perfect mojito. Includes two classic tom collins glasses and a wooden muddler for crushing ingredients

Features :

- Glass capacity 12 oz (375 ml) each

- Metropolis skyline inspired glass pattern

- Thick glass walls and base

- One-piece solid maple wood muddler with grooved end

Easy recipe:

Ingredients: • 8 fresh mint leaves • ¼ lime, cut into 2 wedges • 1 to 2 tablespoons white sugar, or to taste • 4-8 ice cubes • 1 to 1½ shots white rum • ½ cup club soda

Directions: Place mint leaves, 1 lime wedge and sugar into the glass. Use the muddler to crush the ingredients to release the mint oils and lime juice. Fill the glass with ice then pour the rum over the ice and fill the glass with club soda. Stir, taste, and add more sugar if desired. Garnish with the remaining lime wedge.

name: Nonino  Grappa Tradizione  (1 L)
food_matching: Chocolate, Dried Fruits, Cheese, After Dinner
desc_en_short: Grappa obtained by white and red pomace . Typical and elegant.
desc_en_full:
Grappa Tradizione Nonino 41°

Grappa obtained by white and red pomace .

Typical and elegant.

FEATURES

Fermentation

Controlled fermentation. This is the followed immediately by distillation in order to avoid harmful secondary fermentations.

Primary product

Fresh, soft, selected pomace of white and red grapes.

Artisanal Distillation

Batch method according to our ancient family tradition in special artisanal batch steam stills renovated by Benito Nonino. The distillation happens very slowly to allow the volatile components, responsible for the perfumes, a proper evaporation period and subsequent condensation, in order to keep intact the precious organoleptic characteristics of the fresh pomace.

Production

Dependent on the individual year's harvest.

Maturation

From six months to a year in stainless steel tank.

Alcohol content

41% vol

Bottling

1000 ml bottle in transparent glass, not hand-made, without a case.

Optimum temperature

It should be served at 12°C in a glass with an open tulip shape.

Tasting

Before tasting, Grappa should be allowed to rest in the glass for some minutes: on exposure to oxygen the perfumes are enhanced and the nose/palate harmony is perfected.

When and how to drink Grappa

Its particular elegance and personality is the perfect end to a meal, perhaps accompanied by a good cigar or to be savoured with one's friend"

name: Glen Scotia  Festival Scotch Whisky (700 ml)
food_matching: Dark Chocolate, Smoked Meats, Aged Cheese, Nuts
desc_en_short:
Glen Scotia was born in a place like no other – the smallest whisky region on the west coast of Scotland that was to become the Victorian Whisky Capital of the World. We’re once again delighted to be opening our distillery doors to friends from near and afar, in a celebration of both Campbeltown and its rich whisky-making heritage.

The Campbeltown Malts Festival takes place for one week every May, and allows those few distilleries that have endured, to step away from their stills and fully open their doors to the local community and visitors from afar. For the workers at our Glen Scotia Distillery, the festival represents their favourite and most anticipated moment of the year – a time for celebration and raising a dram with old and new friends alike.
desc_en_full:
Glen Scotia

Glen Scotia is one of just three distilleries in Campbeltown and the only one actively releasing single malt - the newly founded Mitchell's Glengyle has some years still to go. Glen Scotia was founded in 1832 by Stewart, Galbraith and Co and sited to the north of the town. The distillery draws its cooling waters from the Crosshill Loch and two bore holes, drilled over twenty metres into the rock below. Campbeltown was the capital of Scotch whisky production before the US Prohibition was enacted. There were some twenty-eight distilleies, though after the act was passed only three survived.

Glen Scotia was acquired by West Highland Malt Distillers in 1919 and five years later the company went bankrupt. Duncan MacCallum, a director, purchased the company. The distillery was closed again in 1928 and a couple of years later, following great financial trouble, Duncan MacCallum threw himself into the very loch from which the Glen Scotia distillery draws its water. It is said that his ghost haunts the distillery. Glen Scotia survived the Second World War, having restarted production in 1933 after the repeal of Prohibition. In 1954, Hiram Walker acquired the distillery and shortly after it was purchased by A Gillies and Co.

In 1979, £1 million was spent on reconstruction which lasted until 1982 and two years later the distillery closed once more. Glen Scotia was acquired by Glen Catrine Bonded Warehouse Ltd and in 2000 Loch Lomond distillery took over, providing staff and running the operations. Today, the Glen Scotia produces 100,000 litres annually - substantially lower than its three quarter million capacity. The distillery now has a staff of just two; James Grogan, the long time stillman, and Hector Gatt of Springbank fame. There have been several official releases and a few independent bottlings.

name: Le Puy Rose-Marie
desc_en_short: The Amoreau family,15 generations of winemakers since 1610. Located at the top of the same clay and fossil-rich limestone plateau as Saint-Emilion, overlooking the districts of Pomerol and Saint-Emilion, le Puy dominates the Dordogne Valley. At an altitude of 107m, it is the second-highest geodesic point of the Gironde department. This position lent its name to the site as of Roman times; le Puy means a high place, a podium, or a mountain peak.

name: Chateau L'Ecuyer
desc_en_short: A beautifully composed Pomerol with velvety tannins, round, plush fruit filled with plums and blackberries. Fairly long length.
desc_en_full:
Chateau L'Ecuyer [lay kwe ya] is a tiny estate of only 3 hectares located in the small prestigious Pomerol appellation. It was originally known as Chateau de Bourgueneuf, but was re-baptized L'Ecuyer (squire) when the Petit and Cambier family took over in 2004. L'Ecuyer benefits from an exceptional terroir. 1 hectare is located on the summit of the famous Pomerol plateau next to Clos L'Eglise, Chateau Clinet and Chateau Rouget. The other 2 hectares are located in the heart of Pomerol.

Mr. Emeric Petit, the owner/winemaker of L'Ecuyer, learned how to make wine under the guidance of famous oenologists Mr. Jean-Claude Barrouet and Ms. Catherine Cohen. Today, Emeric is a rising star and making his own wines. The 2004 is his first vintage at L'Ecuyer and it is an example of the outstanding wines coming from the right bank of Bordeaux at attractive pricing. Emeric also owns several other estates in Lalande de Pomerol, Pomerol and St. Emilion.

name: VIK  La Piu Belle
desc_en_short:
La Piu Belle is the goddess of nature, love, fertility, beauty and art. She is the goddess of VIK, our place of gold in the Millahue Valley. She is the mystical world’s personification of our incredible terroir. She embodies the sun enlightening the mist covering the Millahue Valley at dawn. She incarnates the freshness of the Pacific coastal and Andean mountain breezes cooling our grapes during the luminescent summer. She is the gentle rains giving life to our vines, enhancing and liberating the fragrance of the green and luxurious native flora and fauna surrounding our vineyard. She is the complexity of our soils, the roundness of our hills and beauty of our valleys.

La Piu Belle is intensely emotional and very beautiful; the most beautiful. She has been given to VIK to give us our beautiful wines. She gave birth to our vineyards and cries tears of red gold out of joy for what she has created. These tears of red gold, our wines, are her gifts to the world.
desc_en_full:
THE PERFECT TERROIR

VIK vineyard was born amidst the foothills of the Andes mountains, within the Millahue Valley, named "Lugar de Oro" or "Golden Place" by the native Mapuche people: 4,300 hectares of astonishing Chilean wilderness where they planted over 327 hectares of vines.

VIK’s terroir offers a majestic and natural landscape composed of 12 valleys, each one with its own micro-climate, distinct exposures, all within a wind-tunnel cooled by Pacific coastal breezes and winds from high up in the Andes mountains that provide VIK with enormous complexity and exceptional variety.

name: Les Licornes De Cantenac Brown Margaux 2016 (Second Label of Château Cantenac Brown)
desc_en_short:
From the storied Château Cantenac Brown, a Third Growth (Troisième Cru Classé) estate of the 1855 Bordeaux Classification, comes its refined second wine — Les Licornes de Cantenac Brown. The château, founded in the early 19th century by John-Lewis Brown, a Scottish-born artist and visionary, is one of the most architecturally striking in Margaux, with its Tudor-style façade symbolizing both heritage and creativity.

The name “Les Licornes” (The Unicorns) reflects the emblem of the Brown family and the estate’s enduring pursuit of rarity and grace. Crafted with the same care as the Grand Vin, but sourced from younger vines and select parcels, this wine captures the signature silkiness, perfume, and balance that define the Margaux appellation. The 2016 vintage, one of the greatest of the decade, offers remarkable harmony — power and purity wrapped in velvet.

name: Cavicchioli & Figli  Cavicchioli 1928 Lambrusco Rosso dell’Emilia IGT
wine_color: Light ruby red  colour with purple tinges
desc_en_short: Cavicchioli's Lambrusco Emilia Amabile is a lightly sparkling, gently sweet red that captures the heart of Emilian table culture — fresh red cherry, strawberry, and a hint of violets, with soft bubbles and an easy, food-friendly character.
desc_en_full:
Lambrusco Amabile is perhaps the most Italian of wines — a frizzante (lightly sparkling) red with a touch of natural sweetness that has been the everyday table wine of Emilia-Romagna for centuries. Cavicchioli, based in San Prospero near Modena, is one of the region's most trusted producers of this beloved style.

Made from local Lambrusco grape varieties grown on the Po Valley plains, the wine is ruby-violet in colour with a lively pink foam. The nose is fresh and inviting — red cherry, wild strawberry, raspberry, and violet — with a light earthy, almost rustic quality that speaks of its central Italian origins. On the palate it is gently sweet and softly sparkling, with refreshing acidity that keeps it lively and far from cloying.

This is a wine made for food — and specifically for the rich cuisine of Emilia: cured meats, mortadella, Parma ham, grilled sausages, and aged Parmigiano. Serve lightly chilled at around 12°C.

name: Cavicchioli  Lambrusco Grasparossa di Castelvetro Amabile
wine_color: Deep ruby red colour with purple tinges.
flavor_tags: Dark Chocolate, Blackberry, Chocolate, Chestnut, Cherry, Violet, Plum
desc_en_short: Grasparossa di Castelvetro is the most structured of the Lambrusco varieties — this Amabile version from Cavicchioli delivers deep ruby colour, intense dark cherry and plum fruit, bold tannins, and gentle sweetness with satisfying effervescence.
desc_en_full:
Among the many Lambrusco varieties grown in Emilia-Romagna, Grasparossa di Castelvetro stands apart for its deeper colour, more pronounced tannins, and richer fruit concentration. Its name — 'red-stemmed' — refers to the distinctive red hue the vine's stems take on in autumn. Grown on the hillsides around Castelvetro di Modena, it produces the most structured and age-worthy of the Lambrusco wines.

Cavicchioli's Amabile version retains a touch of natural sweetness that balances Grasparossa's naturally firm tannins. Deep ruby-violet in colour with a persistent pink-purple foam, it opens on intense aromas of dark cherry, blackberry, dried violets, and a hint of dark chocolate. On the palate it is medium-bodied with firm, grippy tannins, refreshing acidity, and a sweet-fruity finish that is quintessentially Emilian.

The wine of Modena's trattorias — made for zampone, cotechino, tigelle with salami, and the full range of Emilian cured meats. Serve lightly chilled at 10–12°C.

name: Louis Roederer  Cristal Rose 2013
desc_en_short:
THE ESSENCE OF A GREAT FRAGRANCE Cristal Rosé

This wine, infused like a fine perfume, unfolds with a medley of eternally fresh and intermingling flavours. Its fragrance is one of soft tangy fruit and flower petals along with a profusion of almond, bread and spicy nuances. Cristal Rosé 2013 reflects the light from its soil, a few drops of a perfect distillate, cast into a pure mould. It is the quintessence of a great Champagne poised to transcend the boundaries of time!
desc_en_full:
TASTING NOTES

Light, delicate soft pink hue.

Bright, vibrant, ultra-fine bubbles.

Refined bouquet of freshly-picked tart red fruit (wild strawberry, raspberry) and lemon scented flower petals. The pure, precise fragrances delicately intertwine and are gradually joined by overtones of fresh almonds, bread and spices (cocoa bean). These nuanced, intricately interwoven fragrances are ethereal and transcendent. Intense, velvety and captivating on the palate. What else would you expect after such perfume? The juicy, concentrated red fruit gradually pulls towards a raspberry liqueur character complemented by invigorating, sweet citrus aromas. The harmony and delicacy of the wine is reinforced by the creamy bubbles which create a sensation of roundness and concentration. The mineral freshness lengthens the palate; it becomes vertical, chalky, saline and sapid with a sensation of perpetual acceleration created by the pure, bright limestone.

name: Jansz  Tasmania Premium Cuvée
desc_en_full:
Jansz Premium Brut Cuvée

Tasmania is renowned as Australia's home of great sparkling wines. The fruit used in this Cuvée is grown on selected, cool climate vineyards across Tasmania.

To maximize blending options and complexity in the final wine, batches are kept separate during winemaking. Secondary fermentation takes place in the bottle and is aged on yeast lees for as long as possible. The perfect balance for our Premium Cuvée is reached at just over two years.

Honeysuckle and citrus scents are immediately apparent with slight aromas of nougat, roasted nuts and a hint of strawberry from the Pinot Noir. A well-balanced palate of delicate fruits and creaminess fills the mouth with a lingering finish of citrus and nougat.
