name: Dom Perignon 2015
wine_color: Glistening Colour With Light Amber Highlights.
desc_en_short: The 2015 winegrowing year was marked by striking contrasts in climate, starting with a particularly cold spring, followed by a prolonged period of hot, dry weather that lasted until the end of August. When the harvest began on 7 September, in brilliant sunshine, the vines were impeccably healthy, revealing the exceptional generosity and balance of the grapes. This vintage aromatic wine promises a remarkable aromatic richness, capturing the unique essence of the year.
desc_en_full:
Dom Pérignon, a seventeeth-century cellarmaster of the Abbey of Hautvillers, is revered as the spiritual father of winemaking in the Champagne region. Keen observation, respect for nature, pragmatic creativity, technical innovation, the courage of his convictions, and patience were the instruments serving his vision. He constantly mastered and incorporated all its components and the stages of its production, from the vineyards themselves to the pressing and clarification of the wines and their preservation.

Dom Pérignon was the originator of new techniques for cultivating vines and making wine. These innovations spread rapidly throughout the region.

Today, Dom Pérignon is produced by France's largest Champagne house, Moët et Chandon. Dom Pérignon’s principle contribution in the 20th century has been its commitment to vintage years. In addition to the exclusive use of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay grapes from only the finest growths and vineyards, Dom Pérignon strictly limits itself to wines of the very best years, which have undergone long ageing.

The Dom Pérignon style is constructed by the Chef de Cave to create a range of sensations on the palate that reflect all the complexity of the structure, and the aromas and characteristics of the vintage.

name: Champagne Chavost Assemblage Brut Nature
desc_en_short:
Our estate was created in 1946 by the will of our elders, winegrowers of Chavot-Courcourt.

Chavost is the ancestral name of the village, which formerly was located between the Saint-Martin Church and the Mount Felix hill, upon which a castle was established.

Our ancestors have given us their strong collective values, solidarity, sharing, and the love of our lands. Now more than ever, we put all our forces in common to grow high quality grapes, giving us the chance to elaborate unique wines of Champagne.

Our commitment in preserving the environment, our land and the excellence of our wines, is expanding along with a deep conviction. In harmony with our philosophy and values, we decided to produce and launch a new range of wines :

Thanks to a mastered vinification, that does not include added sulfites, the range « Et sans sulfites ajoutés ! » ( meaning « No added sulphites ! »), is composed of a singular and atypical wines. It comes in addition to the traditional range.

name: Zonin  Ripasso Valpolicella Superiore DOC
desc_en_short: Elegant and medium bodied revealing stewed mixed berries, smoking tobacco and exotic spice that leads to plush and moderate tannins. The finish is long and reveals the wines complexity.
desc_en_full:
A story dating back almost two hundred years and seven generations, with its roots in Gambellara, in the heart of the Veneto region, a land with an extraordinary vocation for wine production: these are the origins of the Zonin Family, which today controls one of Italy's – and indeed the world's - most important private wine companies. Even though the Zonin Family now owns over 1,800 hectares of vineyards in Italy's outstanding vine growing areas, Cavaliere Gianni Zonin has not given up his family-style approach, both in his relationships with his colleagues and with the market.

VINIFICATION

The grapes are handpicked are crushed and fermented preserving its fresh and young character. The grapes undergo a cold pre-fermentation for about 5 days,total contact with the skins lasts about 20 days. When drawn off, the highly aromatic wine has obtained good color and structure. It is stored cold for approximately 4 months before undergoing the "ripasso" process, which entails fermenting the wine on Amarone must for approximately 20 days to increase color, aroma, body, and fruit flavor. The wine is then aged for approximately 18 months in casks and barrels to add complexity, structure and refinement, and an additional 1 month in the bottle before release.

COLOR

Saturated ruby-red with purple hues.

BOUQUET Complex showing brandied cherry, mature plum, and fig, with notes of violet, dried tobacco and dark chocolate.

PALATE Elegant and medium bodied revealing stewed mixed berries, smoking tobacco and exotic spice that leads to plush and moderate tannins. The finish is long and reveals the wines complexity.

FOOD PAIRINGS This wine is a perfect match for roasted meats and pasta with garlic sauce.

SERVING TEMPERATURE 61°-64°F.

ALCOHOL LEVEL 14%

BOTTLE SIZE 750 ml

desc_en_short: Remy Martin's 1738 Accord Royal Cognac was created to sit between their VSOP and XO. It is named in honour of the Accord Royal granted by King Louis XV to Rémy Martin in 1738, allowing the firm to use his land to plant vines. This is a rich and robust Cognac.
desc_en_full:
A Cognac that celebrates the Louis XV's decision to grant a young Rémy Martin the right to plant new vines on his land (banned in France at the time) with the Accord Royal in 1738. It's said that the Cognac was enjoyed at the King's court at the time, with Rémy having planted his first vineyard in 1724. This unique blend has been made with majesty very much in mind.

Palate: Rounded and balanced vanilla and dried fruits.

Finish: Pronounced, but not overpowering, oak finish.

Overall: Rémy with a little extra majestic gusto thrown in

name: Chabot Armagnac  XO Superior (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Chabot's flagship Armagnac, the X.O. Superior, shimmers in a crystal cut glass bottle that is itself a work of art. The brandy is a unique blended of mature Armagnacs aged up to 35 years. With a satin golden amber color and a nose of almond and plum, this Armagnac is full in the month with a powerful finish and spicy, long lasting aromas. X.O. is statement piece in any fine spirit collection.
desc_en_full:
History of Chabot

Chabot established its name in the wines and spirits trade in 1828. Based in the rolling hills of Gascony in the village of Labastide d'Armagnac, the family produced the wine necessary to the distillation of Armagnac. For over a century, the finely crafted Armagnac remained a well-kept secret, shared among close friends, Gascon locals and the French nobility. That was until 1963, and Chabot's first foray into bringing Armagnac from France to the rest of the world.

The big international development of Chabot took place under the leadership of John Gentzbourger. The brandy became prized for its superlative quality and distinctive taste. Today, Chabot Armagnac has circumnavigated the globe and can be found in 96 countries. It is now the best selling Armagnac brand in the world.

desc_en_short: The VSOP with the deepest floral aromas in the category. Featuring in its blend some of the best eaux-de-vie from the Borderies Cru, our VSOP distinguishes itself for the high level of Terpenols, resulting into unmatched scents of fine flowers.
desc_en_full:
The VSOP with the deepest floral aromas in the category. Featuring in its blend some of the best eaux-de-vie from the Borderies Cru, our VSOP distinguishes itself for the high level of Terpenols, resulting into unmatched scents of fine flowers.

CAMUS VSOP is an intensely aromatic cognac, whose floral and fruity profile with notes of oak, spice and vanilla derive from a careful selection of the eaux-de-vie reflecting the quintessential flavors of the Terroir, some of which coming from the renowned Cru of Borderies. All the barrels used for the ageing are exclusively “fine grain” and low in tannins, thanks to the peculiar ‘French Light Toasting’ process, allowing to balance oakiness with fresh flavors.

Tasting Notes

This VSOP Cognac shows an elegant golden tint with amber undertones. On the nose, it reveals a perfect balance between precious tannins and fresh notes of tropical fruits, followed by generous floral tones. On the palate, it resonates with lavish fruity notes accompanied by accents of spices and vanilla. Awarded Double Gold at the World Spirit Competition in San Francisco.

name: Coutanseaux Aîné XXO Cognac (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Coutanseaux Aîné stands among the great houses that shaped the golden age of Cognac. Founded in Jarnac in 1767, the Maison was one of the earliest négociants to export French brandy to the royal courts of Europe. From the rolling chalky hills of the Grande Champagne and Petite Champagne crus, its legacy was built on purity, patience, and the art of long maturation.

The XXO (Extra Extra Old) expression represents the rarest tier of the house’s craft — a blend of eaux-de-vie aged a minimum of 14 years, though many in this cuvée rest far longer in old Limousin oak casks. Each barrel tells a story of quiet evolution: the spirit breathing through oak grain, exchanging air, and gaining the deep amber hue and complex bouquet that only time can bestow.

Coutanseaux Aîné XXO pays homage to centuries of distillers who shaped Cognac’s soul — where science meets poetry, and where time becomes flavor.
desc_en_full:
A grand Cognac of meditation and grace, Coutanseaux Aîné XXO unfolds like a long conversation between wood, fruit, and time.

Aroma: Deep layers of candied orange, old leather, toasted almond, and fig compote, followed by hints of spice box, rancio, and tobacco leaf.

Palate: Rich and seamless, with flavors of dried apricot, honeyed oak, dark chocolate, and roasted coffee beans. The maturity reveals elegant oxidative tones and a subtle sweetness of preserved fruits.

Texture: Luxuriously smooth, almost creamy, coating the palate with warmth and finesse.

Finish: Exceptionally long, echoing notes of toffee, cedarwood, and hazelnut praline, leaving a contemplative afterglow.

name: G'Vine  Floraison (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Produced in the French region of Cognac, G'Vine is a hint as to what this unique gin contains. It's actually infused with Ugni Blanc grape, as well as some more standard botanicals including ginger root, liquorice, cardamom, cassia bark, coriander, cubeb berries, nutmeg, lime and even a bit of juniper.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Note :

Nose : Doesn't immediately smell like a gin... the grape notes are prevalent, but there are balancing notes of citrus and cardamom.

Palate : The grapes run the show here too. It's tasty with pronounced, fresh botanicals, particularly the high, citric and ginger notes. The grapes add a pleasant sweetness. It's almost like a flavoured vodka.

Finish : Very clean, very pleasant. Herbs and grassy notes with a slight mineral taste.

Overall : Almost halfway between a gin and a flavoured vodka, this is a unique spirit. We could (and probably will!) spend hours playing around with cocktail recipes using G'Vine.

name: Four Pillars  Blood Shiraz Gin (700 ml)
desc_en_short: The resultant gin is an incredibly rich, deep purple colour, which turns iridescent pink when mixed with tonic or soda. Aromatically it has fresh pine needles, citrus and spice, and a clear peppery, dense raspberry character. The palate is lovely and sweet, with a long juniper and spice character on the finish.
desc_en_full:
Four Pillars steeped Yarra Valley Shiraz grapes in gin for eight weeks. The fruit was de-stemmed but kept mainly as whole berries, and the tanks were kept cold and stirred daily. After eight weeks we pressed the fruit before blending with more Rare Dry Gin.

The resultant gin is an incredibly rich, deep purple colour, which turns iridescent pink when mixed with tonic or soda. Aromatically it has fresh pine needles, citrus and spice, and a clear peppery, dense raspberry character. The palate is lovely and sweet, with a long juniper and spice character on the finish.

Unlike Sloe Gin, the alcohol is 37.8% (rather than <30%) so it packs a punch and is ideal in a range of cocktails. Four Pillars 'Bloody Shiraz Gin' is best consumed within two years of vintage. The gin is unfiltered and will develop secondary fruit characters beyond two years.

name: Sabatini  Gin (700 ml)
food_matching: Tonic, Seafood Cocktails, Light Appetizers, Citrus Dishes
desc_en_short:
Nine botanicals for a special Gin

An excellent Gin requires constant testing, a careful combination of flavours and choice of various home-grown botanicals.

There are precisely nine Tuscan botanicals in SABATINI GIN: juniper, coriander, iris, fennel, lavender, olive leaves, thyme, verbena and sage. The goal is to create a traditional London Dry Gin uniquely expressing the inimitable scent of Tuscany and its hillsides.
desc_en_full: A passion for Tuscany and a passion for good Gin. These two concepts might seem to have nothing in common to those who are not experts in this particular spirit, because few people know that the very best juniper in the world, used in fact to produce many high quality Gins, actually comes from Tuscany. Here, in Teccognano, near Cortona in the province of Arezzo, the Sabatinis, a family deeply attached to this corner of the world, have decided to strengthen this bond by making their Gin even more Tuscan. They have done this by not only using fine Tuscan juniper, but also a whole series of other botanicals, grown mainly on the family’s own land and in the surrounding area, creating an excellent London Dry Gin that embodies the scents and images of the Tuscan hills.

name: Citadelle  No Mistake Old Tom Gin (500 ml)
desc_en_short:
In 1836, as he loitered in the bars of London, Charles Dickens marveled at “the ingenuity employed to give sobriquets to gin, with one term more seductive than the next, to describe it in multiple ways.” “No Mistake” is one of the nicknames Dickens noted and it seemed appropriate for our Old Tom, which we created in honor of this superb elixir.

Alexandre Gabriel’s challenge was to set it in context by starting from a base of a popular gin of the 19th century. In the course of his frequent travels in the Caribbean, he discovered a marvelous brown sugar, like Demerara, moist and heavy. It was the missing ingredient. This sugar is toasted in copper cauldrons for a first stage of caramelization, then lightly thinned and finally alcoholized.

It is then aged for three to four months in casks. To create his Old Tom, the expertise of the Master Blender consists of supplementing Citadelle Réserve – selected for its floral aspect – with aged sugar, then putting it all back into casks for several months, in order to obtain a perfect blend of all the elements, an ideal integration at 46% alcohol.
desc_en_full:
Tasting notes: 

Color : gold

Nose: fresh and forceful, the attack reveals juniper, orange zest and cardamom, then it becomes more floral and vegetal, with jasmine and honeysuckle and notes of fennel and cumin, and almost sweet, with coffee.

Mouth: full and elegant, with a sweet and sour profile of preserved lemon zest, génépi, anise and almond, lengthening to spicy notes of nutmeg, cinnamon and cubeb pepper. Finish: lovely length, floral, spicy and woody, with notes of coriander, violet, angelica, cumin, grains of paradise, and licorice.

name: Four Pillars  Fresh Yuzu Gin (700 ml)
food_matching: Tonic, Seafood Cocktails, Light Appetizers, Citrus Dishes
desc_en_short: At Four Pillars, our passion for pushing flavour boundaries of gin has taken us around the world in search of botanicals and ingredients. Our travels to Japan and our collaborations with Japenese distillers and chefs gave us a particular appreciation for the strange and wonderful yuzu. And now here is our ultimate expression of Fresh Yuzu Gin
desc_en_full:
We've had a thing for yuzu for a while now, and with three limited-edition trial runs under our belts, we're ready to launch the newest member of our core range: a stand-alone, honed and perfected Fresh Yuzu Gin. A bright, aromatic and (yes) fresh dry gin made with locally-grown yuzu and inspired by our adventures making gin with Japanese friends.

At Four Pillars, we’ve always had a well-known love of bright flavours and the cornucopia of great Australian-grown citrus.

It started with oranges in Rare Dry Gin, finger limes in Navy Strength Gin and continued through blood orange, grapefruit, cumquat and bergamot – the list goes on. In fact, if you can name a citrus, chances are we’ve distilled it.

Over the past four or five years, there’s been another citrus slowly edging its way into our hearts. It’s yellow and looks pretty ugly (it’s all in the eye of the beholder, of course) but it tastes and distills great. Yep, we’re talking about the mighty yuzu.

name: The Botanist  Islay Cask Rested Gin (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
The Botanist Cask Rested Gin embodies Bruichladdich Distillery’s Whisky-making heritage and botanical expertise. Our Islay dry gin has been matured for a minimum of six months in a selection of fine casks.

The fragrant notes of 22 hand-foraged botanicals combined with wood spices create a vibrant and complex cuvée that redefines what gin can be.
desc_en_full:
Nose

This blush liquid delivers initial notes of warming spices and candied citrus peels, a sweet, enticing bouquet of perfumed juniper, baking spices and oak. The delicate floral, herbaceous notes of The Botanist combine with the subtle hint of dried fruits from the wood. Each nose of the glass entices you to discover a new element as candied fruits, sweet, fleshy orange, warming cinnamon, coconut and almonds emerge, complimenting the classic juniper style.

Taste

On the palate, the softness of the spirit is a delight. The gentle elegance of the cask rested Botanist holds a complexity and balance that is a joy to taste. The cask influence shapes the spirit, bringing extra viscosity to the palate while the symphony of botanicals combines with the spices of the wood to create a deep mouthfeel. Captivating oak, expert distillation and botanicals combined with the influence of wood and time create a magical experience.

Finish

On the finish, the oak influence begins to fade as the heart of The Botanist reveals itself in a rush of fresh herbal notes, juniper, coriander and citrus.

Character

The complexity of The Botanist is wonderfully enhanced by its resting period in the finest quality casks. There is a vibrancy and excitement about this exceptional spirit that puts it in a territory all of its own.

The Torii Shoten Store, the predecessor of Suntory, opened in 1899 as a result of the dream of young Shinjiro Torii, who at the age of 20 wanted to "create original Japanese Spirits the whole world can enjoy, uniquely made from Japan's nature and her people. "

Overcame Numerous Hardships Shinjiro To Make His Dream A Reality, Creating Wine, Whisky And Brandy, And Launching Hermes Gin - The First Suntory Gin -. In 1936. Shinjiro And His Successors Continued To Work Hard On Building A Deep Knowledge Of Distilling Alcohol

The Creation and perfection of ROKU are based on more than 100 years of expertise.

"Someday, spirits produced in Japan will be loved all over the world."

ROKU is the result of an history of over a century of dreams.

6 English botanicals nurtured in Japan's beautiful nature

Sakura Flower, Sakura Leaf, Yuzu Peel, Sencha Tea (Green Tea), Gyokuro Tea (Refined Green Tea) And Sansho Pepper.

Six Japanese Botanicals Cultivated Over Four Seasons, The Six Japanese Botanicals Have Been Carefully Selected To Create ROKU, Japan'S Authentic Craft Gin.

Each Botanical Has Been Harvested At The Peak Of Its Season To Extract The Best Flavour, And Distilled To Fully Embody The Blessings Of Nature.

The Harvest Of The Four Seasons Has Been Blended To Create ROKU'S Balanced Flavour.

name: G'Vine  June Watermelon (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
June by G’Vine Watermelon is a delicate and joyful expression of French artistry, born in the vineyards of Cognac — where the iconic G’Vine Gin de France is crafted. It captures the spirit of French summer, celebrating sunshine, friendship, and freshness in a bottle.

Unlike traditional gins, G’Vine is made from grape-based spirit, not grain, giving it an elegant softness and fruity texture. The June series takes that refinement further, infusing the gin with natural watermelon and summer fruits, creating a refreshing, modern twist that feels effortlessly French.

Vibrant yet refined, June Watermelon embodies the lightness of a warm afternoon by the Riviera — a gentle sweetness balanced with floral notes, made for easy enjoyment under the sun
desc_en_full:
A bright, fruit-forward gin liqueur that blends elegance, freshness, and joy.

Aroma: Inviting notes of fresh watermelon, white grape, and subtle florals, with a touch of citrus.

Palate: Smooth, juicy, and balanced — bursting with ripe watermelon, melon, and hints of elderflower.

Texture: Soft and velvety, thanks to its grape-spirit base.

Finish: Refreshing, crisp, and lightly sweet — evoking the feeling of summer’s first sip.

Pallini Limoncello is a natural liqueur that has been crafted by the Pallini family in Italy since 1875. It is made from prized, organic Sfusato lemons, exclusive to the Amalfi coast. PALLINI LIMONCELLO Based on a family recipe that is over 100 years old PALLINI LIMONCELLO is crafted using only the Costa d'Amalfi lemons, organically grown on the hillsides of the Amalfi coast.. name: Bitter Truth  Golden Falernum Liqueur (500 ml)
desc_en_short: This rum based liqueur is rich and complex in flavor. The Nose is Spicy ginger with clove and a hint of vanilla. On The Palate A more subtle ginger, followed by almond and vanilla. An allspice tingle on the tongue develops into a long finish with a zest of lime.
desc_en_full:
The Bitter Truth Golden Falernum Liqueur is an exotic spiced liqueur on the base of the finest Caribbean Rum. In ancient Rome Falernum was the name of the most favoured and most expensive wine. Modern Falernum has almost nothing in common with its ancient predecessor except its rich golden tint. This sweet liqueur was usually flavoured with limes, almonds, cloves and ginger, and gained in importance during the tiki era in the middle of the 20th century.

With an ABV of 18 % Golden Falernum is a very distinctive feature among its category. The full-bodied Caribbean Rum which is used as a base supports the complex exotic flavour profile composed of spicy ginger, citrus fruits, almonds and allspice. This Falernum can be sipped neat or on ice but it also goes well with aged spirits like Whisk(e)y, Rum and Brandy. And of course it's an indispensable ingredient in numerous Tiki Drinks.

desc_en_short: Extraordinary bitters, Bittermens Hopped Grapefruit Bitters are made with grapefruit essences and peels, hops for bitterness, and bottled at 53% abv. We recommend these be used in cocktails such as Daiquiris, Gimlets, Gins and Tonic or even Tequila.
desc_en_full:
The essence of grapefruit combined with the most noble of hops. We recommend adding a dash to stirred cocktails made with tequila and long drinks made with gin.When is a grapefruit not just a grapefruit?

Avid beer drinkers know that certain varieties of hops, when used in high concentration, deliver a strong grapefruit flavor. We've decided to use the grapefruity quality of Pacific Northwest hops along with grapefruit peel to help create a concoction with layer upon layer of vegetal bitterness. Designed to enhance young (silver) rums, tequilas and mezcal, the Hopped Grapefruit bitters play well with most cocktails where you need a little citrus punch. Try it in a daiquiri, gimlet, or even a gin and tonic (with a splash of maraschino).

name: Plantation  3 Stars White Rum  (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Plantation 3 Stars is a blend of rums from three islands: Trinidad (rum aged two or three years), Barbados (young rum), and Jamaica (old rum). This final blend was carefully thought out by our cellar master and each island brings its own aromatic characteristics.
desc_en_full:
Distillation

Barbados: Column & Pot, Trinidad Column, Jamaica Pot

Tropical and continental ageing

Blend of Barbados unaged & Trinidad 2 to 3 years filtered on Plantation proprietory filtration process by which we filter out the wood tannins whilst keeping the aromatic compound of wood aging. Much better than charcoal filtration which is non selective and wipes out all color of taste. Jamaica Unaged as well as a bit of 10 years Pot Still Jamaica.

Freshness and Complexity

A large majority of rum producers offer a white rum variety. Maison Ferrand wanted to produce a white rum that would stand out from the others using rums aged and unaged and taking advantage of the exceptional talent of the House for blending cognacs. Plantation 3 Stars is a blend of rums from three islands: Trinidad (rum aged two or three years), Barbados (young rum), and Jamaica (old rum). This final blend was carefully thought out by our cellar master and each island brings its own aromatic characteristics.

Taste Profile

"Opening aroma offers delicate scents of brown sugar, honey, coffee bean, cocoa bean; later whiffs following further aeration encounter baking spices, especially, vanilla, allspices, cloves; a bouquet of delicacy. Entry is biscuity, batter-like, yet is bittersweet and light-bodied; mid-palate features round, supple flavors of dark chocolate, root beer, toasted marshmallow, egg cream, vanilla soda. Finishes fast, refined, almost fragile. Serious white rum at its grassy, herbal best."

name: The Pirates Gold Rum (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
The Pirates Gold Rum was inspired by the legendary seafarers and privateers who once chased fortune and freedom across the Caribbean’s sunlit waters. It pays homage to their courage, their rebellion, and their endless love for life at sea.

Crafted from the finest molasses and pure sugarcane, this golden rum is double-distilled in traditional copper pot stills to preserve its deep, aromatic character. It is then aged in American oak barrels, previously used for whiskey, where time imparts warmth, richness, and complexity.

The result is a golden spirit that glows like the setting sun over the ocean — smooth, elegant, and layered with flavors shaped by the sea breeze and the patient touch of oak.

The Pirates Gold Rum isn’t merely a drink; it’s a legend reborn — a celebration of freedom, craftsmanship, and the golden heart of the Caribbean.
desc_en_full:
A rich and full-bodied rum, elegantly balanced between sweetness, spice, and oak.

Aroma: Deep and inviting, with notes of caramel, brown sugar, and vanilla, accented by hints of dried fruits, toasted oak, and spice.

Palate: Smooth and warming, revealing layers of burnt sugar, ripe banana, black pepper, and milk chocolate, touched with a trace of maritime saltiness.

Texture: Full and silky, offering a luxurious mouthfeel with perfect balance.

Finish: Long and elegant, leaving behind lingering tones of toffee, vanilla bean, and charred oak.

BODY & COLOR

desc_en_full:
OUR STORY

We work closely with local, family-owned farms in Jalisco, Mexico to bring you the smoothest, most natural, and best tasting tequila possible. Only the highest quality ingredients are used in its creation.

The area code 818 is home to me, but the earth is home to all of us. So, from the agaves we grow, to the production of the bottle, sustainability and transparency of our supply chain was our core focus. We feel a great sense of responsibility and pride to have a positive impact on individuals, communities, and the environment. We've had so many magical nights drinking 818 and we hope you enjoy it as much as we do.

Awards

- NY int'l spirits competition Gold medal 2022

- Sip awards innovation award 2020

name: Los Cabos Silver Tequila (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
Los Cabos Silver Tequila is a celebration of Mexico’s sun, sea, and spirit — inspired by the iconic coastal beauty of Cabo San Lucas, where the desert meets the Pacific Ocean. This tequila embodies the purity of the landscape: fresh, bright, and full of life.

Crafted from 100% Blue Weber Agave, grown in the highlands of Jalisco, each agave plant is harvested by hand at full maturity, ensuring rich sugar content and deep natural flavor. The hearts of the agave (piñas) are slowly roasted in traditional stone ovens, then double-distilled in copper pot stills to preserve their crisp, herbal essence.

Unaged and crystal clear, Los Cabos Silver Tequila represents tequila in its most authentic form — clean, luminous, and refreshing. It is the spirit of freedom, ocean breeze, and the Mexican soul captured in a glass.

A pure, vibrant tequila that celebrates freshness, clarity, and craftsmanship.

Texture: Crisp, clean, and silky — perfectly refreshing.

Finish: Medium-long and elegant, with lingering notes of citrus, pepper, and sea salt.

name: Douglas Laing's  Big Peat (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Douglas Laing's Big Peat is a Blended (as we have to call them now) Islay malt containing spirit from Ardbeg, Caol Ila, Bowmore and, shockingly, Port Ellen. Not as full-on as the name might suggest, but with pedigree like that at this price Big Peat is a nailed-on winner. Picked up the top prize in its category at the World Whisky Awards 2010.
desc_en_full:
Our "Vatted" Islay Malt

Big Peat: A small batch bottling traditionally non chill-filtered, not coloured and containing ONLY Islay Malt Whiskies! 
 
 But what exactly is a Vatted Malt? It is what the Scotch Whisky industry has traditionally called "a marriage of Malts". Big Peat contains Malt Whisky ONLY from the island of Islay – some say it's the ultimate taste of Islay… we'd be bound to agree!
 
 With a PPM of 40, Big Peat is a feisty Islay character but with a sweet side too – 'he' carries a big peaty ("phenolic"), beachy, oceanic, rather ashy selection of malts only from the magical Whisky Island of Islay… Ardbeg, Caol Ila, Bowmore and (even the now closed) Port Ellen to name but a few!
 
 We do not chill-filter – we prefer a more old fashioned traditional approach – so you will detect a massive amplification of sea-faring qualities on the nose, palate, and finish…
 
 Nose:  Fresh, salty and clean, developing to malted barley dried over peat with a damp earthy character
 
 Palate: Ashes, sweet tar, beaches and smoking chimneys 
 
 Finish: Long and lingering, it replicates the palate with salty, tangy liquorice, smoke, bonfire ashes - being foremost: PHENOLIC

name: Johnnie Walker  Blue Label (200 ml)
food_matching: Dark Chocolate, Smoked Meats, Aged Cheese, Nuts
desc_en_short: Johnnie Walker  Blue Label is a big flavoured whisky that reflects the Walker family's belief that neither whisky age alone, nor whisky from a single location, is enough to achieve the creation of an unrivalled masterpiece today. Layers of big flavour, deep richness and smoke, layers of honey and fruit and an incredibly smooth finish deliver the truly rare character of Johnnie Walker  Blue Label.
desc_en_full:
JOHNNIE WALKER BLUE LABEL is a commemoration of Alexander Walker's remarkable 1867 Old Highland Whisky. Its epic story is one that has seen it travel it from the four corners of Scotland, to the four corners of the earth.

Using blending skills learnt from his father, carefully honed through the blending of teas and spices from the New World, Alexander Walker harnessed whisky flavours from across Scotland to craft a blend of truly rare quality and sent it 'wherever ships could sail'.

Crafted in a time of remarkable achievements, in an age that would change the world forever, Old Highland Whisky was seen as a defining statement in whisky excellence and quickly became a dominant force in the global Scotch Whisky market.

Alexander's entrepreneurial vision knew no bounds, and as a result, JOHNNIE WALKER whiskies were being sold in more than 120 countries by 1920 winning worldwide acclaim.

desc_en_full: "Rich and weighty with heady and smoky aromatics, this expression of Ardbeg is both intensely flavoured and perfectly integrated. At full strength, the initial aroma is a beguiling mix of warm Christmas cake, walnut oil and parma violets fused with fresh ocean spice, cedar and pine needles falling from the Christmas tree. A smouldering smoky coal fire and the deep scent of well-oiled leather brings warmth to the scene. The sweetness of treacle toffees and chocolate-coated raisins bring indulgence through the smoke. With water, the deep smokiness increases in intensity, reminiscent of a Christmas pudding on fire. Tarry smoke and diesel engine oil enter the room bringing smells of an age gone by. Rich flowering currants and warm baked banana and walnut bread are served with simmering mocha espresso."

The Balvenie Caribbean Cask 14 year old single malt whisky has been matured in traditional oak whisky casks for 14 years, and then ‘finished’ in casks that previously held Caribbean rum.

name: Woodford Reserve (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
Woodford Reserve would be considered a more complex bourbon - with over 200 flavours notes, including orange, vanilla, tobacco, cocoa, grain and wood. This complexity is mainly attributed to extra time in the spent barrel and its higher rye content, which provides a slightly spicier, less sweet proposition.

Awards :

Master American Whiskey - 2018 : The Travel Retail Masters (The Spirits Business)

Gold Bourbon - Premium - 2018 : The American Whiskey Masters (The Spirits Buiness)

Woodford Reserve Old Fashioned Recipe :

  • Woodford Reserve = 45 - 60ml
  • White Sugar 1 teaspoon or Syrup = 10 ml
  • Bitter = 3 drops 
  • Stir and garnish with Orange Peel
desc_en_full:
5 SOURCES OF FLAVOR

GRAIN

The best grains make the best bourbon. Woodford Reserve is made with 72% corn, 18% rye, and 10% malted barley. Our grain recipe is uniquely defined by our higher rye content. Rye's higher price is worth the investment as it allows our whiskey to have a spicy character.

WATER

Woodford Reserve uses iron-free, limestone filtered spring water, which is full of rich minerals that are key in our unique fermentation process. Kentucky's climate and watershed make it the best place in the world to make bourbon whiskey. This unique water source contributes to building the overall flavor of Woodford Reserve.

FERMENTATION

Our traditional cypress fermentors stand like silent giants in the Woodford Reserve distillery. Visitors are overwhelmed by the sweet aroma of corn, rye and malted barley preparing for distillation. Our fermenation process lasts for up to 7 days, which is among the longest in the industry. Slower fermentation allows Woodford Reserve to develop a complex range of flavors. What is sacrificed in time, is rewarded in quality.

DISTILLATION

Imported from Scotland, our copper pot stills go about their business with a quiet determination, giving Woodford Reserve the deep, robust flavor we're known for. The only hint of their efforts comes in the hiss and pressure of triple pot distillation. Our dynamic flavor combination is crafted using both triple pot distillation and double distillation in a column thumper still. It's not the easiest way to make bourbon, but we feel that it is the best.

MATURATION

Tucked deep in the heart of Woodford County hides one of the oldest steam-heated barrel houses in Kentucky. Our barrel houses were built to encourage the mingling of bourbon and wood. The aroma that lingers in the air is the personification of our care and concern of craft and process.

desc_en_short:
90 Points in Whisky Advocate - Unlike their collegues in Scotland, Japanese distillers do not exchange stock for their blending requirements. This means that each distillery is set up to produce a wide range of styles. Hakushu uses four different types of malt (unpeated to heavy) run through four pairs of differently-shaped stills. Each single malt is a different blend of these bases. While still herbal, this example shows more sherried notes along with sour orange zest, tropical fruit, and some smoke.

Awards :

Category Winner Japanese Single Malt 13 to 20 Years - 2020 : World Whiskies Awards
desc_en_full: 90 Points in Whisky Advocate - Unlike their collegues in Scotland, Japanese distillers do not exchange stock for their blending requirements. This means that each distillery is set up to produce a wide range of styles. Hakushu uses four different types of malt (unpeated to heavy) run through four pairs of differently-shaped stills. Each single malt is a different blend of these bases. While still herbal, this example shows more sherried notes along with sour orange zest, tropical fruit, and some smoke.

desc_en_short:
The 12 year old expression from Old Pulteney released in mid-2018 was entirely aged in bourbon casks, resulting in a heavy influence of bourbon sweetness - which helps to balance the characteristic coastal salinity of the whisky. An excellent introduction to Old Pulteney (though well worth a look even if you've already been introduced).

Double Gold Award The San Francisco World Spirits Competition 2023

GOLD win at the San Francisco World Spirits Competition 2021
desc_en_full:
History

Old Pulteney Distillery is located in the far north of the Scottish mainland. Wick is an area rich in maritime history, once known for its herring fisheries, and the distillery's opening coincided with this boom in the fishing trade. The fishing industry all but died out there, but the distillery has long endured. Fun fact: the shape of the bottle with the bulbous neck is a reflection of the unique stills, which are missing the traditional "swan neck" because they were too tall for the original still house. This single malt is matured entirely in American oak ex-bourbon barrels, mostly second-fill. 

Tasting Note

Nose : Honeycomb, vanilla tablet, soft sawdust oakiness and oatcakes.

Palate : Chocolate peanuts, mint, citrus peels, maritime elements and white pepper.

Finish : Toffee, brine and lemon.

Tamnavulin Double Cask was the first release from the distillery since the 1990s! Over the past 20 or so years, the distillery changed hands from Invergordon Distillers to Whyte & MacKay, closed a few years later and reopened in 2007. Much of its single malt output is used in blends, but a few casks of single malt would sneak out as indie bottlings to allow us a rare taste of Tamnavulin - but now they've got their very own expression! name: Tamdhu  12 Year Old Speyside Single Malt Whisky (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
The new 12 Year Old single malt reflects Tamdhu’s commitment to producing ‘only the best’ sherry cask matured whisky, and will replace the existing Tamdhu 10 Year Old.

Benefiting from an extra two years of maturation, and bottled at 43% ABV, Tamdhu 12 Year Old has been 100% matured for a full 12 years in first fill and refill American and European Oloroso sherry casks from the cooperages of Jerez, in Spain’s ‘sherry triangle’. The result is a complex and rich single malt whisky that delivers a wealth of complexity, with flavours of cinnamon, dried fruit, banana and classic sherry oak depth.

Tamdhu 12 Year Old launches globally with a bold new marketing campaign and packaging design, including a new strapline, ‘Only the Best’ and activation, ‘From Spain to Speyside’; a reference to the top-quality Spanish Oloroso sherry oak casks which were recorded as being first used by Tamdhu Distillery to mature its Speyside single malt whisky 120 years ago, in 1898.

Awards :

Gold Distillers' Single Malts 12 years and under - 2020 : International Spirits Challenge
desc_en_full:
Tasting Notes

Nose : Rich and enticing; iced cinnamon rolls, orange boiled sweets, fresh sweet oak with raisins and a faint hint of mint.

Palate : Silky texture that coats the mouth with banana, berry jam, malt biscuit and classic sherry oak depth.

Finish : Pleasantly long and balanced with ground spice, dried fruit and soft Scottish tablet, finally unveiling the merest whisper of peat smoke.

desc_en_short:
Our deep and complex sherry cask finish richly illustrates our expertise in marrying casks, a skill we've been honing since our distillery was founded more than 120 years ago. We begin by maturing our spirit in American oak casks, for our traditional Speyside style. Then when the whisky is ready, we finish our single malt for several months in Oloroso sherry casks, sourced by our Master Distiller from Jerez in Spain. Our single malt emerges from those sherry casks filled with notes of dried fruity, nuts and spice, to be savoured both by long-time single malt fans and those who are new to whisky.

name: Arran  Quarter Cask (Bothy 4) (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Our Quarter Cask expression is a complex, rich and intensely fruity Single Malt, a cask strength delight and the perfect partner to our Sherry Cask Single Malt!

The Arran Single Malt selected for this cask strength expression was initially matured in first-fill ex-Bourbon barrels before being transferred for secondary maturation into smaller Quarter casks also made of the finest American oak. The process of transferring our whisky into the smaller 125 litre sized casks produces far greater contact between the whisky & oak and a faster, more intense maturation.

The end result is a full-bodied expression of Arran Single Malt combining power and finesse full of rich vanilla sweetness and bold peppery spice. Bottling at natural Cask Strength without chill-filtration or artificial colouring of any sort presents this whisky in its purest form. Small casks would have been a common feature of the Arran whisky trade in the eighteenth & nineteenth centuries as they were easier to handle and transport around the island. With our much-loved Quarter Cask expression we proudly bring this tradition back to life.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Notes

Nose

Apple tart, pineapple brioche

Palate

The citrus hallmarks of Arran welcome you, and the sweet spice of cinnamon and vanilla promises a memorable dram.

Finish

Sweetness, Honey, Pineapple, Vanilla, Coconut.

name: Bruichladdich  18 Aged Years Old Whisky (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
RE/DEFINE EIGHTEEN

"This is an ultra-high provenance single malt. Every single element of The Bruichladdich Eighteen - from the specific harvest and barely varietals used to the exact casks it has matured in - is fully traceable. Which, for a whisky of this age, is quite incredible.

18 years ago, we distilled spirit from our first ever crop of home-grown Islay barley and our passion to diversify and champion our cereal growing programme has never wavered. It’s about making delicious whisky with real provenance which also benefits our community – and The Bruichladdich Eighteen is the embodiment of this ethos.”

MATURATION PROFILE

The single malt has matured exclusively on Islay primarily in ex-bourbon casks. These bourbon casks, together with a smaller number of Sauternes and Port casks, are vatted and married for nine months before bottling to balance Bruichladdich’s signature floral and fruity character.
desc_en_full:
TASTING NOTES

 COLOUR : Apricot.

NOSE : Beautifully fragrant with ripe peach and honeysuckle, hints of violet and lavender come through on the nose – balanced with vanilla and creamed coconut from the toasted American oak. A medley of tropical fruit comes to the fore, with melon, peaches, apricots, and vibrant citrus, while the influence of the oak brings honey, caramel vanilla custard and rich crème brûlée.

PALATE : Luxurious and viscous, honey, butterscotch and fresh fruits coat the palate, followed by cereal notes of sweet, syrup soaked porridge, barley sugar and toasted oak. Vibrant tropical fruit and the subtle presence of toasted oak with a hint of leather follow, with honeycomb adding to the smooth, sweet character.

FINISH : The quintessential character of Bruichladdich single malt comes through on the finish. The 18 Year Old has the classic minerality for which Bruichladdich is renowned, perfectly balanced with oak, stoned fruit and a delicate floral bouquet.

desc_en_full:
Jack Daniel's Old No.7 McLaren X JD Edition 2024 (700 ml)

In celebration of Jack Daniel’s partnership with McLaren Formula 1 team, fans now have the opportunity to own a piece of brand history - in a bottle. This limited edition bottle design for Jack Daniel’s Tennessee Whiskey commemorates the two pioneering founders behind each of these iconic brands – Mr. Jack Daniel himself and Bruce McLaren.

This limited edition bottle will be available in select Formula 1 race cities across the globe as early as February 2024.

Master distiller Jeff Arnett of Lynchburg, Tennessee first selects one out of every hundred barrels for its unique and expressive qualities. Once these special casks have been identified, they are moved to the very top of the distillery’s rickhouses, where extreme temperature changes facilitate dramatic interactions between the whiskey and the wood (by law, “Tennessee whiskey” must be aged new, charred American oak barrels).

On the nose, Jack Daniel's Black Label offers gentle aromas of caramel, vanilla, and butterscotch. A hint of wood is apparent and toffee notes begin to appear in the glass as it opens up.

Body and Palate :

Since switching to its 80 proof formula from 86 proof, some claim Jack Daniel's Black Label has lost some of its luster. I disagree but I do think that the 80 proof formulation results in a lighter mouthfeel, which isn't unpleasant, just different.

On the palate, Jack Daniel's has a smooth smoky sweetness. One reviewer has descibed it as having a corn syrup note to it and I agree. Jack Daniels has a reputation for a light licorice note to all of their whiskies and its readily apparent in Black Label. The licorice note isn't overwhelming and lends a nice background note to flavors of vanilla, toasted oak, fresh brewed coffee, nuts and English toffee. What is most interesting about Jack Daniel's Black Label to me is the number of flavors that come into play over the course of drinking it. I challenge you to sit down and think about the flavors appearing on your palate. You'll be surprised at just how much complexity and depth there is hidden under the blanket of vanilla at the front of the palate.

Finish :

Jack Daniel's Black Label offers a quick, sweet finish. Nice oak notes, a hint of spices, and perhaps a touch of cedar mingle with a warm caramel finish that gives way to white pepper and walnuts.

name: Lark  Tasmanian Peated Single Malt (500 ml)
desc_en_short:
Tasmanian Peated shows that where there's smoke, there's feeling. This is a new frontier, our island's own peat bog & post malt smoking process, creates a lightly peated whisky for a bold palate. A delicately smoky dram to ignite the senses, best enjoyed by anyone, any way they like.

Awards

2024 Global World Whisky Master - Gold Medal

2023 Global World Whisky Master - Master Medal
desc_en_full:
Born from fire and fervour, Tasmanian Peated shows that where there's smoke, there's feeling. This is a new frontier, our island's own peat bog and post malt smoking process, creates a lightly peated whisky for a bold palate. This single malt whisky in The LARK Signature Collection is signed by Master Blender Chris Thomson, representing his mark on the new world of whisky. Best enjoyed by anyone, any way they like.​

A taste of the Tasmanian spirit. ​ A delicately smoky dram to ignite the senses. ​ Embark on a journey through the rugged depths of Tasmania, where enticing hints of woodsmoke dance on beautifully pristine air. This special single malt starts where our Central Highlands peat bog and the wild landscape meet. ​ ​

A wonderfully warming drop for the bold palate, where earthy peat and delicate florals transport you to the untamed.​ ​ Enjoy neat by the crackle of a roaring fire, or elevate your favourite cocktail with a delicate touch of smoke.

Post malt smoking is quite unique in the whisky world. Traditionally the barley is kiln dried after the malting process. Our barley arrives at our Tasmanian distillery already malted, so ours is a process of post malt smoking. We smoke 2 tons of malted barley with 100kg of our very own peat over an extended period. This style of peat smoked barley imparts a different flavour profile than traditionally peated whisky. Expect more chocolate and earthy roasted coffee notes to come through.​

name: Arran Sauternes Cask Finish (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Matured in oak casks for eight
years and is then 'finished' in a
selection of Sauternes wine
casks. These Bordeaux sweet
white wine casks are
specifically selected by our
Master Distiller from a talented
small producer to ensure the
notes from both wine and
whisky make the perfect
marriage.
desc_en_full:
The Arran Sauternes Cask Finish is often described as the "dessert wine" of the whisky world. It utilizes casks from the Sauternes region of Bordeaux, France, famous for producing some of the most expensive and luscious sweet white wines on the planet. Obtaining these casks is rare and costly, but the Arran distillery uses them to create a whisky of exceptional elegance and sophistication.

Sauternes wine is made from grapes affected by "noble rot," which concentrates the sugars and acidity. When the Arran spirit enters these casks for its final maturation phase, it extracts these concentrated honey and apricot notes. The result is a whisky that is incredibly bright and golden, bursting with stone fruit flavors and a distinct floral quality that highlights the lighter side of the Arran character.

Despite the sweetness suggested by the cask, the whisky retains a savory, salty edge—a reminder of the distillery's island location. The interplay between the honeyed wine influence and the saline, malted barley creates a complex "sweet and savory" experience. It is a luxurious spirit that pairs beautifully with food or serves as a sophisticated digestif.

name: Seghesio  Sonoma Old Vin Zinfandel
desc_en_short:
Bold and spicy with rich, briary fruit, our Old Vine Zinfandel showcases the complexity
and intensity that only truly old vines can yield. For us, “Old Vine” means that the vines are at least 50 years
old, and we source grapes for this wine from exceptional vineyards that are closer to 75 years old on
average.
desc_en_full:
VITICULTURE & WINEMAKING

The grapes were harvested and sorted by hand to ensure uniformly ripened fruit is delivered to the winery. After being lightly crushed they were fermented in small open-top stainless-steel tanks and mixed using pneumatic punch down devices - to facilitate extraction and pumped over to allow for gentle aeration. Freerun wine is drained to barrel after 8 to 12 days on skins and allowed to complete alcoholic fermentation and undergo malolactic conversion. The wines are allowed to evolve for 10 months prior to blending and returned to barrel to integrate for 5 more months prior to bottling. Aged 15 months in 13% New French Oak; 87% Neutral French and American Oak. The grapes were harvested between September 1st and September 27th, 2021.

name: Antinori  Chianti Classico Riserva "Marchese" Tignanello DOCG
wine_color: An intense ruby red in color
style: Sangiovese, 10% Cabernet, And Other Complementary Red Varietals
flavor_tags: Mineral, Leather, Tobacco, Cherry, Earthy, Cedar, Plum
desc_en_short: The Marchese Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva is one of Tuscany's most prestigious single-estate Riservas — deep, complex Sangiovese from the historic Tignanello estate with concentrated dark cherry, tobacco, cedar, and the firm mineral backbone of Chianti Classico at its finest.
desc_en_full:
The Marchese Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva represents the pinnacle of the family's Chianti Classico production, made only in the finest vintages from select parcels of the Tignanello estate. This is Sangiovese pushed to its fullest, most noble expression — powerful yet refined, structured yet harmonious.

Aged in French barriques followed by extended bottle maturation, the wine develops extraordinary complexity over time. Deep ruby-garnet in colour, it opens on a rich, layered nose of concentrated morello cherry, dried plum, tobacco, cedar, leather, and earthy mineral notes with a hint of iron. The palate is full-bodied and structured, with firm but polished tannins, bright acidity, and a finish of remarkable length that evolves in the glass for hours.

This Riserva rewards patience — it is approachable young but truly shines after 5–10 years of cellaring. Ideal partners: bistecca alla Fiorentina, roast lamb, aged Pecorino or Parmigiano, wild boar ragù.

name: Castello Della Sala By Antinori  Pinot Nero Umbria IGT
desc_en_short: This Pinot Nero from Antinori's Castello della Sala estate in Umbria is a refined, elegant red — light-to-medium bodied with cherry, raspberry, earthy spice, and silky tannins, showing how well Burgundy's great grape adapts to central Italy's cooler hillside terroir.
desc_en_full:
The Castello della Sala estate in Umbria is better known for white wines — particularly the legendary Cervaro della Sala Chardonnay — but Antinori also produces a Pinot Nero that demonstrates the estate's range and ambition. Planted on the estate's cooler hillside vineyards at altitude, where the diurnal temperature range preserves acidity and aromatic complexity, the Pinot Nero finds an unexpected but convincing home.

Fermented in open-top tanks with gentle extraction to preserve delicacy, and aged in French oak barriques, the wine is translucent ruby in colour — almost Burgundian in appearance. The nose is subtle and refined: morello cherry, raspberry, dried rose, subtle earthiness, and warm spice. On the palate it is light-bodied and silky, with vibrant acidity, fine tannins, and an elegant, long finish.

Serve slightly cool (around 16°C) with roast chicken, duck breast, salmon, mushroom-based dishes, and mild charcuterie.

name: Marques De Riscal  Gran Reserva, DOC Rioja
desc_en_short: Marqués de Riscal Gran Reserva is a benchmark Rioja built from Tempranillo, Graciano, and Mazuelo, aged to develop its signature garnet-brick hue alongside deep layers of black cherry, marzipan, dark chocolate, and a long, refined finish that defines classic Rioja elegance.
desc_en_full:
Few names in Spanish wine carry the weight of Marqués de Riscal. Founded in 1858 in Elciego, Rioja Alavesa, the estate is widely credited with introducing Bordeaux-influenced winemaking to northern Spain, and its Gran Reserva remains one of the clearest expressions of why that legacy endures. Crafted from a classic Rioja blend of Tempranillo, Graciano, and Mazuelo, this is wine that earns its classification honestly — the DOCa Rioja Gran Reserva designation demands a minimum of five years' maturation, with at least two in oak and two further years in bottle before release.

The 2019 vintage emerges with a deep garnet core edged in characteristic brick, a visual cue to the careful cellaring behind it. On the nose, black cherry and dried plum unfold alongside a seductive undercurrent of marzipan and bitter dark chocolate — the hallmarks of time in American oak married to the structured fruit Rioja Alavesa vineyards reliably deliver. The palate is full-bodied with high, well-knit tannins and brisk acidity that keeps everything in motion through a long, seamless finish.

This is a wine made for the table. Serve it alongside a slow-roasted leg of lamb with rosemary and garlic, a chargrilled Ibérico ribeye, or a generously aged Manchego or Idiazábal. A decant of 30–45 minutes will reward patience, opening the wine to its full aromatic complexity. Those who can resist will find it continues to develop beautifully over the next decade.

name: Haras de Pirque By Antinori  Cabernet Sauvignon Albis
desc_en_short: Albis is the flagship wine of Haras de Pirque, Antinori's prestigious Chilean estate in the Maipo Alto — a powerful, refined Cabernet Sauvignon blend showing the best of the Andes' influence: dark fruit concentration, elegant structure, and mineral depth.
desc_en_full:
Haras de Pirque is Antinori's joint venture with the Matte family on a historic horse-breeding estate in the Maipo Alto, one of Chile's most prestigious wine zones at the foot of the Andes. Albis is the estate's flagship wine — the result of bringing Antinori's Tuscan winemaking philosophy to one of South America's greatest terroirs.

Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant with complementary Carmenère, the wine is aged in French oak barriques. The Maipo Alto's unique combination of altitude, Andean soils, and extreme diurnal temperature variation creates wines of remarkable concentration and freshness. Deep ruby-purple, Albis opens on intense aromas of blackcurrant, plum, eucalyptus, cedar, graphite, and a distinctive Andean mineral quality. The palate is full-bodied and structured, with fine-grained tannins, vibrant acidity, and a long, complex finish.

Pair with prime beef cuts, lamb, game, and mature hard cheeses. A wine that rewards 5–10 years of cellaring.

name: Chateau Belgrave  Haut-Medoc Grand Cru
desc_en_short: The nose opens with an intense, complex bouquet, unveiling aromas of blackcurrant and blackberry. These black-fruit notes are joined by nuances of cedar and licorice, enhanced by subtle touches of spice and toasted oak. On the palate, the attack is powerful and generous, carried by a full structure and refined, velvety tannins. The palate reveals a fine concentration of ripe fruit, supported by balanced acidity that brings freshness and elegance. The finish lingers with persistence, leaving a harmonious, complex impression.
desc_en_full: Founded in 1845 by Bruno Devez, Château Belgrave is a 5th Grand Cru Classé (1855 Classification) located in the heart of the Médoc, in Bordeaux, on 60 hectares of deep gravel soils at the gateway to Saint-Julien. At the beginning of the 20th century, Marcel Alibert definitively established the name “Belgrave” and distinguished himself in defending the crus classés. Since 1979, the estate has belonged to Maison Dourthe. The ferret on the label recalls the former hunting lodge the estate originally was.

name: Duckhorn  Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 10% Merlot, 6% Petit Verdot, 2% Cabernet Franc
desc_en_short: Duckhorn's signature Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon blends estate and top-grower fruit into a rich, seamlessly balanced red with dark fruit depth, polished oak, and firm tannins — expressive enough to enjoy now, yet structured for years of rewarding cellaring.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in Napa Valley as Duckhorn Vineyards. Founded in 1976 and producing Cabernet Sauvignon since 1978, the winery helped define the modern benchmark for premium Napa red wine — and this bottling remains one of the most consistent expressions of that legacy. Drawing on a carefully curated blend of estate vineyard fruit alongside grapes sourced from trusted top-tier Napa Valley growers, the wine captures the full spectrum of the valley's celebrated terroir in a single, compelling bottle.

The blend is anchored by 82% Cabernet Sauvignon, with 10% Merlot lending plush mid-palate softness and early approachability, 6% Petit Verdot contributing deep colour and floral intrigue, and a touch of Cabernet Franc adding aromatic lift and savory complexity. The result is a full-bodied wine that feels both generous and precise — ripe blackcurrant, dark cherry, and plum fruit framed by notes of cedar, dark chocolate, and warm baking spice, all woven through with well-integrated French and American oak.

Tannins are firm but refined, giving the wine genuine cellar potential, while the medium acidity keeps the palate fresh and focused through a long, satisfying finish. Pour this alongside a prime ribeye, slow-roasted lamb shoulder, or an aged Napa cheddar for a pairing that matches the wine's scale without overshadowing its nuance. Equally impressive at a special dinner or simply as the centerpiece of a serious mid-week pour.

desc_en_full: Our Zinfandel is unique in that it’s blended with one of our favorite selections of Petite Sirah. Even though the Zinfandel from this terrific vintage showed great complexity on its own, we added Petite Sirah to enhance the structure of the wine. A combination of French and American oak enhanced the fruit’s spice qualities, giving the wine some shoulders as well. The Director’s Cut Zinfandel demonstrates a lush full body and velvety palate. name: Grosset  Gaia
desc_en_short: An outstanding Gaia from an outstanding Clare Valley vintage. This single vineyard red is a blend of cabernet sauvignon and cabernet franc from a rocky, isolated, wind-swept two hectare block close planted by Grosset. It’s a remarkable blend with voluminous red berry aromatics, deep opulent blackcurrant, dark plum and redcurrant characters with hints of licorice, chocolate and wild brambles. It has layer after layer of concentrated flavours, power and density, balanced by substantial fine-grain tannins. Complex, arresting and satisfying.
desc_en_full: Ageing potential: up to 20 years Site: Grosset Gaia Vineyard, Clare Valley, the highest (and coolest) vineyard in the region. East facing site (ACO certified) Fruit Flavours: Brambly aromas, blackcurrant, black plum Profile: Harmonious, balanced, elegant, complex Fining: Nil- suitable for vegans and vegetarians

name: Chateau De Fieuzal  2020
wine_color: Bright cherry red colour with purple highlights.
grape_variety: Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot
desc_en_short:
Chateau de Fieuzal is named in honour of the family that owned it at the start of the nineteenth century. After that, the Bordeaux wine property was owned by the Griffon family. The Griffon’s, who were lucky to have connections to Pope Léon XIII in Bordeaux, were able to fill the Vatican’s cellars with their wines from Pessac Leognan. de Fieuzal was purchased by the Ricard family. The Ricard’s owned multiple Bordeaux wine estates including Domaine de Chevalier and Malartic-Lagravière. Today, Chateau de Fieuzal is owned by Lochlann Quinn, the chairman of Allied Irish Banks. 

The Bordeaux wine vineyards are planted on sand and gravel soils. The 39 hectare vineyard is planted to red and white grape varietals. For the reds, 60% Cabernet Sauvignon, 33% Merlot, 4.5% Cabernet Franc and 2.5% Petit Verdot.

name: Montecillo  Grand Reserva
wine_color: Ruby red with cherry highlights and a bright rim.
desc_en_short: Montecillo Gran Reserva from Rioja delivers a compelling blend of 90% Tempranillo and 10% Graciano, earning 93 points from Wine Enthusiast for its layered black cherry, caramel, and coffee character, plush tannins, and an elegantly violet-scented finish.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in Rioja as Montecillo, a historic bodega with roots stretching back to 1874 and a long-standing reputation for crafting wines that honour the region's classical traditions while ageing gracefully over decades. The Gran Reserva sits at the pinnacle of the Montecillo range — a wine only released in the finest vintages and shaped by years of patient ageing in both American oak barrels and bottle.

Anchored by Tempranillo (90%) and lifted by a precise dose of Graciano (10%), the blend brings together Rioja's most iconic grape with a variety celebrated for its vivid acidity and aromatic intensity. The result is a wine of considerable complexity: aromas of black cherry and butterscotch give way to layers of pomegranate, caramel, coffee bean, and a subtle whisper of cured bacon — a signature of beautifully integrated oak. Plush, full-bodied tannins build steadily on the palate before softening into a long, violet-strewn finish that lingers with elegance.

Awarded 93 points by Wine Enthusiast (Mike de Simeone, May 2024), the 2015 vintage is drinking magnificently now yet has every structural element to reward further cellaring. Serve it alongside a slow-roasted leg of lamb, a prime rib of beef, or an aged Manchego — this is a wine built for the table and for conversation.

name: Chateau Côtes De Chambeau  Montagne Saint-Emilion
desc_en_short: Brilliant garnet colour. Powerful, slightly spicy nose of wild fruits. Few vanilla and toasted notes can even be detected in some vintages. Initially clean, powerful palate of ripe fruit. A great deal of weight. A full bodied, structured finish showing very good balanced and fine tannins.
desc_en_full:
OVERVIEW

Château Côtes de Chambeau has been a family-run property for three generations. It is located to the North of the Montagne appellation, close to the Lussac border. The vineyard was taken over by Alain and Christophe Baudet, who inherited it from their father, around 2000s.

VINEYARD

Soil : Chalky-clay / Surface : 8 hectares Average age of the vineyard : 20 years Location : The vineyard is located to the North of the Montagne-SaintEmilion appellation and in the commune of Lussac-Saint-Emilion. Grape varieties : 90 % Merlot / 10 % Cabernet Franc

VINIFICATION

Classical vinification in thermo-regulated concrete and stainless steel vats after a period of pre-fermentation maceration. Possibility of microoxygenation. Maceration for 20 to 25 days. Punching on the cap for Cabernet Franc grapes. Vertical pressing.

AWARDS

> 2011 vintage : Gold medal Bordeaux 2012 / Hachette Guide 2015 > 2014 vintage : Silver medal Bordeaux 2015

Food Pairing

name: Chateau Lynch-Moussas  Grand Cru Classé AOC Pauilac
desc_en_short: The wines of Lynch-Moussas are today well known for their deep and dark colour, their red-fruity nose. They are round with a beginning of ripe tannins, followed by the typical hints of blackcurrant of the prestigious wines of Pauillac. These full-bodied wines have a significant length in the mouth.
desc_en_full: Château Lynch-Moussas was once part of the vast estate owned by Count Lynch in the 18th century (along with Lynch-Bages). By the early 20th century, it had fallen into disrepair, only to be saved and painstakingly restored by the Castéja family starting in 1919. The 2021 vintage represents the continuation of this century-long recovery, showcasing a wine that is true to its roots.The estate's terroir is classic Pauillac gravel, capable of producing wines of great structure. However, Lynch-Moussas is often styled to be lighter and more approachable than its massive neighbors. The 2021 vintage, with its cool summer, emphasized this characteristic. The Cabernet Sauvignon provides a sturdy frame of cedar and spice, while the lower alcohol keeps the wine digestible and energetic.This is a "lunch wine" par excellence. It is not built to win blind tastings with brute force; it is built to accompany a meal. It is a savory, refreshing, and historically accurate example of Claret—a wine that speaks softly but with the distinct accent of Pauillac nobility.

name: Domaine Philippe Colin  Santenay Clos Saint Jean
desc_en_short: From a walled premier cru plot in the Grand Clos Rousseau area of Santenay, Philippe Colin's Clos Saint Jean delivers earthy, sappy Pinot Noir with black plum, raspberry, gentle pepper, and smooth tannins — a characterful, terroir-driven Burgundy from young vines on clay-limestone soils.
desc_en_full:
Domaine Philippe Colin, based in Chassagne-Montrachet and better known for its celebrated white Burgundies, has been quietly building an impressive portfolio of reds — and the Santenay Clos Saint Jean is one of its most compelling recent additions. This bottling replaces a former Santenay village blend, stepping up in identity and precision thanks to a single, clearly defined site.

The Clos Saint Jean takes its name from a neighbouring church that watches over the hillside, and the vineyard itself sits within the Grand Clos Rousseau premier cru area of Santenay — one of the Côte de Beaune's most underrated appellations. The vines, now in their early twenties, are planted in a compact, square parcel on classic Burgundian clay and limestone soils, lending the wine its characteristic mineral tension and earthy grip.

In the glass, the nose opens with forest floor, damp mulch, and subtle woodland spice — quintessentially Santenay in its character. The palate is more animated: ripe black plum and sappy dark cherry fill the mid-palate, while a gentle twist of white pepper adds lift. The finish is long and tinged with fresh raspberry, carried across smooth, low-key tannins that make this remarkably easy to enjoy relatively young.

A natural partner for roasted pork loin, duck breast, or a board of aged Époisses and hard Comté, the Clos Saint Jean also shines alongside beef dishes with earthy accompaniments — think mushroom-crusted fillet or slow-braised short rib.

name: Luigi Bosca  Finca Los Nobles Cabernet Bouchet
desc_en_short:
Luigi Bosca Finca Los Nobles · Field Blend · Cabernet Bouchet is a deep ruby
red, robust field blend with mahogany reflections. It has balanced and elegant
aromas, with ripe fruit, spices, moca and smooth smoked notes from its ageing
process. It is impressive in the mouth, with balanced freshness and an intense
spicy character. Well-bodied, mature, with fine and strong tannins and a persistent
finish that allows us to perceive its complexity. It is a terroir red, the pride of the
family, with great aging potential.
desc_en_full:
Wine making

The grapes are hand-picked in 18 to 20 kilograms boxes,
clusters are selected, then they are destemmed and after that
the best grains are chosen. The must is cold-macerated for 3
to 5 days at 10°C to 12°C. Then it is fermented with selected
yeast in 6,000 liters stainless steel tanks, with extraction
works such as delestage and pressings. Once the alcoholic
fermentation is finished, the wine is aged in new oak barrels
where the malolactic fermentation takes place and it rests
for 18 months. After the ageing, the wine is stabilized, filtered
and bottled. It is stored in the winery for at least 12 months
before release.

name: Louis Jadot  Pommard Clos de la Commaraine Premier Cru
desc_en_short:
Characteristics

The Clos de la Commaraine is 4 hectares large ; it is oriented to the East next to the village. The soil is deep and well drained on the surface. Some springs in the underground bring freshness to the soil. Moreover, the soil is quite stony .

Wine making

This premier cru is vinified in vats (the grapes are destemmed) during to 3-4 weeks in order to obtain the real expression of the soil (terroir). It is then put into oak barrels for aging between 15 to 18 months.

name: Louis Jadot  Volnay
desc_en_short:
The Volnay wines combine distinction, nobility, finesse and femininity. They offer an incomparable bouquet of red berries, violets and vanilla.

Elegant, full and generous, Volnay will happily accompany the finest meals like roast meat and poultry. With mellow cheeses like Brie, Chaource or Reblochon, drink an old vintage

This wine will age for 7-10 years in good conditions of constant temperature and humidity.
desc_en_full:
Vineyards situation

Volnay lies roughly in the centre of the Côte de Beaune, bordered by Pommard to the Northeast, Monthélie to the West and Meursault to the South. The south-easterly exposed vineyards produce only red wines and 26 Premiers Crus.

Characteristics

Crowned by a limestone plateau, the Volnay vineyards have a sub soil composed of Argovien marlstone and in its bordering parts of Pommard and Meursault, a Bathonien clay and limestone scree. High in pebbles. The altitude varies between 230 and 370 m above sea level.

Wine making

The fermentation takes place in tank for 3-4 weeks, after which they are aged in barrel for an average of 12 months before being bottled.

name: Henschke  Mount Edelstone Shiraz, Eden Valley
desc_en_short: Deep ruby with ruby hues. Spicy, complex and dark fruited aromas of blackberry, dark red plum and blueberry, with sage, bay leaf, thyme, anise, black pepper, nutmeg and cedar. A rich, plush and beautifully integrated palate offers layers of spicy dark fruits, pepper, sage, musk and clove, balanced by bright acidity, excellent depth and long and elegant fine, velvety tannins.
desc_en_full: The beautiful and historic name Mount Edelstone is a translation from the German Edelstein meaning ‘gemstone’, a reference to the small yellow opals once found in the area. The Mount Edelstone Vineyard was planted in 1912 by Ronald Angas, a descendant of George Fife Angas who founded The South Australian Company and played a significant role in the formation and establishment of South Australia. Unusual for its time, the vineyard was planted solely to shiraz. The ancient 500-million-year-old geology in the vineyard has given rise to soils that are deep red-brown clay-loam to clay, resulting in low yields from the dry-grown, ungrafted centenarian vines. First bottled as a single-vineyard wine in 1952 by fourth-generation Cyril Henschke. By the time Cyril purchased the vineyard from Colin Angas in 1974, Mount Edelstone was already well entrenched as one of Australia’s greatest shiraz wines. Crafted by the Henschke family for over 65 years now, Mount Edelstone is arguably the longest consecutively-produced, single-vineyard wine in Australia.

name: Yalumba  Distinguished Sites Paradox Shiraz
desc_en_short: The Paradox is not what you might expect from a wine from the northern Barossa. It’s not the high octane, lots of new oak stereotypical northern Barossa Shiraz, but rather a softer, more gentle velvet-like and savoury style of wine.
desc_en_full:
Paradox Shiraz

A wet winter and dry spring resulted in smaller berries and bunches, producing grapes of great aromatics and flavour. A long and mild summer with very few heat spikes and cool nights, has contributed to a wine of great balance and beauty.

Made with little intervention, the 2015 Paradox Shiraz used small parcels of grapes fermented with wild yeast in French open top oak vats. The ferment naturally gets warm, allowing the fruit flavours and textures to express themselves, while the tannin extraction is minimal. Natural malolactic fermentation occurs in oak, the majority of which is old and larger format puncheons.

Tasting notes :

An inky crimson wine with complex aromas full of freshly baked plum cake crushed black pepper, violets and fennel with hints of blueberries. The palate is lush with layers of velvet, great structure and flavours reminiscent of satsuma plums, olives, black pepper and anise.The wine finishes savoury and is driven by fine powdery tannins. A great match with Grilled lamb backstrap with chimichurri & roasted potatoes

name: Penfolds  RWT Bin 798 Shiraz
wine_color: Deep mauve – impressive colour and brightness.
desc_en_short: RWT Barossa Valley Shiraz presents an admirable alternative to the multi-regional sourcing and American oak maturation that are hallmarks of Grange, expressing instead, single region Barossa Valley shiraz matured only in French oak. The initials RWT stand for ‘Red Winemaking Trial’, the name given to the project internally when developmental work began in 1995. Naturally, now no longer a ‘Trial’, RWT Shiraz was launched in May 2000 with the 1997 vintage. Its style is opulent and fleshy, contrasting with Grange, which is more muscular and assertive. RWT is made from fruit primarily selected for its aromatic qualities and lush texture. The result is a wine that helps to redefine Barossa shiraz at the highest quality level.
desc_en_full: The initials RWT stand for ‘Red Winemaking Trial’, the name given to the project internally when developmental work began in 1995. Naturally, now no longer a ‘Trial’ RWT Shiraz was launched in May 2000 with the 1997 vintage. Since the 2014 vintage it has been allocated Bin number 798, based on the alpha-numeric telephone punch dial, to reflect its established status within the Penfolds portfolio. These wines have the precision, concentration, persistency and balance to age for many years. The best vintages should last at least thirty years.

name: Famille Perrin  Chateauneuf du Pape - Les Sinards
desc_en_short: Beautiful garnet-red colour with shiny reflections. The nose reveals a complex and warm aromatic bouquet with notes of leather. The palate is elegant with delicious aromas of dried fruit, dates, dried figs and candied grapes with a long finish.
desc_en_full:
TERROIR

Miocene marine deposits covered with alpine diluvium (rounded pebbles). One parcel on Urgonian limestone. The young Beaucastel vines produce more abundant and slightly less concentrated grapes, enabling us to make a wine that is immediately accessible. The grapes from Clos de Châteauneuf-du-Pape offer spicy, complex, dense notes, whilst Le Grès is planted with Grenache on round pebbles that absorb the heat of the day and release it during the night, giving warmth and finesse to the wine. Les Sinard is composed mainly of Grenache, unlike Beaucastel.

name: Vasse Felix  Premier Range Cabernet Suavignon
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 9% Malbec, 2% Petit Verdot, 1% Merlot
desc_en_short: A landmark Margaret River Cabernet Sauvignon with roots stretching back to 1972, the Vasse Felix Premier Range delivers classic varietal purity — rich, structured, and deeply expressive of Wilyabrup's ancient gravel soils — elevated by small additions of Malbec and Petit Verdot.
desc_en_full:
Few wines carry the historical weight of the Vasse Felix Premier Range Cabernet Sauvignon. Vasse Felix was the founding estate of Margaret River wine country, and this bottling has been produced without interruption since 1972 — making it one of Australia's most enduring red wine expressions. That continuity is a testament to just how perfectly Cabernet Sauvignon finds its home in this coastal, Mediterranean-influenced corner of Western Australia.

The fruit draws predominantly from the Wilyabrup sub-district, where ancient gravel loam soils offer exceptional drainage and force the vines to dig deep, yielding grapes of striking concentration and varietal definition. In the winery, individual parcels are fermented separately using indigenous yeasts — a choice that honours the distinct personality of each vineyard block. Extended maceration periods of up to 40 days build the wine's signature tannic architecture and dark fruit depth, while traditional oxidative handling preserves freshness and clarity of character.

The blend is thoughtfully composed: Malbec adds plushness and a velvety mid-palate weight, while Petit Verdot — partly fermented via alternative methods — lifts the nose with violet-edged perfume and a bright, nuanced complexity. The result is a wine of earthy depth and mineral precision, with gravelly tannins that are firm but refined.

Expect dark plum, blackcurrant, and dried herb on the palate, underpinned by pencil shaving, cedar, and iron-tinged minerality. This is a natural partner for slow-roasted lamb shoulder, a seared côte de bœuf, or aged hard cheeses. Equally rewarding with charcuterie and cured meats on a long, relaxed evening.

name: Duckhorn  Decoy Limited Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: This alluring Pinot Noir offers vibrant aromas of red cherry and raspberry, as well as hints of savory spices and forest floor. On the palate, silky smooth tannins and refined notes of toasted French oak accentuate the lush flavors, while carrying the wine to an elegant red berry and spice finish.

Crafted using grapes from some of the most acclaimed Napa Valley and Sonoma Coast vineyards, including our own estate properties, these wines showcase the pinnacle of Decoy winemaking.
