name: Almaviva  2019
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 23% Carmenère (From Peumo), 5% Cabernet Franc, 3% Petit Verdot And 1% Merlot
desc_en_short: Almaviva 2019 is a landmark Maipo Valley Cabernet Sauvignon–led blend, elevated by a generous 23% Carmenère from Peumo, delivering balsamic depth, camphor, dark fruit, and a silky, velvety finish — one of Chile's most celebrated fine-wine icons scoring 97 points from James Suckling.
desc_en_full:
Almaviva is the storied joint venture between Chile's Concha y Toro and Bordeaux's Baron Philippe de Rothschild, crafted to produce a Chilean First Growth of uncompromising ambition. Sourced from the prestigious Puente Alto vineyards in the Maipo Valley — long regarded as Chile's premier Cabernet Sauvignon terroir — Almaviva has consistently ranked among South America's most collectible red wines since its debut vintage in 1996.

The 2019 vintage is a compelling expression shaped by a warm, dry growing season that coaxed exceptional ripeness while preserving structure. The blend — led by 68% Cabernet Sauvignon and unusually enriched by 23% Carmenère sourced from the Peumo district — benefits enormously from the vintage conditions: moderately high temperatures allowed Carmenère to reach its aromatic potential without losing its signature herbal lift. Fermentation with fully destemmed grapes in stainless steel was followed by 18 months of maturation in French oak, 75% of it new, lending polish and depth without overwhelming the fruit.

On the nose, dark ruby concentration gives way to blackberry, blackcurrant, and wild strawberry layered with tobacco, liquorice, and espresso. The palate is full-bodied and round with a pulpy, silky texture — balsamic and camphor-kissed, with fine-grained, saturated tannins woven through a long, serious, dry finish. It is at once powerful and poised.

This is a wine built for the table: pair with a slow-roasted prime rib, a rack of lamb with rosemary, aged Manchego, or a charcuterie selection of fine cured meats. Cellaring through 2030–2035 will reward patience handsomely. Rated 97 points by James Suckling and 95 by Robert Parker's Wine Advocate.

desc_en_short: 93 points Decanter - "Gloria is fast becoming one of the most exciting insider wines of the entire Bordeaux region, and that rare species of a non-classified St-Julien. The fruit here is brambly, textured and autumnal, and again the alcohol seems generous but balanced, with plenty of St-Julien finesse. name: Chateau Mouton Rothschild  2015 (Premiers Crus)
wine_color: The wine has an attractive, deep and dark colour with a purple tint.
desc_en_short: About blend, 2015 Mouton-Rothschild is made from 82% of Cabernet Sauvignon, 16% of Merlot and 2% of Cabernet Franc. This Pauillac has an attractive, deep and dark colour with a purple tint. The refined and elegant nose reveals fruity wild blackberry and bilberry aromas. Notes of toast, liquorice and blond tobacco then emerge to fill out a rich and complex range of aromas. The fresh, full-bodied and slightly saline attack shows great intensity, opening on smooth and creamy tannins which enfold a silky texture lifted by a touch of minerality. The impression on the palate is very full, rich in ripe fruit and peppery spice flavours. An exceptionally long finish concludes a remarkably balanced wine.
desc_en_full:
Château Mouton Rothschild 2015

Climatic conditions

Although 2015 was slightly warmer than average, it was above all very dry (706 mm of rain, compared with an annual average of 862 mm), especially between February and July, from budbreak to veraison. As a result the berries remained small, which explains why the yield was rather low even though we had hopes of a normal crop after flowering.

Higher-than-average rainfall in August and September helped to prevent the ripening process from coming to a halt in a vineyard already stressed by the lack of water. Consequently the grapes matured at rather different rates from one parcel to another, according to the terroir, the grape variety and the age of the vines.

In order to ensure that each variety and each parcel was picked at optimum maturity, the harvest period was the longest in living memory. It lasted 23 days between our three estates, from the first Merlots on 14 September to the last Cabernet Sauvignons on 6 October.

The grapes were magnificent and fermentation took place quickly. The wines immediately showed plenty of colour and a complete array of very intense aromas ranging from red and black fruit to spice and incense.

The tannins are generally very well-rounded, dense and seamlessly smooth. After blending, the wines still have the same aromatic intensity and complex, full-bodied structures which barrel-maturing should further enhance. Our 2015 vintage in Pauillac is very similar in its heft and opulence to the 2005 vintage.

 

Tasting notes

The wine has an attractive, deep and dark colour with a purple tint.

The refined and elegant nose reveals fruity wild blackberry and bilberry aromas. Notes of toast, liquorice and blond tobacco then emerge to fill out a rich and complex range of aromas.

The fresh, full-bodied and slightly saline attack shows great intensity, opening on smooth and creamy tannins which enfold a silky texture lifted by a touch of minerality.

The impression on the palate is very full, rich in ripe fruit and peppery spice flavours. An exceptionally long finish concludes a remarkably balanced wine.

name: Belle Glos  "Las Alturas Vineyard" Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: Rich crimson in color withfragrant aromas of blackberry, sage, cherry, and hints of rich dark chocolate. Bold and elegant on the palate with boysenberry pie, black currant jam, dark plum, and subtle notes of ground nutmeg. Balanced and structured, this wine has beautiful acidity that lingers through to a long, mouthwatering finish.
desc_en_full:
SANTA LUCIA HIGHLANDS

The Spanish term “Las Alturas” means “the heights,” a fitting name for this vineyard located on one of the highest grape-growing benches in the Santa Lucia Highlands AVA up to 1,200 feet. At this elevation, the sunny but very cool climate creates a wine bearing all the hallmarks of this coastal region: robust, expressive, full-bodied, and balanced by a firm acidity

We harvested early in the morning so the fruit would arrive at the winery while the grapes were still cool to preserve the vineyard-fresh flavors. After destemming (but not crushing) the berries, we cold-soaked the fruit for up to two weeks to soften the skins and allow for ideal extraction. During fermentation in a combination of closed and open-top stainless steel fermenters, we used both punch-downs and pump-overs to extract color and tannins, and also to control temperatures and maximize flavors. We aged the wine in 100% French oak (60% new) for up to nine months. After the wine underwent malolactic fermentation, we racked it twice before making the final blend.

name: Louis Jadot  Morey Saint Denis 1er CRU Clos
desc_en_short: Louis Jadot's Morey-Saint-Denis Premier Cru Clos is a structured, aromatic Côte de Nuits Pinot Noir with a blackberry-laden nose, vibrant acidity, and firm tannins that promise a rewarding evolution in the cellar.
desc_en_full:
Nestled within the Côte de Nuits, Morey-Saint-Denis is one of Burgundy's most compelling appellations — often overshadowed by its illustrious neighbours Gevrey-Chambertin and Chambolle-Musigny, yet quietly home to five Grand Crus and a clutch of Premier Cru sites that punch well above their weight. Louis Jadot, the legendary Beaune-based négociant and domaine whose roots trace back to 1859, brings its characteristic precision and reverence for terroir to this Premier Cru Clos bottling — a wine drawn from the appellation's walled vineyard sites that concentrate expression in the classic Burgundian tradition.

The 2018 vintage in Burgundy was a golden year — generous sunshine produced ripe, plush fruit with enough natural acidity to maintain freshness and structure. In the glass, this 100% Pinot Noir unfolds on the nose with vivid blackberry, bilberry, and a whisper of dark cherry, underpinned by a sense of earthy depth and floral lift that is unmistakably Côte de Nuits in character. On the palate, it delivers medium body with a slightly muscular grip — chewy, fine tannins give it a structured backbone that calls for either time in the bottle or the right food companion at the table.

This is a wine made for the dinner table. Serve alongside a rack of lamb with herbed jus, a slow-braised beef short rib, or a generous plateau of soft-rind cheeses such as Brie de Meaux or Époisses. Decanting for at least 30 minutes will open up its more reticent mid-palate and allow the finish to gain the flesh and generosity this Premier Cru promises.

name: Louis Jadot  Vosne Romanee
wine_color: Medium to deep ruby red, reflecting freshness and age-worthy depth.
desc_en_short: Louis Jadot's Vosne-Romanée is a quietly commanding village Burgundy from one of the Côte de Nuits' most storied appellations — delivering intricate layers of red fruit, forest floor, and spice with the structure and elegance to reward patience in the cellar.
desc_en_full:
Few village names in wine carry as much weight as Vosne-Romanée. Nestled in the heart of the Côte de Nuits, this limestone-and-clay hillside commune is the address of Burgundy's most revered Grand Crus — Romanée-Conti, La Tâche, and Richebourg among them. A village-level wine from here still draws on soils and a microclimate that rank among the finest on earth for Pinot Noir.

Maison Louis Jadot, founded in Beaune in 1859, has built its reputation on a philosophy of minimal intervention — trusting the terroir to do the talking rather than imposing a winemaker's hand. The result in this bottling is exactly what fine Burgundy should be: precise, detailed, and unmistakably Vosne. The nose opens with ripe red cherry, wild strawberry, and dried rose petal, slowly giving way to a hauntingly earthy undercurrent of forest floor, truffle, and warm spice, with oak playing a supporting — never dominant — role.

On the palate, Jadot's Vosne-Romanée shows the appellation's hallmark tension: bright, silky acidity and finely grained tannins framing a core of pure fruit, underpinned by a mineral salinity that lingers long after the finish. It drinks beautifully now alongside a herb-roasted rack of lamb or an aged, soft Époisses, but rewards those willing to cellar it for five years or more, when secondary complexity and further integration bring out its most noble character.

name: Two Hands  Holy Grail Shiraz Barossa
desc_en_short: Two Hands Holy Grail Shiraz is an estate single-vineyard wine from the prized Seppeltsfield sub-region of the Barossa Valley — a meticulously planted site delivering concentrated, terroir-driven Shiraz that stands among Two Hands' most coveted single-vineyard expressions.
desc_en_full:
Two Hands Wines has long been one of the Barossa Valley's most celebrated producers, and the Holy Grail Shiraz represents the pinnacle of their single-vineyard philosophy. Sourced exclusively from the Two Hands estate vineyard in Seppeltsfield — one of the Barossa's most distinguished sub-regions — this wine is the result of meticulous long-term vision. The site was first acquired in 2008, with an additional parcel added just before the 2015 vintage, bringing 16 acres of established Shiraz vines into the fold alongside 52 acres of carefully planted new land.

Seppeltsfield's ancient soils, warm days, and cooling maritime influence from the Gulf St Vincent corridor give Shiraz grown here a particular depth and structure. Expect the full, generous character typical of Barossa Shiraz — lush dark fruit of blackberry, plum, and dark cherry — layered with notes of mocha, cracked black pepper, cedar, and a whisper of dried violet. The wine's medium tannin framework and integrated oak lend elegance and length without sacrificing the region's signature richness.

Holy Grail sits confidently alongside the celebrated Two Hands Ares and Bella's Garden in the producer's prestige tier, making it both a compelling drink and a worthy cellar candidate. At the table, it shines beside a slow-braised beef short rib, a herb-crusted rack of lamb, or aged hard cheeses. For collectors and enthusiasts alike, this is Barossa Shiraz at its most site-specific and soulful.

name: Chateau Mouton Rothschild  2019 (Premiers Crus)
wine_color: The wine is an intense garnet red with a purplish hue.
style: Cabernet Sauvignon Or Merlot And Supported By Cabernet Franc, Malbec And Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: Château Mouton Rothschild 2019 is a landmark vintage from Pauillac's most celebrated First Growth — an opulent, majestic Cabernet Sauvignon-dominated blend with extraordinary concentration, precision, and the architectural grandeur that defines great Bordeaux.
desc_en_full:
Château Mouton Rothschild is one of the most storied estates in the world of wine. Elevated to Premier Grand Cru Classé status in 1973 — the only château to have been reclassified since the famous 1855 classification — it occupies a unique place in Bordeaux's pantheon. Each vintage is graced by an original artwork on the label, from Picasso to Warhol to Jeff Koons, making these bottles as sought-after as art objects as they are as wines.

The 2019 vintage is widely regarded as one of the finest produced in Pauillac in a generation. Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant with smaller contributions of Merlot and Cabernet Franc, it spent 18 months in 80% new French oak barrels. Deep, brilliant ruby-purple in colour, it opens with extraordinary complexity: fresh blackcurrant and cassis, crushed violets, cigar box, graphite, dark chocolate, and a seamless overlay of perfectly integrated new oak. The palate is profound — massive but poised, with layers of dark fruit, mineral depth, and velvety tannins of extraordinary refinement. The finish is seemingly endless.

This is a wine to cellar for 15–20 years minimum. It will reward patience with a lifetime of drinking pleasure. A once-in-a-generation Mouton.

name: VIK  Stonevik
desc_en_short: An extraordinary natural wine from VIK's Millahue Valley estate, Stonevik blends Cabernet Franc, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Carménère fermented by the lunar cycle in oak barrels, then aged in hand-crafted clay amphorae aligned in an astronomical mandala atop a 1,000-metre peak.
desc_en_full:
Few wines anywhere in the world are made with the philosophical and ritualistic intention that VIK pours into Stonevik. Born on the vast 4,300-hectare Millahue estate in Chile's Cachapoal Valley — a place the indigenous Mapuche called Lugar de Oro, the Golden Place — this is a wine shaped as much by cosmic thinking as by viticulture.

The fruit is sourced from organically and biodynamically farmed blocks planted within VIK's mosaic of twelve distinct micro-climates, cooled by both Pacific breezes and Andean winds. The blend of 77% Cabernet Franc, 19% Cabernet Sauvignon, and 4% Carménère is fermented using the proprietary Barroir method — spontaneous fermentation inside toasted oak barrels fashioned from trees that have fallen naturally on the property, with each cycle timed to a single lunar month.

What follows is unlike any conventional ageing regime: the wine rests in seven hand-thrown amphorae crafted from the very clay of VIK's own terroir, arranged in a mandala formation said to simulate an electromagnetic field. These vessels are semi-buried at La Roblería, a sacred grove of ancient oaks standing above 1,000 metres, and the wine is released with each summer solstice.

In the glass, Stonevik delivers a seamless interplay of crushed graphite, iron-rich earth, and dried violet, underpinned by fine tannins and a cool mineral precision that speaks directly of those Andean foothills. Serve alongside a prime grass-fed ribeye or a slow-braised lamb shoulder for a pairing that honours the elemental spirit of this remarkable wine.

name: Georges Duboeuf Beaujolais Villages Nouveau 2025
desc_en_short:
Beaujolais-Villages Nouveau is considered to be a fuller-bodied
alternative to Nouveau. It is a more complex wine for Beaujolais connoisseurs looking for
a singular expression of “primeur” Gamay. The grapes are sourced from the same 38
Villages of the Beaujolais-Villages AOC, where there is more granite and schist in the
terroir, compared to Beaujolais AOC. A difference in altitude and latitude influences the
style of the wine. Some of the grapes were picked manually, in whole clusters, while
others were machine-harvested. The average age of the vines are 50 years old, and they
were trained in the Goblet Trellis System.
desc_en_full:
TASTING NOTES:

Very fruity, with intense aromas of dark berries, cherries, and flower petals. There is pure and enveloping fruit on the palate, with great finesse. A silky Gamay with a flavor that is broader, rather than long.

VINIFICATION:

Traditional semi-carbonic maceration lasted four to six days, and was followed by malolactic fermentation in stainless steel tanks, at temperatures between 66-68ᵒ F.

Beaujolais region, which is a patchwork of terroir ranging from sand, clay and

name: Chateau Brane Cantenac Margaux 2017
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 21% Merlot, 4% Cabernet Franc, 1% Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: An elegant Second Growth Margaux (1855) from Château Brane-Cantenac — refined layers of cassis, violet, and cedar with silky tannins and a graceful, long finish. A hallmark of finesse from the 2017 vintage.
desc_en_full:
Château Brane-Cantenac Margaux 2017 showcases the timeless elegance and aromatic precision that define the great wines of Margaux. As a historic **Second Growth (Deuxième Grand Cru Classé)** estate from the 1855 Bordeaux Classification, Brane-Cantenac continues to craft wines of sophistication and balance under the stewardship of the Lurton family.

Tasting Notes: Deep ruby in color, the wine opens with captivating aromas of blackcurrant , violet and cedarwood, intertwined with notes of mocha and subtle spice. On the palate, it is medium to full-bodied, with beautifully integrated tannins and a refined, silky texture. The finish is long, elegant, and marked by purity of fruit and freshness — a true expression of Margaux finesse.

Winemaking & Terroir: The vineyard sits on deep gravel soils over clay and limestone, ideal for Cabernet Sauvignon’s structure and aromatics. The blend consists of **74% Cabernet Sauvignon, 21% Merlot, and 5% Cabernet Franc**, vinified with precision and aged for 18 months in French oak barrels (70% new). The 2017 vintage was shaped by cooler conditions, yielding wines with exceptional balance, aromatic clarity, and classical restraint.

name: Famille Masse Givry1er Cru Vieilles Vignes
desc_en_short:
The name Givry carries a long and storied history—once the favorite wine of King Henry IV, and still today a symbol of the Côte Chalonnaise, representing both elegance and great value. The term Vieilles Vignes on the label refers to “old vines” aged between 40–60 years. Though they yield fewer grapes, the fruit is more concentrated and complex, giving the wine a depth that younger vineyards cannot achieve.

Winemaking : Pinot Noir from Premier Cru vineyards undergoes long maceration to extract maximum color and aromatics, followed by 12–14 months of aging in French oak, with around 30% new barrels. This careful balance preserves the freshness of the fruit while adding structure and refinement.

Character : Opens with ripe cherry and blackcurrant, layered with spice and wild floral notes. Fine-grained yet powerful tannins are lifted by vibrant acidity, giving the wine both immediate charm and the structure to age gracefully, revealing even greater depth over time.

“Dense and refined, with aging potential.” – James Suckling

name: Domaine Piguet Girardin Auxey Duresses 1er Cru Rouge
desc_en_short:
Though Auxey-Duresses may not be as widely recognized as its celebrated neighbors Meursault or Volnay, it is often hailed as one of Burgundy’s hidden gems—a village whose wines linger in memory once discovered. Nestled among hillside vineyards with limestone soils, Auxey-Duresses produces wines with a distinct charm: aromatic brightness paired with delicate structure, offering a taste of “true Burgundy” without relying on the fame of more illustrious appellations.

Winemaking: Pinot Noir from Premier Cru vineyards, hand-harvested with care, then vinified using traditional Burgundian methods to preserve fruit character and achieve balanced structure. Aged 10–12 months in French oak to add roundness and complexity, while keeping the fruit’s natural purity intact.

Character: Fragrant notes of raspberry and violet, layered with subtle hints of wet earth that speak of the terroir. Silky, fine-grained tannins provide structure and length, making this wine both approachable in its youth and elegant enough for short-term cellaring.

“Supple fruit and perfumed bouquet — an irresistible Premier Cru.” – Wine Enthusiast

name: Domaine Piguet Girardin Chassagne Montrachet 1er Cru Rouge
desc_en_short:
When people think of Chassagne-Montrachet, the mind often goes straight to its world-renowned Chardonnay. Yet this village also produces Pinot Noir of remarkable power and individuality. The reds here reflect the complex terroir of the Côte de Beaune—combining intensity with refinement. This wine demonstrates that Chassagne-Montrachet Rouge is not merely the “shadow of its whites,” but a wine that stands proudly with its own character and charm.

Winemaking: Pinot Noir from Premier Cru vines over 40 years old, hand-harvested with care. The grapes undergo cold maceration to preserve vibrant fruit aromatics before being fermented and aged in high-quality French oak. The process builds structure and depth without overwhelming the wine’s natural fruit expression.

Character: Aromas of black cherry and blackberry unfold alongside dried rose and roasted oak spice. The palate is concentrated and layered, with powerful yet polished tannins and impressive length. It is equally rewarding when enjoyed young for its vivid fruit or cellared further to develop greater complexity.

“Structured, brooding, and contemplative.” – La Revue du Vin de France

name: Château Lafon-Rochet 2019
desc_en_short: Château Lafon-Rochet 2019 is a polished, confident Saint-Estèphe from a landmark vintage, where clay-rich soils and Basile Tesseron's meticulous stewardship produced a Cabernet Sauvignon-led blend of ripe, sun-soaked fruit, stony minerality, and impressive cellar potential.
desc_en_full:
Nestled between two Bordeaux titans — Lafite-Rothschild and Cos d'Estournel — Château Lafon-Rochet is one of Saint-Estèphe's most distinctive estates, instantly recognisable by its iconic sunflower-yellow façade. A classified Fourth Growth, the estate sits on clay-dominant soils that set it apart from its gravelly neighbours, and that distinction proved decisive in the exceptional 2019 vintage.

The summer of 2019 was searingly hot and dry across Bordeaux, but Lafon-Rochet's clay terroir acted as a natural reservoir, retaining moisture and shielding the vines from hydric stress. The result is a wine of genuine solar generosity — ripe and broad-shouldered — yet anchored by the appellation's signature stony, mineral backbone. The blend is led by 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, which delivers structure, dark-fruited depth, and a firm tannic frame, while 30% Merlot — thriving in the clay — adds plush weight and a velvety mid-palate. Five percent Cabernet Franc contributes a lifted floral and herbal nuance that keeps the wine fresh and complex.

On the nose, expect notes of blackcurrant, dried violets, and pencil shavings over a bed of warm earth and subtle toasty oak. The palate is polished and cohesive, with medium-weight tannins and enough acidity to promise a graceful evolution over the next decade and beyond.

Pour this alongside a slow-roasted rack of lamb or a prime côte de bœuf to match its depth and Saint-Estèphe nobility. The 2019 vintage also marks the close of Basile Tesseron's tenure as estate director — making this a collector's milestone as much as a pleasure to drink.

name: Château Dauzac 2019
desc_en_short:
Château Dauzac, recognizable by its striking yellow label, has established itself as a laboratory of innovation in the Margaux appellation. Under the recent leadership of Laurent Fortin, the estate made headlines by becoming the first 1855 Classified Growth to produce a wine certified as purely vegan, using plant-based fining agents instead of traditional egg whites. The 2019 vintage is a shining example of this modern, ethical approach, capturing the generosity of the year without compromising on finesse.

The estate's vineyard is situated on a unique terroir of deep gravel over a layer of clay, which proved vital in 2019. The clay subsoil acted as a water reservoir during the hot, dry summer, allowing the Cabernet Sauvignon to ripen fully while maintaining freshness. The winemaking team also utilizes wooden vats and terracotta amphorae for aging, a technique that preserves the purity of the fruit and softens the texture.

Dauzac 2019 is a wine of conviction. It represents a bridge between the history of the 1855 classification and the future of sustainable viticulture. It is lush, fruit-forward, and polished, offering a drinking experience that is both hedonistic and aligned with contemporary values of transparency and respect for nature.

name: Torbreck Descendant 2008
wine_color: Deep, inky purple with a vibrant, glass-staining magenta rim.
desc_en_short: Torbreck Descendant is a pioneering Barossa Valley Shiraz co-fermented with Viognier — a first in the region when introduced — sourced from a single vineyard on Roennfeldt Road planted in 1994 with cuttings from some of Australia's oldest genetic vine material.
desc_en_full:
Torbreck's Descendant occupies a singular place in Barossa Valley history: when winemaker David Powell first co-fermented Shiraz with Viognier here, it was an unprecedented move in the region, echoing the ancient practice of the Northern Rhône's Côte-Rôtie. Today, many producers have followed suit — a quiet tribute to how forward-thinking this wine truly was.

The Descendant Vineyard sits on Roennfeldt Road, its vines planted in 1994 from cuttings taken directly from the celebrated RunRig grower blocks — some of the oldest genetic vine material in Australia. Deep red clay soils drive the wine's character, lending glorious perfume, savoury depth, and a satin-textured palate that sets it apart from the broader Barossa Shiraz conversation.

Winemaking follows a rigorous, minimalist path: whole Shiraz is co-fermented with Viognier skins in open-top fermenters with extended maceration before basket pressing. Twenty months in second-use French oak barriques — the same barrels previously employed for RunRig — add integration and spice without masking the fruit. The wine is bottled unfined and unfiltered.

The 2008 vintage delivers lifted violet and white peach florals from the Viognier co-ferment, followed by dark plum, blackberry, cured meat, and warm earth on the palate. Tannins are polished yet purposeful, with a long, savoury finish.

Pour this alongside slow-roasted lamb shoulder or a pepper-crusted côte de bœuf for an occasion that matches the wine's ambition.

name: Chateau Clerc Milon  2022
wine_color: The wine is a deep cherry red with garnet highlights.
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 32% Merlot, 8% Cabernet Franc, 1% Carmenère
desc_en_short: A Fifth Growth Pauillac of remarkable ambition, Château Clerc Milon 2022 delivers the classic marriage of Cabernet Sauvignon power and Merlot richness from some of the Médoc's finest gravel and clay-limestone terroirs — structured, mineral, and built unmistakably for the cellar.
desc_en_full:
Nestled in the heart of Pauillac, one of Bordeaux's most celebrated appellations, Château Clerc Milon holds the distinguished rank of Fifth Growth (Cinquième Grand Cru Classé) in the 1855 Classification — a status it has carried with quiet confidence for over a century and a half. The estate's vineyards straddle two contrasting soil types: deep Günzian gravel that drains freely and concentrates aromatic intensity, and richer clay-limestone pockets that lend Merlot its characteristic roundness and mid-palate generosity. This duality is precisely what gives Clerc Milon its distinctive character.

The 2022 vintage reflects a warm, sun-drenched Bordeaux growing season that produced wines of exceptional ripeness and natural concentration. In the glass, this blend of 59% Cabernet Sauvignon, 32% Merlot, 8% Cabernet Franc, and 1% Carmenère opens with layers of ripe blackcurrant, crushed violets, and cigar box cedar, underpinned by the chalky mineral signature that defines great Pauillac. The palate is full-bodied and authoritative — dense, fine-grained tannins frame a core of dark plum, graphite, and dried herbs, while lively acidity ensures the finish is long and precise rather than heavy.

Patience is rewarded here. Decant generously if opening young, or lay down for a decade or more to watch this wine fully unfurl. At the table, it is superb alongside a rack of lamb with herbed crust, a well-aged côte de bœuf, or a wedge of mature hard cheese such as Comté or aged Cheddar.

These wines are the perfect introduction to the well-known wines from the Veneto area: Prosecco, Valpolicella, Ripasso and Amarone. These traditional styles are complemented by single-varietal crowd-pleasers such as Pinot Grigio and Chardonnay.

name: Bottega  Prosecco Gold Brut (1.5 L)
wine_color: Brilliant, fine and persistent perlage. Light straw yellow.
desc_en_short: Brilliant, fine and persistent perlage. Light straw yellow. Clear, characteristic, fruity and flowery with scents of Golden apple, Williams pear, acacia flowers and lily of the valley. Quite tasty, harmonious, elegant and fresh. As aperitif, in cocktails, with starters, first courses, fish and poultry.
desc_en_full:
Bottega Gold guarantees rare and elegant fragrances, thanks to the fermentation process which allows to obtain the sparkling wine directly from the must. A packaging with a great emotional effect makes it a really prestigious present. Bottega Gold is obtained by the vinification in white of the Glera grapes, grown in the Conegliano and Valdobbiadene hills. These grapes are hand picked and taken directly into the winery where the stalks are eliminated and the grapes are softly pressed. The must obtained is maintained in stainless steel containers at 0° degrees and then fermented in autoclave for nearly 40 days, at a controlled temperature of 14-15°C, with the addition of selected yeasts (Charmat method).

Brilliant, fine and persistent perlage. Light straw yellow. Clear, characteristic, fruity and flowery with scents of Golden apple, Williams pear, acacia flowers and lily of the valley. Quite tasty, harmonious, elegant and fresh. As aperitif, in cocktails, with starters, first courses, fish and poultry.

name: Cavicchioli  Prosecco Extra Dry
desc_en_short: Cavicchioli's Prosecco Extra Dry is a bright, fruit-forward Italian sparkling wine — lively and refreshing with green apple, pear, white peach, and delicate floral notes, finishing with a gentle sweetness and clean effervescence.
desc_en_full:
Cavicchioli is one of the Emilia-Romagna region's most established sparkling wine producers, known throughout Italy for their accessible, well-made bubbles. Their Prosecco Extra Dry is made from Glera grapes grown in the Prosecco DOC zone of the Veneto, using the Charmat (tank) method to preserve its fresh, aromatic character.

Note that 'Extra Dry' in Prosecco terminology is slightly sweeter than 'Brut' — a traditional style that balances the grape's natural fruitiness with a gentle touch of residual sugar. Pale straw with fine persistent bubbles, it opens on fresh green apple, ripe pear, white peach, and delicate acacia blossom. The palate is clean and refreshing, with a soft, approachable sweetness and a lively, satisfying finish.

A versatile aperitif that pairs beautifully with light antipasti, prosciutto and melon, mild soft cheeses, and fresh fruit desserts. Also excellent in a classic Aperol Spritz.

name: Antinori  Marchese Cuvee Royale Franciacorta DOCG
wine_color: A light yellow in color with a creamy bubbling and an elegant and persistent sparkle.
style: Chardonnay, Pinot Nero, And Pinot Bianco
desc_en_short: Antinori's Marchese Cuvée Royale is a prestigious Franciacorta DOCG — Italy's answer to Champagne — with fine persistent bubbles, toasted brioche, ripe citrus, and stone fruit complexity, showing the elegance and refinement this appellation delivers at its best.
desc_en_full:
Franciacorta DOCG, produced in the hills south of Lake Iseo in Lombardy, is Italy's most serious traditional-method sparkling wine — made by the same labour-intensive méthode champenoise as Champagne, with extended lees ageing required by law. Antinori's Marchese Cuvée Royale is a reflection of the house's commitment to quality across all wine categories.

Made predominantly from Chardonnay with Pinot Nero and Pinot Bianco, the wine undergoes secondary fermentation in bottle and extended ageing on its lees to develop its characteristic complexity and creamy texture. Pale straw-gold with fine, persistent perlage, it opens on a sophisticated nose of toasted brioche, ripe lemon, white peach, almond cream, and a subtle mineral thread. The palate is refined and full, with lively acidity, a creamy mousse, and a long, elegant finish.

Serve as a celebratory aperitif, with oysters and shellfish, light risotto, grilled white fish, or lobster.

name: Champagne Charles Orban  Carte Noire Brut
wine_color: A clear golden colour, fine, elegant bubbles.
desc_en_short: A clear golden colour, fine, elegant bubbles. A delicate nose dominated by citrus fruit, with hints of fudge. Superb length, smooth with brioche aromas and subtle notes of honey, pine nuts and light tobacco.
desc_en_full:
History

The Orban family has been growing grapes in Troissy, a village on the left bank of the Marne, upstream from Dormans, since 1770. Troissy's vineyard covers 331 hectares, mostly planted with Pinot Meunier (86%). Pinot Noir and Chardonnay represent 8% and 6% respectively. This region is in fact recognized for its Pinot Meunier, but our estate's vineyard has the privilege of cultivating the three types of grape in order to ensure the authenticity of our vintages.

Towards the end of the 1950s, soon after he got married, Charles Orban left the Troissy cooperative and decided to produce his own champagne and sell it under his own name. He even went as far as preparing his grafts himself.

In about 1960, Charles bought a press and built a fermenting room to become completely independent and able to create a HOUSE STYLE. Over the years, from 1949 until the present day, production has increased from 800 to 200,000 bottles. At the end of the 20th century, the house was sold to a champagne family that has managed to preserve and perpetuate Orban family tradition.

name: Champagne Gremillet  Blanc de Blancs Brut
wine_color: Sublime, pale color with delicate greenish glints. Handsome, fine, long-lasting creamy bubbles, with a regular slow flow.
desc_en_short:
A bewitching and seductive champagne, that of the finest tables par excellence, whose lightness sublimates the most refined dishes. “Its freshness make it an easy champagne to drink, which give the irresistible desire for more”.

TO OBSERVE

Sublime pale golden color with delicate greenish glints. Handsome, fine, long-lasting creamy bubbles, with a regular slow flow.

TO BREATH

The nose is a bouquet of flowers in full bloom that invites to a second breath, with white hawthorn and honeysuckle blossoms, rose hips, followed by multicolored roses and peonies further revealing fresh hints of citrus fruit and citronella. Upon airing it opens slowly to reveal notes of bread, caramel and of candied fruit…

TO APPRECIATE

On the taste we find the same charming aromas as on the nose as we are carried away by this floral magic, perfect harmony between the wine and its “liqueur”. Its long, fragrant finish underlines its wonderful charm.
desc_en_full:
OENOLOGY

Blending of four to five vintages, 20% of reserve wine. This Champagne is composed of only the cuvée (first press).

Alcoholic fermentation at 16°C and malolactic fermentation at 18°C in stainless steel temperature-controlled vats. Ageing of 30 months minimum. BRUT dosage at 9 gr/l with a «liqueur d'expédition» coming from finished bottles of Champagne.

AWARDS

AWSA : Gold 2018, Gold 2016

Gilbert & Gaillard : Gold 2018, 2016

wine_color: Brilliant straw yellow, with fine and elegant perlage.
desc_en_short:
Our Moscato is an experience of fragrant sweetness
captured in a bottle, where light bubbles dance among
intense aromas of flowers and citrus. Perfect for celebrating
festive moments or adding a touch of sweetness to a special
occasion. Each sip takes you on a journey through delicate
aromas and fresh sensations, wrapping the senses in an
elegant and harmonious embrace. A toast that turns every
occasion into an unforgettable memory.
desc_en_full:
From the Hill Beside the Church

Rive della Chiesa takes its name from a beloved hillside vineyard plot nestled beside an old rural church in Montello, Treviso. The Gasparetto family has nurtured this land for generations, combining deep-rooted tradition with a vision for accessible, joyful sparkling wines.

This Moscato Spumante is a testament to their approach: harvesting early to retain freshness, using temperature-controlled fermentation to preserve the grape’s intense aromatics, and finishing with a refined sparkle that dances across the palate.

Bright, sweet, and refreshing, this wine embodies the spirit of celebration. Whether shared at the end of a meal or opened just for the pleasure of its floral, fruit-laced sparkle, it brings a piece of Veneto’s festive soul to every glass.

name: Monchiero Carbone  Roero Arneis Cecu d la Biunda DOCG
desc_en_short: Bright, intense straw-yellow notes. Great intensity on the nose, showing great depth that evolves slowly into sensations of aromatic freshness, ranging from white currants to hints of mint to sage. The entry onto the palate is strongly marked by a splendid freshness of flavour, which gradually shows its true power and mouth-filling qualities, developing into a rich and very long finish on which white currants stand out. Its finesse on the nose and mouth make it a marvellous match for seafood risottos and oven-baked fish.
desc_en_full:
Roero Arneis DOCG Cecu d'la Biunda

The Arneis vine, which is grown almost exclusively on the steep hills of the Roero, is the architect - along with Nebbiolo - of our area's fine reputation in the world of wine. This has inspired us to craft this selection, with the intention of offering a further opportunity to highlight the breeding and potential of this great variety. The grapes are grown on the soft, limy soil of hills surrounding the Vezza d'Alba Valley and the slopes that act as a buttress against the Rocche of Canale on the renowned Renesio hill (where this particular white variety is said to have once been known as the "white Nebbiolo" to underline its quality and importance). On this type of ground Arneis is able to express all its aroma and elegance. Intense, floral nuances are prominent on the nose, while the refinement of its lingering, structured taste that charms the palate is set to improve over the years to come. We have chosen to dedicate this selection to our grandfather Francesco Carbone, better known as "Cecu d'la Biunda", out of gratitude for both the Arneis he planted in his vineyards, and the seeds he instilled our hearts.

name: Sato  Central Otago Riesling
desc_en_short: Straw yellow colour. Gentle but uplifting nose of lemon, citrus, splash of lime, crushed apple, touch of pine resin followed by creamy yeast, baked bread like complexity. Soft suppleness on the pallet. Great texture. Tasty bright citrus fruit. Splashing beautiful acidity runs through in the mouth and leads to the long finish supported by wet stone minerality.
desc_en_full:
Winemaking

Although we have aimed to have our own winery facility at our estate, we rent space at Rockburn Winery in Cromwell to make wines at this moment. We set our equipments there and all the wines are handled by Kyoko or me.

Our goal is to make wines which can heal people; soft, delicate, supple, smooth mouth filling wines rather than powerful, heavily structured, aggressively appealing wines. So we decided to take the way of making natural wines – clean and tasty natural wines, but not dirty natural wines. We use only organic or biodynamic grapes and would like to process them in gentle and organic way at the winery; minimum handling of juice, must or wine and, importantly, avoiding adding additives or chemicals to wine during vinification and maturation. We refuse to use SO2. We are keen on maintaining the life of micro organism in wines brought from our vineyards. Chemicals such as SO2 act like herbicide in vineyard; killing wild yeast or bacteria and destroy the beautiful natural harmony among them. We always try to employ as many species of bacteria and yeast as possible in order to preserve natural purity and to create harmonious complexity in our wines. SO2 also prevents juice or wine in process from oxygen contact. We believe Oxygen is quite important to maintain healthy fermentation and keep stability in wines then after. We 3 normally keep our wines in barrels or tanks in much longer period than the other growers here.

The Black Oak Chardonnay is golden straw in color, it is pretty and bright. Wonderful aromas of summer-ripe peaches with tropical fruits and flowers. The silky and smooth flavors are reminiscent of baked apples, and toasty vanilla bean. The lively acidity makes this an extremely well-balanced Chardonnay. FOOD PAIRINGS:

name: Jermann  Vintage Tunina DOC
wine_color: It is bright straw-colored with golden reflections.
style: Sauvignon, Chardonnay, Ribolla Gialla, Malvasia And A Little Percentage Of A Sweet Local Grape Variety
grape_variety: Chardonnay, Ribolla Gialla, Malvasia, Sauvignon Blanc
desc_en_short: Vintage Tunina is Jermann's most iconic wine — a complex, age-worthy white blend from Friuli of extraordinary depth and aromatic intensity, combining Sauvignon, Chardonnay, Ribolla Gialla, and Malvasia Istriana into a wine of rare richness and mineral elegance.
desc_en_full:
Vintage Tunina is one of Italy's most celebrated white wines and a benchmark for the entire Friuli-Venezia Giulia appellation. Created by Silvio Jermann in the 1970s, it was among the first Italian whites to prove that the country could produce world-class dry whites capable of ageing for a decade or more.

The blend — Sauvignon Blanc, Chardonnay, Ribolla Gialla, Malvasia Istriana, and Picolit — is harvested late to maximise complexity, then fermented with indigenous yeasts and aged on fine lees for months in large Slavonian oak casks. The result is a wine of remarkable multi-layered complexity: golden amber in colour, it opens on a rich, evolved nose of ripe apricot, mango, white truffle, honey, toasted almonds, and a distinctive floral-mineral thread. The palate is full-bodied and textured, with seamless oak integration, vibrant acidity, and a finish of extraordinary length.

Best enjoyed with 2–3 years of age or more. Pair with lobster bisque, crab, foie gras, truffle risotto, or aged Montasio cheese.

name: Vasse Felix  Icon Range Heytesbury Chardonnay
desc_en_short:
97 points in James Suckling - "A very complex flinty and hazelnut style that has a strong play of fine French oak on the nose. Plenty of layers to the aromas here from white peaches and nectarine fruits to grapefruit and lemons. Immense grapefruit and citrus depth on the palate with warm oak spices and well-layered savory threads. Long and effortless power."

95 points in Wine Enthusiast - "This wine delivers all of the sea spray, melon rind, gunflint and oyster shell characteristics that Margaret River Chardonnay is known for. The palate shows power but not heaviness, textural weight but not richness, with a long line of salty minerality and mouthwatering acidity to boot"

93 points in Wine Advocate - "The Heytesbury Chardonnay is a pristine, focused example of the variety as grown in Margaret River. It seems more crystalline and pure, less opulent and showy than previous vintages, although I must confess a bit of ambivalence about that. Pineapple and citrus dominate the nose with just subtle oak shadings (although it's typically 50% or more new). It's medium-bodied in the mouth with a soft, custard-like richness and a silky texture that fades slowly away on the lemon-tinged finish."

91 points in Wine Spectator - "Lip-smacking, with juicy forwardness, offering tangerine, mango and citrus flavors and a tease of spice and toast lingering in the background. Shows plenty of grace and elegance. Drink now."
desc_en_full:
Vasse Felix Heytesbury Chardonnay

A selection of the most unique and striking vineyard sections, recognised for its innovative winemaking and distinguishing 'Heytesbury' character.

Winemaker's Notes

Our most unique small sections within our vineyards where clone, aspect and soil interplay in such a way that sets these parcels apart from the rest. Each batch was very gently pressed and the juice then transferred directly to barriques with the inclusion of all solids to achieve more of the unique vineyard character. Each batch was fermented wild with no yeast addition and was stirred throughout the nine months of maturation, allowing the yeast lees to impart further complexity, texture and palate weight to the wine. With bright, high acidity in 2016, natural malolactic fermentation was encouraged and completed.

name: Dalrymple  The Cave Block Chardonnay
desc_en_short: Delicate white floral notes initially lift out of the glass before the more noticeable aromas of ripe fresh cut nectarine combines with nougat, hazelnuts and mixed dried spices. Flavours of nectarine and citrus form the core of the fruit characters, with brioche and crushed nuts adding complexity and a sense of texture. The warmer vintage gives a more generous finish, but the hallmark fine acidity is still the driver through the palate, giving life and focus to the fruit flavours.
desc_en_full:
Winemaker Notes

Free draining soils and steep slopes results in our Chardonnay vineyard producing low yields of intensely concentrated fruit. The Cave Block saw a relatively calm growing season, with warm and dry conditions from early October to mid-May. Infrequent rain events during this time gave the vines a welcome drink. Flowering occurred during the mild spring producing an above average crop which was allowed to ripen fully through the warm summer and autumn.

The fruit was meticulously picked by hand, before whole bunch pressing to a mix of new and older French oak barriques. Winemaking included a parcel of wild fermentation, light lees contact and partial malolactic fermentation to naturally adjust acidity for balance and structure.

The warmer season is immediately evident with the addition of ripe nectarine, peach skin and orange blossom complimenting the fine citrus characters that identify the Cave Block Chardonnay. The palate is generous in texture and softness, due mainly to the sense of riper fruit flavours carrying over from aromas, as well as a softening of acidity from the warmer season. French oak and subtle lees contact give complexity to the long fresh finish.

name: Chateau Musar  White 2010
desc_en_short:
This was the year of the heatwave in Lebanon which lasted for 23 days as from the 10th July. The Chateau White 2010 was a very small vintage despite the fact that the indigenous white varieties, Obaideh and Merwah were less affected by the intense heat as the microclimate of the Lebanese mountains resisted the heat far better than the Bekaa Valley floor.

Tasting Notes

It is bright lemon in colour with pears, thyme, pine resin and lemongrass on the nose. The palate has an oily character but with zingy lemon acidity on the finish. There are herbs, pine and citrus flavours – a distinctive white vintage with excellent ageing potential.
desc_en_full:
Chateau Musar White 2010

The 2010 vintage was fermented and aged partly in new oak barrels for 9 months and partly in stainless steel vats with temperatures ranging between 18 and 22 degrees Celsius. Normally Obaideh reaches higher alcohol than the Merwah but they balance each other in the blend – this year however, both reached lower alcohol levels (Merwah 11.5%) and when it has lower alcohol, it tends to dominate the Obaideh, so although it is only 40% of the blend, it is more obvious.

A guide to the style

In their youth: yellow-gold, subtly oaky, and creamy-textured, rich yet dry and intensely citrusy, with honeyed nuances. Wholly unique, the style has been described as resembling 'dry Sauternes' or mature white Graves. Chateau Musar Whites develop tawny hues and mellow spicy characters as they age. The cellars at Ghazir holds bottles of this wine dating as far back as 1954.

Grapes and vines

Seven years in the making, Chateau Musar White is a blend of ancient grape varieties Obaideh and Merwah, indigenous to the mountains of Lebanon and said to be related to Chasselas Chardonnay and Semillon. The Obaideh vineyards are in the foothills of the Anti-Lebanon mountains on stony, chalky soils, while the Merwah vines are on the seaward side of Mount Lebanon, on calcareous gravels. Yields are very low for these untrained bushvines: 10 – 20 hl per hectare. At high altitude (around 1400m) they are still on their own roots, among very few vineyards in the world of this calibre.

name: Duckhorn  Napa Valley Chardonnay
desc_en_short: This tantalizing Chardonnay begins with alluring aromas of lemon cream, orange blossom, Key lime and candied pineapple. On the palate, a lush, silky mouthfeel and energetic acidity frame lively orchard-fruit flavors of pear and white peach, with hints of brioche emerging on the long, focused finish.
desc_en_full:
Winemaker Notes

This tantalizing Chardonnay begins with alluring aromas of lemon cream, orange blossom, Key lime and candied pineapple. On the palate, a lush, silky mouthfeel and energetic acidity frame lively orchard-fruit flavors of pear and white peach, with hints of brioche emerging on the long, focused finish.

Production

Despite an unusually dry winter and warm spring, budbreak was delayed due to light rain in March. The overall lack of rainfall led to light clusters and small berries, which resulted in reduced yields and great concentration. Harvest began on August 5th with Semillon, and early heat generated a fast and furious first few weeks of picking. A much-needed mid-September cool-down allowed for sugars to realign with flavor development. Moderate temperatures continued throughout the rest of the season, providing additional hangtime for the grapes. Our last red grapes were harvested before the rains in late October, resulting in beautiful wines that are dense, pure and polished.

name: Maison Jaffelin  Meursault
desc_en_short:
Meursault has long been regarded as the crown jewel of the Côte de Beaune, a name coveted by collectors and critics alike. Celebrated for producing legendary Chardonnay with creaminess, complexity, and timeless elegance, this Meursault from Maison Jaffelin interprets the village with both grandeur and a sense of modern drinkability.

Winemaking : Chardonnay from limestone-clay soils, fermented and aged in French oak (partly new), with bâtonnage on the lees to enrich texture and creaminess.

Character : Hazelnut, toasted almond, and buttery brioche, unfolding into layers of fresh lemon, golden apple, and precise limestone minerality. Dense and long on the palate, balancing richness with bright acidity. A classic Meursault that impresses both now and with cellaring potential.

“Creamy yet precise, a Meursault of remarkable depth.” – Wine Advocate
desc_en_full: The grapes are harvested by hand and then directly pressed in a pneumatic press. Alcoholic fermentation takes place entirely in French oak barrels, of which 40% are new. The lees are stirred (bâtonnage) occasionally during ageing, which lasts 11 months.

name: Ricasoli  Rocca Guicciarda Chianti Classico Riserva DOCG
desc_en_short: Rocca Guicciarda was in the past one of the most important feudal estates belonging to the Ricasoli family. Complex and harmonious wine in the traditional style, with an unmistakable label.
desc_en_full:
VINTAGE

This was an atypical year, perhaps unlike any ever registered. Winter was fairly mild and warm. The frost that arrived on 18‐20 April, the drought, the extreme heat that lasted for more than two months, all scorched the earth and the grapevines. For this reason, the leaf removal typically carried out before grape harvest was avoided; instead, the grapevines were left "untidy", only slightly trimmed, to protect the fruit from the rays of the sun. At the end of August and the first ten days of September, rains came and the temperatures dropped drastically, all the way to the seasonal average.

FERMENTATION

Fermentation and maceration in stainless steel tanks at a controlled temperature of 24°C-27°C (75.2°-80.6°F) with 14-16 days of skin contact.

AGEING 

18 months in tonneaus and large casks with 3 to 6 months in the bottle.

name: Michele Chiarlo  Barolo Cannubi DOCG
desc_en_short: Ruby red in color, this aristocratic and complex wine offers notes of small fruits, licorice, and sweet tobacco. A full-bodied wine with silky tannins
desc_en_full:
Cannubi is perhaps the most famous hill in Italy and is the most celebrated and prestigious vineyard with the Barolo designation. Historically, it is the oldest Italian cru when it was recognized in 1752. All the elements including altitude, exposure, soils, layout, and microclimate come together to result in a Barolo at the top of the designation. Lying on a steep slope, Cannubi was the first vineyard in Langhe to be terraced. Michele Chiarlo owns 4.5 acres of the vineyard with vines that are over 20 years old set in Tortonian and Helevetian-era calcareous marl. The terraces maximize an exceptional hillside exposure with a more than 50-degree gradient. The vines are thinned of excess bunches at the end of summer, leaving 5-6 bunches per vine on average. Hand harvested grapes are fermented on the skins in oak vats and then aged for two months in average sized oak casks before refinement in the bottle.

Tasting Noted   Ruby red in color, this aristocratic and complex wine offers notes of small fruits, licorice, and sweet tobacco. A full-bodied wine with silky tannins

name: Chateau La Croix Meunier Saint -Emilion Grand Cru
wine_color: Deep garnet red with violet reflections.
desc_en_short: Elegant Saint-Émilion Grand Cru blending Merlot and Cabernet Franc, with deep dark fruits, soft spices, and silky tannins — perfect for red meats and fine dining.
desc_en_full:
Château La Croix Meunier Saint-Émilion Grand Cru 2019 is a wine that speaks of elegance and finesse from one of Bordeaux’s most prestigious Right Bank appellations. This vintage is a beautifully crafted blend, combining the lush richness of Merlot (เมอร์โลต์) with the floral finesse of Cabernet Franc, offering a truly refined Bordeaux experience.

Tasting Notes: Deep garnet red with violet reflections. On the nose, layers of ripe blackberries, cassis (แบล็กเคอร์แรนท์), and dark cherries mingle with hints of cocoa, cedar, and subtle spices. The palate is smooth and velvety, showing soft, well-integrated tannins wrapped around a core of rich black fruits, licorice, and a touch of minerality. The finish is elegant and long, leaving lingering notes of plum and spice.

Winemaking & Terroir: Château La Croix Meunier is located on the limestone and clay-rich soils of Saint-Émilion (แซงต์-เอมิลิยง), which give the wine its signature depth and complexity. Hand-harvested, carefully sorted, and vinified in temperature-controlled vats before aging in French oak barrels (some new, some used) for added finesse and structure. 2019 is considered an exceptional vintage in Bordeaux, showing concentration and balance.

Food Pairings & Serving Tips: A perfect match for grilled or roasted lamb, beef tenderloin, duck breast with berry sauce, or even rich mushroom risotto. Also wonderful with hard cheeses like aged Comté or Manchego. Serve at 16-18°C, and decant for at least 1 hour before serving to fully express its bouquet and complexity.

Aging Potential & Cellaring: Already enjoyable now with decanting, but has excellent aging potential — can be cellared for 10-15 years, developing more earthy, leathery, and truffle nuances over time.

name: Chateau Cazauviel Margaux
wine_color: Deep ruby red with glimmers of garnet.
desc_en_short: Classic Margaux elegance, blending Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot — rich dark fruits, soft tannins, and hints of oak and spice, perfect for fine dining.
desc_en_full: Château Cazauviel, Margaux, is a refined and beautifully balanced Bordeaux from one of the most esteemed appellations on the Left Bank — a wine that reflects Margaux’s hallmark elegance, structure, and finesse, making it a perfect choice for both collectors and those looking to elevate special occasions. Tasting Notes: Deep ruby red with glimmers of garnet. Aromas of blackcurrants, ripe plums, and dark cherries layered with subtle violet, cedarwood, and hints of vanilla and fine spices. On the palate, expect a silky texture with well-integrated tannins, juicy black fruits, and a touch of minerality and toasted oak, leading to a lingering, graceful finish. Winemaking & Terroir: Château Cazauviel is crafted from vineyards planted on Margaux's famed gravel and clay soils, ideal for Cabernet Sauvignon (คาแบร์เนต์ โซวีญง), Merlot (เมอร์โลต์), and Petit Verdot (เปอตี แวร์โด). The wine is carefully vinified and aged in French oak barrels, adding complexity, subtle spices, and enhancing structure, while preserving the vibrant fruit character typical of Margaux wines. Food Pairings & Serving Tips: A natural match for classic French cuisine — grilled lamb, roast beef, duck confit, or mushroom-based dishes. Also pairs beautifully with mature cheeses like Comté and Gruyère. Serve at 16-18°C and decant for 1 hour before serving to fully reveal its layered bouquet and finesse. Aging Potential & Cellaring: Ready to enjoy now with decanting, but has excellent aging potential for 8-12 years, allowing it to develop more complexity, earthy notes, and fine leather nuances over time.

The Spirit of Shiraz, Without the Alcohol Edenvale is Australia’s pioneer in premium alcohol-removed wines, combining advanced winemaking technology with the country’s best vineyard resources.

Using the innovative spinning cone process, Edenvale captures the full aroma and taste of wine while gently removing alcohol at low temperatures to preserve integrity and balance. This South Australian Shiraz reflects the same craftsmanship and terroir as its traditional counterparts — full-bodied, fruit-driven, and softly spiced — yet crafted for those who seek moderation without compromise. name: Renzo Masi Contrappasso Rosso di Toscana
desc_en_short: A bold, expressive Tuscan red where the freshness of Sangiovese meets the depth and plush texture of semi-dried Syrah — a modern blend rooted in tradition.
desc_en_full:
Contrast in Harmony — A New Voice from an Old Land

In the Chianti Rufina zone, just east of Florence, the Masi family has long balanced tradition with forward-thinking winemaking. With Contrappasso, their goal was clear: craft a wine that sings in dual voices — the lift and acidity of Sangiovese paired with the richness and velvety depth of Syrah given a gentle appassimento treatment.

This “counterpass” the name itself a play on contrast — begins with separately harvested grapes. The Syrah is partially dried to concentrate flavor and texture, while the Sangiovese is picked at peak freshness. After separate fermentation, the blend is composed with precision, yielding a wine that is both structured and generous, vibrant and dark-fruited.

Contrappasso is a Tuscan red with dual personality : at once bold and refined, approachable yet layered. It offers a new way to understand the land — not through imitation, but through the thoughtful combination of tradition and technique, freshness and depth.

desc_en_short: Animating fruit with balanced acidity and sustainable body. By the juicy citrus and stone fruit flavors and subtle minerality a perfect accompaniment to meals to various dishes.
desc_en_full:
Weingut Robert Weil Riesling trocken Kiedricher

The Weingut Robert Weil Riesling Kiedricher is a local wine from the pre-selection of the first and great locations. The Rheingau Riesling grows on the layers Kiedricher Turmberg, Kiedricher Graefenberg and Kiedricher mountain monastery. It grows best in medium to deep soils from phyllite slate and loam with sometimes inclines up to 60%. At an altitude of about 240 above sea level, the layers are on the sunny side of Kiedrich. The winds aerate the optimal positions. The phyllite slate is slender and dünnschiefrig with a high mineral content. The slate proportion makes the soil loose and the roots of the Riesling can penetrate deep into the earth. The Lösslehmanteil ensures good water storage power supplies and the vines with water.

So the Weingut Robert Weil Riesling tastes Kiedricher The characteristic of the Rheingau Riesling Kiedricher: Animating fruit with balanced acidity and sustainable body. By the juicy citrus and stone fruit flavors and subtle minerality a perfect accompaniment to meals to various dishes. A Riesling from Weingut Robert Weil, a lot of fun.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Laforet Chardonny de Bourgogne
desc_en_short: "Full of life! A bouquet of citrus aromas, and then we find honey and vanilla. In the mouth, it's funky, spontaneous, striking. Really charming!".
desc_en_full:
Vineyard

Site: LaForêt Bourgogne Chardonnay is made with the Chardonnay grape coming from the whole of Burgundy. It is often the entry level wine leading to the great white Burgundies: to drink it provides the simple pleasures of a classic Burgundy.
History & tradition: the name LaForêt (pronounced "La-fo-ray") is synonymous with quality. Joseph Drouhin takes great care in selecting the various cuvées.
Soil: clay and limestone.

Vinification

Supply: grapes and must purchased from regular suppliers.
Press: pneumatic press.
"Débourbage": decanting of white wine to reduce sediment.

Composed like a perfume. Véronique Drouhin and the blend of LaForêt.

"We have a good palette of aromas and flavours. Chablis, in the north, for the freshness and character, the Côte d'Or for finesse and elegance, the Mâconnais to the south for the charm and fruitiness. With this structure, LaForêt is a faithful wine, and we rediscover these traits from year to year".

Ageing

Stainless-steel vats (7 to 8 months).
One to two year old barrels for the wines of higher provenance.
Part of the wine is aged in oak barrels, and that changes a lot. Why? Because the marriage with oxygen happens more gently (as said by Véronique Drouhin!). Result: the wine is not overly woody but is rich in aromas".

Throughout the ageing process, decisions are taken only after careful tasting evaluation. The data obtained is then completed through technical analysis. As with every other Joseph Drouhin wine, absolute priority is given to the true expression of terroir and character of the vintage.

Tasting note by Véronique Boss-Drouhin

"Full of life! A bouquet of citrus aromas, and then we find honey and vanilla. In the mouth, it's funky, spontaneous, striking. Really charming!".

Vintage

The wines are highly seductive and pleasant; they are well-balanced and offer an attractive richness. The Grands Crus from the Côte de Beaune deserve to be kept some extra years. The wines from the Mâcon area display riper aromas, providing a lot of charm.

desc_en_full:
Neleman Organic Vineyards was founded on a clear philosophy: organic farming, minimal intervention, and wines that express pure fruit without unnecessary manipulation. Based in Valencia, the winery works exclusively with certified organic vineyards, embracing sustainable viticulture in a warm Mediterranean climate.

“Hey You White” reflects the approachable and energetic side of the portfolio. name: La Bastarda Bianco di Toscana
desc_en_short: A bright and value‑driven Tuscan white from the Masi family stable, offering clean fruit, fresh acidity and a lively, accessible profile.
desc_en_full:
A Playful White from the Heart of Tuscany

In the gently rolling hills of Tuscany the Masi family now in their third generation of winemaking — launched La Bastarda as an expression of approachable quality and fun. Their ambition has always been to combine sound winemaking fundamentals with everyday drinkability and unmistakable Tuscan character.

The Bianco di Toscana is crafted from Trebbiano and Chardonnay grapes selected for freshness and vitality. The grapes are harvested at ideal ripeness, gently pressed and fermented in stainless steel at low temperature to preserve brightness and aromatic clarity.

The result is a wine that remains free from pretension and full of personality. Its label may smile, but the wine delivers purity, value and a sense of place — a white that reflects Tuscany’s sunlit charm, crisp energy and the Masi family’s commitment to accessible excellence.

desc_en_full:
ABOUT

The Matusalem Rum company was started by the Camp brothers in 1872 as a family affair. The "new" Cuban rum rapidly caught on in the world's markets. In the early 1940's the company experienced a period of tremendous growth and by the 1950's it had already captured 50% of the Cuban market, making it the leading rum sold in Cuba. Today, descendents of the founders produce Ron Matusalem in the Dominican Republic. The brand "Matusalem" was named after the Biblical Patriarch, Methuselah who was said to have lived for 969 years, so symbolising the patient ageing and old world craftsmenship used to give Ron Matusalem Rum its exceptional flavour.

Solera 7 is a new addition to the range, produced from molasses and drawn from the company's Solera system. Expect a fruity, medium bodied rum with plenty of sweet spice flavours. The brand "Matusalem" was named after the Biblical Patriarch, Methuselah who was said to have lived for 969 years, so symbolising the patient ageing and old world craftsmanship used to give Ron Matusalem Rum its exceptional flavour. 40% Alc./Vol.

desc_en_full:
A high quality set of 20cl bottling from the Matsui distillery in Japan.

This set contains three different whiskies: the Sakura cask, a peated expression, and the Mizunara Cask.

The Sakura cask is a rather fascinating dram, having been matured in cherry wood casks, providing a unique flavour profile. Drinkers can expect notes of almond, orange rind, fresh grapes, honeydew melon, cinnamon, flowers, soft red cherries, subtle raspberries and fragrant wood.

Matsui Peated offers up notes of intense roasted grains, burnt malt, earthy peat, woodsmoke, vanilla, brown sugar, apples, ash, and hay. An entirely different approach to Matsui’s spirit, but no less delicious!

Mizunara is the famed and rather rare Japanese oak. Casks made of this wood are notoriously difficult to manage, with the barrels given to leaking – the name literally means “water oak”.

desc_en_full:
The Médoc is a region of France, well known as a wine growing region, located in the département of Gironde, on the left bank of the Gironde estuary, north of Bordeaux. Its name comes from (Pagus) Medullicus, or "country of the Medulli", the local Celtic tribe. The region owes its economic success mainly to the production of red wine; it is home to around 1,500 vineyards.

Mouton Cadet Bordeaux Rose is Made from combination grape of Merlot 65%, Cabernet france 20% and Cabernet Sauvignon 15%. Brilliant deep pink in color, wild strawberry and red fruit on aroma. The attack is full and round, dominated by fresh fruit. A pleasant and succulent wine, it has a supple and elegant tannin structure and a fresh, remarkably long finish that lingers on red fruit flavour. Food and wine pairing

Veal sweetbreads, celeriac, grilled fish, barbecued sea bream, grilled poultry with herbs, chicken brochettes marinated in lemon juice, Provencal dishes, non-spicy Asiatic food.

name: Cantine Petrosino Petros Syrah
wine_color: This Syrah shines a deep ruby with violet glimmers.
desc_en_short: Vibrant Sicilian Syrah with bold cherry and raspberry notes, silky tannins, and elegant spice. Perfect with rich Italian dishes and red meats.
desc_en_full:
Take a journey to the sun-drenched landscapes of Sicily with Petros Syrah 2020 — a wine that captures the wild beauty of the Mediterranean in every glass, balancing richness and elegance effortlessly.

Tasting Notes: This Syrah shines a deep ruby with violet glimmers. Bursting with ripe cherries, raspberries, and a whisper of black pepper, the palate is smooth and full-bodied, framed by velvety tannins and a polished finish. The fruit purity and vibrant texture make it both powerful and refined.

Winemaking & Terroir: Grown in the volcanic soils of western Sicily under the Mediterranean sun, the grapes are harvested at optimal ripeness to capture their natural vibrancy. Fermentation in stainless steel preserves the fresh fruit character, while a touch of aging softens the tannins and adds elegance.

Food Pairings & Serving Tips: Perfect with Ragu alla Bolognese, grilled lamb chops, and rich red meat dishes. Also excellent with medium-aged cheeses like Pecorino or Manchego. Serve slightly cool at 16-18°C to enhance its fruit and spice layers.

Aging Potential & Cellaring: Enjoy now for its youthful vibrancy or cellar for up to 5-6 years for a more integrated, complex profile.

desc_en_short: Elegant Pinot Noir from Languedoc with delicate red fruits, silky tannins, and a refreshing finish — perfect for light meats and cheeses.
desc_en_full:
Domaine Boyer de Bar, Pinot Noir - Les Rives de l'Estang , is a refined and expressive Pinot Noir from the sun-drenched vineyards of Languedoc-Roussillon. Crafted to highlight the delicate side of this noble grape, this wine delivers freshness, elegance, and effortless drinkability — perfect for casual meals or elegant dinners alike.

Tasting Notes: Showing a deep ruby red hue, this Pinot Noir opens with a bouquet of bright red cherries, raspberries, and subtle hints of violets and earth. On the palate, it is light to medium-bodied with silky tannins and a refreshing acidity that carries flavors of juicy red fruits, soft spices, and floral undertones. The finish is smooth and lingers with finesse.

Winemaking & Terroir: Grapes are carefully sourced from Languedoc-Roussillon’s coastal vineyards, where cooler breezes help preserve freshness in the fruit. Fermentation in stainless steel emphasizes purity and aromatics, followed by aging to enhance balance without adding heavy oak influence. The result is a Pinot Noir that is vibrant, elegant, and true to its variety.

Food Pairings & Serving Tips: A versatile wine that pairs beautifully with cold cuts, roast poultry, beef carpaccio topped with parmesan, and semi-soft cheeses such as Saint Nectaire, Livarot, and Charolais. Ideal serving temperature is 12-14°C to maintain its bright, fresh character.

Aging Potential & Cellaring: Best enjoyed now for its lively fruit and floral qualities but can be cellared for up to 3-4 years for more mellow and earthy nuances.

The Power of Fire and Earth in a Glass Bodega Volcanes

de Chile was founded on one simple yet profound belief — that Chile’s volcanic soils give life to wines of unparalleled energy, minerality, and expression. “Born from Fire” is more than a motto; it’s a philosophy that celebrates the dramatic landscapes that shape Chile’s terroir.

Sourced from vineyards in Central Valley and Maipo Valley, where ancient volcanic activity has enriched the soils with minerals and ash, this Cabernet Sauvignon captures both strength and finesse. The high-altitude vineyards produce grapes that achieve full ripeness while retaining vibrant acidity, resulting in wines that are powerful yet balanced.

Fermented and matured in French oak barrels, this Cabernet reflects the perfect equilibrium between ripe fruit, spice, and subtle wood influence — a true representation of Chile’s volcanic soul, where nature’s extremes meet elegance and craft.

desc_en_full:
Borsao Selección Garnacha is often called the “benchmark of value in Spanish wine” — a bottle that proves that great craftsmanship doesn’t have to come with a grand price tag. Produced by Bodegas Borsao, founded in 1958 in Campo de Borja (Aragón), the estate has become world-famous for its mastery of the Garnacha grape, or Grenache as it’s known in France.

The vineyards lie at the foothills of the Moncayo Mountains, where warm days and cool nights nurture grapes of perfect ripeness and natural balance. Aged partly in French oak, this wine showcases the intensity and charm of old-vine Garnacha — fruit-forward, generous, and full of Mediterranean soul.

Praised by critics as one of the best value reds on earth, Borsao Selección captures the energy of Spain: vivid, honest, and full of life.

name: Catena D.V. Historico Red Blend
desc_en_short: A vibrant, polished red combining mountain freshness with old-world grace.
desc_en_full:
Catena D.V. Catena Histórico Red Blend is a heartfelt homage to the vision of Don Domingo Vicente Catena, who first believed that Mendoza’s high-altitude vineyards could produce wines rivaling the world’s best. Created by the Catena Zapata family, Argentina’s most revered wine dynasty, this blend embodies over a century of passion and innovation.

The term “Histórico” (meaning “historic”) reflects the family’s long legacy of winemaking since 1902, when they began planting vines in the foothills of the Andes Mountains. The vineyards — located between 950 and 1,500 meters above sea level — benefit from pure sunlight, cool mountain nights, and alluvial soils rich in limestone, creating wines of purity and balance.

This red blend combines Malbec, Bonarda, and Petit Verdot, each variety adding a distinct voice: Malbec for depth and velvet, Bonarda for brightness, and Petit Verdot for structure. The wine is aged for 12 months in French oak, resulting in a complex, elegant expression that bridges Argentina’s history with its contemporary excellence.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild Mouton Cadet Majorie Collas
wine_color: Deep, brilliant ruby red with purple highlights.
desc_en_short: A limited-edition Bordeaux Rouge featuring a stunning artistic collaboration with Marjorie Colas, offering rich fruit and elegant spice.
desc_en_full:
Artistry in Paper and Vine.

The story of Mouton Cadet is one of vision and accessibility, dating back to 1930 when Baron Philippe de Rothschild decided to release a branded wine that would bring the prestige of Bordeaux to a wider audience. Today, it stands as a global ambassador for the region, celebrated for its consistency and its deep connection to the terroir. This limited edition continues the brand's long-standing tradition of uniting winemaking excellence with the world of art.

For this special cuvée, Mouton Cadet collaborated with Parisian paper artist and set designer Marjorie Colas. Known for her delicate, poetic sculptures that play with light and shadow, Colas designed the packaging to metaphorically depict the "four seasons of the grapevine." Her intricate paper-craft aesthetic mirrors the patience and precision required in the vineyards, transforming the bottle into a celebration of nature's cycle and the winemaker's hand.

Inside the bottle lies the classic Mouton Cadet Rouge, a wine crafted primarily from Merlot for roundness, supported by Cabernet Sauvignon for structure and Cabernet Franc for elegance. Sourced from partner winegrowers committed to sustainable viticulture, this vintage offers a modern expression of Bordeaux: fruit-forward, supple, and perfectly balanced—a true sensory tribute to the artistic spirit that adorns it.

name: Vasu Vasu Pinot Grigio Colline Pescaresi I.G.P.
flavor_tags: Green Apple, Mineral, Citrus, Apple, Pear
desc_en_short: Vasu Vasu Pinot Grigio is a fresh, light-bodied white from the Abruzzo hills — clean and approachable with crisp green apple, pear, subtle citrus, and a gentle mineral note on the finish.
desc_en_full:
Colline Pescaresi IGP covers the gentle hillside vineyards of the Pescara province in Abruzzo, central Italy, where cooler elevations temper the Mediterranean warmth and allow aromatic varieties like Pinot Grigio to develop freshness and definition.

Vasu Vasu's interpretation is clean, bright, and unoaked — fermented at low temperature to preserve its natural fruit character. Pale straw in colour, it delivers fresh aromas of green apple, ripe pear, citrus zest, and delicate white flowers. The palate is light and crisp, with lively acidity and a clean mineral finish that makes it effortlessly drinkable.

Ideal as an aperitif or paired with light seafood, grilled fish, salads, soft cheeses, and simple vegetable dishes.

name: Golden Paws Chardonnay
desc_en_short: A soft, fruit-forward, and lightly oaked Chardonnay, designed for easy drinking.
desc_en_full:
The Gentle Giant of Australian White Golden Paws Chardonnay is focused on delivering a balanced, appealing white wine.

It typically employs a slight touch of oak to round out the palate, but the emphasis remains on the ripe, accessible fruit of the South Eastern Australia region.

The style is designed to be highly versatile and easy to drink, appealing to a broad audience who appreciate a clean yet slightly richer style of Chardonnay. This Chardonnay is a simple, soft, and reliable wine for everyday enjoyment.

desc_en_full:
Step by Step to Quality 'Paso a Paso' (Step by Step) represents the meticulous process of improving wine quality from La Mancha. This Verdejo is a pure expression of the indigenous white grape.

Verdejo is known for its intense aromatic profile and characteristic savoury finish. The wine is made in an un-oaked, reductive style to emphasize freshness and aromatic purity.

This Verdejo is a clean and crisp wine. It is a highly food-friendly white wine.

name: Finca Fella Cala Rey Verdejo Sauvignon Blanc
desc_en_short: A lively white blend that fuses Verdejo’s herbal freshness with Sauvignon Blanc’s zippy citrus character — crisp, expressive, and perfect for warm days.
desc_en_full:
Highland Whites with a Zest for Life

At over 1,000 meters above sea level in Spain’s Alpera region, Finca Fella cultivates grapes with uncommon vibrancy. Their Cala Rey Verdejo Sauvignon Blanc blend draws from this elevation — where cooler nights lock in aromatic freshness and slow ripening allows for balanced complexity.

Verdejo, a Spanish classic, brings herbaceous lift and textural weight, while Sauvignon Blanc contributes brisk acidity and citrus-driven energy. Together, they form a bright, mouthwatering white that is as refreshing as it is versatile.

This cuvée captures the spirit of modern Spanish whites: expressive, honest, and crafted for everyday elegance. It’s a bottle that feels at home from patio lunches to seaside dinners — always ready to add a spark of freshness to any table.
