wine_color: Pale straw with delicate green highlights.
desc_en_short: A light, gently sweet Moscato bursting with fresh grape, peach, and floral notes—vibrant, refreshing, and effortlessly enjoyable.
desc_en_full:
Crafted by Jacob's Creek, one of Australia’s most recognized wine producers, this Moscato reflects a modern, fruit-forward style designed for easy enjoyment. Sourced from vineyards across South Eastern Australia, the warm sunshine encourages ripe, aromatic fruit while careful winemaking preserves natural freshness.

Made primarily from Muscat varieties, fermentation is controlled at low temperatures to retain aromatic intensity and delicate sweetness. A slight spritz enhances liveliness, making it a perfect choice for relaxed occasions, casual gatherings, and those who prefer a lighter alcohol style.

name: Nusa Cana  Tropical Island White Rum (700 ml)
food_matching: Tropical Fruit, BBQ, Spiced Desserts, Chocolate
desc_en_short: Inspired by the Island of the Gods. Blending vibrant and expressive Indonesian rum with mellow, aged, Caribbean rum, to create Nusa Caña
desc_en_full:
Nusa Cana

At Nusa Caña's heart is the strong, rich Indonesian Rum, known as Batavian Arrack. Centuries ago this evocative spirit was tipple of choice for European society, it was the first international rum, and is the godfather of the modern rum industry.

With their packaging design already in hand, the guys at Island Beverages commissioned Boldinc to go beyond the bottle and create an exciting lifestyle driven platform to launch Nusa Caña rum in it's native Bali and bring back the forgotten story of Indonesian rum.

desc_en_short: A lightly sparkling, aromatic, and beautifully balanced Pink Moscato.
desc_en_full:
Sweet Elegance, Naturally Free from Alcohol Edenvale redefines modern enjoyment with its collection of premium alcohol-removed wines, offering the pleasure of fine wine without the effects of alcohol.

Produced from South Australian Muscat grapes, this Pink Moscato captures the essence of sunshine, freshness, and celebration in every sip.

Using advanced spinning cone technology, the alcohol is gently removed at low temperatures to preserve the wine’s natural aromas, sweetness, and effervescence. The result is a delightfully refreshing, lightly sparkling wine, perfect for relaxed afternoons, picnics, or elegant occasions where flavor matters most.

name: Campo Viejo Cava Brut (750 ml)
desc_en_short: A vibrant and refreshing Spanish sparkling wine that offers fine bubbles, lively fruit and elegant balance — a versatile choice for celebrations or casual pairing.
desc_en_full:
Sparkling Spirit from Catalonia’s Traditions

The wine comes from a renowned Spanish house founded in 1959 that built its reputation on quality and consistency across many styles. Though best known for red Rioja, the producer applied its winemaking expertise to the classic sparkling tradition of northeast Spain, embracing the local heritage of Cava.

Using indigenous grapes Macabeo, Xarel·lo and Parellada, the Brut is crafted by the traditional method known in Champagne world — with second fermentation in bottle and careful aging on lees — to offer a bright, aromatic and balanced sparkling wine suitable for many occasions.

The result is a friendly, expressive Cava: easy to enjoy on its own, yet versatile enough to complement tapas, fish, rice, mild meats or cheese — a testament to Spain’s capacity to offer quality bubbles beyond champagne.

name: Riedel  Superleggero : Champagne Glass
desc_en_short: Recommended for: Blanc de Blancs, Cava, Champagne, Franciacorta, Kōshū, Prosecco, Sekt, Sparkling Wine
desc_en_full:
The Champagne Wine Glass is perfect for developing and displaying champagne’s complex characteristics. This glass allows the wide range of aromatics to unfold. Unlike a flute, the egg-shaped design allows the complex layers to grow and intensify, and the larger rim diameter enables them to be released in a way which is not achieved with a narrow glass. The glass also includes a 'sparkling point' to aid the formation of the Champagne bubbles.

This glass is perfect for light, fresh, dry champagnes, proseccos and sparkling wines.

This product is made of clear crystal glass. RIEDEL Superleggero is a masterpiece of state-of-the-art technology, confirming RIEDEL's position as a technical pioneer. The perfection of premium machine-made glass compared to handmade lies in the precision of the bowl and rim diameter and its consistent quality, guaranteeing a product to maximally support wine enjoyment. The collection includes the largest, thinnest, and lightest glasses with the widest base diameter ever produced on machine. Perfectly balanced and with the look and feel of a handmade glass. Each glass carries a grape-varietal specific code on the center of the base to identify the grape-variety.

This item is premium machine-made. “PREMIUM” defines the advantage of a machine blown RIEDEL glass vs. a hand-made glass. The difference lies within the precision.

name: Bols  Triple Sec (700ml)
desc_en_short: BOLS Triple Sec is a crystal clear liqueur flavoured with Curaçao orange peel and hints of citrus. Excellent in cocktails and mixed drinks. Well-known for its use in the Margarita cocktail.
desc_en_full:
BOLS Triple Sec is a crystal clear liqueur flavoured with Curaçao orange peel and hints of citrus. Bols Triple Sec is without a doubt the most essential liqueur in a modern bar. At least half of all classic and modern drinks are made with Triple Sec or a variant, like Dry Orange Curaçao. From the Sidecar, the Long Island Iced Tea and the White Lady to the Cosmopolitan and the Kentucky Lemonade, Bols Triple Sec has always delivered the full flavour of Curaçao oranges. 
     
     The secrets of the recipe for Triple Sec Curaçao have been carefully preserved by Bols through the centuries. Many of these recipes are based on prescriptions for medicines. In the old days ship's doctors would mix some triple sec through their medicines in order to make them more palatable. They consequently played an important part in spreading the popularity of this liqueur.
     
    Straight, on the rocks, excellent in cocktails and mixed drinks. Well-known for its use in the Margarita cocktail.

name: Bols  Apricot Brandy (700 ml)
desc_en_short: BOLS Apricot Brandy uses fresh apricot juice supported by tones of orange to give it a richer, fuller flavour that matches its golden-amber colour.
desc_en_full:
BOLS Apricot Brandy

BOLS Apricot Brandy uses fresh apricot juice supported by tones of orange to give it a richer, fuller flavour that matches its golden-amber colour.

Bols Apricot Brandy has been the apricot liqueur for a very long time: as well as being the original apricot liqueur in the Mai Tai, Zombie and Apricot Fizz, it is surprisingly tasty just with orange juice or soda.

Colour : Golden Amber.

Aroma : Mild aroma of juicy apricots.

Flavour : Distinctly apricot with a hint of cognac and almond.

Drinking : Straight, on the rocks, excellent in cocktails and mixed drinks.

name: Averna  Amaro Siciliano (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Averna is an excellent liqueur with full body and smooth taste, extremely pleasant for every moment of the day.
desc_en_full:
Averna is an excellent liqueur with full body and smooth taste, extremely pleasant for every moment of the day. You can drink it or taste it straight, with ice or frozen. It is produced using an ancient recipe made of herbs, which has been passed down from one generation to another since 1868.

Each of the most delicate phases of the production of the bitter, all of which take place in the herbalist's laboratory, are stringently checked in order to guarantee the authenticity of the ingredients and the high quality of the product. This is why Averna is one of the most famous bitters appreciated worldwide.

name: Aultmore  12 Years Old Speyside Single Malt Scotch Whisky (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Part of Dewar's The Last Great Malts range launched in 2014, this 12-year-old Aultmore is the first release from the distillery for a few years and is elegant, light and fruity.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Notes

Nose : Fresh and vibrant with crunchy apples and pears, floral notes and gentle creaminess.

Palate : Richer than the nose suggests, with fruit, sweet pastry, custard and more floral notes.

Finish : Medium length, with the fruit slowly fading to leave soft spice.

name: Laphroaig  Quarter Cask Islay Single Malt Scotch Whisky (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Laphroaig® Quarter Cask is twice aged to amplify deep peat and add a surprising sweetness. Contact with the wood of smaller quarter-sized casks makes it our most lively expression.
desc_en_full:
BIG FLAVOUR FROM SMALL BARRELS

Bigger barrels don’t mean better flavour. The proof is Laphroaig® Quarter Cask, inspired by the old tradition of transporting whisky on packhorses. Fortunately for said horses, it was stored in smaller quarter casks that carry flavour into your dram today. Their reduced size means the spirit has more contact with the wood, amplifying the natural flavours of whisky first matured in ex-Bourbon barrels. Hints of coconut, banana and vanilla complement our instantly recognisable heavy peat flavour, followed by a long, sweet and smoky finish. This quarter cask whisky is barrier filtered to retain all the character developed in our small but mighty barrels. Enjoy a soft and velvety single malt full of unexpected vivid notes, bigger flavour and aroma. ​

Tasting Notes

Colour : Full sparkling gold

Nose : Smouldering peat with aromas of coconut and banana​

Palate : Deep, complex, smoky. And a surprising gentle sweetness. 

Finish : Really long, drying with appropriate smoke and spice.​

name: Umani Ronchi  Cùmaro – Conero Riserva DOCG
wine_color: Deep ruby red colour with garnet tints.
desc_en_short: Cùmaro is one of the most important wines in the story of Umani Ronchi, and it can also be numbered among the founder members of the family of fine wines from the Conero area.
desc_en_full:
Cúmaro - Conero Riserva DOCG

It was created in the mid eighties to celebrate the Montepulciano variety of vine, and an area close to the sea with highly calcareous soils, using methods based on very strict selection of grapes in the vineyard and what were, at least for then, modern techniques of vinification and ageing. The name Cùmaro is a tribute to Mount Conero, komaros in Greek, which means 'arbutus': an evergreen shrub which grows freely in its woodlands. Since 2004 the wine has been entitled to the appellation DOCG (Guaranteed and Controlled Denomination of Origin) Conero Riserva.

name: bodegas Valduero  Reserva Premium " 6 Anos"
desc_en_short: A powerful and age-worthy Ribera del Duero with concentrated black fruit, refined oak, and profound structure—intense, elegant, and matured for six years to achieve remarkable depth.
desc_en_full:
Crafted by Bodegas Valduero, one of Ribera del Duero’s respected family-owned estates, the Reserva Premium “6 Años” represents extended maturation and uncompromising quality.

Made primarily from old-vine Tempranillo (locally called Tinto Fino), the wine undergoes six years of ageing—combining extended barrel maturation with additional bottle ageing. Ribera del Duero’s high-altitude vineyards, extreme day–night temperature variation, and limestone-clay soils produce grapes with intense concentration and natural structure.

name: Chateau Haut-Brion  1er Grand Cru Classe 2019
wine_color: The color is a beautiful purplish red.
desc_en_short: A magnificent and refined wine from one of Bordeaux’s oldest estates; Château Haut-Brion 2019 balances richness and structure, offering deep complexity with poise and aging potential.
desc_en_full:
Heritage Meets Precision at Haut-Brion

Among the oldest and most storied of Bordeaux’s First Growths, Château Haut-Brion stands out not only for its legacy but also for its unique location in the southern Graves, apart from its Left Bank peers. Its vineyards, with deep gravelly soils, have been cultivated for over five centuries, producing wines admired by royalty, philosophers, and connoisseurs alike.

The 2019 vintage emerged from a season marked by early flowering, dry midsummer, and optimal ripening conditions, followed by careful harvest selections. The balance between the richness of Merlot and the structure of Cabernet, enhanced by fragrant Cabernet Franc, resulted in a wine of exceptional depth and elegance.

Château Haut-Brion 2019 is both a reflection of the estate’s terroir and a tribute to its continuous pursuit of excellence. While powerful in youth, it promises an unfolding of layers with decades of cellaring, revealing the soul of Pessac-Léognan in every glass.

name: Torbreck The Pict 2006
wine_color: Deep, glass-staining ruby with garnet hues.
desc_en_short: A powerful, single-vineyard expression of Barossa Mataro that combines richness with wild, earthy complexity.
desc_en_full:
A Celtic Tribute to Ancient Earth

Torbreck Vintners, founded with a deep affinity for the Rhône Valley and a nod to Scottish heritage, named this cuvée after the Picts—the "painted people" of ancient Scotland who defied the Roman Empire. This name reflects the wild, rugged, and unyielding character of the Mataro grape (Mourvèdre), a variety often relegated to blending but here given center stage to showcase its raw power and earthy soul.

The 2006 vintage of The Pict is sourced from a tiny, dry-grown vineyard block in the Northern Barossa, planted in the 1920s on soil rich in clay and ironstone. These gnarled, ancient vines struggle for survival, producing tiny berries with incredibly thick skins and intense concentration. The wine is vinified with traditional techniques, including the use of open fermenters and basket pressing, before aging in new French oak to polish its formidable structure.

This wine stands as a testament to the potential of Mataro in the Barossa Valley, proving it can rival the great wines of Bandol. It captures the essence of the hot, dry Australian summer, delivering a wine that is dense, savory, and profound—a bold expression of history, both ancient and viticultural.

name: Champagne Charles Orban  Blanc de Noirs Brut
desc_en_short: A very fresh nose with strong plum and blackberry aromas. Powerful on the palate, with wild berry aromas enhanced by hints of cinnamon.
desc_en_full:
History

The Orban family has been growing grapes in Troissy, a village on the left bank of the Marne, upstream from Dormans, since 1770. Troissy's vineyard covers 331 hectares, mostly planted with Pinot Meunier (86%). Pinot Noir and Chardonnay represent 8% and 6% respectively. This region is in fact recognized for its Pinot Meunier, but our estate's vineyard has the privilege of cultivating the three types of grape in order to ensure the authenticity of our vintages.

Towards the end of the 1950s, soon after he got married, Charles Orban left the Troissy cooperative and decided to produce his own champagne and sell it under his own name. He even went as far as preparing his grafts himself.

In about 1960, Charles bought a press and built a fermenting room to become completely independent and able to create a HOUSE STYLE. Over the years, from 1949 until the present day, production has increased from 800 to 200,000 bottles. At the end of the 20th century, the house was sold to a champagne family that has managed to preserve and perpetuate Orban family tradition.

name: Champagne H.Blin  Brut Rose
desc_en_short: Fresh, with aromas of wild strawberry. Rich and dense, with notes of forest fruit. A beautiful finish, with a certain tension showing through.
desc_en_full:
Champagne H. BLIN is a union of vintner (Vincelles Cooperative) founded in 1947 by Henri BLIN and 28 other growers.

Located in Vincelles in Marne Valley (25 minutes west of Epernay and Reims), Champagne H. BLIN is known for its cuvees with a strong identity, crafted to express their unique character. These wines are based around the historic local grape variety, Pinot Meunier.

Our 110 hectares of vines, planted around Vincelles, are predominantly Pinot Meunier (70%), but we also grow Chardonnay (16%) and Pinot Noir (14%).

name: Franzini Prosecco DOC Extra Dry
wine_color: A beautiful pale yellow color with fine, lively bubbles.
desc_en_short: Elegant Prosecco from Veneto, Italy — light, fresh, and bubbly with floral and fruit notes. A perfect choice for celebrations, aperitifs, and desserts.
desc_en_full:
Franzini Prosecco DOC Extra Dry is a refined sparkling wine crafted in the heart of Veneto , Italy — a region renowned worldwide for producing some of the finest Prosecco. Made from 100% Glera grapes, this wine embodies Italian elegance and festive charm, perfect for every joyful occasion.

Tasting Notes: In the glass, it shows a beautiful pale yellow color with fine, lively bubbles. The nose opens with delicate aromas of rose petals, crisp green apple, and subtle hints of dried fruit. The palate is light, refreshing, and perfectly balanced between sweetness and acidity, finishing with a soft and pleasant touch.

Winemaking & Terroir: Sourced from vineyards in Veneto’s hills, where cool breezes and mineral-rich soils create an ideal environment for Glera to thrive. Made using the Charmat method to preserve its fresh and fruity character, with a fine mousse that enhances its elegance and drinkability.

Food Pairings & Serving Tips: A versatile sparkling wine, ideal as an aperitif, with appetizers like seafood, shrimp cocktail, or smoked salmon. Pairs beautifully with light desserts such as fruit tarts and panna cotta. Serve well-chilled at 10°C for the best experience.

Aging Potential & Cellaring: Best enjoyed fresh within 1-2 years for optimal aromatics and bubbles.

name: Perrier-Jouet  Blason Rose (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Perrier-Jouët Blason Rosé is like a rose petal, with a delicate texture on the top and, hidden underneath, the veins which bring strength, structure and tension.
desc_en_full:
Legacy of the “Blason”

Cuvée Founded in 1811, the house of Perrier‑Jouët has long cultivated elegance, floral expression and refined Champagne style. This cuvée draws on that heritage and adds a rosé dimension anchored by the depth of Pinot‑Noir and the freshness of Chardonnay.

The “Blason” name evokes the prestige cuvées introduced in 1955; this rosé version carries that tradition into a more daring, meal‑ready expression of Champagne that defies the usual light rosé stereotype.

Created for gastronomy and bold pairings, it confidently plays across a whole menu—not just as an aperitif or dessert wine—but elegantly through to richer savoury dishes and red‑meat accompaniments.

name: Zonin Prosecco Rose Brut
desc_en_short: A bright, light‑hearted sparkling rosé with fruity clarity and crisp finish.
desc_en_full:
A New Rosé Chapter in Prosecco Territory In the rolling vineyards of Veneto (Italy), the historic house of Zonin expands its sparkling line‑up with this rosé version of Prosecco. The wine blends the region’s signature Glera grape with a portion of Pinot Nero (Pinot Noir) to give it that delicate pink hue and added complexity.

The method : Glera and Pinot Nero are harvested the same season, vinified separately (Glera as white‑press, Pinot Nero red pressed) and then undergo secondary fermentation using the Charmat (tank) method for at least 60 days — resulting in lively, fresh bubbles and straightforward charm.

name: Masottina Contradagranda Prosecco Superiore Brut DOCG
wine_color: Pale yellow with greenish highlights and fine, lingering perlage.
desc_en_short: A top-tier, structured, and dry Prosecco Superiore, showcasing the finesse and minerality of the DOCG hills.
desc_en_full:
The Grand Counterpoint 'Contradagranda' refers to a special vineyard zone in the heart of the Conegliano Valdobbiadene DOCG hills. This bottling is one of Masottina's finest, focused on the unique minerality and structure imparted by the steep slopes.

The Brut style emphasizes a drier, more structured palate. This allows the delicate aromatics of the Glera grape to be framed by the wine’s high natural acidity and subtle mineral character.

This Prosecco Superiore Brut is a complex and focused sparkling wine. It stands as proof of the seriousness and quality potential of the DOCG region.

name: Jacob's Creek Reserve Sparkling Rose (750 ml)
desc_en_short: A fresh and elegant Australian sparkling rosé with vibrant fruit and soft mousse — perfect for any celebration or relaxed gathering
desc_en_full:
A Sparkling Rosé of Australian Sun and South Australian Fruit

From the sun-drenched vineyards of South Australia, the Reserve range was created to capture the best expressions of regional fruit and fine winemaking. Each vintage, select Pinot Noir and Chardonnay grapes are chosen for their lively acidity and vibrant flavor to form the backbone of this sparkling rosé.

Using traditional sparkling winemaking techniques, the wine undergoes careful fermentation and lees aging to enhance complexity and texture. The result is a wine with fine, persistent bubbles and a creamy finish that complements its fresh berry character.

Jacob’s Creek Reserve Sparkling Rosé offers a refined yet approachable bubbly experience. Whether toasting a special occasion or enjoying a sunny afternoon, this wine brings Australian brightness and balance to every glass.

name: Rockford  Local Growers Semillon
desc_en_short: Aromas and richness of citrus, lemon, lanolin, toasty oak hum. Good body and acidity. Creamy texture with a touch of butter and almond.
desc_en_full: Aromas and richness of citrus, lemon, lanolin, toasty oak hum. Good body and acidity. Creamy texture with a touch of butter and almond.

name: Robert Mondavi  Woodbridge Pinot Grigio
desc_en_short: Delightful aromas of nectarine, fresh peach, and citrus. Well-rounded with bright, acidic, and flavorful notes, this crisp white wine pairs well with seafood and other light dishes.
desc_en_full: Delightful aromas of nectarine, fresh peach, and citrus. Well-rounded with bright, acidic, and flavorful notes, this crisp white wine pairs well with seafood and other light dishes.

name: William Fevre  Saint-Bris
desc_en_short: Fresh, intense bouquet with asserted blackcurrant bud and boxwood notes. Fresh and round on the palate, with spontaneous expression. A wine with exquisite lightness.
desc_en_full: Fresh, intense bouquet with asserted blackcurrant bud and boxwood notes. Fresh and round on the palate, with spontaneous expression. A wine with exquisite lightness.

name: Montecillo Albarino
wine_color: Crystalline and with a seductive brilliance, a lemon yellow tone with greenish highlights.
desc_en_short: A vibrant and refreshing Albariño with bright citrus, white peach, and saline minerality—crisp, aromatic, and beautifully coastal.
desc_en_full:
Produced by Bodegas Montecillo, this Albariño reflects the fresh Atlantic character of Rías Baixas in northwestern Spain. While Montecillo is historically known for Rioja wines, its Albariño showcases the estate’s commitment to regional expression beyond Rioja.

Rías Baixas is shaped by cool maritime influence, granite soils, and high humidity, conditions that produce Albariño with vibrant acidity and distinctive saline minerality. Fermentation in stainless steel preserves aromatic intensity and fruit purity, resulting in a clean, crisp white wine that captures the freshness of the Galician coast.
